En: I'll help you all right.
Zh: “我会帮助你的。”

En: I didn't see a case in the church story.
Zh: “我在教堂没听说过这种情况。”

En: I've yet to make up my mind.
Zh: “我还没想好呢。”

En: A shaggy-looking girl in her late teens or early twenties kneels on the ice with an electronic contraption in her hand. Hearing you approach, she looks up.
Zh: 一个十几或二十出头的、头发蓬乱的女孩正跪在冰上，手中拿着一个电子装置。听到你走近，她抬起头来。

En: Dear child, it's freezing. Where's your hat?
Zh: “亲爱的小朋友，这里真冷啊。你的帽子呢？”

En: So, you wanna *party*?
Zh: “所以，你想参加*派对*吗？”

En: I have some questions for you.
Zh: “我有几个问题想问。”

En: Huh?
Huh? She looks up at you, distracted.
Zh: “哈？”她心烦意乱地抬头看着你。

En: I said, you should have a hat on.
Zh: “我是说，你应该戴上帽子。”

En: It's cold out here, but she's not wearing a hat! She must be freezing.
Zh: 外面天寒地冻，她却没戴帽子！她一定冻坏了。

En: So should you.
Zh: “你也一样。”

En: Come to think of it -- I *should*. Where would I get one around here?
Zh: “仔细想想——我的确*需要*。这附近哪里能买到呢？”

En: But I am! (Point at your hat.)
Zh: “但我有戴呀！”（指着你的帽子。）

En: I should -- and I do. (Point at your hat.)
Zh: “我要的——我也有。”（指着你的帽子。）

En: I don't know, some kind of a... store? She pauses to think. "Maybe a general store... Look man, fuck the hat."
Zh: “我不知道，某个……店里吧？”她停下来思索了一会儿。“杂货店里可能会有……听着老兄，干你的帽子去吧。”

En: Is that kind of language really necessary?
Zh: “这样的用语真的合适吗？”

En: *I* don't have to do anything. I am the law. *You* have to do what the law says.\n
Zh: “*我*不需要做任何事。我就是法律。反倒是*你*，必须按照法律说的做。”

En: *I* don't have to do anything, I'm a police officer. *You*, however, have to do what I say.
Zh: “我不需要做任何事，我是个警察。反倒是*你*，必须按照我说的去做。”

En: Real cool... She looks down at the device, mumbling under her breath.
Zh: “真的挺酷的……”她低头看向设备，低声咕哝着。

En: Way to abuse that power, man... she says, thinking you can't hear her.
Zh: “这是滥用职权，老兄……”她自以为你听不见地说着。

En: She bats away your questions like flies. She's not intimidated by mere police officers.
Zh: 她像闪躲蝇虫一般回避了你的问题，她可不会被区区警察吓到。

En: Oh, I didn't notice that.
Zh: “哦，我没注意。”

En: I'm sorry I said 'fuck the hat.' I was concentrating on something else...
Zh: “抱歉我说了‘干你的帽子’。我在专心做别的事呢……”

En: It's nice. You should wear one, too, if you plan on staying outside in this weather.
Zh: “感觉不错。如果你打算在这种天气待在室外的话，也应该戴一个。”

En: Now that that's all cleared up, I have some questions for you.
Zh: “现在一切清楚了，我有几个问题想问你。”

En: We're done here. [Leave.]
Zh: “这里没什么事了。”[离开。]

En: I'm done here. [Leave.]
Zh: “我结束了。”[离开。]

En: And the law says you have to wear a hat in this weather.
Zh: “法律规定在这种天气里你必须戴上帽子。”

En: So keep that in mind while I ask some questions.
Zh: “所以在我提问的时候，请记住这一点。”

En: Tsk... She lets a quiet *tsk* roll off her tongue. "What kind of law says that?"
Zh: “啧……”她轻轻地*啧*了一声。“这算什么法律规定？”

En: The Hat Law.
Zh: “帽子法。”

En: We're done here. Wear the hat or get sick, I don't care. [Leave.]
Zh: “这里没什么事了。戴上帽子或者感冒，随便你。”[离开。]

En: That's it, I'm going. Wear a hat or catch a cold, I don't care. [Leave.]
Zh: “算了，我走了。戴上帽子或者感冒，随便你。”[离开。]

En: What are we even talking about?
Zh: “我们到底在说什么？”

En: The Law of Seasons. You'll get sick if you don't.
Zh: “季节法则，否则你会生病的。”

En: Man, I have no clue, either.
Zh: “老兄，我也不明白啊。”

En: This is not a good questioning technique.
Zh: “这可不是好的提问技巧。”

En: Yeah, well... She tries to think of something to say, but can't. "Look man, fuck the hat."
Zh: “这个……”她想说点什么，却又无从开口。“听着，老兄，干你的帽子去吧。”

En: Yeah, well... She tries to think of something to say. "Look man, fuck the hat."
Zh: “这个……”她努力想说点什么。“听着，老兄，干你的帽子去吧。”

En: Sorry, man. My mind was elsewhere. She puts the device down. "Shoot."
Zh: “抱歉，老兄，我没注意。”她放下了设备。“该死。”

En: What? She looks up at you, confused.
Zh: “什么？”她困惑地抬头看你。

En: (Say it again louder, she might not have heard you over the wind.) "DO YOU WANT TO PARTY?!"
Zh: （再大声说一遍，她在风中可能听不到。）“你想参加派对吗？！”

En: Nothing. I have some questions for you.
Zh: “没什么，我有几个问题想问你。”

En: I said, 'You should have a hat on.'
Zh: “我是说，‘你应该戴上帽子。’”

En: Yeah, I heard you, but what do you *mean* by 'party'?
Zh: “是啊，这句我听到了，但你说的*派对*是什么意思？”

En: Yes, what *do* you mean? The lieutenant sounds serious.
Zh: “是啊，你*是*什么意思？”警督听起来很严肃。

En: I mean: we get drinks, come back here, sit on the frozen sea ice and drink until we start talking about fucked-up emotional stuff. The *worst*.
Zh: “我的意思是：我们弄点喝的，回到这里，坐在冰冻的海面上喝起来，直到我们开始讨论那些糟糕的感情问题。*最糟糕*的那种。”

En: I mean: we buy drinks, and, right there in the liquor store, we get those bad boys in us, and then *you* tell *me* what you mean when *you* say *party*!
Zh: “我的意思是：我们买点喝的，就在旁边卖酒的店里，等酒意正酣，*你*就告诉*我*你口中的*派对*是什么意思！”

En: Thing is, I haven't said *party*...
Zh: “问题是，我没有说过要*派对*……”

En: You will once you get those bad boys in you.
Zh: “一旦酒劲上头，你就会了。”

En: ... and neither have you. Had you let me in on the script of this play before you performed it, I could have gone along. As it stands, the entire line of questioning has been compromised.
Zh: “……而且你也没说过。如果你在做戏前让我看一眼剧本，我是会同意的。照目前的状况来看，整个审讯流程都被打乱了。”

En: We have nothing left to do but hope this *was* a play on your part -- and to move on. Now. He turns to the young woman. "We have some questions for you."
Zh: “除了希望*这是*你的鬼把戏之外，我们别无选择——只能继续了。现在。”他转向年轻的姑娘。“我们有几个问题要问你。”

En: (Whisper.) "Kim, stop it, you're spoiling everything!"
Zh: （低语。）“金，住手，你把一切都搞砸了！”

En: Um, yeah, and I would like to ask some questions now. So, questions.
Zh: “呃，是啊，我现在想问你几个问题。那么，问题。”

En: I mean: we get drinks -- and we also get drugs.
Zh: “我的意识是：我们有喝的——还有毒品。”

En: You know, that sounds kinda nice...
Zh: “这其实听起来挺不错的……”

En: No, it doesn't.
Zh: “不，并不好。”

En: Oh, of course. She doesn't sound surprised.
Zh: “哦，当然。”她听起来并不惊讶。

En: Odd. Something about the way she says it makes you think she's not bothered by any of the cop-show questioning.
Zh: 奇怪。她说话的方式让你觉得她根本不在乎警察秀的那种盘问。

En: You. The lieutenant turns to you. "If you were intending to use an unorthodox questioning technique for this branch of the investigation -- which, by the way, is tangential at best -- you should have let me in on it."
Zh: “你。”警督转向你。“如果你打算对这个分支调查使用非正统的提问技巧——顺带提一句，最多是扯一些无关紧要的东西——最好先让我知道。”

En: We have some questions for you.
Zh: “我们有几个问题想问你。”

En: Uh... we'll be back later. [Leave.]
Zh: “呃……我们还会回来的。”[离开。]

En: Questions, right! I had some for you.
Zh: “问题，没错！我有些要问你。”

En: Like what? She straightens out slightly, propping herself up with her hands.
Zh: “比如呢？”她微微伸直身子，用手支撑着身体。

En: Let's talk about your contact mic again.
Zh: “我们再来谈谈你的接触式麦克风吧。”

En: What's that device you have there?
Zh: “你那是什么设备？”

En: What are you doing out here in the cold?
Zh: “你在大冷天里干什么呢？”

En: You seem surprisingly comfortable with being questioned. Why's that?
Zh: “就算被质问，你好像也挺自在的，为什么呢？”

En: Go ahead.
Zh: “说吧。”

En: Believe me, dude, it's better we talk about something else.
Zh: “相信我，兄弟，我们还是谈点别的吧。”

En: Sure.
Zh: “当然。”

En: Tell me more about this *music place* you've been planning in the church.
Zh: “再跟我多说些你们计划在教堂里建个*音乐场所*的事吧。”

En: Actually, that's it for now. [Leave.]
Zh: “实际上，暂且先这样吧。”[离开。]

En: So you talked to my associates, right? Are you gonna help us? With the church, I mean.
Zh: “所以你跟我的同伴们谈过了，是吗？你愿意帮助我们吗？我是指教堂的事。”

En: So, like... The girl on the ice looks up at you. "Seriously, what's eating you, man?"
Zh: “那么……”冰上的女孩抬头望着你。“说真的，你在困扰什么老兄？”

En: There is a hint of pity in her eyes.
Zh: 她的眼睛里有一丝怜悯。

En: I think it's the plight of the working class.
Zh: “我想这就是工人阶级的困境。”

En: Everyone's just mooching off the entrepreneurial class. Shackling the *doers*.
Zh: “每个人都在搜刮企业家阶级的油水。给*实干家*戴上了镣铐。”

En: I think it's... all these *foreign people* taking our jobs.
Zh: “我想是因为……那些*外国人*抢走了我们的工作。”

En: Exactly. I don't wanna talk about it.
Zh: “没错。我不想谈这个。”

En: You sure?
Zh: “你确定吗？”

En: Yes, I'm sure. Now, I had some questions for you.
Zh: “是的，我确定。现在，我有几个问题想问你。”

En: Actually....
Go ahead...
So, the thing that's got you crying in front of strangers... is social justice?
Zh: “所以，让你在陌生人面前嚎啕大哭的事……是社会公义？”

En: Wow. Social justice really matters that much to you? That's commendable. It really had you shaken up there... are you *sure* that's it, though?
Zh: “哇哦，社会公义对你来说真有那么重要吗？真是值得褒奖。它真的让你十分激动呢……不过，你*确定*就这些吗？”

En: I haven't seen much of this world, but from what I've seen *social justice* is an adolescent term. Sounds almost liberal... what's got me shaken up is the *people's struggle* and it's got me shaken up *bad*.
Zh: “我的眼界还不够开阔，但在我看来*社会公义*就是个青少年用语，听起来几乎等同于自由了……真正让我激动的是*人民的斗争*，这让我*非常*震惊。”

En: Yeah, man, they're pretty bad, she says, without much conviction.
Zh: “是啊老兄，真是糟透了，”她毫无说服力地说了一句。

En: It makes me sick, thinking about the thousands... millions... billions? How many people are there, actually?
Zh: “真让我感到恶心，一想到有成千上万……上百万……上亿？实际上有多少人来着？”

En: Um...
How many people are there in the world?
Zh: “世界上一共有多少人？”

En: Three-point-six billion, not counting those on Seol.
Zh: “不算西奥人的话，一共36亿。”

En: Really? That many?! Reduces me to tears thinking of the 3.6 billion and god knows how many more in that Seol place, crushed under the tyranny of the market.
Zh: “真的吗？那么多的吗？！想到那36亿人，还有天知道那个西奥还有多少人，全部都被市场的暴政压垮了，我真是眼泪都要掉下来了。”

En: Yeah, that's pretty bad, she nods. "Are you sure you're not just hung up on some chick though?"
Zh: “是啊，真是糟透了，”她点点头。“不过，你确定自己不是为了某个小妞在黯然伤神吗？”

En: So, the thing that's got you crying in front of strangers... is rich people not having enough money?
Zh: “所以，让你在陌生人面前嚎啕大哭的事……是富人的钱还不够多？”

En: So, the thing that's been eating you... is rich people not having enough money?
Zh: “那么那个一直在困扰你的事……是富人的钱还不够多？”

En: You can roll your eyes all you want, but will it bring us any closer to a knowledge-based economy? I haven't seen much of this strange world, but: Not with these taxes it won't!
Zh: “你想怎么翻白眼都可以，但是这样就能让我们更接近一个以知识为基础的经济吗？我可能对这个奇怪的世界了解不多，但是：有了这么些税收，那是不可能的！”

En: Then you really haven't seen much of this *strange world*, because we literally haven't got any income taxes. Just a 20% value-added tax and that's, like... *it*.
Zh: “那你还是真没怎么见识过这个*奇怪的世界*，因为我们其实没有缴纳任何所得税。只征收了20%的增值税，还有，比如……*这个*。”

En: No way! You have no idea how many taxes there *really* are -- you've never paid any! There are all these disguised, invisible taxes. Look, there's one right now! (Point to the stained glass window.)
Zh: “不可能！你根本不知道*究竟*有多少税种——你从来没有缴过！它们都是经过伪装，无形的苛捐杂税。看吧，那边就有一个！”（指向彩绘玻璃窗。）

En: No way! You have no idea how many taxes there *really* are -- you've never paid any! There are all these disguised, invisible taxes. Look, there's one right now! (Point at the sea.)
Zh: “不可能！你根本不知道*究竟*有多少税种——你从来没有缴过！它们都是经过伪装，无形的苛捐杂税。看吧，那边就有一个！”（指向大海。）

En: Startled, she turns her head and looks at the window. No modes of indirect taxation catch her eye. She looks back at you.
Zh: 她吓了一跳，转头看着窗户。她没能发现任何间接征税的痕迹，于是又看向你。

En: Startled, she turns her head and looks at the sea. No modes of indirect taxation catch her eye. She looks back at you.
Zh: 她吓了一跳，转头看着大海。她没能发现任何间接征税的痕迹，于是又看向你。

En: You have an issue with... glass art tax?
Zh: “你对玻璃艺术税……有异议吗？”

En: You have an issue with... the sea monster tax?
Zh: “你对海怪税……有异议吗？”

En: No. Not *just* the glass artwork tax. It's all of it taken together that's strangling economic growth.\n
Zh: “不，不*仅仅*是玻璃艺术税。所有这些因素加在一起，扼杀了经济增长。”

En: No. Not *just* the sea monster tax. It's all of it taken together that's strangling economic growth.
Zh: “不，不*仅仅*是海怪税。所有这些因素加在一起，扼杀了经济增长。”

En: Uh huh. She collects herself. "And none of this has anything to do with -- oh I don't know -- some chick?"
Zh: “嗯哼。”她回过神。“而这一切都跟——我说不好——某个小妞无关吗？”

En: So, the thing that's got you crying in front of strangers is... national pride?
Zh: “所以，让你在陌生人面前嚎啕大哭的事……是民族自豪？”

En: Yeah, man, she nods. "That's why you were crying over a hat, yelling about regaining your authority. National pride."
Zh: “是啊，老兄，”她点点头。“所以你会因为一个帽子哭得淅沥哗啦，叫喊着要重新获得自己的权威。民族自豪。”

En: That's those foreign ideas messing with your head. You don't even realize you're *literally* on your knees right now! Our women -- literally on their knees!
Zh: “那是因为那些外国思想扰乱了你的大脑。你甚至都没有意识到你其实*已经*跪倒在地了！我们的女人——已经跪下了！”

En: Man, that's because my equipment's on the ground.
Zh: “老兄，那是因为我的设备都在地上呢。”

En: I'm pretty sure I've got issues with our young women kneeling in the snow with this *cosmopolitan* racket going on in the background. (Nod towards the stage.)
Zh: “我很确定自己对我们的年轻女性跪在雪地里、背景还是那种*世界主义*的屁话感到不满。”（朝着站台点头。）

En: I'm pretty sure I've got issues with our young women kneeling in the snow with this *cosmopolitan* racket going on in the background. (Nod towards the tent.)
Zh: “我很确定自己对我们的年轻女性跪在雪地里、背景还是那种*世界主义*的屁话感到不满。”（朝着帐篷点头。）

En: Or, or... I'm just gonna put this out there -- are you sure you're not having *chick* issues, with some chick?
Zh: “或者，怎么说呢……我还是直说吧——你确定自己不是对某个*小妞*有意见？”

En: Okay, why do you think that?
Zh: “好吧，你怎么会这么想呢？”

En: They were written in a woman's hand. And oh boy did reading them make me not feel good.
Zh: “上面是女人的笔迹。哦天呐，看这些东西真让我不舒服。”

En: First, they had just the faintest scent of chewing gum on them. I could still smell it under the... shit.
Zh: “首先，那上面还残留着一点口香糖的味道。我还能闻到……该死。”

En: Wow, man. She raises her eyebrow. "That's pretty symbolic, don't you think?"
Zh: “哇哦，老兄。”她扬起眉毛。“你不觉得这很有象征性吗？”

En: To hell with this. Questions -- I had them. And you have answers.
Zh: “见鬼去吧。问题——我已经想好了，而你也有答案。”

En: Okay, of course... What did you want to know?
Zh: “好吧，当然……你想知道什么？”

En: Yes, I found that to be very symbolic too.
Zh: “是的，我也觉得这很有象征性。”

En: No. Why?
Zh: “不觉得，为什么？”

En:
Used to be sweet, now it's shit -- seems pretty symbolic to me. Anyway, what else?	“曾经很甜美，现在却臭不可闻——在我看来就很有象征性。总之，还有什么事吗？”
0x0100000400002812	Dialogue Text	ICE / ACELE	Acele	Okay, what else?"
Zh: “好吧，还有什么事吗？”

En: There you have it then -- chick trouble. Not political after all. Who was she?
Zh: “这样就明白了——小妞的问题，终究跟政治无关。她是谁？”

En: There you have it then. Chick trouble. Who is she?
Zh: “这样就明白了，小妞的问题。她是谁？”

En: I don't remember.
Zh: “我不记得了。”

En: Really? She appears to believe you. "You seem pretty upset about this *chica*... are you sure you don't remember anything about her?"
Zh: “真的吗？”她似乎是相信了。“你看上去对那个*妞*很烦恼……你真的一点也记不起来了吗？”

En: Yup. Next to nothing.
Zh: “是啊，什么都不记得了。”

En: I remember her scent and that's all.
Zh: “我还记得她的气味，仅此而已。”

En: Wow, man. That's some pretty strange shit... She rubs her temple in thought. "Are you sure the letters were for you?"
Zh: “哇哦老兄，那可真是怪了……”她揉着太阳穴思索着。“你确定这些信是写给你的吗？”

En: Wow, man. That's some pretty strange shit... She rubs her sides for warmth. "Are you sure the letters were for you?"
Zh: “哇哦老兄，那可真是怪了……”她搓着身体取暖。“你确定这些信是写给你的吗？”

En: Yeah, I'm sure. Why would I have reacted so strongly otherwise?
Zh: “是啊，我确定。不然我的反应为什么会如此强烈呢？”

En: Come to think of it -- the *whore* could have written them to her *lover*!
Zh: “仔细想想——那个*婊子*可能是写给她的*情人*的！”

En: C'mon, man. Don't be like that. I mean, maybe she did, but you don't know that.
Zh: “拜托老兄，别这样。我是说，就算事情真是这样的，但你也不知道呀。”

En: What's there to know in this shit hole, anyway?
Zh: “这个烂地方有什么好问的？”

En: How come you don't remember though? Is it, like, some selective memory thing?
Zh: “你怎么会不记得了？是，那种，选择性记忆吗？”

En: Beats me. She exhales, slowly, pondering. A moment passes.
Zh: “难倒我了。”她缓缓地呼吸着，沉思着，一段时间过去了。

En: Beats me. She exhales a puff of shimmering cold air. A moment passes.
Zh: “难倒我了。”她呼出一片闪着微光的冷气，一段时间过去了。

En: Her hair is dyed blonde, with dark roots showing, and she wears thick black eye-liner. Most men wouldn't call her pretty -- there is a manliness to her. A coarseness.
Zh: 她的头发染成了金色，发根处透着黑，还画着浓烈的黑色眼线。多数男人不会称赞她漂亮——因为她有些男子气概，有点粗糙。

En: Wait, what do you mean by *selective memory*?
Zh: “等等，你说的*选择性记忆*是什么意思？”

En: I think it's more about me getting so unbelievably drunk I completely erased all memory of this world.
Zh: “我感觉更像是因为我喝的太醉，以至于我对这个世界的所有记忆完全被抹去了。”

En: Man, when I get hurt I just wanna forget that shit, you know? That kind of selective memory.
Zh: “老兄，当我受伤的时候，也只想忘记那些破事。你懂的，这种选择性记忆。”

En: You might have a point there. Selective memory.
Zh: “也许你说的也有点道理。选择性记忆。”

En: You're right. It doesn't help.
Zh: “你说得对，这没什么用。”

En: How come you don't remember though? Is it, like, some *selective memory* thing?
Zh: 她点点头。“不过你怎么会不记得了呢？是，那种，*选择性记忆*吗？”

En: Yeah. Or it might be that. This one time I did so much... booze that I forgot, too...
Zh: “是啊，也可能是这样。有次我喝了实在太多……酒，也忘了……”

En: It's obvious she's "done" more than "booze."
Zh: “显然，她*喝*的不止是酒。”

En: Or it might just be psych bullshit, you know? Königstein wank.
Zh: “或许也可能是心理上的鬼扯，柯尼希斯什么的。”

En: What is this *Königstein wank*?
Zh: “*柯尼希斯*是什么？”

En: Alright, I had some questions for you.
Zh: “好了，我有几个问题想问。”

En: You know: the psych thing they've got going on there. Rich people like it. People in Königstein are mostly rich.
Zh: “你懂的：所谓的心理问题，有钱人的常见病。柯尼希斯人大多都很有钱。”

En: Thanks for the bullshit psych thing then.
Zh: “那真是谢谢你告诉我了。”

En: You know, I'm not sure this made things any better.
Zh: “不过，我不觉得这会让事情变得更好。”

En: Yeah, I'm sorry. She nods. "Might be for the best to keep that shit forgotten, actually. Just my opinion. If it itches, don't scratch."
Zh: 她点点头。“是啊，我很抱歉。其实吧，也许忘了那些破事反而最好。只是我的个人意见。痒处勿挠。”

En: You're welcome. She thinks for a second, stretching her jaw. "Might be for the best to keep that shit forgotten though. Just my opinion. If it itches, don't scratch."
Zh: “没关系。”她想了一会儿，扬起下巴。“那些破事还是忘记的好。在我看来，痒处勿挠。”

En: The tape recorder lies on the ice like a discarded toy. Pick it up.
Zh: 录音机如同被丢弃的玩具一般躺在冰面上，把它捡起来。

En: Angry at Acele.
Zh: 冲阿西尔发火。

En: Gave her a hat.
Zh: 给她一顶帽子。

En: More like Contact Mike now.
Zh: 现在更像一击即溃·迈克了。

En: Icosahedral Red Die.
Zh: 二十面体红骰子。

En: The device feels light. An angular "Omicron" logo adorns the yellow plastic cover. Underneath you see a reel of tape rolling. You put the device back on the floor.
Zh: 装置感觉很轻，黄色的塑料外壳上装饰着一个棱角分明的*奥米龙*标志。你注意到底下有一卷正在转动的磁带，便又把装置放回到地板上。

En: The device is cold to the touch. An angular "Omicron" logo adorns the yellow plastic cover. Underneath you see a reel of tape rolling. You put the device back on the ice.
Zh: 装置摸上去冰凉，黄色的塑料外壳上装饰着一个棱角分明的*奥米龙*标志。你注意到底下有一卷正在转动的磁带，便又把装置放回到冰上。

En: Yeah, man... The lieutenant suddenly appears to your right with his collar popped *insanely* high.
Zh: “是啊，老兄……”警督突然出现在你右边，他的衣领竖得*高耸入云*。

En: Got any dope? We need some dope bad. He scratches his nose, then his armpit through the jacket. "I got the *Boogie Street* shakes."
Zh: “有药吗？我们急需嗑一点儿。”他挠了挠鼻子，又隔着夹克挠起了咯吱窝。“我对*布吉街*上瘾了。”

En: Um... Kim? What are you doing?
Zh: “呃……金？你在干什么？”

En: Wow!
Zh: “哇哦！”

En: Let the lieutenant do his thing.
Zh: 让警督做自己的事。

En: Tryin'a score some *dope*, man. Like we planned. Tryin'a score some smack -- you got any? He hops from one foot to the other in anticipation.
Zh: “我想搞点毒品，老兄，正如我们计划的那样。想搞点白粉呢——你有吗？”他满怀期待地单脚蹦跶着。

En: Tryin'a score some *dope*, man. Tryin'a score some smack -- you got any? He hops from one foot to the other.
Zh: “我想搞点*毒品*，老兄，想搞点白粉呢——你有吗？”他单脚蹦跶着。

En: What's *smack*?
Zh: “*白粉*是什么？”

En: The *D*, man, gotta hit that D!
Zh: “*粉*，伙计，得吸点*白面*！”

En: What's... 'the D'?
*Diamorphine*, girl. Quit clownin'. We need a hook-up for that D... He breathes in through his teeth.
Zh: “*海洛因*，姑娘，别犯傻了。我们得快点搞到白面……”他咬牙切齿地喘息着。

En: Kim, she seems legitimately confused. Tell her what this *D* is.
Zh: “金，她看起来是真的很困惑。告诉她*白面*是什么吧。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Diamorphine? But that hasn't been around for years -- five or more, like, seven years maybe? Everybody just does *hunch* now.
Zh: “海洛因？但它已经绝迹很久了——五年或者更久，得有七年了？现在大家都嗑*灵感*了。”

En: Really? What's that?
Zh: “是吗？那是什么？”

En: Hunch is like ten times stronger than diamorphine and *waaay* more lethal. I think the name is... B-hydroxy-something.
Zh: “灵感的效果比海洛因强十倍，而且*更加*致命。好像是叫羟基什么的……”

En: Okay. The lieutenant stops hopping. "That's... that's real bad. Someone should definitely look into that..."
Zh: “好吧。”警督停止了跳动。“那……那真是太糟了，得有人去调查一下才行……”

En: He rolls his collar down to normal human level and pulls out his familiar notebook. "We're from the police, by the way."
Zh: 他把衣领翻到正常人的高度，掏出了他熟悉的笔记本。“顺便说一句，我们是警察。”

En: Oh, of course. She doesn't look surprised.
Zh: “哦，当然了。”她看起来并不吃惊。

En: Something about the way she says it makes you think she's not bothered by any of the cop-show questioning.
Zh: 她说话的方式让你觉得她根本不在乎警察的盘问。

En: What is going on?
Zh: “怎么回事？”

En: Yes, and we have some questions for you.
Zh: “是的，我们有些问题想问你。”

En: Uh... we'll be back later. [Leave.]
Zh: “呃……我们还会回来的。”[离开。]

En: It's not that she has to run around and make money, it's more like she's concerned about her well-being. It is also not entirely because she's afraid of you. You can't really put your finger on it.
Zh: 并不是说她必须奔波赚钱，而是她更关心自己的幸福生活，也不完全是因为她害怕你。你不能这么说。

En: For some reason, the words don't come out. Instead you instinctively touch your collar to check your necktie.
Zh: 出于某种原因，这些话没说出口。相反，你本能地摸向你的衣领，以检查自己的领带。

En: It's crooked again, it always is. It's a traffic accident and a noose. Also, you should ask if she wants to party. Or better yet, ask her about that hat! Lest the necktie think you're boring...
Zh: 又搞砸了，总是这样，这是次車祸和圈套。另外，你应该问她是否想要参加派对，或者问她帽子的事！免得被领带认为你很无聊……

En: (Bad necktie! Let go of the tie.) "I wanted to ask you some questions."
Zh: （坏领带！松开领带。）“我想问你几个问题。”

En: I thought you were *cool*. What happened, man?
Zh: “我还觉得你*挺不错*的呢，怎么了，老兄？”

En: Okay, bye. She turns her attention back to the recording device.
Zh: “好吧，拜拜。”她把注意力转回录音设备上。

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: I know. Her teeth rattle. She takes the device from you and places it in her lap. "I'll stick to it."
Zh: “我知道。”她的牙齿咯咯作响，又从你手里拿过设备，放回自己的腿上。“我会坚持的。”

En: The dark.
Zh: “黑暗。”

En: Put the tape recorder down.
Zh: 放下录音机。

En: Your words echo through the hall. The wooden boards all around groan and creak. She remains silent.
Zh: 你的话在大堂里回响着，四周的木板嘎吱作响，她沉默着。

En: The wind howls. She remains silent.
Zh: 狂风咆哮，她沉默着。

En: Nothing, if you got this. Don't be scared.
Zh: “虚无，如果你能明白的话。不要被吓到了。”

En: The device is still warm from her touch -- and heavy as a brick, from the batteries inside. The company logo "Omicron" adorns its yellow plastic cover. Inside, the tape is rolling -- the girl looks at the device in your hands.
Zh: 设备还残存着她的体温——里面的电池沉重地像块砖头。黄色塑料外壳上装饰着公司的标志*奥米龙*。里面，磁带仍然转动着——女孩看着你手中的设备。

En: Okay. Her teeth rattle. She takes the device from you and places it in her lap. "I'll stick to it."
Zh: “好吧。”她的牙齿咯咯作响，又从你手里拿过设备，放回自己的腿上。“我会坚持的。”

En: What is...? Her shoulders shake a little.
Zh: “是什么……？”她的肩膀微微颤抖。

En: It is not a childish fantasy. It can be a real weapon against what's coming for you now.
Zh: “这不是幼稚的幻想。在你面对即将到来的挑战时，它可以成为真正的武器。”

En: I'm once again reminded of how Contact Mike rose from the slums of Saint-Batiste to the top of the boxing world, overcoming adversity and serious brain trauma. Nothing is coming -- nothing he wouldn't knock out in three rounds. The real fight is for the right attitude.
Zh: “我再次回想起一击即溃·迈克是如何从圣巴蒂斯特的贫民窟，克服重重困难与严重的脑损伤，最终站在拳击世界之巅的。永远不会——不会有三轮之内不被他击倒的东西。真正的斗争其实就是端正态度。”

En: The people who built this world intended it to be better for you, but they failed. It is easier to live in their failure with this by your side. (Tap on the tape recorder.)
Zh: “我相信那些建造了这个世界的人，本想给你带来更好的生活，但他们失败了。身边有了这个，生活在他们的失败中会变得容易一些。”（敲着录音机。）

En: I can't believe this turned into another Mike thing... Fine, okay, I'll stick to it. She takes the device from you and places it in her lap. "I'll knock it out in three rounds."
Zh: “真不敢相信这又扯到迈克身上去了……行了，好吧，我会坚持的。”她从你手里拿过设备，放回自己的腿上。“我会在三回合之内战胜它。”

En: I'm sorry you have to sit here on the ice with the drugs wearing off. At your age -- or at any age -- in this weather... waiting for it to get dark.
Zh: “很遗憾，毒品的效力消散，你却不得不坐在冰上。以你的年龄——或任何年龄——在这种天气里……等待天黑。”

En: I'm sorry you have to sit here on the ice, feeling miserable. At your age -- or at any age -- in this weather... waiting for it to get dark.
Zh: “很遗憾，你不得不坐在冰上煎熬着，以你的年龄——或任何年龄——在这种天气里……等待天黑。”

En: She looks you in the eye, her pupils wide, surrounded by a ridiculous amount of make-up.
Zh: 她望着你的眼睛，瞳孔放大，眼周是一圈厚厚的化妆品。

En: There is little you can do to help her now, but given the chance you feel like you *should*. There is something about her. A weightiness.
Zh: 现在你几乎是无能为力的，但如果有机会，你认为自己*应该*帮忙。她有些特别，有些重要。

En: And the crab-man hasn't shown himself much, thank god.
Zh: “谢天谢地，蟹男还没露面呢。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Maybe she didn't hear you. A little louder.
Zh: 也许她听不见你说的话。再大声一点。

En: You don't *have* to do anything.
Zh: 你*不需要*做任何事。

En: Your pulse rises.
Zh: 你的脉搏加速了。

En: He's really shaking and everything!
Zh: 他真的在发抖！

En: Interesting.
Zh: 有意思。

En: B-hydroxy-phenothiazine. Somewhere in the soggy remains of your factual memory, you know the name. From the streets.
Zh: B-氢氧根-硫代二苯胺。在你的真实记忆那被水浸透的残余部分里，你知道这个名字。来自街头。

En: It's the dark lord himself, put into chemical form. Even I don't suggest it.
Zh: 这是黑暗之主本人，幻化成了化学形态。即便是我，也不会建议。

En: C'mon. She must have known it the moment you walked up to her.
Zh: 拜托。当你走向她的那一刻起，她肯定就已经知道了。

En: Teenagers skulking around. Gang-members looking for a hole in the Union's defences? Maybe Ruby too, but she wouldn't be able to tell from here.
Zh: 有些鬼鬼祟祟的年轻人。帮派分子在寻找工会防御里的漏洞？也许露比也是，但在这里她也看不出来。

En: Long shadows, short shadows. Quiet shadows of local children, loud shadows drunks and also a shadow made of sticks... you breathe on your hands for warmth.
Zh: 长长的阴影，短短的阴影。本地小孩那安静的阴影，醉汉那吵闹的阴影，还有树枝形成的阴影……你朝着手哈了口气，寻求温暖。

En: It's real. Tell her.
Zh: 这是真的。告诉她。

En: After a moment of silence she speaks again: "So, thanks. I guess. For the psych session. I guess that means we're... even?" She smiles a little.
Zh: 片刻沉默之后，她再次开了口：“所以，谢谢你。谢谢你的这堂心理课。我猜那就代表我们……扯平了？”她微微笑了笑。

En: Something changes between you two. She looks at you differently now -- as an equal. A fellow human being.
Zh: 你们之间有些东西变了。现在她对你的看法不一样了——是平等的。一个人类同胞。

En: She means your little talk before, after your breakdown. It's all right, she means to say. You returned the favour.
Zh: 她指的是你之前闲聊时说的那些话，在你崩溃之后。她是想说，没关系的。你得到了回报。

En: Hmmm... *perhaps*?
Well... think about, anyway. Was there something else?
Zh: “呃……总而言之，考虑一下吧。还有别的事吗？”

En: Feels *good* though, doesn't it? The sting.
Zh: 不过感觉*挺好*的，不是吗？这种刺痛。

En: Yeah, or it might just be psych bullshit, you know? Königstein wank.
Zh: “是啊，或者，你知道的，它可能只是精神病的胡话。柯尼希斯的亵渎。”

En: Yes, but it itches really, really bad...
Zh: 是的，但是这种痛楚真的非常，非常糟糕……

En: No idea. Haven't seen her, definitely haven't *called* her. I don't remember a single thing about her.
Zh: “不知道。没见过她，肯定没有给她打过*电话*。关于她的事，我一点都不记得。”

En: Really? She appears to believe you. "You seem pretty upset about this *chica*... are you sure you don't remember anything about her? Eyes? Hair colour?"
Zh: “真的吗？”她好像信了你的话。“你看起来对这位*女性*感到很不安……你确定真不记得关于她的任何事了？眼睛？发色？”

En: To the cops.
Zh: 警察。

En: A silver bird... feels strangely familiar. Was it an expression? And if yes, then for what? Then your mind slips and the thought is gone.
Zh: 一只银色小鸟……感觉有种奇怪的熟悉感。这是一种表达方式吗？如果是的话，又是什么呢？然后你的思想开了个小差，这种想法消失了。

En: She's honest.
Zh: 她很诚实。

En: C'mon. This is all fine, but you don't have anything on Evrart now. That's not how these things work.
Zh: 拜托。其实一切都还好，但是你现在还没拿到任何指向艾弗拉特的证据。事情不应该是这个样子的。

En: Hear that? Set justice on its feet. CONFRONT EVRART.
Zh: 听见了吗？让正义站稳脚跟。跟艾弗拉特对峙。

En: What's your name?
Zh: “你叫什么名字？”

En: I'm positive. Now, I had some questions for you before all this started.
Zh: “我确定。在开始之前，我有几个问题想问你。”

En: Oh, really?
Zh: “哦，是吗？”

En: The golem of capital runs rampant, smashing creator and slave alike. I fear the process is irreversible.
Zh: “资本的魔像横行，同时粉碎了创造者与奴隶。恐怕这个过程是不可逆转的了。”

En: Yeah, people just can't get off their asses -- zero hireable talent in this city. Zero. No wonder everyone's poor as potatoes.
Zh: “是啊，人们就是不行动起来——这座城市没有可雇佣的人才，没有。难怪大家都穷得揭不开锅了。”

En: Yeah, I can tell this was once a proud nation. Now all this internationalist bullshit has brought Revachol to its knees.
Zh: “是啊，我能看出这曾经是个骄傲的国家。现在，所有这些国际主义者的扯淡让瑞瓦肖屈服了。”

En: Oh, really?
Zh: “哦，是吗？”

En: Oh, really?
Zh: “哦，是吗？”

En: Yeah, pretty sure. Now, I had some questions about something else entirely.
Zh: “是啊，很肯定。现在，我还有几个完全无关的问题想问你。”

En: Now that you mention it, I found these letters I'd thrown in the trash. They *might* have something to do with it.
Zh: “既然你提到了，我发现了这些应该被扔进垃圾箱的信。它们*可能*会与此事有关。”

En: ... but I shouldn't, really. I have stuff planned for tomorrow. Gotta run around, try to make some money.
Zh: “……但我真的不行，我明天还有事呢。我得四处奔波，赚钱生活。”

En: Goddamnit, why's there no one to drink with by the seaside! (Raise your hands in lamentation.)
Zh: “该死，为什么没人跟我在海边喝酒呢！”（举起手来哀嚎。）

En: Sorry.
Zh: “抱歉。”

En: Oh well. You up for a conversation, at least?
Zh: “好吧，至少你有空跟我谈谈吧？”

En: We can do that.
Zh: “可以的。”

En: Money -- just gotta keep making it, huh? But, in the meantime -- care to tell me something of interest?
Zh: “钱——不得不继续努力，是吧？但与此同时——有兴趣告诉我一些事吗？”

En:
Like what?	“比如呢？”
0x0100001400001E1B	Dialogue Text	ICE / ACELE	You	There's some stuff I want to ask about."
Zh: “有些事情我想问问你。”

En: Okay, I guess I'll fuck off then. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，我是时候滚蛋了。”[离开。]

En: I'd better be going. [Leave.]
Zh: “我该走了。”[离开。]

En: Sorry, mister, I don't really do drugs. Especially with people I just met.
Zh: “抱歉，先生，我不吸毒的，特别是跟我刚认识的人。”

En: Don't we have a bit of a logical inconsistency here? A categorical *no* and a conditional *no*? Poor logic is a good sign of *lies*."
Zh: “我们是不是有点逻辑上的矛盾？一句无条件的*否定*和一句有条件的*否定*？糟糕的逻辑即为*谎言*的征兆。”

En: It just means that even if I did do drugs, I would not do them with a person I just met. She looks confused.
Zh: 她看起来有些困惑。“这只意味着，即便我吸毒，我也不会跟一个刚认识的人一起吸。”

En: Why would a categorical *no* need a conditional *no* to support it?
Zh: 为什么一句无条件的*否定*需要一句有条件的*否定*来支撑呢？

En: Maybe we should get acquainted, then?
Zh: “那么，或许我们应该认识一下？”

En: She looks up, not entirely sure what that means.
Zh: 她抬起头，不太确定你是什么意思。

En: What's with the not doing drugs thing? You done enough already? So many nice drugs left undone...
Zh: “不吸毒是什么情况？你已经吸够了？还有那么多好东西你没试过呢……”

En: Sorry? She looks confused.
Zh: 她看起来有些困惑。“什么？”

En: I'm going to have to accept your apology, aren't I? What else is there to do? Now tell me about stuff, who you are, what you're doing here, things like that.
Zh: “看来我只能接受你的道歉了，不是吗？不然还能如何？现在告诉我，你是谁，你在这里做什么这类的事吧。”

En: Where do I start?
Zh: “我要从何说起呢？”

En: Yeah, yeah. Sorry I asked. [Leave.]
Zh: “是啊，是啊，抱歉我开口问了。”[离开。]

En: Oh, I see. I'm not good enough for the lady. She's accustomed to more gentlemanly folk. Well, so be it. [Leave.]
Zh: “哦，我明白了。是我配不上那位女士，她更适合有绅士风度的人。得了，就这样吧。”[离开。]

En: A city has millions in it, so the world would have a lot more. I think I knew the number once...
Zh: “一座城市就有数百万人口，全世界会有更多，我以前是知道这个数字的……”

En: Too many millions to even know, all squashed under the tyranny of the market. It's terrible!
Zh: “数不清有多少个百万，都被市场的暴政压垮了。太可怕了！”

En: I'll help you all right.
Zh: “我会帮助你的。”

En: Great! She shivers. "Let us know if there's any progress, will you? We've been waiting for weeks here."
Zh: “太好了！”她颤抖着。“如果有任何进展请务必告诉我们，好嘛？我们已经在这里等了好几个星期了。”

En: She must be Acele, the last of the speedfreaks' 'posse'.
Zh: 她一定就是阿西尔，最后一个‘老铁’了。

En: You must be Acele.
Zh: “你一定就是阿西尔了。”

En: That's my name. I take it you have met the others. Did they tell you about the church and the club... thing?
Zh: “那是我的名字，我猜你已经见过其他人了。他们跟你说教堂和俱乐部……的事了吗？”

En: They did.
Zh: “说了。”

En: Church, what church? (Act confused.)
Zh: “教堂，什么教堂？”（表现出困惑。）

En: Will you help us?
Zh: “你能帮帮我们吗”

En: C'mon, quit playin'. Will you help us with the church?
Zh: “拜托，别演戏了。你愿意帮助我们进入教堂吗？”

En: I didn't see a case in the church story.
Zh: “我在教堂没听说过这种情况。”

En: Okay... but you'll still let us know if you learn something about the church, right? She rubs her sides. "We don't have anywhere else to go anyway."
Zh: “好吧……但如果你了解到教堂的情况，还是会通知我们的，对吗？”她搓了搓身体。“反正我们也无处可去了。”

En: The others told me you went inside the church. What did you see in there?
Zh: “其他人跟我说你去过教堂。你都看到了什么？”

En: I've yet to make up my mind.
Zh: “我还没想好呢。”

En: Take your time, I guess. We don't have anywhere else to go anyway.
Zh: “不着急，慢慢想。反正我们也没有别的地方可以去。”

En: I'd like to know more about your associates.
Zh: “我想多了解一些你的同伴。”

En: How did you know I'd met them?
Zh: “你怎么知道我见过他们？”

En: I, like, saw you go into the tent. It's right there.
Zh: “我，就是，看见你进了帐篷。就在那儿。”

En: Yes, your associates tried to use me to set up a drug lab and I'm guessing you knew of their plan.
Zh: “是的，你的同伴想利用我建一个毒品实验室，我猜你应该知道这个计划吧。”

En: Your associates tried to use me to set up a drug lab. I'm guessing you knew of this plan.
Zh: “你的同伴想利用我建一个毒品实验室，我猜你应该知道这个计划吧。”

En: Right.
Zh: “没错。”

En: She's waiting.
Zh: 她在等待着。

En: Can't say. Gotta ask questions first.
Zh: “说不好。我要先问你几个问题。”

En: What happens in the tent stays in the tent.
Zh: “帐篷里发生的事就留在帐篷里。”

En: That's cool by me. She nods knowingly.
Zh: “我觉得没问题。”她会意地点点头。

En: She looks prettier all of a sudden. The muscles of her face have relaxed. She must be quite pleased.
Zh: 她一下子变得漂亮许多，脸部的肌肉都放松了，她一定很高兴。

En: It's not that she has to run around and make money, it's more like she's concerned about her well-being. It is also not entirely because she's afraid of you. You can't really put your finger on it.
Zh: 并不是说她必须奔波赚钱，而是她更关心自己的幸福生活，也不完全是因为她害怕你。你不能这么说。

En: Sorry, I have stuff planned for tomorrow. Gotta run around, try to make some money.
Zh: “抱歉，我明天还有事呢。我得四处奔波，赚钱生活。”

En: Sure. Contact mic. She nods, staring at the recording device in her hands.
Zh: “当然，接触式麦克风。”她点点头，盯着手中的录音设备。

En: Excuse me, what did you just say?
Zh: “请原谅，你刚说什么？”

En: She looks down, pretending to be busy with the device.
Zh: 她低下头，假装忙着摆弄设备。

En: The kid isn't arrogant, she's just afraid to look you in the eye. You already put her in her place.
Zh: 她只是不敢直视你的双眼，这并非出于傲慢。你已经重挫了她的锐气。

En: (Kick some snow into her face with your boot.) "You did NOT just speak like that to the police."
Zh: （用脚把雪踢到她脸上。）“你不能像这样跟警察说话。”

En: I am the police. You would do well to avoid this kind of language with me.
Zh: “我是警察。你最好别跟我说这种话。”

En: I'm sorry, sir. I'm sorry. I was trying to concentrate on this thing here and I'm sorry I said that. My dad swears, my boyfriend swears... my whole family does. I've caught the habit. I didn't mean to... and I'm sorry.
Zh: “对不起，长官。我很抱歉。我只是专心在这里做事，很抱歉我说了那样的话。我爸爸会骂人，我男朋友也会骂人……我整个家庭都会。我也染上了这种习惯。我不是有意的……而且我很抱歉。”

En: She looks up with a pained expression, her face caked with a thick layer of make up. With her left hand she's wiping some of it off to get the snow out of her face.
Zh: 她抬起头，表情十分痛苦，脸上涂了一层厚厚的化妆品。她用左手擦掉了脸上的雪。

En: Her hair is dyed blonde, with dark roots showing. Most men wouldn't exactly call her pretty -- there is a manliness to her. A coarseness.
Zh: 她的头发染成了金色，发根处透着黑。大部分男人不会觉得她漂亮——她带着一丝男子气概。一种粗糙。

En: Now. I have some questions for you.
Zh: “现在，我有些问题要问你。”

En: She gathers herself for a moment, then says, "Okay."
Zh: 她收拾了一下情绪，然后开口说到，“好吧。”

En: You're right, I shouldn't have sworn. I'm sorry. She looks up like a scolded school girl, her years concealed beneath thick make up.
Zh: “你说得对，我应该发过誓的。我很抱歉。”她抬起头，就像个被训斥的女学生，她的年纪被厚厚的化妆品掩盖了。

En: Oh my god! FUCK THE HAT?! Is that what you just said to me? I can't *BELIEVE* you told me to fuck the hat!
Zh: “天啦！干你的帽子去吧？！你真这么跟我说话了？我简直不敢*相信*，你居然说干你的帽子去吧！”

En: You're saying it really loud, but it's not coming out right. Maybe add more indignation?
Zh: 你说的非常大声，但进展的并不顺利。也许应该再加一点愤慨？

En: So I should just... just... get a hat and FUCK it, right?! (Point at your head, where the hat should be.) "Engage in sexual intercourse with a hat right here in front of you, because you told me so? On the SEA ICE?!"
Zh: “所以我应该……就这么……找顶帽子，然后干它，是吗？！”（指着你的脑袋，帽子所在的位置。）“在你面前跟一顶帽子发生性关系，就因为你让我这么做？在海冰上？！”

En: RIGHT HERE ON THE SEA ICE?!
Zh: “就在海冰上？！”

En: GIVE YOU A LITTLE... ICE-COP-HAT-FUCK-SHOW?!
Zh: “给你上演一场……警察干帽子秀？！”

En: (Puff your cheeks as if you can't even *breathe* from the sheer indignity of the affront!)
Zh: （鼓起你的脸颊，就好像你被这样直接的侮辱弄到无法*呼吸*了！）

En: I didn't mean to, I'm sorry... The girl is visibly shaken.
Zh: “我不是故意的，抱歉……”这姑娘显然已经动摇了。

En: IS THAT HOW YOU SEE ME?
Zh: “在你眼中，我就是这个样子的吗？”

En: The girl looks down, slowly curling into a ball.
Zh: 女孩低下头，慢慢缩成了一团。

En: Start crying.
Zh: 开始哭泣。

En: Anyway... I can't believe you used obscenities like that in front of a police officer. And you should... you should...
Zh: “总之……我真是不敢相信，你居然在一个警官面前使用那种猥亵的言语。而且你应该……你应该……”

En: I didn't mean to, I'm sorry... The girl is visibly shaken.
Zh: “我不是故意的，抱歉……”这姑娘显然已经动摇了。

En: (Start jumping up and down on the sea ice, gesturing toward it with both hands, as if to say, *this* is where it was supposed to take place!)
Zh: （站在海冰上，开始上蹿下跳，用两只手朝它比划，好像在说，*这里*就是它应该发生的地方！）

En: The girl looks down, slowly curling into a ball.
Zh: 女孩低下头，慢慢缩成了一团。

En: Lots of feeling. Feel it up, *way* up.
Zh: 很多感觉在你心中激荡。感受它，*好好*感受。

En: More.
Zh: 再来。

En: MORE.
Zh: 不要停。

En: Across the bay, two figures watch the scene unfold through coin-operated binoculars. "My god," one says in a concerned voice. "Let me see," says the other.
Zh: 海湾对面，两个人影正透过投币式望远镜观看这一场面。“我的天啦，”一个人关切地说到。“让我看看，”另一个人说到。

En: Three figures on the spring ice. One, a shortish man, is standing with his shoulders slightly hunched. Another, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the third, a middle-aged man with spectacular sideburns, jumping up and down on the sea ice...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着三个人。其一是个稍矮的男人，站在那里，肩膀微微蜷缩着。其二的年轻女人正努力想要无视第三人，一个蓄着浓密鬓角的中年男人，正站在海冰上，上蹿下跳……

En: Two figures on the spring ice. One, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the other, a middle-aged man with spectacular sideburns, jumping up and down on the sea ice...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着两个人。一个年轻女子正努力想要无视另一个人，一个蓄着浓密鬓角的中年男人，正站在海冰上，上蹿下跳……

En: Two figures on the spring ice. One, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the other, a middle-aged man with bloated cheeks, jumping up and down on the sea ice...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着两个人。一个年轻女子正努力想要无视另一个人，一个脸颊肿胀的中年男子，正站在海冰上，上蹿下跳……

En: Three figures on the spring ice. One, a shortish man, is standing with his shoulders slightly hunched. Another, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the third, a middle-aged man with bloated cheeks, jumping up and down on the sea ice...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着三个人。其一是个稍矮的男人，站在那里，肩膀微微蜷缩着。其二的年轻女人正努力想要无视第三人，一个脸颊肿胀的中年男子，正站在海冰上，上蹿下跳……

En: That's it, you've earned it -- start crying, life's hard.
Zh: 就是它了，这是你应得的——哭出来吧，生活太不容易了。

En: Your shoulders have already tensed up with emotion. Let it all out.
Zh: 你的肩膀已经因为情绪而绷紧了。全都释放出来吧。

En: The lieutenant is watching the scene unfold before his eyes, unsure how to react.
Zh: 警督看着眼前的情景，不知道该如何是好。

En: The lieutenant is watching the scene unfold before his eyes, unsure how to react.
Zh: 警督看着眼前的情景，不知道该如何是好。

En: Two figures on the spring ice. One, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the other, a middle-aged man with spectacular sideburns, who's screaming at her with all his might...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着两个人。一个年轻女子正努力想要无视另一个人，一个蓄着浓密鬓角的中年男人，正拼命对着她尖叫……

En: Two figures on the spring ice. One, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the other, a middle-aged man with bloated cheeks, who's screaming at her with all his might...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着两个人。一个年轻女子正努力想要无视另一个人，一个脸颊肿胀的中年男人，正拼命对着她尖叫……

En: Three figures on the spring ice. One, a shortish man, is standing with his shoulders slightly hunched. Another, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the third, a middle-aged man with spectacular sideburns, who's screaming at her with all his might...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着三个人。其一是个稍矮的男人，站在那里，肩膀微微蜷缩着。其二的年轻女人正努力想要无视第三人，一个蓄着浓密鬓角的中年男人，正拼命对着她尖叫……

En: Three figures on the spring ice. One, a shortish man, is standing with his shoulders slightly hunched. Another, a young woman, tries her best to ignore the third, a middle-aged man with bloated cheeks, who's screaming at her with all his might...
Zh: 春季的冰面上站着三个人。其一是个稍矮的男人，站在那里，肩膀微微蜷缩着。其二的年轻女人正惊恐地抬头望着第三人，一个脸颊肿胀的中年男人，正拼命对着她尖叫……

En: This... isn't really about the hat, is it?
Zh: “这……其实跟帽子没关系，对吧？”

En: Looking up she sees a grown man -- on the wrong side of forty, his face bloated from alcohol and god knows what else -- not so quietly crying.
Zh: 她抬头望见了一个成年男子——已经过了40岁，他的脸部因为酒精或者其它东西浮肿着——哭的一点都不安静。

En: The young woman has kept her eyes trained on her wires, waiting for the sorry spectacle to end, but it's gone awful quiet up there. Then, the sound of sobbing...
Zh: 年轻女子的眼睛一直盯着她的电线，等待这令人遗憾的一幕结束，但那里却安静的可怕。紧接着，传来了啜泣的声音……

En: No...
Zh: “没关系……”

En: It is! It *so* is!
Zh: “有关系！*就*是的！”

En: Okay, sure. You're not the only one with issues. There's a motherly hint to her voice. "I self-medicate, shit... life's a horror, you know."\n
Zh: “好吧，当然。有问题的不只有你一个人。”她的声音中有一丝母性。“我就在自我治疗，该死……生活就是恐怖，懂吗？”

En: You know, you're not the only one with issues. There's a motherly hint to her voice. "I self-medicate, shit... life's a horror, you know."
Zh: “知道吗，有问题的不只有你一个人。”她的声音中有一丝母性。“我就在自我治疗，该死……生活就是恐怖，懂吗？”

En: Crying helps, though. Get it out of your system and then maybe we can talk, okay? I'll be here.
Zh: “不过，哭泣是有帮助的。好好发泄出来后我们再谈，好吗？我会一直待在这儿的。”

En: I swear to god, I will always wear a hat from now on.
Zh: “我向天发誓，从现在起我会一直戴着帽子的。”

En: Would it be alright... if I asked you some questions now?
Zh: “如果我现在想问你一些问题……可以吗？”

En: (Wipe away the tears.) "Anyway... I can't believe you would use obscenities like that in front of a... a police officer. And you should wear a hat, too."
Zh: （擦干眼泪。）“总而言之……我不信你会在一名……一名警察面前说出这种下流话。而且你应该戴上帽子。”

En: Good, then my work here is done. [Leave.]
Zh: “很好，那么我的工作就完成了。”[离开。]

En: Wait...

So I should just... just... take this hat I'm wearing and FUCK it, right?! (Point at your head, where the hat is located.) "Engage in sexual intercourse with a hat right here in front of you, because you told me so? On the SEA ICE?!"
Zh: “所以我应该……就这么……找顶帽子，然后干它，是吗？！”（指着你的脑袋，帽子所在的位置。）“在你面前跟一顶帽子发生性关系，就因为你让我这么做？在海冰上？！”

En: Your eyes are fixed on her as everything gets very still -- dangerously so...
Zh: 你用眼睛死死地盯着她，就好像一切都完全静止了——还很危险……

En: Make her meet your eyes.
Zh: 让她与你对视。

En: The words are stuck in your throat. All that comes out is tears, pouring down your face. The young woman has kept her eyes trained on her wires, waiting for the lecture to end, but now she hears sobbing...
Zh: 年轻女子的眼睛一直盯着她的电线，等待这令人遗憾的一幕结束，但那里却安静的可怕。紧接着，传来了啜泣的声音……

En: This... isn't really about the hat, is it?
Zh: “这……其实跟帽子没关系，对吧？”

En: Looking up she sees a grown man -- on the wrong side of forty, his face bloated from alcohol and god knows what else -- not so quietly crying.
Zh: 她抬头望见了一个成年男子——已经过了40岁，他的脸部因为酒精或者其它东西浮肿着——哭的一点都不安静。

En: (Wipe away the tears.) "And you should wear a hat, that's it, that's what I wanted to say."
Zh: （擦干眼泪。）“而且你应该戴上那顶帽子，就这样，我想说的就是这个。”

En: Good, then my work here is done. [Leave.]
Zh: “很好，那么我的工作就完成了。”[离开。]

En: Pain radiates through your leg. It still hurts to use it -- even for kicking snow into someone's face.
Zh: 疼痛在你的大腿里辐射开来。用它还是很痛——甚至是把雪踢到别人脸上。

En: I got shot in the leg. (Point at your limb.)
Zh: “我腿部中弹了。”（指向你的腿。）

En: Ouch. She looks at your leg. "I did notice you limping, but I thought maybe it was your 'thing' or something..."
Zh: “看着就疼。”她看着你的腿。“我确实注意到你一瘸一拐的，但是我想这可能是你的‘问题’或者什么的……”

En: When I was sixteen I used to date this guy who had a limp. But it only showed when he was sober, so I guess it wasn't real or something. I don't know. She shrugs, eyes glazed over...
Zh: “我16岁的时候，曾经跟一个腿有点瘸的家伙约会过。但是他只有在清醒的时候才会表现出来，所以我以为那并不是真的。我不知道。”她耸耸肩，目光呆滞……

En: Anyway… She turns back to you. "Shot in the leg... I'm sorry, man, that must suck."
Zh: 然后她转向你：“总之……腿部中弹……我很抱歉，老兄，那种感觉应该糟糕透了。”

En: That's good. No neighbours to complain about noise when you get the club going.
Zh: “那就好。你们俱乐部开张的时候就没有邻居抱怨噪音了。”

En: Remember those bold, new exotic revenue streams Evrart said he was going to fund his harbour with? You should go back and ask him about that.
Zh: 还记得艾弗拉特说要用那些大胆的、新的外国收益流来为港口提供资金吗？你应该回去问问他。

En: Like what? She straightens out slightly, propping herself up with her hands, waiting for you to speak.
Zh: “比如说什么？”她微微伸直身子，用手支撑着身体，等着你开口。

En: Like what?
Zh: “比如呢？”

En: Uh huh...
Never mind. [Leave.]
Zh: “算了吧。”[离开。]

En: The tape recorder lies on the wooden floorboards like a discarded toy. Pick it up.
Zh: 录音机如同被丢弃的玩具一般躺在木地板上，把它捡起来。

En: Angry at Acele.
Zh: 冲阿西尔发火。

En: Gave her a hat.
Zh: 给她一顶帽子。

En: More like Contact Mike now.
Zh: 现在更像一击即溃·迈克了。

En: Icosahedral Red Die.
Zh: 二十面体红骰子。

En: Nothing to emote over. You put the device back on the floor.
Zh: 没什么好看的了，你把设备放回地板上。

En: Nothing to emote over. You put the device back on the ice.
Zh: 没什么好看的了，你把设备放回冰上。

En: I'm sorry you have to sit here in the damp church with the drugs wearing off. At your age -- or at any age -- in this place... waiting for it to get dark.
Zh: “我很遗憾毒品的效力消散，你却不得不坐在潮湿的教堂里。以你的年龄——或任何年龄——在这种天气里……等待天黑。”

En: I'm sorry you have to sit here on this coast, feeling miserable. At your age -- or at any age -- in this place... waiting for it to get dark.
Zh: “我很抱歉你必须坐在这个海岸上，感觉苦不堪言。以你的年龄——或任何年龄——在这种地方……等待天黑。”

En: After a moment of silence she speaks again: "So, thanks. I guess. For the psych session. Maybe I can return it -- what's been eating you, officer?"
Zh: 沉默了一会儿后，她再次开口：“是啊，那谢了。感谢你这堂心理课。也许我能回报你一下——你有什么困扰，警官？”

En: C'mon, I can tell. She shakes her head slowly. "But, okay, be a boiadeiro about it if you want to."
Zh: “拜托，我看得出来。”她慢慢摇摇头。“但是，好吧，如果你愿意的话，那就假装自己不介意吧。”

En: Yeah, can we talk for a minute?
Zh: “是啊，我们能聊一会儿吗？”

En: I guess... there is something... that's been making my life hell.
Zh: “是啊……确实有事……让我的生活变得一团糟。”

En: What is it? She listens intently.
Zh: “是什么？”她专心听着。

En: As it stands, I'm going to have to thwart your plan by explaining to the woman that we are police officers.
Zh: “从目前的状况来看，我将不得不向那位女士解释我们警察的身份，从而挫败你的计划了。”

En: My 'associates'? She blows on her chilled fingers. "I haven't got much to say about them."
Zh: “我的‘同伴’？”她对着冻僵的手指吹了口气。“我真的没什么好说的。”

En: Feels like you missed something here. What was that about a bird? Forget it. Maybe later.
Zh: 你似乎错过了什么。那只鸟是怎么回事？忘了吧。也许之后再说。

En: Hey, there. She gives you a shy smile. "I've been recording some new audio from all these beams and rafters. The sounds travelling through the wood are pretty cool -- creaks and stuff. Like you're underwater, you know... But, like, underwater inside a tree."
Zh: “嘿。”她对你露出了羞涩的微笑。“我一直在横梁和椽子上录制一些新的音频，透过木头传递的声音很酷——嘎吱嘎吱的，好像在水下一样，知道吗……但好像，是在水下的一棵树里面一样。”

En: I really wanted to say: thanks for getting me and my associates in here.
Zh: “我真的想说：谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来。”

En: I really wanted to say: thanks for getting me and my friends in here. And we even found some new... associates, such as they are.
Zh: “我真的想说：谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来，我们甚至找到了一些新……同伴，比如他们。”

En: She's whistling a melody, her trusty contact mic attached to a wooden pulpit. At the sound of your footsteps, she stops what she was doing and turns to you.
Zh: 她吹着小曲，她那可靠的接触式麦克风正连在一个木质的讲道坛上。听到你的脚步声后，她停下了手上的动作并面向了你。

En: The girl stands behind the row of pulpits, whistling a melody with the electronic device pressed against the wood. At the sound of your footsteps, she stops what she was doing.
Zh: 女孩站在一排讲道坛后面，吹着小曲把电子设备压在木头上。听到你的脚步声后，她停下了手上的动作。

En: Welcome back. She nods to you in greeting.
Zh: “欢迎回来。”她向你点头致意。

En: Why does that trill sound familiar?
Zh: 为什么这份激动感觉很熟悉？

En: Good, I think. Noid is getting a read on the place, I think he finds the carpentry very impressive. Andre's been setting up the compressor and... dancing. Egg Head's keeping the party up, he's got the stage under control.
Zh: “很好，我认为。诺伊德正在了解这个地方，我想他肯定对这里的木匠工艺印象深刻。安德烈一直在安装压缩机和……跳舞。爱凡客负责让派对继续进行，他掌控着整个舞台。”

En: Soona, the programmer, she's doing... whatever she does, behind that radiocomputer of hers. She doesn't talk to us much. And the crab-man hasn't shown himself, thank god.
Zh: “苏娜，程序员，她正在……正在她的无线电脑后面，不管在干什么。她不经常跟我们说话。另外谢天谢地，蟹男还没露面呢。”

En: *Now* can you tell me about your associates?
Zh: “*现在*你能跟我说说你的同伴了吗？”

En: I'd like to know more about your associates...
Zh: “我想多了解一些你的同伴……”

En: I wasn't going to --
Zh: “我没打算——”

En: But Contact Mike is --
Zh: “但一击即溃·迈克——”

En: No-no. She cuts you off before you can finish the sentence. "I actually wanted to thank you. For getting me and my friends in here."
Zh: 你话没说完，就被她打断了。“不-不，其实我真要谢谢你。谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来。”

En: No-no. She cuts you off before you can finish the sentence. "I actually wanted to thank you. For getting me and my friends in here. And we even found some new... associates, such as they are."
Zh: 你话没说完，就被她打断了。“不-不，其实我真要谢谢你。谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来，我们甚至找到了一些新……同伴，比如他们。”

En: How's everyone doing?
Zh: “大家都怎么样？”

En: And yes, I'm being more like Contact Mike, alright. Don't even bring it up.
Zh: “没错，我更像一击即溃·迈克了行吧，别再提这件事了。”

En: This? She taps on the electronic device. "Yeah, it's a portable recording device. For field recording. Low quality, but still."
Zh: “这个？”她轻敲着电子设备。“没错，这是个便携式录音设备，用来做环境录音的。质量很差，但也能用。”

En: And the wires?
Zh: “那些电线呢？”

En: A contact mic is a microphone that records sounds from inside things. Like all this wood.
Zh: “接触式麦克风能从物体内部记录声音，比如这些木头里面。”

En: What is a *contact microphone*?
Zh: “*接触式麦克风*是什么？”

En: Actually just one wire, I picked on it 'til the braiding came loose. The wire leads to a contact microphone.
Zh: “其实只有一根线，我把编线挑开了。电线连着一个接触式麦克风。”

En: And no, it's got nothing to do with Contact Mike.
Zh: “不，这跟一击即溃·迈克没关系。”

En: Okay, fine.
Zh: “行吧。”

En: Oh, is that what the device is for?
Zh: “哦，这就是设备的用途吗？”

En: Gotcha.
Zh: “懂了。”

En: Why would anyone do that?
Zh: “为什么会有人这么做？”

En: Okay, it's an odd hobby, but who am I to judge.
Zh: “好吧，真是个奇怪的癖好。但我又有什么资格去评判呢？”

En: But that's not really what I wanted to talk about. I wanted to thank you instead.
Zh: “但这并不是我真正想说的，我只是想谢谢你。”

En: What for?
Zh: “为了什么？”

En: For getting me and my associates in here.
Zh: “谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来。”

En: For getting me and my friends in here. And we even found some new... associates, such as they are.
Zh: “谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来，我们甚至找到了一些新……同伴，比如他们。”

En: Makes sense. I've likely deserved some praise and adoration.
Zh: “有道理，我的确应该得到一些赞美和崇拜。”

En: Yeah, so thanks for getting me and my associates in here. She ignores your vapid egocentrism.
Zh: 她无视了你的自我为中心主义。“是啊，所以谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来。”

En: Yeah, so thanks for getting me and my friends in here. She ignores your vapid egocentrism. "And we even found some new associates, such as they are."
Zh: 她无视了你的自我为中心主义。“是啊，所以谢谢你带我和我的同伴进来，我们甚至找到了一些新同伴，比如他们。”

En: It's... it's not important, actually. Never mind, I actually wanted to thank you.
Zh: “这……这其实不重要。算了，我只是想谢谢你。”

En: Yeah, well... actually, it's not important. I actually wanted to thank you.
Zh: “这个嘛……这其实不重要，我只是想谢谢你。”

En: What do you mean? You must know *something* about them.
Zh: “你是什么意思？你一定对他们*有些*了解吧？”

En: Just answer the question, please.
Zh: “请回答我的问题。”

En: Of course I do. I just don't tell people about my friends and who they are and so on. I don't provide information on them.
Zh: “我当然知道。我只是不会跟别人聊起我的朋友，他们是什么样的人之类的。我不会提供他们的信息。”

En: Sorry. I just don't tell people about my friends and who they are and so on. I don't provide information on them.
Zh: “抱歉。我只是不会跟别人聊起我的朋友，他们是什么样的人之类的。我不会提供他们的信息。”

En: A-ha. Okay. Maybe I'll ask later about all this.
Zh: “啊哈，好吧，或许我以后还会来问的。”

En: What about you? Tell me something about yourself.
Zh: “那你呢？来谈谈你自己吧。”

En: Me? I'm a silver bird.
Zh: “我？我是一只银翅鸟。”

En: Don't know what makes you think it'll be any different later, but...
Zh: “不知道你为什么觉得以后会有所不同，但是……”

En: High above and to the east, the cold winds blow over the feathers of an early songbird. She lands on the stone ledge of a tall building. Her beak a silvery grey.
Zh: 高高的上空，东边的方向，寒风吹拂过一只早起夜莺的羽毛。它降落在一座高大建筑的岩壁上，鸟嘴是银灰色的。

En: Catch the silver bird.
Zh: 抓住银翅鸟。

En: Dresscode: Avian investigator.
Zh: 着装要求：鸟类研究员。

En: O pasăre de argint -- it flutters by your ear.
Zh: “哦，银色的鸟儿”——它从你的耳边飞过。

En: The silver bird has already flown away... I will catch it later.
Zh: 银翅鸟已经飞走……我一会儿再去找她。

En: O pasăre de argint.
Zh: “哦，银色的鸟儿。”

En: All right, she concedes. "My father was a Zemylaki. He died years ago. He was a bad man. Not a lot of good things to say about him and what he did."
Zh: “好吧，”她承认了。“我父亲就是个泽姆拉基。几年前死了。他是个坏人。对于他还有他做的那些事，我说不出什么好话。”

En: So after his death we had nothing left. And we were in danger. My mother had to change her name, mine too. We left it all behind.
Zh: “所以在他死后，我们什么也没剩下。我母亲改了名字，我也一样。我们把一切都抛在脑后了。”

En: That's nice to hear. You kids are good these days.
Zh: “很高兴听你这么说。现在的年轻人都挺不错的。”

En: Are you sure about that? What about this drug lab plan?
Zh: “你确定吗？那这个毒品实验室计划怎么说？”

En: Yeah, kinda. I'm the head of the operation. Coordinated it with the Débardeurs, else they might get... unhappy.
Zh: “是啊，有点。我是业务负责人。跟装卸工会有配合，不然他们可能会……不高兴。”

En: Aren't there any local authorities who might look down on such activities?
Zh: “难道就没有地方政府注意到这类活动吗？”

En: So, what you're saying is... Evrart authorized this?
Zh: “所以你的意思是……艾弗拉特授权了？”

En: Not in person, but I let them know. You can't do anything without the fat ones getting wind. It wasn't too difficult to convince them, really...
Zh: “本人没有，但是我会让他们知道。你做什么都得让那个胖子知道。说真的，说服他们并不是太难……”

En: It's a good thing you ended that mess, though. I felt I was turning dad-wise, into a corrupt business-person. Unpleasant.
Zh: “不过，你结束了这场混乱也好。我感觉自己越来越像父亲，变成一个腐败的商人了。很令人不爽。”

En: I was kinda hoping you'd end this mess, though. It feels like I've been turning dad-wise, into a corrupt business-person. I don't like it.
Zh: “不过，我一直隐约期望着你能结束这场混乱。我感觉自己越来越像父亲，变成一个腐败的商人了。我不喜欢这样。”

En: That melody is oddly familiar... Why does it feel like some gang-related thing?
Zh: 那旋律出奇得熟悉……为什么感觉跟帮派有关？

En: It's what the "Zemlyaki" -- the Graadian community in Revachol -- call a person who never breaks under interrogation. It's an organized crime term.
Zh: 这就是“泽姆拉基”——瑞瓦肖的格拉德社区——对于在审讯中从不屈服的人的称呼。这是个犯罪团伙的术语。

En: Excuse me? She casually brushes some snow off her sleeve.
Zh: “不好意思？”她漫不经心地拂去了袖子上的落雪。

En: Excuse me? She casually brushes her hand through her hair.
Zh: “不好意思？”她漫不经心地用手梳理着头发。

En: She's attempting to remain calm, but the phrase made her flinch.
Zh: 她试图保持冷静，但这句话让她退缩了。

En: I remember now... it's a Zemlyaki term. For someone who won't break under questioning. An organized crime term.
Zh: “我想起来了……这是个泽姆拉基的术语。用来形容审讯中从不屈服的人。犯罪团伙的用语。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: The silver bird.
Zh: “银翅鸟。”

En: That's why you've handled my questioning so calmly.
Zh: “所以你才能那么冷静地回答我的问题。”

En: He bought the family a huge house, so we got to live... at least temporarily... in a giant *castle* in Jamrock. And then he died.
Zh: “他给家里买了栋大房子，所以我们可以……至少是暂时生活在……加姆洛克的一座巨大的*城堡*里。然后他就死了。”

En: What happened next?
Zh: “之后发生了什么？”

En: Yes, better that way...
Zh: “是啊，这样更好……”

En: Oh, hmm, I can imagine what followed after.
Zh: “哦，嗯，我能想象接下来会发生什么了。”

En: Never mind, I don't really want to hear any of this.
Zh: “没关系，我并不是非得听这些。”

En: You and I both, although... I don't have to *imagine*. The competition came and took everything away. It was like in a war zone... She's gritting her teeth.
Zh: “我跟你一样，但是……我不需要*想象*。竞争来了，带走了一切，就像在战区一样……”她咬紧了牙关。

En: What do you think? The competition came and took everything away. It was like in a war zone... She's gritting her teeth.
Zh: “你以为呢？竞争来了，带走了一切，就像在战区一样……”她咬紧了牙关。

En: It was a stupid idea and I'm still disappointed *I* came up with it.
Zh: “那是个愚蠢透顶的想法，我现在还在后悔*自己*的决定。”

En: 'Left it all behind.' Are you sure you did? The lieutenant narrows his eyes at the girl. "Who can tell with all the delinquent behaviour?"
Zh: “‘都抛在脑后’。你确定你做到了？”警督眯起眼睛盯着这位姑娘。“考虑到这些违法乱纪的行为，这要如何断定呢？”

En: So you're the main drive behind everything?
Zh: “这么说，你就是一切背后的主因？”

En: I took that into account, of course. Coordinated the operation with the Débardeurs, else they might get... unhappy.
Zh: “当然，我也考虑到了这一点。跟装卸工会一起行动，否则他们可能会……不高兴。”

En: I doubt that. You're probably up to no good.
Zh: “我很怀疑，你很可能没安好心。”

En: Turned you into a new generation drug user, though?
Zh: “虽然把你变成了新一代的吸毒者？”

En: Anything to get through. She shrugs. "That's how it is after the fall."
Zh: 她耸耸肩。“只要能熬过去就行。在那之后就是这个情况了。”

En: Gee, thanks. Now, was there anything else on your mind?
Zh: “天呐谢谢。现在，你还有别的事情吗？”

En: Who isn't? It's all *no good* in Martinaise. Now, was there anything else on your mind?
Zh: “谁不是呢？在马丁内斯，一切都很*糟糕*。现在，你还有别的事情吗？”

En: Yeah, no, don't worry, I was just saying... I don't mind drugs, I'm all into drugs myself, yeah, drugs.
Zh: “不啊，别担心，我的意思是……我不介意嗑药，我自己也嗑啊，没错，嗑药。”

En: I'm not going to take you in for such a tiny infraction, but I better not find anything else suspicious going on.
Zh: “我不会因为小小的过失就拘留你，但你最好别再被我发现任何可疑的事情。”

En: Who doesn't just *love* drugs? All the drugs. The lieutenant's voice is flat, although with a seeming undertone of sarcasm.
Zh: “有谁不*爱*毒品呢？是药三分毒啊。”警督的语气平淡，虽然似乎颇有讽刺之意。

En: A life full of suspicion. Perhaps you should talk to Noid, you'd get along nicely... Now, was there something else on your mind?
Zh: “充斥着怀疑的生活。或许你应该去跟诺伊德谈谈，你们绝对能合得来……现在，你还有别的事情吗？”

En: Good for you. Now, was there anything else on your mind?
Zh: “挺好的。现在，你还有别的事情吗？”

En: Have you seen a red-haired woman around?
Zh: “你在附近见过一个红发女人吗？”

En: Have you seen a suspicious woman around here? A woman who looked like she might be on the run?
Zh: “你在这附近见过一个可疑的女人吗？一个看起来像是在逃亡的女人？”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: Just no?
Zh: “就一句没有？”

En: It's pretty desolate here, I only hear the dogs bark at night and see the shadows move down the coast.
Zh: “这一片很荒凉的，我只在晚上听见过狗叫声，看见影子沿着海岸游移的样子。”

En: Considering what we're trying to do here this doesn't look like a good sign.
Zh: “考虑到我们正着手做的事，这感觉不像是个好兆头。”

En: Alright, I got some questions for you.
Zh: “好吧，我有几个问题想问你。”

En: Sure.
Zh: “行啊。”

En: I'm kinda hoping you'll end this mess before we get going... I feel as if I'm slowly turning dad-wise, into a corrupt business-person. I don't like it.
Zh: “我一直隐约期望着你能在我们开始之前结束这一切……我感觉自己越来越像父亲，变成一个腐败的商人了。我不喜欢这样。”

En: Acele stares at him blankly. Two unflinching stares in collision course.
Zh: 阿西尔面无表情地盯着金，两人坚定的目光针锋相对。

En: You don't call a sudden drug lab inside this church organized crime?
Zh: “你不认为教堂里冒出来的毒品实验室是场组织犯罪吗？”

En: That's nice to hear. You kids are good these days.
Zh: “很高兴听你这么说，现在的年轻人都挺不错的。”

En: Gee, thanks. Now, was there anything else on your mind?
Zh: “天呐谢谢。现在，你还有别的事情吗？”

En: A shame you didn't...
Zh: “可惜你没有……”

En:
Yeah... I was kinda hoping you'd end that mess before we got going. It feels like I've been slowly turning dad-wise, into a corrupt business-person.	“是啊……我一直隐约期望着你能在我们开始之前结束这一切……我感觉自己越来越像父亲，变成一个腐败的商人了。”
0x0100005A000118E2	Dialogue Text	ICE / ACELE	You	This is all good to know... but let's leave it at that." (Leave it off the list.)
Zh: “很高兴听到这些……但我们就到此为止吧。”（把它从清单上划掉。）

En: We should confront Evrart about this! Maybe we can manipulate him using the information. (Add it to the list.)
Zh: “我应该去跟艾弗拉特当面对质！也许我们可以用这条信息操纵他。”（添加到清单上。）

En: I should confront Evrart about this! Maybe I can manipulate him using the information. (Add it to the list.)
Zh: “我应该去和艾弗拉特当面对质这件事！说不定可以利用这个消息操纵他。”（记到待办事项清单上。）

En: No, I don't think it would lead anywhere with Evrart. He's just going to deny it.
Zh: “不，我不觉得艾弗拉特那里会有什么进展，他会直接否认的。”

En: Yeaaahhhh... She really draws out the word. "Have you seen Evrart?"
Zh: “是啊啊啊……”她真的把这个词拖得很长。“你见过艾弗拉特吗？”

En: That's not going to lead anywhere with Evrart. He'll just deny everything. I mean, have you seen the guy?
Zh: “艾弗拉特那里不会有任何进展的，他会直接否认的。我是说，你们见过那家伙吗？”

En: Do. It's for the best.
Zh: “没错。这是最好的办法。”

En: Exactly. It's our chance to turn the grim desolation into an overwhelmingly fun dance party!
Zh: “的确。这是我们把残酷的荒芜变为一场有趣的舞会的机会！”

En: Superstition. We can turn the grim desolation into a fun dance club, and thus also help turn this hell-hole around.
Zh: “迷信。我们可以把这个荒凉的地方改造成一个有趣的夜店，从而扭转这里糟糕的颓势。”

En: Hey there. The girl with an electronic recording apparatus turns to you as you approach.
Zh: “嘿，”当你走近时，那个手持电子录音设备的女孩转向你。

En: Thanks for helping us out. My associates and I are settling in here nicely. Even found some new associates. She turns back to her work, whistling softly...
Zh: “谢谢你的帮忙，我和我的同伴们都安顿下来了。甚至还找到了一些新伙伴。”她轻轻吹着口哨，投身工作之中……

En: Recording the audio coming through the wooden framework. I bet it's pretty cool. Creaks and stuff. Sounds like when you're underwater, you know?
Zh: “录制通过木质框架传递来的声音，我敢说一定很不错。嘎吱嘎吱的，听上去就像你在水下一样，你懂的。”

En: What are you doing?
Zh: “你在做什么？”

En: Why does that melody sound so familiar?
Zh: 为何那段旋律听起来如此熟悉？

En: You're Acele.
Zh: “你是阿西尔。”

En: Yeah, she responds, turning back to her device.
Zh: “是啊，”她应了一句，又转向她的设备上去了。

En: Okay, it's an odd hobby, but who am I to judge.
Zh: “好吧，真是个奇怪的癖好。但我又有什么资格去评判呢？”

En: This? She taps on the electronic device. "Yeah, it's a portable recording device. For field recording. Low quality, but still."
Zh: “这个？”她轻敲着电子设备。“没错，这是个便携式录音设备，用来做现场记录的。质量很差，但也能用。”

En: And the wires?
Zh: “那些电线呢？”

En: A contact mic is a microphone that records sounds from inside things. Like all this wood.
Zh: “接触式麦克风能从物体内部记录声音，比如这些木头里面。”

En: What is a *contact microphone*?
Zh: “*接触式麦克风*是什么？”

En: Actually just one wire, I picked on it till the braiding came loose. The wire leads to a contact microphone.
Zh: “其实只有一根线，我正提着它等线团松开。电线连着一个接触式麦克风。”

En: Gotcha. I have some questions for you.
Zh: “明白了，我有几个问题想问你。”

En: Why would anyone do that?
Zh: “为什么会有人这么做？”

En: Oh, is that what the device is for? (Point to it.)
Zh: “哦，这就是那个装置的用途吗？”（指着它。）

En: Hello again. The girl looks up at you for a moment before turning back to her work.
Zh: “又见面了。”那个女孩抬头看了你一会儿，然后又埋头工作了。

En: I'm listening. A cold wind blows in from the sea. She breathes on her fingers for warmth, looking up at you.
Zh: “我听着呢。”冷风从海面上吹来，她对着手指呵气取暖，抬头望着你。

En: I'm listening. She puts the device down and props herself up a bit, waiting for you to speak.
Zh: “我听着呢。”她放下设备，直起身子，等你开口。

En: Because... Listen, man, why does anyone do anything?
Zh: “因为……听着，老兄，为什么会有人做这些事呢？”

En: She shrugs, obviously eager to go back to whatever she's doing.
Zh: 她耸耸肩，显然很渴望回去做她手头上的工作。

En: I just need a few minutes of your time. Police business.
Zh: “我只需要占用你几分钟的时间，警察办案。”

En: I'll leave you to it, then. [Leave.]
Zh: “那么，我就不打扰了。”[离开。]

En: Well, if it's not that important, you won't mind if I ask you a few questions.
Zh: “如果不是那么重要的话，你不介意我问你几个问题吧？”

En: I'm listening. She puts the contact mic down and props herself up a bit, waiting for you to speak.
Zh: “我听着呢，”她把麦克风放下，站起身，等你开口。

En: I'm listening. She puts the contact mic down and props herself up a bit, waiting for you to speak.
Zh: “我听着呢，”她把麦克风放下，站起身，等你开口。

En: Oh, hello there.
Zh: “嗨，你好啊。”

En: The shaggy-haired girl kneels on the sea ice. She looks up as you approach.
Zh: 一个头发蓬乱的女孩跪在冰冻的海面上，当你走近时，她抬起头来。

En: Shoot. A cold wind blows. She breathes on her hands for warmth.
Zh: “说吧。”寒风吹过，她对着手哈气取暖。

En: Is this about the hat again? She looks up at you, distracted. "I don't see you wearing a hat..."
Zh: “又来说帽子的事？”她心烦意乱地抬头看着你。“我看你也没戴帽子啊……”

En: My whole family swears and it rubbed off on me... There's a pained expression on her face. She'll answer your questions now.
Zh: “我全家人都会说脏话，这对我影响很大……”她脸上露出了痛苦的表情，现在她可以回答你的问题了。

En: Hard-cop. She nods to you with respect and turns off her recording device.
Zh: “硬核警探。”她带着敬意朝你点点头，然后关掉了她的录音设备。

En: What did she just say? That's not how a civilian is supposed to address an officer of the law.
Zh: 她刚才说什么？一个平民不应该这样跟执法人员讲话。

En: Regain authority in her eyes.
Zh: 重新获得她眼里的权威。

En: Only wearing one shoe.
Zh: 只穿了一只鞋。

En: Dresscode: Ceramic knight.
Zh: 着装要求：陶瓷骑士。

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: Dresscode: Pseudo-SKULL.
Zh: 着装要求：伪骷髅头。

En: Everyone knows drugs make you invulnerable to cold. You bet this one likes to *party*.
Zh: 每个人都知道毒品能让你不再畏惧寒冷，你敢说这个人一定很喜欢*派对*。

En: You said eating me? There's nothing eating me.
Zh: “你说我有困扰？我没有任何困扰。”

En: I was wondering when you would come around. What's up?
Zh: “我还在想你什么时候会过来呢，有什么事吗？”

En: People just keep putting their selfish interests ahead of the greater good.
Zh: “人们总是把私利置于更伟大的利益之上。”

En: Oh, really?
Zh: “哦，是吗？”

En: Yeah. No one understands that sometimes you've got to make sacrifices for the sake of progress. It's all very distressing.
Zh: “是啊，没人明白有时候为了进步是必须要做出牺牲的。实在令人心痛。”

En: No, that's probably not it, is it?
Zh: “不，可能不是，对吗？”

En: So, the thing that's got you crying in front of strangers... is the slow pace of social progress?
Zh: “所以，让你在陌生人面前嚎啕大哭的事……是社会进步太过缓慢？”

En: So, the thing that's been eating you... is the slow pace of social progress?
Zh: “所以，那件一直困扰你的事……是社会进步太过缓慢？”

En: Don't get me wrong, I'm all for slow and steady progress. I just wish people would be a little more *reasonable*, is all.
Zh: “别误会，我举双手赞成缓和而稳健的进步。我只希望人们能更通情达理一些，仅此而已。”

En: Okay, so you're not *that* upset, it sounds like.
Zh: “好吧，那听起来你也不是*那么*丧气嘛。”

En: I told you, I'm *highly* distressed by this state of affairs.
Zh: “我告诉过你，我对这种情况感到*非常*忧虑。”

En: Uh huh, and you're sure it's social progress you're distressed about and not, I don't know, some chick?
Zh: “嗯哼，你确定困扰你的是社会进步而不是，我也不好说，某个小妞吗？”

En: No, it sounds like you've just got chick issues.
Zh: “不，听起来你只是因为女人的问题在苦恼。”

En: You could be right, but I don't know for certain.
Zh: “你或许是对的，但我也不能确定。”

En: Uh... she's a bit odd, I have to say. Doesn't talk much. I'm not really sure how to vibe with her, y'know? Seems like she's not in a very good mood most of the time.
Zh: “呃……不得不说她有点怪。不爱说话，我也不知道该如何跟她相处，你能明白吗？感觉她大部分时间心情都不太好。”

En: But earlier today she told me about... welkins? Acele grimaces, as if it's the first time hearing the word. "And she seemed oddly happy. Like she had had some idea with those... little creatures. Some artistic idea. I didn't really listen, I was busy with my mic."
Zh: “但今天早些时候，她跟我提起了……精灵？”阿西尔做了个鬼脸，仿佛是第一次听说这个词。“她看上去出奇的高兴，好像她对那些……小生物有了什么灵感似的，某种艺术灵感。我也没仔细听，正忙着弄我的麦克风呢。”

En: I just try to stay away from the crab-man, but he talks to Noid. They seem to have some 'thing' going on.
Zh: “我只想远远躲开蟹男，但他会跟诺伊德说话。他们似乎有什么‘事情’要做。”

En: A very common name.
Zh: 一个非常普通的名字。

En: Sure. You helped us out, I can repay the favour. What do you want to know?
Zh: “当然。你帮了我们，我也会报答你的。你想知道什么？”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Why wouldn't it be?
Zh: “为什么呢？”

En: Tell me about Andre.
Zh: “跟我说说安德烈吧。”

En: I don't know man, have you *been* down Boogie Street? It's a little bewildering.
Zh: “我也不好说，老兄，你*去过*布吉街吗？有点让人摸不着头脑。”

En: Tell me about Noid.
Zh: “跟我说说诺伊德吧。”

En: Tore it to pieces.
Zh: “把它撕碎。”

En: Oh, I'm sorry... have I not told you I'm a raging alcoholic who recently drank himself into an oblivion so deep he can't even remember what sounds like the biggest street in the city?
Zh: “哦，我很抱歉……我难道没告诉你我是个暴躁的酒鬼吗，最近喝到连城里最大的那条街是什么样子都记不清了。”

En: Andre? He's a cool guy. Doesn't really come off as one, but he is. To me at least...
Zh: “安德烈？他是个不错的家伙，虽然看上去不像，但他确实不坏。至少对我是不坏……”

En: The worst of the *banlieues*. A wretched heap of closed-down mines even west of Jamrock, on the dusty slope of Mont Martin. The remotest possible area of Revachol, no one even wants to exploit those people anymore.
Zh: 那是*郊区*中最糟糕的一种。在加姆洛克西部，马丁山尘土飞扬的山坡上，几座倒闭的矿井留下了一堆悲惨的居民。在瑞瓦肖最偏远的地区，甚至都没人想去剥削那些家伙。

En: Who do you want to know about?
Zh: “你想打听谁呢？”

En: He's in some kind of a self-destruction mode with that hair of his. Bleaching it like that. Probably wants to get rid of it altogether.
Zh: “他把头发漂白成那样，有点自我毁灭的味道。可能是想要完全摆脱它吧。”

En: Is there a law that would stop me from lying, though?
Zh: “有什么法律能够阻止我说谎吗？”

En: She rubs her sides, but not from cold. Her shoulders are relaxed. She must have taken a hit or she's on an upswing.
Zh: 她并非出于寒冷才摩擦自己的身体。她的肩膀松弛着，一定是受了什么打击，或者正在好转中。

En: But I'm a police officer. You *have* to answer me.
Zh: “但我是警察，你*必须*回答我。”

En: What does he do?
Zh: “他是做什么的？”

En: He's a Faubourger I guess, like the rest of us. Okay, maybe not Egg, I don't know about him, but Noid and the rest are from Faubourg, making the pilgrimage up north to visit The Paliseum.
Zh: “他应该是郊区的人吧，跟我们一样。好吧，除了爱凡客之外，我并不了解他。但诺伊德跟其他人都来自郊区，一路向北，完成通往帕里瑟姆的朝圣之旅。”

En: You wish you'd been there.
Zh: 你希望你也在场。

En: This is the first time you've heard her laugh.
Zh: 这是你第一次听到她的笑声。

En: It means I don't answer questions about myself.
Zh: “这意味着我不会回答跟自己有关的问题。”

En: Come to think of it, yes! She laughs.
Zh: “仔细想想，没错！”她笑着说。

En: What do you want to know?
Zh: “你想知道什么？”

En: Could it be that he's balding? Him thinking it's his last chance?
Zh: “会不会是因为他在掉头发？他以为那是自己最后的机会了？”

En: Okay, lets leave it at that then.
Zh: “好吧，我们就到此为止吧。”

En: He takes care of shit. Sorry, I mean he's got a *vision*. Of what life should be, you know? He tries to push things until everything falls into place. He's an organizer.
Zh: “他掌管一切。抱歉，我的意思是他很有*远见*，关于生活应该如何之类的。他会推动全局直到一切各就各位，是一个组织者。”

En: What's the deal with Egg Head?
Zh: “爱凡客是怎么回事？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: What does that mean?
Zh: “那是什么意思？”

En: Thank you. Some other questions.
Zh: “谢谢你，我还有一些问题。”

En: Uh, yes. Anyway.
Zh: “呃，是吧。无所谓了。”

En: What does he think he's doing when he yells all that stuff?
Zh: “他大喊的时候以为自己在干什么呢？”

En: Where's he from? How long has he been with you guys?
Zh: “他从哪儿来的？和你们在一起多久了？”

En: Tell me about the others.
Zh: “跟我说说其他人吧。”

En: Oh, that... he's the party boy.
Zh: “那个嘛……他是个派对男孩。”

En: He told me as much, but what exactly is a party boy?
Zh: “他也是这么跟我说的，但到底什么是派对男孩？”

En: What's a party boy?
Zh: “什么是派对男孩？”

En: I told you, I'm a silver bird.
Zh: “我告诉过你，我是一只银翅鸟。”

En: I'm a silver bird.
Zh: “我是一只银翅鸟。”

En: Anodic music doesn't really do vocals in the traditional sense. Vocals are thought of as *rock*. That's to say they're a bit *backward*. No offence if you like rock music, though. Rock music is cool by me.
Zh: “阳极音乐并不像传统意义上的音乐会有人声，那通常是*摇滚乐*，可以说它们有点*落伍*。不过如果你喜欢摇滚乐的话，请别见怪，我对摇滚乐没有任何意见。”

En: On his mother's love?
Zh: “母亲之爱？”

En: Uh, okay... She nods.
Zh: “呃，好吧……”她点点头。

En: Anyway, even if you don't have vocals you still need someone to say something every now and then, right? To urge things on. That's where the party boy comes in...
Zh: “不论如何，即使没有人声，你仍然需要有人时不时说点什么，对吧？把气氛推向高潮，这就是派对男孩的用武之地了……”

En: God damn right, rock music is the coolest. Rock music forever!
Zh: “太他妈对了，摇滚乐是最棒的，摇滚不死！”

En: No offence taken, go on.
Zh: “没关系，继续吧。”

En: You don't have to tell *me* rock is backward. I am The Futureman. I abandoned rock in the Thirties. Stupid rock. (Spit.)
Zh: “你没必要跟*我*说摇滚乐落伍。我可是未来人。我早在三十年代就抛弃摇滚乐了。傻逼摇滚。”（啐。）

En: You don't have to tell *me* rock music is dead. I abandoned rock music a long time ago. Can't disco to rock!
Zh: “你不必告诉*我*摇滚乐已经死了，很久之前我就放弃摇滚了。迪斯科没办法摇滚！”

En: Ptew ptew! Sweeesh! Your credentials as the resident Futureman of Revachol are being questioned. Show her you're hip with the times, gramps!
Zh: 唷唷！噫！你作为瑞瓦肖未来居民的资格正在遭到质疑，告诉她你完全走在时代前沿，老头！

En: On life?
Zh: “生活？”

En: Unfortunately, I can confirm that.
Zh: “不幸的是，我可以证明这一点。”

En: Tore that shit up.
Zh: “那把东西撕了。”

En: Tell me about the others.
Zh: “跟我说说其他人吧。”

En: Yes. She nods studiously. "Forever..."
Zh: “没错。”她刻意地点点头。“不死……”

En: Yes, better that way...
Zh: “没错，那样更好……”

En: He basically just stands on the stage and dances and yells how awesome everything is. It's very catchy.
Zh: “他基本就是站在舞台上，边跳边喊着一切是多么的美好。还是挺吸引人的。”

En: Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢你。”

En: Fair enough.
Zh: “有道理。”

En: I understand. People are usually afraid to do things if others aren't already doing them. Dancing makes you dance like sneezing makes you sneeze. Or yawning makes you...
Zh: “我明白。人们总会惧怕他人还没做过的事情，舞蹈会带动着你舞动起来，正如打喷嚏会让你也想打喷嚏，或者打哈欠也会让你……”

En: Yes, I suppose we did.
Zh: “是的，我想我们都是。”

En: Anyway... He looks around, a little embarrassed of the enthusiasm of his interjection.
Zh: 他环顾四周，为自己的热情欢呼感到有些尴尬。“总而言之……”

En: Actually, no.
Zh: “其实吧，还是算了。”

En: What do you want to know? She smiles a little.
Zh: “你想知道什么？”她微微一笑。

En: Yes, I forgot. Tell me about yourself.
Zh: “是啊，我忘记了。跟我说说你自己吧。”

En: That would depend on the circumstances. The lieutenant taps his foot.
Zh: “那要视情况而定。”警督轻轻踱步。

En: Actually, we don't know where he's from. Or who he is, really.
Zh: “实际上，我们不知道他来自哪里，或者他是谁。真的。”

En: Me? Again?
Zh: “又是我？”

En: Me?

Egg was yelling along to some jams someone was spinning, all night long. Just kept yelling until he didn't have a shred of voice left. When the sun came up over the mines... there were mines? Yeah, it was in Coal City! She nods.
Zh: “爱凡客跟着别人的节拍，尖叫了整整一夜，直到他的嗓子哑掉为止。当太阳从矿区升起……有矿区？当然，这可是煤城啊！”她点了点头。

En: Egg came with us. He made this wheezing puppy dog sound all the way back. Couldn't even speak.
Zh: “爱凡客是跟我们一起来的。一路上都在发出那种狗狗喘气的声音，甚至话都说不了了。”

En: Actually, do tell me about *yourself*.
Zh: “说真的，请告诉我一些关于*你自己*的事吧。”

En: One time we were out partying, somewhere in backwater Faubourg. Or maybe even Coal City, I can't remember. Maybe it was Coal City...
Zh: “有一次我们出去聚会，在偏僻的市郊或者是煤城，我记不清了。或许就是煤城……”

En: It was definitely Coal City, because it took us two days to walk back to the Fau. He just wheezed the whole way, we never really asked him why he came with us. Or who he was. I think his name is Germaine...
Zh: “绝对是在煤城，因为我们花了两天时间才走回市郊。他一路上都在呼哧呼哧地，我们从未问过他为什么要跟我们一起，或者他究竟是谁。我想他的名字应该是杰曼尼……”

En: People are sweet, she says quietly.
Zh: “人是很可爱的，”她平静地说。

En: It's unlikely I'll make it there. Chest pains. Need to continue drinking. But thank you.
Zh: “我不太可能撑到那里。胸部疼痛。需要继续喝酒。但还是谢谢你。”

En: You can see it must have been a great night. The memory causes her to go silent for a moment or two.
Zh: 你能看出那一定是个美好的夜晚，回忆让她沉默了一会儿。

En: Okay. She shrugs. "To each his own."
Zh: “好吧。”她耸了耸肩。“各有所爱。”

En: Is Andre your boyfriend?
Zh: “安德烈是你男朋友吗？”

En: It's just something poor Faubourg kids do every spring, to pass the time. We walk the entire length of Boogie Street, up to Jamrock. Or as much as possible.
Zh: “那是福布的穷孩子们每年春天都会做的事，只是为了打发时间。我们会沿着布吉街走到底，一直走到加姆洛克。或是走到走不动为止。”

En: What is this pilgrimage you're talking about?
Zh: “你说的朝圣是指什么？”

En: A quick response. She's very sure of it. He's someone she feels she can trust and even control, if need be.
Zh: 迅速地回答，说明她对此很有把握。她觉得自己能够信任他，甚至在必要的时候可以控制他。

En: I might just do that if I make it there alive.
Zh: “如果我能活着到那里，可能就会这么做。”

En: Let's say I *haven't* been down Boogie Street.
Zh: “这么说吧，我*从未*去过布吉街。”

En: Oh, and I'm also a raging fascist who doesn't approve of *different people* -- so no, I don't think I'll be going.
Zh: “哦，我同时还是个狂热的法西斯分子，看不惯*各种各样的人*——所以，我还是免了吧。”

En: Tell me more about Egg.
Zh: “跟我说说爱凡客吧。”

En: We certainly saw that in practice, didn't we?
Zh: “我们在实践中确实看到了这一点，不是吗？”

En: What has he organized?
Zh: “他组织了什么？”

En: Nothing. But then again... there's nothing to organize around here either. He really wants this church thing to work...
Zh: “没什么。但话又说回来……这里也没什么好组织的。他真的希望教堂的事能成功……”

En: I've never seen him so psyched about anything though -- and he's often psyched. Looked sort of desperate, like it's his last chance or something. Or maybe he was just high...
Zh: “我从没见过他对任何事情如此兴奋过——而且他很容易兴奋。感觉有点绝望了，好像这是他最后的机会，又或者他只是嗨了……”

En: I mean, not that he does drugs. Just *high* you know.
Zh: “我并不是说他在吸毒，只是*嗨*起来了。”

En: Yes. She nods.
Zh: “是的。”她点点头。

En: Relax girl. We're police officers, but we're corrupt, rotten to the marrow. You can tell us about drugs and shit, I don't give fuck.
Zh: “放轻松，姑娘。我们是警察，但是腐败透顶的那种。你可以尽情地说毒品之类的玩意，我不在乎。”

En: Relax, girl. I am a police officer, but I'm perfectly corrupt. You can tell me about drugs and shit, I don't care.
Zh: “放轻松，姑娘。我是名警察，但是腐败透顶的那种。你可以尽情地说毒品之类的玩意，我不在乎。”

En: Uhm... kind of? I'm gonna say: yes.
Zh: “呃……有点吧？我会说：是的。”

En: Uh... no?
He abides by the hard core, sir. You would have to ask him yourself.
Zh: “他非常硬核的，长官。你得自己去问他了。”

En: Yeah. Drugs. Shit. Fuck. We don't care. He smirks.
Zh: “是啊。毒品。狗屎。干。我们不在乎。”他假笑了一下。

En: Tell me about the others.
Zh: “跟我说说其他人吧。”

En: Actually, tell me about the others.
Zh: “说真的，跟我说说其他人吧。”

En: What do you mean, 'do'? Like for a living?
Zh: “你说的*做*是什么意思？谋生吗？”

En: Okay, then you should go and take a look, I guess. Boogie Street is cool. It's got a lot of immigrants and all kinds of different people.
Zh: “好吧，我想你真应该去看看。布吉街相当不错，很多移民和各种各样的人。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: There is.
Zh: “有的。”

En: No, I meant for breakfast. Of course for a living.
Zh: “不，我是说早餐吃什么。吃早餐当然是为了谋生啦。”

En: Alright. She picks up the tape recorder and looks you in the eye...
Zh: “好吧。”她拿起录音机，望着你的眼睛……

En: He's a carpenter. Trained and all. He's very good, he just doesn't have the mindset to work like that -- in a shop somewhere.
Zh: “他是个训练有素的木匠，手艺娴熟，只是心思不在工作上——一直待在店里之类的。”

En: Sure... she says, slightly disoriented. "What do you want to know?"
Zh: “当然……”她略有些茫然地说。“你想知道什么？”

En: That's because Noid is violently hard core. I have internalized it. I understand why he doesn't stoop to servitude. Do you?
Zh: “那是因为诺伊德极端的硬核，我已经内部消化过了。我明白他为什么不愿屈尊做奴隶，你呢？”

En: Is that because of his beliefs? The hard core and the ribs, the... spinal cord and stuff?
Zh: “是因为他的信仰吗？硬核和肋骨，还有……脊髓之类的？”

En: What kind of mindset is that?
Zh: “什么样的心思？”

En: Not to sound too dramatic, but I wouldn't make light of his belief system when he's around. Noid's a serious person. He's serious about the lifestyle.
Zh: “我不想说的太夸张，但当他在场的时候，我不会轻视他的信仰体系。诺伊德是个很严肃的人，他对于生活方式很认真。”

En: Tell me more about him.
Zh: “跟我说说他吧。”

En: Wow. You really *have* talked to him. She smiles. "Indeed. Noid serves no master but the beat."
Zh: “哇哦，你真的跟他*谈过*了。”她笑着。“确实，除了节奏诺伊德不会为任何东西服务。”

En: There's more to you than you let on. What am I not seeing?
Zh: “还有比你表现出来的更深的东西，我忽略了什么？”

En: And you?
Zh: “那你呢？”

En: Because I'm not. Now tell me something else about Noid.
Zh: “因为我不是。现在再告诉我一些诺伊德的事吧。”

En: Sir, she gives you a switchblade smile. "I abide by *the law*."
Zh: “长官，”她给了你一个出其不意的笑容。“我很*遵纪守法*的。”

En: A strange feeling... like before, when you caught her lying better than she should have. Every now and then something feels off about the way she speaks. She doesn't exactly change tone, but you feel as though there's more about her than she lets on.
Zh: 一种奇异的感觉……就像之前，你发现她意外地会说谎。她说话的方式时不时就会让人感到不对劲，虽说她并没有刻意改变语气，但你知道她隐瞒了更多。

En: Yeah, she says, then adds: "I hope you do."
Zh: “是啊，”她说，然后又补充道：“我希望如此。”

En: Yeah, she says, then opens her mouth like she's about to add something, but doesn't.
Zh: “是啊，”她说，然后张开嘴巴好似要补充什么，却又没有。

En: Well, I am.
Zh: “嗯，我就是。”

En: A strange feeling... Every now and then, something feels off about the way she speaks. She doesn't change tone, but you feel as though there's more about her than she lets on.
Zh: 一种奇异的感觉……她说话的方式时不时就会让人感到不对劲，虽说她并没有改变语气，但你知道她隐瞒了更多。

En: They do say death is an avenue like any other. She's silent for a second. "I still think you'll make it to Boogie Street, though."
Zh: “他们总说死亡就像其它手段一样。”她沉默了一会儿。“不过，我还是觉得你能坚持到布吉街的。”

En: They do say death is an avenue like any other. She's silent for a second. "I still think you'll make it to Boogie Street, though."
Zh: “他们总说死亡就像其它手段一样。”她沉默了一会儿。“不过，我还是觉得你能坚持到布吉街的。”

En: They do say death is an avenue. Like all the others. She's silent for a second like she's going to add something. But she doesn't.
Zh: “他们总说死亡也是一种手段，就跟其它的一样。”她沉默了一会儿，似乎要补充什么，却又没有。

En: Acele.
Zh: “阿西尔。”

En: And your surname?
Zh: “那你姓什么呢？”

En: Acele who? I'm not a young suitor, this is official police business.
Zh: “阿西尔什么？我不是你的追求者，现在是警察在办案。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么要问？”

En: Her hair is dyed blonde, with dark roots showing. There's a coarseness to her features, some masculinity below that timidness.
Zh: 她的头发染成了金色，发根处透着黑。她的面容有些粗糙，羞怯之下还隐藏着一些男孩子气。

En: Okay... She hesitates. "It's Berger."
Zh: “那好吧……”她犹豫了一下。“是伯格。”

En: You have little reason to doubt that's her real name, but... she's not all that surprised by this cop-show questioning, is she?
Zh: 你没有理由质疑那个是不是她的真名，但是……她对警察秀里的这种质问似乎并不惊讶。

En: Did I not say I was from the police? It's for the paperwork.
Zh: “我不是说过我是警察了吗？这是为了文书工作。”

En: I'm from the police. It's for the paperwork.
Zh: “我是警察，这是为了文书工作。”

En: Why indeed? Forget it.
Zh: “是啊，为什么要问呢？算了吧。”

En: Must have taken it as a sign when he found it abandoned like that. Said it was an *augury*. I don't know where he got that from. She smiles. "Andre's not super intelligent..."
Zh: “当他看到那个被遗弃的地方时，一定把它当成了一个象征，说它是个*预兆*。我不知道他是从哪儿听来的。”她笑着。“安德烈不是特别聪明……”

En: He's real hard core about the lifestyle.
Zh: “他的生活方式相当硬核。”

En: What about Tiago?
Zh: “那蒂亚戈呢？”

En: What do you think of the crab-man?
Zh: “你对蟹男有什么看法？”

En: Tell me about Soona.
Zh: “跟我说说苏娜吧。”

En: Oh, the crab-man. She shudders. "Still gives me the creeps, the way he moves... But he doesn't actually come down that much, just climbs around the rafters."
Zh: “哦，蟹男啊，”她颤栗了一下。“他移动的样子还是会让我起鸡皮疙瘩……但其实他也不经常下来，只是绕着椽子爬来爬去。”

En: What does he do up there?
Zh: “他在上面干什么？”

En: Thanks. About the others...
Zh: “谢谢，那其他人……”

En: Oh, he talks to Noid? What for?
Zh: “哦，他会跟诺伊德说话？说什么？”

En: Beats me... Noid said they get along, somehow. They're both crazy enough, I guess.
Zh: “问倒我了……诺伊德说他们相处得很好，不知道为什么。我想是因为他俩都足够疯狂吧。”

En: Who knows? She shrugs. "He doesn't really answer our questions, see. Not that it's easy to ask them. What are we supposed to do, yell up at the tower?"
Zh: “谁知道呢？”她耸耸肩。“要知道他并不会真正回应我们的问题。倒不是说这些问题很容易回答。我们该怎么做，对着塔顶大喊大叫吗？”

En: There's that phrase again. It really reminds you of... *something*.
Zh: 又是那句话，这真的让你想起了……*某样东西*。

En: Hang on, that phrase. You're suddenly reminded of... *something*.
Zh: 等等，那句话。你突然想起了……*某样东西*。

En: It's creaking really loud.
Zh: “嘎吱嘎吱得吵死了。”

En: Sounds like it's smiling.
Zh: “听起来像是在笑一样。”

En: Damn it's cold outside...
Zh: “该死的，外面好冷……”

En: Oh, here we go...
Zh: “哦，开始了……”

En: The frequency can't be *that* low...
Zh: “频率不可能*那么*低的……”

En: *teeth clattering*
Zh: *牙齿打颤*

En: Much warmer in here.
Zh: “这里面暖和多了。”

En: Beautiful...
This place must have a mind of its own...
Zh: “这地方一定有自己的想法……”

En: You see clear, beautiful, violent flashes of light. Light cutting through a smoke-filled darkness. That is what the future will look like -- if it ever comes.
