Zh: 你看见清晰、美丽又强烈的闪光。光线刺穿了烟雾弥漫的黑暗。那就是未来的模样——如果它还能到来。

En: This is an evolutionary step up from amplified instruments, which in turn are a step up from acoustic instruments. What comes after it, you wonder: a black-tinted nothingness, or something finer?
Zh: 这是自扩音乐器以来革命性的一步，反过来也是音响乐器前进的一步。在那之后是什么，你开始好奇：一种黑色的虚无，或者是比它还要好的东西？

En: Maybe you could *talk* to Andre and the guys? Because there are some strange things going on in that church... if you're police you should look into it, right?
Zh: “也许你可以跟安德烈和那些人*聊聊*？因为那座教堂里发生了一些怪事……如果你是警察，那就应该去调查一下，对吗？”

En: But not too long. This is important to her. She has the courage to ask.
Zh: 但是没有很久。这对她来说很重要。她开口询问是需要勇气的。

En: Should you? *Weirdos* sounds bad somehow.
Zh: 应该吗？不知怎么的，*怪人*听起来挺糟糕的。

En: She's less prone to blurting out 'crab-man!' than the others.
Zh: 相比其他人，*螃蟹人*这个词并不容易从她嘴里脱口而出。

En: A crab *and* a banger? The lieutenant raises an eyebrow.
Zh: “一只螃蟹，*而且*还是个黑帮分子？”警督扬起眉头。

En: A crab *and* a banger?
Zh: “一只螃蟹，*而且*还是个黑帮分子？”

En: Like a *crab*, you say? The lieutenant nods. His face is stone.
Zh: “你说像*螃蟹*？”警督点了点头，面无表情。

En: Yes, a 'banger', as in a Mesque gang-member. I know what it sounds like, but that's what I saw.
Zh: “是的，‘混混’，就像梅斯克帮派混混一样。我知道这听起来不靠谱，但这是我亲眼所见。”

En: Yes, a 'banger', as in a Mesque gang-member. I know what it sounds like, but that's what I saw.
Zh: “是的，‘混混’，就像梅斯克帮派混混一样。我知道这听起来不靠谱，但这是我亲眼所见。”

En: C'mon. She obviously could. She already went into detail.
Zh: 拜托。她明显就是可以。她已经在详细描述了。

En: Once I cracked their play I didn't really care. I'm not an arbiter. You're someone else's problem.
Zh: “一旦我看透了他们的把戏，我就不再关心了。我不是什么独裁者，你是其他人的问题了。”

En: Do what you will with your dance club plans... just no drug labs, please.
Zh: “就按照你们的舞蹈俱乐部的计划去做吧……但是别想着毒品实验室了，拜托。”

En: The lieutenant eyes you suspiciously.
Zh: 警督怀疑地盯着你。

En: Thank you, the young woman says blithely. "I'll get them under wraps, I promise."
Zh: “谢谢你。”女青年面露喜色。“我向你保证，绝对会保密的。”

En: I can see there's more to this story, detective, he says quietly. "But you're not the first corrupt cop in the RCM."
Zh: “我看得出这其中还有更多隐情，警探。”他平静地说。“但你也不是RCM里第一个腐败的警察了。”

En: (Nod.) "I saw him. I saw the crab-man."
Zh: （点头。）“我看到他了，我看到蟹男了。”

En: You *saw* him?! She leans closer. "Does it mean that you went *in* there? Did you see the other spooker, the one in grandma's clothes?"
Zh: “你*看到*他了？！”她凑得更近了。“这是不是说明你*进去*了？那你看到另一个幽灵了吗，穿着老太太衣服的那个？”

En: No, just the crab-man.
Zh: “没有，只有蟹男。”

En: I did see them both, yes.
Zh: “我确实看到了他们两个，没错。”

En: I prefer not to share this information with you.
Zh: “我不想跟你分享这个消息。”

En: Oh. Okay. She sits back on the blanket, ears red from cold. "What about Andre? You should go talk to Andre if you don't want to talk to me."
Zh: “哦，好吧。”她坐回到毯子上，耳朵被冻得通红。“那安德烈呢？如果你不想跟我说话，那就去找安德烈吧。”

En: Good, so you believe me. She looks at the tent. "You should go tell this to Andre, he'll know what to do next."
Zh: “很好，所以你相信我了。”她看着帐篷。“你应该把这件事告诉安德烈，他会知道下一步要做什么的。”

En: Hmm... She sits back on the blanket, ears red from cold. "That's odd. You should search her machine, maybe it says where she went?"
Zh: “嗯……”她坐回到毯子上，耳朵被冻得通红。“真是怪了。你应该搜查一下她的机器，也许里面有她的去向？”

En: That I'm going to decide after I've looked into the church situation.
Zh: “我会在调查了教堂的情况后再做决定。”

En: Oh, right. That's wise. She nods. "Take your time."
Zh: “哦当然，那很明智。”她点了点头。“慢慢来。”

En: This is why I didn't go into the tent. The lieutenant looks at the ocean, squinting his eyes. "Typical delinquency."
Zh: “所以我才没有进入帐篷。”警督眯起眼睛望向大海。“典型的违法犯罪。”

En: I did and I'm sorry. For what it's worth... which isn't much.\n
Zh: “是的，我很抱歉。不管怎么说……这不算什么大事。”

En: I did and I'm sorry. She doesn't appear surprised. "For what it's worth... which isn't much."
Zh: “是的，我很抱歉。”她看起来并不惊讶。“不管怎么说……这不算什么大事。”

En: You don't get to choose your posse, they choose you. Mine are idiots, but they're mine. I tried to talk Andre out of it, I even tried not to lie to you...
Zh: “你无法选择自己的老铁，是他们选择你。我的老铁们都是白痴，但那也是我的老铁。我试着说服过安德烈，我甚至试过不对你说谎了……”

En: You don't get to choose your posse, they choose you. Mine are idiots, but they're mine. I tried to talk Andre out of it...
Zh: “你无法选择自己的老铁，是他们选择你。我的老铁们都是白痴，但那也是我的老铁。我试着说服过安德烈……”

En: Indeed. She merely tried to omit the truth instead.
Zh: 的确，她反而只是想掩盖事实真相。

En: Instead you opted to omit the truth. It's the same thing.
Zh: “相反，你选择了掩盖真相。这是一回事。”

En: Should've tried harder. Misleading a cop is no joke.
Zh: “你应该更努力一点。误导警察可不是闹着玩的。”

En: I don't care, I'm *loco*. I just wanted you to know that I know about the plan.
Zh: “我不在乎，我是个*疯子*。我只想让你知道，我已经知道那个计划了。”

En: Okay, but I still regret it. I should have been able to control them. And I will in the future. I promise.
Zh: “好吧，但我仍然很后悔。我本应该能够控制住他们的，以后我会做到的，我保证。”

En: I know. I should have been able to control them. And I will in the future. I promise.
Zh: “我知道，我本应该能够控制住他们的。以后我会做到的，我保证。”

En: I know. But I knew you'd see through their plan too. I'm not an idiot. I should have been able to control them. I will in the future. I promise.
Zh: “我知道，但我知道你也能看穿他们的计划。我不是傻瓜，我本应该能够控制住他们的。以后我会做到的，我保证。”

En: She doesn't get confused often, but she is definitely confused now.
Zh: 她并不经常感到困惑，但她现在确实困惑起来了。

En: May I ask, what did you tell them?
Zh: “我能问问，你都告诉他们什么了吗？”

En: Soooo...? The young woman looks at you, then the lieutenant, then you again. Then at the shore. She seems uncomfortable.
Zh: 年轻女人看着你，然后看向警督，又看向了你，最后望向了海岸。她看起来不太舒服，声音拖得老长：“所以呢……？”

En: I called it crime prevention. You'll have to go at it straight -- if you have it in you.
Zh: “我称之为预防犯罪。如果你有这份意愿——就要努力向前。”

En: Oh! She almost smiles, but then decides not to. There's a moment of silence.
Zh: “哦！”她差点笑出来，而后又忍住了。紧接着是片刻的沉默。

En: Okey dokey then.
Zh: “没问题。”

En: Thank you. The young woman nods. "I'll get them under wraps, I promise."
Zh: “谢谢你。”年轻女人点点头。“我会保密的，我保证。”

En: I called it crime prevention. You'll have to go at it straight -- if you have it in you.
Zh: “我称之为预防犯罪。如果你有这份意愿——就要努力向前。”

En: Wonderful, the lieutenant says, still looking at the ocean. "Revachol really needed another cop-for-hire. Maybe later we can go and get some doughnuts?"
Zh: “好极了，”警督仍旧望着大海说到。“瑞瓦肖真的需要再雇一名警察，也许过会我们可以去买些甜甜圈？”

En: Thine lies, they sing too transparent.
Zh: 你的谎言，太容易看穿了。

En: Good call, detective, he says, still looking at the ocean. "You can lose your mind trying to mediate everything these delinquents come up with. Fate will take care of it for us."
Zh: “好主意，警探，”他仍旧望着大海说到。“试图调节这群少年犯惹出来的所有麻烦会让你失去理智的。命运会帮我们解决这一切的。”

En: Please officer, just finish your side-interview. We need to move on to the case at hand.
Zh: “拜托，警官，赶快完成你的次要走访吧。咱们该去处理手头的案子了。”

En: No. Conduct your side-interview and let's move on to the case at hand.
Zh: “不。继续你的走访，然后我们继续处理手头的案子。”

En: We won't be anyone's problem. I'll get them under wraps, I promise.
Zh: “我们不会给任何人添麻烦。我向你保证，绝对会保密的。”

En: She seems incredibly controlled all of a sudden. Her fists are clenched and she looks straight ahead. She's keeping herself together with all her might, rippling on the inside.
Zh: 她似乎一瞬间就克制住了自己。她紧握双拳，目视前方，用自己全身的力量压制住了自己内心的跌宕。

En: Okay. Now conduct your side-interview and let's move on to the case at hand.
Zh: “好吧。现在继续你的走访，然后我们得去处理手头上的案子了。”

En: Your words flow sincere.
Zh: 你的话语流露着真诚。

En: (Tell the truth.) "I took a cut of the action. I'm corrupt to the marrow, you see."
Zh: （说实话。）“我从中分了一杯羹。你看，我已经腐败到骨髓了。”

En: I took a cut of the action. I'm corrupt to the marrow, you see.
Zh: “我从中分了一杯羹。你看，我已经腐败到骨髓了。”

En: I needed some reál, Kim. It's a hard knock life.
Zh: “我需要一点雷亚尔，金。生活太艰难了。”

En: Try to lie about your actions in front of the lieutenant.
Zh: 当着警督的面隐瞒你的行为。

En: Kitsuragi Trust.
Zh: 曷城相信。

En: (Whisper.) "It's all a ruse. I'm actually super deep under cover, blurring the lines between reality and make-believe."
Zh: （低语。）“这都是一个计谋。实际上我是个隐藏极深的卧底，模糊了现实与虚幻之间的界线。”

En: You know a good doughnut joint?
Zh: “你知道什么不错的甜甜圈店吗？”

En: As good a call as any... the lieutenant says, still looking at the ocean. "Better not to have made one at all though. You'll lose your mind trying to mediate everything these delinquents come up with."
Zh: “真是个好主意……”警督仍旧望着大海说到。“不过最好什么都不要做，试图调解这群少年犯惹出来的所有麻烦会让你失去理智的。”

En: You're right. Let me ask you about something else.
Zh: “你说的对。我再问你点别的。”

En: And then, you know, right behind her a man *crawled down the wall*. Upside down, like a crab. Down the church wall. I think the woman didn't even know he was there, he was completely silent...
Zh: “然后，你知道的。就在她身后，一个男人*从墙上爬了下来*。倒挂着，像螃蟹一样，沿着教堂的墙爬下来。我猜那个女人根本不知道他在那里，他非常安静……”

En: C'mon, quit stalling on me. What did he look like?
Zh: “行了，别再拖延时间了。他长什么样子？”

En: You were wrong. I do believe you.
Zh: “你错了，我其实是相信你的。”

En: What would you stand to gain?
Zh: “你撒谎能得到什么呢？”

En: Seems too ludicrous for a lie.
Zh: “作为谎言来说这太可笑了。”

En: *Why?* She raises a brow.
Zh: 她挑起眉毛。“*为什么*？”

En: I guess so. She hesitates. "Anyway -- what else?"
Zh: “我想也是。”她犹豫了。“总之——还有别的吗？”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: Oh, that... you're not gonna believe me. There's no point in me telling you.
Zh: “哦，那个啊……你不会相信的，我也没必要告诉你了。”

En: That's not especially frightening.
Zh: “这并不是特别可怕。”

En: I don't blame you. I mean, I wouldn't believe me either. I'm surprised Noid did. Andre I expected, but Noid... She shakes her head.
Zh: “我不怪你，我的意思是，换我也不会相信的。我很吃惊诺伊德居然信了。安德烈我倒是想到了，但是诺伊德……”她摇摇头。

En: We'll see. Go ahead and tell me.
Zh: “先说来听听吧。”

En: Okay, she nods. "I went in and saw a woman next to one of those machines there. Noid calls it a *mainframe*..."
Zh: “好吧，”她点点头。“我走进去，看到一个女人站在一台机器旁边，诺伊德称之为*主机*……”

En: It was too dark. She shakes her head. "I couldn't tell exactly."
Zh: “实在太黑了。”她摇摇头。“我也看不清楚。”

En: C'mon. She obviously could. She already went into detail.
Zh: 拜托。她明显就是可以。她已经在详细描述了。

En: Strange things happen.
Zh: “发生了一些奇怪的事。”

En: He looked like a banger, okay? He was all muscular and stuff. Had a mesh tanktop. I know it sounds ridiculous, but that only made it scarier, in a way...
Zh: “他看起来像个暴徒，好吗？肌肉发达什么的。还穿了一件网眼背心。我知道这听起来很荒谬，但在某种程度上，这只会让事情变得更可怕……”

En: What did this 'crab-man' look like?
Zh: “这个蟹男看起来什么样？”

En: Nothing, she says. "Anyway -- what else?"
Zh: “什么也得不到，”她说到。“总之——还有别的吗？”

En: Okay then, she nods. "They certainly do around me."
Zh: “那好吧，”她点点头。“他们在我周围确实会这样。”

En: A gangster crab-man... Yeah, I don't believe you.
Zh: “一个黑帮螃蟹男……是啊，我不相信你。”

En: You were right. I don't believe you.
Zh: “你是对的，我不相信你。”

En: Doesn't hurt to ask, I guess... She shrugs.
Zh: 她耸了耸肩。“我猜问问也无妨……”

En: Enough about the church then. I had a another question. (Conclude.)
Zh: “教堂的事就到此为止了。我还有一个问题。”（继续。）

En: It's supposed to become, like, a club. For *anodic dance music*. Like that new style of synthesizer stuff they play at The Paliseum.
Zh: “它本应该，成为，一座俱乐部才对，专门播放*阳极舞曲*。就像他们在帕里瑟姆演奏的那种新型合成器。”

En: Except that... yeah. She looks at the old wooden church up on the poles. As a mean wind comes bellowing in, the six-story structure lets out a doleful shriek.
Zh: “除了那个……没错。”她看着远处那座古老的木质教堂。狂风呼啸而来，那座六层楼高的建筑发出了一声凄厉的惨叫。

En: The floorboards are twisting and the shooting beams are slowly cracking like bones. Far east of the golden Delta, beyond the industrial port, there is a black patch of unlit coast with the smallest creatures on the ice...
Zh: 地板颤颤巍巍，横梁如同骨头一般慢慢裂开。黄金三角洲的远东，比工业港还要遥远的地方，一处没有灯光的黑色海岸，冰面上有着世界上最小的生物……

En: There will never be a club for anodic music here.
Zh: 这里不会有阳极音乐俱乐部的。

En: Not in a million years.
Zh: 永远不会。

En: (Show her the sticker on your ledger.) "So, this isn't yours?"
Zh: （向她展示手册上的贴纸。）“所以，这不是你的咯？”

En: I spoke with the boys. Still undecided on the matter.
Zh: “我跟小伙子们都谈过了，这件事还没有定论。”

En: We'll see. I prefer to remain non-committal.
Zh: “走着瞧吧。我更倾向于不置可否。”

En: Anodic music?! *Now* I'm gonna find out what it is! What is it?
Zh: “阳极舞曲？！我*现在*就要知道那是什么东西！那是什么？”

En:
What is *anodic dance music*?	“什么是*阳极舞曲*？”
0x0100005A00007965	Dialogue Text	ICE / ACELE CHURCH STUFF	You	So you want to turn the church into a club?"
Zh: “你想把教堂变成一个俱乐部？”

En: You know, anodic, cathodic -- music that's made with electronic instruments.
Zh: “就是，阳极，阴极——用电子乐器演奏出来的音乐。”

En: Electronic instruments -- like what?
Zh: “电子乐器——比如什么？”

En: Got it. Now about the church...
Zh: “明白了，至于教堂的事……”

En: Yeah, I've spoken with them. No way in hell am I getting involved in this.
Zh: “是啊，我已经跟他们谈过了。我是绝不可能掺和进来的。”

En: I've got enough on my plate as it is.
Zh: “我的事情已经够多的了。”

En: I know. She nods towards the sloping mass of wood on the coast, then shivers. "It's not my idea. Andre and the boys found the place. It was supposed to be deserted, but now they can't even take it..."
Zh: “我知道。”她朝着岸边倾斜的木头点了点头，然后打了个寒颤。“这不是我的主意，安德烈和其他小伙子们找的地方。它本应该是荒废的，但现在他们甚至都进不去……”

En: Why did Noid put a padlock on the church door?
Zh: “为什么诺伊德要在教堂门上挂把锁？”

En: Why did this Noid-person put a padlock on the church door?
Zh: “为什么那个叫诺伊德的人要在教堂门上挂把锁？”

En: It's Noid's.
Zh: “是诺伊德的。”

En: Yeah, you know, that's what I meant -- Noid did. Noid put the padlock on the door.
Zh: “是啊，我就是这个意思——诺伊德做的。他在门上挂了一把锁。”

En: Okay, well... She hesitates. "Maybe you could *talk* to Andre and the guys? Because there are some strange things going on in that church... if you're police you should look into it, right?"
Zh: “那好吧……”她犹豫着。“也许你可以跟安德烈他们*谈谈*？因为教堂里发生了一些奇怪的事……身为警察的你应该去调查一下，不是吗？”

En: To keep more weirdos from getting in. Fucking Martinaise... I'm sorry. She rattles her teeth. "It's got the worst weirdos. If you get around to it, ask Andre about them, he'll tell you."
Zh: “阻止更多的怪人进去，该死的马丁内斯……抱歉。”她的牙齿咯咯作响。“聚集了最糟糕的怪胎。如果你有空去找安德烈，他会告诉你的。”

En: I don't know where you got that idea.
Zh: “我不知道你为什么会这么想。”

En: Did you put the padlock on the church door?
Zh: “是你把用挂锁锁上了教堂的门吗？”

En: No.
I'll talk to them.
Zh: “我会跟他们谈谈的。”

En: Wait, is *Noid* a friend of yours?
Zh: “等等，*诺伊德*是你朋友吗？”

En: Wait, is this *Noid* a friend of yours?
Zh: “等等，这位*诺伊德*是你朋友吗？”

En: Yeah, I guess you could say that.
Zh: “是吧，可以这么说。”

En: (Show her the sticker in your ledger.) "So, this isn't yours?"
Zh: （向她展示手册上的贴纸。）“所以，这不是你的咯？”

En: Hey... Her black eyes widen. "You two are cops..."
Zh: “嘿……”她瞪大了黑色的眼睛。“你们是警察……”

En: Hey... Her black eyes widen. "You're a cop..."
Zh: “嘿……”她瞪大了黑色的眼睛。“你是警察……”

En: No. We are *the law*.
Zh: “不。我们是*法律*。”

En: No. I am *the law*.
Zh: “不。我是*法律*。”

En: No. We're *the police*.
Zh: “不。我们是*警探*。”

En: No. I'm *the police*.
Zh: “不。我是*警探*。”

En: Yes. Why do you ask?
Zh: “是的，你问这个干什么？”

En: She's trying to get out of a direct lie with semantic tricks. *She personally* didn't put the padlock on the door.
Zh: 她试图用花言巧语来避免直白的谎言，*她本人*并没有把挂锁挂到门上。

En: It's Noid's padlock, for keeping creeps and weirdos out. You should probably talk to him about it.
Zh: “那是诺伊德的锁，用来阻止那些变态怪胎们进去。也许你应该跟他谈谈。”

En: C'mon, man. Don't mess around... She hesitates.
Zh: “拜托，老兄，别浪费时间了……”她有些犹豫。

En: Okay... She pauses, thinking. "Cool. Anything else?"
Zh: “好吧……”她停下来，想了一会儿。“好吧。还有别的事情吗？”

En: They're inside that thing there. She points to the tent. "Would be cool if you did... Was there something else?"
Zh: “他们都在那玩意里面。”她指着帐篷。“如果你可以的随意进出的话，那真的挺酷的……还有别的事吗？”

En: Not really, no.
Zh: “算不上吧，不是。”

En: No?
Synthesizers and tape consoles, microcomputers too. Anything that uses electricity, but isn't guitars... also found sounds. Stuff like that.
Zh: “电子合成器和磁带控制台，还有无线电脑。任何需要电的东西，但不是吉他……同时还能发出声音，诸如此类的吧。”

En: Okay, if *you personally* didn't, then who did?
Zh: “好吧，如果不是*你本人*，那是谁呢？”

En: Hey... don't get me wrong, but you're *cops*, right...
Zh: “嘿……别误会我的意思。但你们是*条子*对吧，对吧……”

En: Hey... don't get me wrong. But you're a *cop*, right...
Zh: “嘿……别误会我的意思。但你是*条子*对吧……”

En: She was dressed like someone who's being raised by their grandmother, you know. Strange old clothes. Had this absent expression, didn't say anything, just stood still.
Zh: “她穿得就像个由祖母抚养长大的人，明白吗。怪异的旧衣服，面无表情，一言不发，就那么站着。”

En: He stopped right before he got to the floor, then just hung there like that, looking at me. Right at me. I fucking turned around and walked out. End of story.
Zh: “他在落地前停了下来，然后就那么挂着，看着我。直直地看着我。我他妈转身就走出去了。故事结束。”

En: What do you need to know?
Zh: “你想知道什么？”

En: I already spoke with them and we've agreed to help.\n
Zh: “我已经跟他们谈过了，我们愿意帮忙。”

En: I already spoke with them and agreed to help.
Zh: “我已经跟他们谈过了，我愿意帮忙。”

En: Wow! Thank you, that's... really cool of you. She clears her throat. "Anything else you want to know?"
Zh: “哇哦！谢谢你，这……真是太好了。”她清了清嗓子。“你还想知道什么？”

En: Okay then. About that church...
Zh: “好吧，那座教堂……”

En: Hey, remember how earlier I said I didn't *need* your help?
Zh: “嘿，还记得我之前说过不*需要*你帮忙吗？”

En: Aha! The gardener was wrong -- I'm not just some disco holdover, I'm going to experience the future.
Zh: “啊哈！园丁错了——我绝不仅是迪斯科老古董，我要体验未来。”

En: Yeah, of course. She seems unsure of it, somehow.
Zh: “是啊，当然。”不知道为什么，她似乎不太确定。

En: (Forgo celebrating your triumph.) "Now about the church..."
Zh: （抛弃庆祝胜利的念头。）“关于这座教堂……”

En: Your mangled brain would like you to know there is a boxer called Contact Mike.
Zh: 你受伤的大脑想让你知道有个被称为一击即溃·迈克的拳击手。

En: Nice. You could maybe bug someone with it? Wire tapping!
Zh: 很好。也许你可以用它来骚扰别人？窃听！

En: Interesting term. Time to glean some knowledge!
Zh: 有趣的术语。是时候收集一些知识了！

En: Jamrock is a district of Revachol comprised of the following quarters: Pox, Villalobos, Central Jamrock, Grand-Couron, Old South and the Valley of the Dogs. Learning is great!
Zh: 加姆洛克是瑞瓦肖的一个区，由以下区块组成：帕克斯，维拉洛博斯，加姆洛克中心，大科戎，旧南城还有野狗山谷。学习真是太棒了！

En: Got it.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: That's not fair. And an understatement.
Zh: 这不公平。而且这是一种保守的陈述。

En: An entire *litany* spews forth!
Zh: 一整个*长篇累牍*倾巢而出！

En: Thinking about it really cheers her up. It's a long way from here, though.
Zh: 一想到俱乐部，她真的振奋了起来。不过离这里挺远的。

En: Women's fencing. It's an elegant activity.
Zh: 女子击剑。这是一种高雅的活动。

En: Women's fencing. Great sport. No problem with that.
Zh: “女子击剑。很棒的运动。完全没问题。”

En: Good for you then. Women's fencing *is* a pretty graceful sight.
Zh: “那对你来说很好啊。女子击剑*的确*很让人赏心悦目。”

En: Agreed, the lieutenant notes.
Zh: “同意，”警督记录到。

En: What happened to the old headphones?
Zh: 旧耳机怎么样了？

En: Everything checks out, sire!
Zh: 一切正常，大人！

En: And who are these boys?
Zh: 这些小伙子是谁？

En: The girl is cold all of a sudden. Very cold.
Zh: 这个女孩突然变得冷漠起来，非常冷漠。

En: Sorry, I don't really want that. She looks away.
Zh: “抱歉，我不是很想要它。”她移开了视线。

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Now, uh... we were talking about my contact mic? She picks up the device and inspects it, avoiding your eyes.
Zh: “现在，呃……我们说到我的接触式麦克风了吗？”她拿起设备检查起来，回避了你的目光。

En: Now about that *Arno* guy you mentioned.
Zh: “现在来谈谈你提到的那个叫*阿诺*的家伙吧。”

En: Yes, but before we return to the contact mic -- tell me more about this *Arno* guy you mentioned.
Zh: “是的，但在我们说回接触式麦克风之前——你提到的那个叫*阿诺*的家伙是谁？”

En: Yes, contact mics.
Zh: “是的，接触式麦克风。”

En: Contact mics, she repeats, turning the device in her hand.
Zh: “接触式麦克风，”她重复道，转动着手中的设备。

En: Hey, didn't Pissf****t and Fuck the World mention van Eyck, too?
Zh: 喂，尿兄和干弟不是也提过凡·艾克吗？

En: Is it like a SKULL thing?
Zh: “是不是像骷髅头那种东西？”

En: A 'SKULL thing'? She shakes her head. "Man, you sound like a hundred years old when you say that."
Zh: “‘像骷髅头那种东西’？”她摇摇头。“老兄，你这么一说，感觉你就像个100岁的老头子一样。”

En: Hey, didn't Pissf****t and Fuck the World mention van Eyck, too?
Zh: 喂，尿兄和干弟不是也提过凡·艾克吗？

En: No, you don't. She's mistaken you for an old person! Say you're cool.
Zh: 不，你没有。她错把你当成一个老人了！快说你很酷。

En: This? She breathes on her freezing fingers. "It's a portable recording device. It's for field recording. Low quality, but still."
Zh: “这个？”她对着冻僵的手指哈气。“这是个便携式录音设备，用来做现场记录的。质量很差，但也能用。”

En: And the wires?
Zh: “那些电线呢？”

En: What's a 'contact microphone'?
Zh: “*接触式麦克风*是什么？”

En: Concentrating on the device seems to calm her nerves. "A contact mic records sounds from inside things. Like this ice."
Zh: 专注于设备似乎能使她平静下来。“接触式麦克风能从物体内部记录声音，比如这片冰里面。”

En: A contact mic records sounds from inside things. Like this ice.
Zh: “接触式麦克风能从物体内部记录声音，比如这片冰里面。”

En: Your mangled brain would like you to know there is a boxer called Contact Mike.
Zh: 你受伤的大脑想让你知道有个被称为一击即溃·迈克的拳击手。

En: You know, Contact Mike doesn't *just beat people up*. Contact Mike is a role model.
Zh: “要知道，一击即溃·迈克不*只能把人揍飞*，他是个真正的榜样。”

En: Actually just one wire, I picked on it till the braiding came loose. The wire leads to a contact microphone.
Zh: “其实只有一根线，我正提着它等线团松开。电线连着一个接触式麦克风。”

En: Um...
Does this have anything to do with Contact Mike?
Zh: “这跟一击即溃·迈克有关系吗？”

En: How does that thing work?
Zh: “这玩意是怎么工作的？”

En: Uh... She's confused. "Yeah, I record stuff with it."
Zh: “呃……”她有些困惑。“是的，我用它来录东西。”

En: The mic? I don't exactly know. Somehow it doesn't pick up vibrations from the air. The box said it only picks up *structure-borne sound*. If you like technobabble.
Zh: “麦克风吗？我也说不好。不知道为什么它无法感知空气的振动，盒子上写着它只能接收*结构音*。如果你喜欢技术呓语的话。”

En: I... don't know what that means.
Zh: “我……不清楚那是什么意思。”

En: No, I mean the boxer Contact Mike.
Zh: “不，我是指拳击手一击即溃·迈克。”

En: No, I mean... forget it.
Zh: “不，我是指……算了吧。”

En: Sorry, I don't know why I said that. There was something else I wanted to know...
Zh: “抱歉，我也不知道自己为什么要提起这个。还有件事我想知道……”

En: Yeah? Any news on my wife's name? How about my mother?
Zh: 是吗？有关于我妻子名字的消息吗？还有我妈呢？

En: Yeah? Any news on my own name?
Zh: 是吗？有关于我名字的消息吗？

En: Nope.
Zh: 没有。

En: What am I supposed to do with this?
Zh: 我该拿这个怎么办呢？

En: No idea.
Zh: 不知道。

En: Ah! No. This is a *contact microphone*, it's for recording *inside* solid objects. Contact Mike just beats people up.
Zh: “啊！不是。这是个*接触式麦克风*，用来记录物体*内部*声音的。一击即溃·迈克不过是把人揍飞罢了。”

En: What's The Paliseum?
Zh: “帕里瑟姆是什么？”

En: Oh man, you haven't been to The Paliseum? She forgets herself for a moment. "It's *the* coolest place in this whole drug-addled shithole."
Zh: “哦老兄，你没去过帕里瑟姆吗？”她一时间忘乎所以了。“那是个*充斥*着毒品的粪坑。”

En: You're welcome.
Zh: 不用客气。

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Sure.
Zh: “当然。”

En: Where did you get the mic from?
Zh: “你的麦克风是从哪儿来的？”

En: Same place I got the recorder from. The Paliseum.
Zh: “和我搞来录音机的地方一样，帕里瑟姆。”

En: Probably a hangout for junior delinquents. Back to the mic, if you will.
Zh: “大概就是少年犯混日子的地方。如果可以的话，再跟我说说麦克风的事吧。”

En: Tell me more about the microphone.
Zh: “再跟我说说麦克风的事吧。”

En: Never mind all that. I had questions about that mic...
Zh: “别把那些话放在心上，我对麦克风还有些疑问……”

En: Mhm... She breathes on her fingers again to warm them. "Thought so."
Zh: “哼……”她再次对着手指哈气取暖。“我也这么想。”

En: Yeah, I got that, but what *don't* you have a problem with?
Zh: “是啊，我明白了，但你有什么*问题*吗？”

En: Oh yeah... She looks you over, assessing your age. "I guess even with your modern take on sexuality you wouldn't be in the van Eyck demographic."
Zh: “对哦……”她打量着你，估摸着你的年纪。“我想即便是你这样对性向有着现代化认知的人，应该也不属于凡·艾克的年龄层。”

En: Oh yeah... She looks you over, assessing your age. "Guess you wouldn't know van Eyck. Or really be a Paliseum-going kind of person... "
Zh: “对哦……”她打量着你，估摸着你的年纪。“我想你应该不认识凡·艾克，也不像是会去帕里瑟姆的人……”

En: Oh yeah, I remember. The Paliseum sounds like a place for congregating homo-sexuals.
Zh: “对哦，我记起来了。帕里瑟姆听上去像是个同性恋者聚集的地方。”

En: Sounds like a place for congregating homo-sexuals.
Zh: “听上去像是个同性恋者聚集的地方。”

En: Yeah, there might be some. I don't think they look down on the homo-sexual underground there. The Paliseum's not that kind of place. It's cool.
Zh: “是啊，可能会有一些吧。我觉得他们那里不会轻视同性恋者地下组织。帕里瑟姆不是那种地方。它很酷。”

En: Interesting. This the guy your friend Egg Head likes?
Zh: “有意思。就像你的朋友爱凡客喜欢的那种？”

En: Sounds interesting. Who is this *Arno* guy?
Zh: “听上去很有趣，那位*阿诺*是谁？”

En: Never mind, let's talk about that contact mic instead...
Zh: “算了，我们还是来谈谈那个接触式麦克风吧……”

En: I get down.
Zh: “我很沮丧。”

En: You're right. Time has deserted me.
Zh: “你是对的，时间抛弃了我。”

En: Because I have a problem with homo-sexuals. Major problem.
Zh: “因为我对同性恋有意见，很大的意见。”

En: Don't get me wrong. I'm all for it. This *Paliseum* just sounded like their kind of place.
Zh: “别误会我。我完全支持同性恋者。那个*帕里瑟姆*听起来就像是他们喜欢去的地方。”

En: What *don't* you have a problem with?
Zh: “你对什么是*没有*意见？”

En: That's real cool. Hope you don't mind missing out on all that great homo-sexual content the world has got to offer.
Zh: “那可真是好极了。如果错过了这个世界所能提供的所有绝赞的同性恋内容，也希望你不要介意。”

En: Okay then. That's cool.
Zh: “那好吧，好极了。”

En: Is that some kind of term from the homo-sexual underground?
Zh: “这是同性恋者地下组织的一种说法吗？”

En: I don't know what that means either.
Zh: “我也不清楚那是什么意思。”

En: On second thought, let's go back to the contact mic.
Zh: “仔细一想，我们还是再来谈谈接触式麦克风吧。”

En: I grind.
Zh: “我很累。”

En: Oh no, you completely misunderstood me. I might be a homo-sexual myself.
Zh: “哦不，你完全误会我了。我自己可能也是同性恋呢。”

En: Oh! Well, good for you.
Zh: “哦！好吧，真有你的。”

En: She breathes on her fingers. Looks like she doesn't know what to say.
Zh: 她对着自己的手指哈气，看来是不知道该说些什么了。

En: Self respect is not meant to float any boats but your own.
Zh: “自尊不是让你满足任何人，而是你自己。”

En: Yes, you heard right. You should try to be more like Contact Mike: a successful athlete and an *inspirational figure* who has overcome social, physical, and mental obstacles.
Zh: “是的，你没听错。你应该试着更像一击即溃·迈克一些：一个成功的运动员，一个克服了社会、肉体与精神障碍的*励志人物*。”

En: Man, you are one weird cop.
Zh: “老兄，你真是个古怪的警察。”

En: Neither do I actually. I have no idea what I'm talking about. Get down? Grind?
Zh: “其实我也一样。不知道自己在说什么。沮丧了？累了？”

En: Okay, if it floats your boat I'll be more like Contact Mike and less like me.
Zh: “好吧，如果这能让你高兴的话，我会更像一击即溃·迈克而不是我自己一点的。”

En: What is it with you and this Mike guy? She pauses. The question is rhetorical.
Zh: “你跟那个叫迈克的家伙到底是怎么回事？”她停顿了一下，这是个反问句。

En: It means I'm hip beyond my years.
Zh: “这说明我比实际年龄更加时髦。”

En: I'm not sure, but I have concrete evidence that I rock. In the form of a wrecked tape player and a totally trashed hostel room.
Zh: “我不能确定，但我有确凿的证据能证明我很摇滚，以一台坏掉的录音机和一间完全报废的旅社客房的形式。”

En: Nor do I, but I have concrete evidence that I rock. In the form of a wrecked tape player and a completely trashed hostel room.
Zh: “我也不知道，但我有确凿的证据能证明我很摇滚，以一台坏掉的录音机和一间完全报废的旅社客房的形式。”

En: That's cool. She breathes on her fingers. Looks like she doesn't know what to say.
Zh: “那可真是棒极了。”她对着自己的手指哈气，看来是不知道该说些什么了。

En: I also have a problem with alcohol.
Zh: “我也有酗酒的问题。”

En: Fuck that! I'm completely problem free -- except for alcohol -- and you can't tell me otherwise.
Zh: “去他的吧！我什么意见都没有——除了饮酒——你不能那么说。”

En: Fuck that! I'm completely problem free, you can't tell me otherwise.
Zh: “去他的吧！我什么意见都没有，你不能那么说。”

En: Actually, I had some non-mic questions for you.
Zh: “实际上，我还有一些麦克风之外的问题想问你。”

En: You do understand it all just sounds like Mike to me, right?
Zh: “你明白这在我听来都是一样的吧？”

En: On second thought, screw Contact Mike. He's not a champion -- you are! Look at you here in front of a saggy tent, picking your nose to drug-addict music. The world of sports is in awe of your faith and dedication.
Zh: “仔细想想，去他的一击即溃·迈克吧。他才不算什么冠军——你才是！看看你，站在一顶松松垮垮的帐篷面前，对着瘾君子的音乐挑三拣四的。体育界应该对你的信念与奉献感到敬畏。”

En: This isn't about me. This is about your lack of respect for one of boxing's greats -- and for *yourself*.
Zh: “这与我无关，而是你对拳击界的泰斗之一——和*你自己*都缺乏尊重。”

En: Yes, that does indeed float my boat.
Zh: “没错，那确实取悦了我。”

En: I'd say I'm just about normal. Now about that mic -- Not Mike. Mic.
Zh: “我想说我只是个普通人而已。现在来谈谈麦克的事吧——不是迈克，是麦克。”

En: Recording, I guess.
Zh: “录音吧，我想。”

En: And what is it you're recording exactly?
Zh: “那你究竟在录什么呢？”

En: I *think* I'm recording cracks in the ice, but there's no way to tell. Not without headphones. I think I just recorded your footsteps, too. Not sure how that will sound... She scratches her forehead.
Zh: “我*想*我是在记录冰块破碎的声音，但如果没有耳机的话，也没法知道。我刚才可能还录下了你的脚步声，不知道听上去会怎样……”她挠了挠前额。

En: Yup. She squints her eyes a little. "They were pretty... I'm sorry we sold those."
Zh: “没错。”她微微眯起眼睛。“是挺好的……很遗憾我们把它卖掉了。”

En: Wait, what happened to the headphones?
Zh: “等等，耳机怎么了？”

En: And what are these recordings for -- the cracks, the footsteps?
Zh: “那这些破碎声和脚步声的录音——要用来干什么呢？”

En: It's probably amphetamine -- It makes you *feel* impervious to the elements, but doesn't actually make you any less cold.
Zh: 可能是安非他命——它能让你对天气的影响*感到*无动于衷，却不会让你不那么冷。

En: My boyfriend sold them.
Zh: “我男朋友把它卖掉了。”

En: Didn't you see it yourself? It's mostly music stuff, like this tape recorder. But bigger.
Zh: “你看不出来吗？主要是音乐方面的东西，就像这台录音机，但要更大一些。”

En: Music stuff mostly. Like this tape recorder, but bigger. And there's piles of it.
Zh: “主要是音乐方面的东西。就像这台录音机，但要更大一些，那还有一堆呢。”

En: What for?
Zh: “卖了做什么？”

En: I don't know, man... things. Just stuff you need for life.
Zh: “我不知道，老兄……买一些东西吧，生活所需的那种。”

En: A lie. They were probably pawned off for something ssssuspicious.
Zh: 一个谎言，他们可能是为了某样可疑的东西把它当掉了。

En: The musicians in The Paliseum use them for making music. They loop the stuff, cutting the tapes together. They make music out of cracks in the ice and keys jangling... crazy sounds like that. It's hard to explain.
Zh: “帕里瑟姆的音乐家们用它们来创作音乐，循环播放，或是把带子剪在一起。他们利用破碎的冰块和晃动的钥匙之类的不可思议的声音……来奏乐。很难解释的。”

En: You mean like those headphones your boyfriend sold.
Zh: “你是指你男朋友卖掉的那种耳机？”

En: Just nod.
Zh: 只是点点头。

En: They got too much stuff crammed in there. No room.
Zh: “里面塞了太多东西，没地方了。”

En: Anyway, I thought I'd make some, too. It's supposed to be, like, a music place anyway... She rubs her shoulders and looks around.
Zh: “总而言之，我想我也可以做一些。反正那里应该，是个，音乐场所……”她揉了揉肩膀，环顾四周。

En: I don't really know what I'm doing. They use synthesizers, too. I don't have a synthesizer.
Zh: “我也不太确定自己在做什么，他们还会用到合成器，我没有合成器。”

En: Stuff like what?
Zh: “什么样的东西？”

En: She looks at the recording device, the thing she thought would fill her hours with joy and escape. It's turning out to be an empty fantasy. She feels childish, very useless all of a sudden.
Zh: 她看着录音设备，那个她本以为能给自己带来欢乐与逃离的东西，已经化为了空想。一时间她觉得自己很幼稚，很没用。

En: The sharp drop in endorphins is almost visible. Like a warm blanket has fallen off her shoulders -- the wave of chill, the quivering jaw. Indications of a drug high.
Zh: 内啡肽肉眼可见地急剧下降着，如同一张温暖的毛毯从她的肩膀上滑落——阵阵寒颤与颤抖的下巴，意味着药物反应。

En: She is on some kind of stimulant -- amphetamine or mephedrone -- and its effects are wearing off fast. She will begin to experience more and more valleys now. And fewer peaks.
Zh: 她服用了某种兴奋剂——安非他命或者甲氧麻黄酮——但药效很快就退去了。她将会经历越来越多的低谷，与几近于无的高峰。

En: Take this, you're cold. The lieutenant begins to take off his jacket.
Zh: “穿上吧，你太冷了。”警督动手脱掉了上衣。

En: Now *this* is where a hat would come in handy.
Zh: “现在*就是*帽子派上用场的时候了。”

En: No man, fuck that, I'm cool... I'm sorry I said that. I'm sorry about the *fuck*.
Zh: “不用了老兄，去他的吧，我没事……抱歉我这么说了，请原谅我的脏话。”

En: Why? Why can you take it?
Zh: “为什么？你为什么能接受？”

En: It's okay. The lieutenant backs up. He throws you a glance.
Zh: “没事的。”警督后退一步，瞥了你一眼。

En: Why not just leave some of it outside so you don't have to freeze?
Zh: “为什么不把一些东西放在外面，这样你就不用受冻了？”

En: Yeah... maybe you were right about the hat.
Zh: “是啊……也许你对帽子的坚持是对的。”

En: I got my recordings to do, I feel better outside... A wave breaks close-by and covers her in a cold spray.
Zh: “我还有录音的事要做，而且我待在外面感觉更好……”一阵寒流袭来，把她淹没在冰冷的浪花之中。

En: You said *it's* supposed to be a music place. Did you mean the one Andre's got planned -- the church?
Zh: “你说*那里*应该是个音乐场所，是指安德烈计划中的——那座教堂吗？”

En: You said *it's* supposed to be a music place. What is?
Zh: “你说*那里*应该是个音乐场所，是哪里？”

En: I had some other questions. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我还有别的问题。”（总结。）

En: Why are you freezing out here, while the boys are inside?
Zh: “小伙子们都待在里面，怎么就你在外面冻成这样？”

En: Yes. She nods toward the church. "The boys think it could be a *place*, like The Paliseum or something. Stupid. It's really..." she pauses, "not gonna be a Paliseum, that's for sure."
Zh: “是的。”她冲着教堂点了点头。“男孩们觉得那里可以成为帕里瑟姆之类的*地方*。愚蠢，那里真的……”她顿了一下。“不可能变成帕里瑟姆的。”

En: That. She nods toward the church. "The boys think it could be a *place*, like The Paliseum or something. Stupid. It's really..." she pauses, "not gonna be a Paliseum, that's for sure."
Zh: “那里。”她冲着教堂点了点头。“男孩们觉得那里可以成为帕里瑟姆之类的*地方*。愚蠢，那里真的……”她顿了一下。“不可能变成帕里瑟姆的。”

En: That stuff is more expensive than I am. More expensive than any of us, really. Doesn't matter. I can take the cold.
Zh: “那东西要比我贵多了，实际上比我们任何一个都要贵。没关系，我能抗住。”

En: The boys?
Zh: “男孩们？”

En: Yeah, Andre and the guys. Inside the tent.
Zh: “是啊，安德烈和其他人，他们都在帐篷里面。”

En: Yeah, Andre and the guys. They're inside. In the tent.
Zh: “是啊，安德烈和其他人，他们都在里面，帐篷里。”

En: She's shivering a bit.
Zh: 她微微颤抖着。

En: All right then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: See? That's the appropriate way to give a girl a hat...
Zh: “看到了吗？这才是送给一个女孩儿帽子的正确方式……”

En: (Let it go.) "Got it..."
Zh: （随它去。）“明白了……”

En: Sigh.
Zh: 叹气。

En: No, man. Fuck it, I'm cool... I'm sorry I said that. I'm sorry about the *fuck*.
Zh: “不，算了吧，去他妈的，我没事……很抱歉说了那种话，我不该说*他妈的*。”

En: Want my jacket? (Start taking it off.)
Zh: “想要我的夹克吗？”（动手脱掉。）

En: (Give her your hat.) "Here. You need this more than I do."
Zh: （把你的帽子给她。）“拿着吧，你比我更需要这个。”

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: And why is that?
Zh: 为什么呢？

En: Have you been questioned before?
Zh: “你以前被问过话吗？”

En: Once or twice, yeah. I'm sorry I haven't had the *Revachol experience* they get east of the river.
Zh: “一两次吧。抱歉，我没有他们在河东岸的那种*瑞瓦肖经验*。”

En: Well, it's just questioning, right? You're questioning me -- it's what cops do.
Zh: “嗯，这只是一次问话，对吧？你在问我问题——这就是警察的工作。”

En: We do, don't we? Mind if I question you some more?
Zh: “的确，不是吗？你介意我再问你一些问题吗？”

En: Fast and clean! A good one. Can't quite say it's a lie, but feels like one. She's better at lying than she'd like everyone to know.
Zh: 干净利落！干得漂亮。不能说这是个谎言，但是感觉如此。相比她想让大家知道的，她真实的撒谎能力还要更厉害一些。

En: (Turn to the girl.) "And why did you have to move out when you were so young?"
Zh: （转向女孩。）“为什么你这么年轻就要搬出去？”

En: Why did you need to move out at such tender age?
Zh: “为什么你年纪轻轻的就要搬出去？”

En: The girl is truly proud of herself.
Zh: 这女孩真的很为自己骄傲。

En: Okay... it's a huge ghetto just south of Jamrock.
Zh: “好吧……是加姆洛克南部的一个大贫民窟。”

En: My dad was a drunk. Plus, I guess I just wanted to drink too, you know? Get my *party on*.
Zh: “我爸爸是个酒鬼。另外，我感觉自己也想要一醉了之，明白吗。找点*乐子*。”

En: What's east of the river?
Zh: “河东岸是什么？”

En: So, what trouble you've gotten into -- with the police.
Zh: “所以，你跟警察之间——有什么过节吗？”

En: I *know* what Jamrock is but... let's say I didn't.
Zh: “我知道*加姆洛克*是什么，但是……就当我不知道吧。”

En: *Rich* people. Rich people are east of the river.
Zh: “*富*人，富人们都在河东岸。”

En: What's *the usual*?
Zh: “*老样子*是指什么？”

En: (Look awestruck.) "Who are these *rich people*? They sound wonderful! I'd like to be one too."
Zh: （敬畏地看着。）“这些*富人*都是谁？听起来太棒了！我也想成为富人。”

En: Good luck crossing that river on your police salary then.
Zh: “那就祝你能靠着警察的薪水跨越那条河了。”

En: (Sneer.) "I bet they're *really* rich. They must be very special people to be so rich..."
Zh: （冷笑。）“我敢说他们*一定*很有钱。他们那么富有，一定非常特别……”

En: Drinking, partying and disco music are bad for you. You should take me as a warning example.
Zh: “酗酒、派对和迪斯科音乐都对你有害，你应该以我为戒。”

En: (Lean in and whisper.) "Let me guess, these rich people are not from around here..."
Zh: （凑过去耳语。）“让我猜猜，这些富人都不是本地人吧……”

En: Strange. The existence of rich people does not stir any emotions in me.
Zh: “奇怪。富人们的存在并没有激起我的任何感情。”

En: You know, drinking, getting into fights. The ugly stuff that happens when you move out of your parents' place at thirteen. In Faubourg.
Zh: “你知道的。酗酒、斗殴，当你十三岁搬离父母住所的时候，在市郊经历的一切丑恶。”

En: Bet they like it that way, she mutters. Quietly, under her breath.
Zh: “我打赌那就是他们期望的。”她的喃喃自语，悄然地随着呼吸吐露出来。

En: I get that. I'm a major party animal myself. MAJOR.
Zh: “我知道了。我自己就是个派对狂人，非常疯狂。”

En: You *keep* them rich.
Zh: “是你让他们*保持*富有的。”

En: I had another question.
Zh: “我还有一个问题。”

En: Aren't they? She seems perplexed. "I thought they were as *Vacholiere* as any of us, just way richer."
Zh: “是吗？”她好像相当困惑。“我还以为他们是和我们一样的*瓦肖人*呢，只是远比我们有钱而已。

En: You know what? I think you've really learned something from all those times you've been questioned. Some of your lies have been pretty good.
Zh: “知道吗？我觉得你从诸多问话中学到了不少东西，有些谎言你说得很不错。”

En: Oh, they are. And I'm scum.
Zh: “哦，他们确实是。而我就是个渣滓。”

En: Hmm... She looks eastward. "Maybe you're right. Most of them do speak three languages."
Zh: “嗯……”她望向东边。“也许你是对的。大部分人都会说三种语言。”

En: I had another question. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我还有一个问题。”（总结。）

En: No. I literally can't remember even the most basic terms sometimes.
Zh: “不，有时候我甚至连最基本的术语都记不起来。”

En: I'm scum too.
Zh: “我也是个渣滓。”

En: You're not scum.
Zh: “你不是渣滓。”

En: Um... thank you?
Zh: “呃……谢谢你？”

En: She nods, apprehensively.
Zh: 她担忧地点点头。

En: Is this a rhetorical question?
Zh: “这是反问句吗？”

En: Oh, but I am.
Zh: “哦，但我就是啊。”

En: Wait, what is *In Faubourg*?
Zh: “*在市郊*是什么意思？”

En: The usual. I had a shitty run as a teenager.
Zh: “老样子吧，我十几岁的生活相当堕落。”

En: We're Jamrock. Sort of. Martinaise is called North Jamrock sometimes. Jamrock is also a ghetto, only smaller than Faubourg.
Zh: “我们在加姆洛克。算是吧。有时候，马丁内斯也被称为北加姆洛克。加姆洛克也是个贫民区，不过比市郊要小一些。”

En: Yes, I can't even remember how many you've told or which ones I picked up on. Now -- another question.
Zh: “是的，我甚至不记得你告诉过我多少东西，也记不清我问过哪些问题了。接下来——另一个问题。”

En: Khm... The lieutenant steps in. "Faubourg -- not *In Faubourg* -- is a massive banlieue south of Jamrock Quarter. It is the largest ghetto in Revachol -- possibly the world."
Zh: “咳……”警督介入了。“市郊——不是*在市郊*——是位于加姆洛克南部的大片郊区，瑞瓦肖——也可能是全世界最大的贫民窟。”

En: I'll try.
Zh: “我会努力的。”

En: Lying to the police is nothing to be proud of. Now, another question, if you can manage to tell the truth.
Zh: “对警察说谎没什么好骄傲的。接下来，另一个问题，如果你能说真话的话。”

En: She nods, very cautiously.
Zh: 她小心翼翼地点了点头。

En: Alright... She smiles a little, without meaning to.
Zh: “好吧……”她下意识地笑了一下。

En: C'mon, man. We're in it. This is North Jamrock -- Martinaise. Sort of. The technicalities are confusing.
Zh: “拜托，老兄。我们就在这里。这里就是北加姆洛克——马丁内斯。算是吧。这些术语让人挺困惑的。”

En: Oh no, they're foreign bodies. Agents of international capital. You only need to look close enough.
Zh: “哦不，他们是外来者，国际资本的代理人。只要你留意观察就能发现。”

En: (Let it go.) "Got it..."
Zh: （随它去。）“知道了……”

En: She looks back at her tape recorder.
Zh: 她回头望着自己的录音机。

En: She looks back at her tape recorder.
Zh: 她回头望着自己的录音机。

En: It's a music club and a synthesizer workshop. On Boogie Street, in Jamrock. Musicians live there, like... real musicians. I once saw Arno van Eyck!
Zh: “那是一间音乐俱乐部和电子合成器工坊，就在加姆洛克的布吉街上。音乐家们都住在那里……真正的音乐家。我有次还看到阿诺·凡·艾克了！”

En: Sucks, man. She squints her eyes for a second trying to remember something... then lets go of it. "Was there something else? About the contact mic, perhaps?"
Zh: 她奇怪地看着你。“糟透了，老兄。”她稍微眯了会儿眼睛，试着记起些什么……随即又放弃了。“还有别的事吗？跟接触式麦克风有关的事？”

En: I... can't think of anything. There's gotta be something, right? That would be normal for a human person.
Zh: “我……什么都想不起来。肯定是有东西的，对吧？对人类来说很正常。”

En: You tell me. She breathes on her fingers again; they're red from the cold.
Zh: “你说呢。”她又在对着手指哈气了，它们被冻得通红。

En: You don't have any fuckin' problems! Not a single one!
Zh: 你他妈的一点问题都没有！一个也没有！

En: Uh huh, good on you. She turns to check her tape recorder.
Zh: “嗯哼，干得漂亮。”她转身去检查自己的录音机。

En: I'll keep that in mind, for future use. She turns to check her tape recorder.
Zh: “我会记住的，以备不时之需。”她转身去检查自己的录音机。

En: Oh, okay. Good to know.
Zh: “哦好吧，很高兴能知道。”

En: Yeah...
Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Thanks. She puts it on. It's a bit large for her.
Zh: “谢了。”她接过来，对她来说有些大了。

En: Oh, yeah. Egg's *really* into him. Maybe even *too* into him. He's a pretty intense personality.
Zh: “对啊，爱凡客*真的*很喜欢他，或许有点*太*喜欢他了。他是个非常热情的人。”

En: I'm pretty intense, too.
Zh: “我也很热情。”

En: Do you, uh, want to *talk* about what just happened?
Zh: “你，呃，想谈谈刚刚发生的事吗？”

En: You get the feeling you're being handled with kid gloves because your cheeks are still soaking wet.
Zh: 你有种自己被小心翼翼地对待着的感觉，因为你的脸颊仍然湿漉漉的。

En: Convince yourself that you *didn't* melt down just now.
Zh: 说服自己刚刚并*没有*崩溃。

En: Good. Controlling your emotions sounds good.
Zh: “很好，控制自己的情绪听上去不错。”

En: A crygasm.
Zh: “一次哭泣的高潮。”

En: Weakness is strength.
Zh: “弱点就是力量。”

En: That may be so, but such is Revachol and this is what is expected of us -- in Revachol.
Zh: “也许是这样，但这就是瑞瓦肖以及它对我们的期待——在瑞瓦肖是如此。”

En: Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢你。”

En: You don't. But you *will*.
Zh: “你没有，但是你*会的*。”

En: Yes. A clever ruse. I see.
Zh: “是的，一个鬼把戏，我看出来了。”

En: A what?
Zh: “一次什么？”

En: Even so, in the future, could you please refrain from emoting too much on duty? The quasi-legal status of the RCM means we need to project strength twice as consistently as any other police force in the world.\n
Zh: “即便如此，以后的工作中，请你务必不要过于情绪化。RCM的准合法地位意味着我们需要投射出两倍于世界上其他任何警力的力量。”

En: In the future, could you please refrain from emoting too much on duty? The quasi-legal status of the RCM means we need to project strength twice as consistently as any other police force in the world.
Zh: “以后的工作中，请你务必不要过于情绪化。RCM的准合法地位意味着我们需要投射出两倍于世界上其他任何警力的力量。”

En: I experienced a minor malfunction. I'm okay now.
Zh: “我遇到了一点小故障，现在没事了。”

En: What? Nothing happened!!
Zh: “什么？什么都没有！！”

En: The lieutenant blinks. "So you're saying you *didn't* break down in tears just now and nearly tear your shirt off?"
Zh: 警督眨眨眼睛。“所以你是说你刚刚*没有*哭得几乎把衬衫都撕裂了？”

En: Okay, then it isn't strength. You're right.
Zh: “好吧，那它就不是力量了。你是对的。”

En: I've never cried once in my life...
Zh: “我这辈子从没有哭过……”

En: Sounds like typical macho bullshit to me.
Zh: “在我听来，这就是典型的大男子主义。”

En: Uh huh.
Zh: “嗯哼。”

En: A performance that would make the star of a radio melodrama blush.
Zh: 一场能使情景剧中的明星面红耳赤的表演。

En: Okay, I'll control my emotions.
Zh: “好吧，我会控制好情绪的。”

En: Not when projecting strength -- then it's just weakness.
Zh: “在投射力量的时候不行——否则那只是弱点了。”

En: Glad to hear it was 'minor.' Can you go on?
Zh: “很高兴听到那只是个‘小’故障。你能继续吗？”

En: Right. I mean, who *cries* in public, like for real?
Zh: “没错。我是说，有谁真的会在大庭广众之下*哭鼻子*呢？”

En: That's us. Revachol Citizens Militia. The police.
Zh: “那就是我们，瑞瓦肖公民武装，警察。”

En: Who indeed?
Zh: “会有谁呢？”

En: It's when you totally break down and it feels a little better afterwards. I don't know where I got the expression from. I may have just invented it.
Zh: “当你完全崩溃过后，就会感觉好一些。我不知道这个说法是从哪里来的，可能是我自己原创的。”

En: The RCM?
Zh: “RCM？”

En: It was nothing. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么。”[离开。]

En: The lieutenant merely nods.
Zh: 警督仅是点了点头。

En: Don't worry, Kim, it was *all a ruse*. (Wink at Kim.)
Zh: “别担心，金，这*只是个鬼把戏*。”（对金使眼色。）

En: Yes, I've definitely never seen you cry before, nor lent you my handkerchief to dry your tears.
Zh: “是的，我从来没见你哭过，也没把我的手帕借给你擦过眼泪。”

En: Absolutely. Superstars always get back up and try again.
Zh: “绝对的，超级巨星总是能够重新站起来，再试一次。”

En: Yes. It is my duty, after all.
Zh: “是的，毕竟那是我的职责。”

En: The end is near anyway, I might as well see this through...
Zh: “反正终结即将来临了，我还是努力工作吧……”

En: I can. And I'm sorry, Kim. I promise this is the last time.
Zh: “可以，还有很抱歉，金。我保证这是最后一次了。”

En: Then let's go.
Zh: “那我们走吧。”

En: Believeth in thyself, my liege! 'Twas all a clever ruse.
Zh: 相信自己，大人！那只是个鬼把戏。

En: Thy pen of lies runneth dry. Best to simplie deny the Truth baldfacedly.
Zh: 您的谎言之笔已经枯竭，最好是直截了当地否认真相。

En: 'Tis possible thou couldst persuade the lieutenant 'twas all a part of thy 'unorthodox' method. But can thou persuade *thyself*?
Zh: 你是有可能说服警督的，这完全是你‘非正统’手段的一部分。但你能说服*你自己*吗？

En: That's probably a good idea.
Zh: “这大概是个好主意。”

En: What's there to talk about?
Zh: “有什么好谈的？”

En: So, what do *you* think happened?
Zh: “那么，*你*觉得发生了什么？”

En: Normally I'm opposed to discussing one's feelings, especially on-duty, but I think this is an exceptional case...
Zh: “通常情况下，我是反对谈论个人感受的，尤其是在上班的时候。但我认为这次是个特例……”

En: ... you could torture me, abandon me, even *divorce* me, and I'd be dry as a desert, yessir. (Wipe your eyes.)
Zh: “……你可以折磨我，抛弃我，甚至跟我*离婚*，而我也会像沙漠一样干枯，绝对如此。”（擦眼睛。）

En: Hrmm, methinks my liege doth protest too much.
Zh: 呵，吾想大人抗议的有些太多了。

En: Okay, right. Enough of this.
Zh: “好吧，行了，足够了。”

En: I was so intimidated, I didn't dare to ask her...
Zh: “我太害怕了，不敢问她……”

En: Come on, man. You've gotta talk to her for us! He attempts to conceal his disappointment.
Zh: “拜托，老兄。你得替我们跟她谈谈！”他试图掩饰自己的失望。

En: Now's not a great time to dance, is it?
Zh: “现在不是跳舞的好时候，不是吗？”

En: No, detective. It is not. The lieutenant looks toward the church door. "We really should get to the Whirling-in-Rags. Now."
Zh: “不，警探。不是。”警督望向教堂的大门。“我们应该尽快赶到褴褛飞旋去。”

En: Who exactly are these people inside the church?
Zh: “教堂里面的那些人究竟是谁？”

En: Yes?
Zh: “你说？”

En: Oh yeah, that's a Meteoran name for the Founding Party. Thought it'd be cool to use it.
Zh: “没错，那是立国党的美特拉语名字。我感觉这么说挺酷的。”

En: Before we go on, what do you mean by *Meteoran*?
Zh: “在我们继续之前，你说的*美特拉*是什么意思？”

En: (Don't mask it.) "I don't even know what the Founding Party is."
Zh: （不要遮掩。）“我甚至不知道立国党是什么。”

En: And what is the *Founding Party*?
Zh: “还有什么是*立国党*？”

En: You know -- of Meteo. Concerning Meteo.
Zh: “你知道的——就是美特拉。跟美特拉有关的。”

En: Meteo?
Zh: “流星？”

En: Sounds like a place. I've had enough of those. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “听上去像是个地方。我受够了，还是谈点别的吧。”

En: If you don't know what the Founding Party is, there might be a way to mask it with minor demagoguery.
Zh: 如果你不知道立国党是什么，或许可以通过煽动他们来遮掩一下。

En: (Mask it.) "Now humour me, Andre -- what is the Founding Party?"
Zh: （遮掩一下。）“满足我一下吧，安德烈——立国党是什么？”

En: Meteo. A country. On Mundi? He looks at you, squinting his eyes to see if you're kidding. "On the Mundi isola."
Zh: “美特拉。一个国家。在蒙迪？”他眯着眼睛打量你是否在开玩笑。“就在蒙迪洲。”

En: Come to think of it, I've never really looked them up, you know. I can't give you a precise definition, but they're a very powerful religious organization.
Zh: “仔细想想，我从来没仔细地了解过他们。我没办法给你一个准确的定论，但他们是一个相当强大的宗教组织。”

En: And?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: (Nod.) "Good. Now, let's talk about something else."
Zh: （点头。）“很好，现在我们来谈点别的吧。”

En: Now Andre -- in your opinion -- would this ancient religious organization who *anoints the innocence* want a club for anodic music in one of their churches?
Zh: “那么，安德烈——在你看来——这个*神化了无罪者*的古老宗教组织会希望他们的教堂里有一间阳极音乐俱乐部吗？”

En: Now Andre -- in your opinion -- would this ancient religious organization who *anoints the innocence* want a dance club in one of their churches?
Zh: “那么，安德烈——在你看来——这个*神化了无罪者*的古老宗教组织会希望他们的教堂里有一间俱乐部吗？”

En: They sound like exactly the kind of who would want loud anodic music in their church!
Zh: “听起来他们完全就是那种希望在教堂里听到吵闹阳极舞曲的人！”

En: They sound like exactly the kind of people who would want loud dance music in their church!
Zh: “听起来他们完全就是那种希望在教堂里听到吵闹舞曲的人！”

En: Or is it because you're balding?
Zh: “或者是因为你谢顶了？”

En: Is it important for you? To be an individual?
Zh: “这对你很重要吗？成为一个独立的个体？”

En: That's idiotic.
Zh: “这太愚蠢了。”

En: Fair enough.
Zh: “有道理。”

En: I wanted to ask you about this tent full of equipment.
Zh: “我想问问你关于这个装满设备的帐篷的事。”

En: Yes -- it's a matter of occupied  ecclesiastical property. I bet you've noticed the derelict hive of *narcomania* on the coast?
Zh: “是的——这是被占领的教会财产问题。我敢说你一定注意到海边那片*毒枭*的废弃窝点了吧？”

En: He's not as glad as he would like you to think. There is concern in his voice.
Zh: 他并不像他希望你认为的那样高兴，反而声音中充满了关切。

En: Oh, so you've met her? Good, good. He nods.
Zh: “哦，那你见过她了？很好，很好。”他点了点头。

En: Hi again. So, uh... How're things going? He looks excited. The tips of his hair are sharp and white. The bleach has consumed almost all of the toothbrush on the mirror in front of him.
Zh: “又见面了。那个，呃……事情进展的如何了？”他看起来很兴奋，他的发梢又尖又白。漂白剂几乎把他面前镜子上的牙刷溶解光了。

En: I knew it! The man is back for those sweet, sweet questions! The young man smiles at you. "What will it be?"
Zh: “我就知道！那个人又回来问问题了！”那个年轻人对你笑着。“会是什么呢？”

En: And they have roots in ancient mass society. He pauses. "And they're the custodians of the Perikarnassian Church. Plus they anoint the innocence. They, like, made the innocentic system, no?"
Zh: “他们扎根于古老的大众社会。”他顿了一下。“是佩里卡纳西斯教堂的守护者。此外他们还神化了无罪者，就是说，他们创造了整个无罪体系，不是吗？”

En: It doesn't sound like they would appreciate a dance club in their church.
Zh: “听起来他们不会喜欢自己的教堂里有一间舞蹈俱乐部的。”

En: You're right! How could the Founding Party be anything but enthusiastic? Dance music *is* about love and so is the Perikarnassian Church. (Proceed.)
Zh: “你是对的！立国党怎么会缺乏激情呢？舞曲*是*有爱的，佩里卡纳西斯教堂也一样。”（继续。）

En: I now understand it was *lame* of me to suggest otherwise. Anodic music *is* about love and so is the Perikarnassian Church. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我现在明白提出异议是多么*愚蠢*了，阳极音乐*是*有爱的，佩里卡纳西斯教堂也一样。”（继续。）

En: Goodbye, officer.
Zh: “再见，警官。”

En: You can always come ask for the key to the church if you change your mind. Keep it cool, officer. He winks at you.
Zh: “如果你改变了主意，随时都可以来拿教堂的钥匙。不着急的，警官。”他冲你眨眨眼。

En: They've got one speaker. One. Uno. Un.
Zh: 他们有一个扬声器。一个。单个。独个。

En: He gave me this odd lecture on alcoholism, before rambling on and on about Mother's love.
Zh: “在他给我发表了一番关于酗酒的奇怪说教之后，又开始漫无边际地大谈特谈母爱了。”

En: You were right, he's a true narcomaniac. And the way he climbs! It was terrifying.
Zh: “你说得对，他就是个麻醉剂狂。还有他攀爬的方式！真是太可怕了。”

En: Just preaching and praying, from the looks of it.
Zh: “从表面看来，只有劝诫和祈祷。”

En: There's something sinister going on under the building's roof... I think he's getting high or something.
Zh: “那个房顶之下有某种不祥之事正在上演……我想他可能是嗑药了。”

En: He clearly enjoys the physical activity. Guy climbs like a freak!
Zh: “他显然很喜欢体育活动，这家伙爬起来跟怪物似的！”

En: It didn't sound like a sober man's talk. He must be getting *visions* up there.
Zh: “听起来不像是清醒的人说出来的话，他一定是在上面产生*幻觉*了。”

En: I'm not sure, I'm just telling you what it *felt* like.
Zh: “我不确定，只是把这种*感觉*告诉你而已。”

En: A *crab-man*, man!!!
Zh: “一个*蟹男*啊，老兄！！！”

En: I hear you, friend. What about it?
Zh: “我听到了，朋友，它怎么了吗？”

En: [Leave the tent.]
Zh: [离开帐篷。]

En: And... you haven't changed your mind about our little side business, have you?
Zh: “那……你对我们的副业并没有产生动摇，对吧？”

En: And... you're still sure about keeping out our little side business, right?
Zh: “那……你还是不赞同我们的副业，对吧？”

En: Oh, you mean the *illegal drug lab*? There will be no shady shenanigans in the church, only love and anodic dance music.
Zh: “哦，你指哪个*非法毒品实验室*吗？不，我不同意。教堂里不允许出现邪恶勾当，只有爱和阳极舞曲。”

En: Actually, I have been thinking....
Zh: “老实说，我一直在想……”

En: No. A smile vanishes from Egg Head's face as he looks around the room. "No, I thought we agreed to do this pure and true. Guys, we're not changing our mind."
Zh: “不行。”爱凡客环顾室内，笑容随之消失。“不行，咱们说好了要以真心相对，干得纯粹。伙计们，咱们可不能变心。”

En: I'm a man of my word.
Zh: “我一向说话算数。”

En: Actually, I have been thinking....
Zh: “老实说，我一直在想……”

En: No. The one with the necklace stares at you coldly. "You don't get to squeeze out every drop of us and then 'change your mind'."
Zh: “不。”戴项链的那个人冷冰冰地盯着你。“你不能在榨干我们之后，又‘改变主意’。”

En: We're setting up the shop. It's too late to turn around now. Deal with it, it was your decision, cop-man.
Zh: “我们正准备要开张了。现在回头已经晚了，认命吧，这就是你的决定，警官。”

En: No. The one with the necklace stares at you coldly. "You don't get to take all our money and then 'change your mind'.
Zh: “不。”戴项链的那个人冷冰冰地盯着你。“你不能在掏光我们所有的钱之后。又‘改变主意’。”

En: Alright! Andre grins as if this settles it. "It's going to take us a bit to move our stuff inside. A couple of hours, maybe. Come check back later!" He waves to the other speedfreaks. "Let's get moving!"
Zh: “太好了！”安德烈咧嘴一笑，似乎问题就解决了。“我们还得花点时间把设备搬进去，大概要几个小时吧。之后过来看看啊！”安德烈朝其他瘾君子们招招手。“行动起来！”

En: DEAL! yells Egg Head.
Zh: “认命！”爱凡客叫嚷着。

En: No. The one with the necklace stares at you coldly. "You don't get to say one thing and then 'change your mind'. We trusted you, cop-man."
Zh: “不。”戴项链的那个人冷冰冰地盯着你。“你不能再承诺过之后又‘改变主意’，我们相信了你，警官。”

En: We're setting up the shop. It's too late to turn around now.
Zh: “我们正准备要开张了，现在回头已经晚了。”

En: TRUST! yells Egg Head.
Zh: “相信！”爱凡客叫嚷着。

En: [Leave the tent.]
Zh: [离开帐篷。]

En: Fine... He seems disappointed. "We can make do. It's going to take us a bit to move our stuff inside. A couple of hours, maybe. Come check back later." Andre waves to the other speedfreaks. "Let's get moving!"
Zh: “好吧……”他的失望之情溢于言表。“可以凑合一下。我们得花点时间才能把行李搬进去。可能需要几个小时。等我们搞定再来吧。”安德烈向其他瘾君子挥了挥手。“咱们开始搬吧！”

En: [Leave the tent.]
Zh: [离开帐篷。]

En: And... what about the side business? Have you made up your mind?
Zh: “那……我们的副业呢？你决定了吗？”

En: You mean the drug lab? Yeah, that's not going to happen. It's illegal, you see. (Refuse to set it up.)
Zh: “你的意思是毒品实验室？是啊，那是不可能的。你要知道那是违法的。”（拒绝建立。）

En: Proceed with the side business, but I want a cut. (Allow them to set it up.)
Zh: “去做你们的副业吧，但我要分一杯羹。”（允许建立。）

En: Side business? I don't know what you're talking about. (Turn a blind eye.)
Zh: “副业？我不知道你在说什么。”（视而不见。）

En: Of course! He gives you a knowing nod. "I meant the bar, obviously. Why am I even talking about this?" Andre grins as if this settles it.
Zh: “当然！”他会意地点点头。“我当然说的是酒吧啦，我干嘛要提起这个呢？”安德烈咧嘴一笑，似乎问题就解决了。

En: No. The one with the necklace stares at you coldly. "You don't get to squeeze out every drop of us after keeping us in the dark for so long."
Zh: “不。”戴项链的那个人冷冰冰地盯着你。“你不能在瞒了我们这么久之后，又继续压榨我们。”

En: Noid! Andre turns to his friend. "Let's be reasonable here! We could use a friendly face in the station." He turns back to you with a wide smile on his face.
Zh: “诺伊德！”安德烈转向他的朋友。“我们讲讲道理吧！我们需要一个警局的好朋友。”他转过身来，脸上挂着灿烂的微笑。

En: How about 50%? It's more than a fair deal. We good?
Zh: “50%如何？这样相当公平了，可以吗？”

En: Alright.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: It's going to take us a bit to move our stuff inside. A couple of hours, maybe. Come check back later! He waves to the other speedfreaks. "Let's get moving!"
Zh: “我们还得花点时间把设备搬进去，大概要几个小时吧。之后过来看看啊！”安德烈朝其他瘾君子们招招手。“行动起来！”

En: [Leave the tent.]
Zh: [离开帐篷。]

En: Hang on, I thought I'd get it right now?
Zh: “等一下，我以为现在就能拿到呢？”

En: Come now, it'll take weeks to get everything set up, start making the produce, and deliver it around to the needy. This is a lengthy process.
Zh: “拜托，把一切都准备好，开始制造，然后分发给有需要的人，得花好几个星期的时间。这是个漫长的过程。”

En: Alright! He grins. "It's going to take us a bit to move our stuff inside. A couple of hours, maybe. Come check back later!" Andre waves to the other speedfreaks. "Let's get moving!"
Zh: “好嘞！”他咧嘴一笑。“我们得花点时间才能把行李搬进去。可能需要几个小时。等我们搞定再来吧！”安德烈向其他瘾君子挥了挥手。“咱们开始搬吧！”

En: Aw man, really?! His smile wanes slightly. "You're a complete ballbuster."
Zh: “哦，认真的吗？！”你的笑容渐渐消失了。“你就是个彻头彻尾的大坏蛋。”

En: Fine... He seems disappointed. "We can make do. It's going to take us a bit to move our stuff inside. A couple of hours, maybe. Come check back later." Andre waves to the other speedfreaks. "Let's get moving!"
Zh: “好吧……”他的失望之情溢于言表。“可以凑合一下。我们得花点时间才能把行李搬进去。可能需要几个小时。等我们搞定再来吧。”安德烈向其他瘾君子挥了挥手。“咱们开始搬吧！”

En: [Leave the tent.]
Zh: [离开帐篷。]

En: What do you propose as the name, Andre?
Zh: “你建议用什么名字好呢，安德烈？”

En: Egg Head, you must have a lot of ideas.
Zh: “爱凡客，你一定有很多想法。”

En: Acele, what would you suggest as the name?
Zh: “阿西尔，你觉得用什么名字好呢？”

En: Soona, do you have an opinion on this?
Zh: “苏娜，你对此有什么看法吗？”

En: Kim, how would you name the club?
Zh: “金，你会给俱乐部起什么名字呢？”

En: I think I've come up with a name... (Proceed.)
Zh: “我想好了一个名字……”（继续。）

En: How about something simple, like 'The Club'?
Zh: “简单点如何，比如就叫俱乐部？”

En: Too modern, Andre shakes his spiky head. "And too ironic. We don't want ironic, we want *real*. Real and true and beautiful, like a morning after the rave."
Zh: “太现代，”安德烈摇了摇他的尖脑袋。“也太讽刺了。我们不需要讽刺，我们要的是*真实*。真实、准确、美丽，如同狂欢后的清晨。”

En: The name... He appears to mull it over, one hand idly touching his hair. "Everything I manage to come up with sounds just *wrong*."
Zh: “名字……”他似乎在沉思着，一只手漫不经心地摸着自己的头发。“我想出来的一切听起来都是*错误*的。”

En: I don't.
Zh: “没有。”

En: No, it has to be bigger than Hard Core. Andre looks worried as he comes to this realization. "Yes, it has to be even bigger than *Hard Core to the Mega*, it has to be bigger than the scene!"
Zh: “不，它必须比硬核更加响亮。”安德烈意识到这一点之后显得有些担忧。“没错，必须必*硬核最强*还要响亮，比那个场面还要响亮！”

En: YEKOKATAA! The freak with the large head yells from the stage, waving his hand in the air. "THE PLACE TO BE!"
Zh: “叶科卡塔！”大脑袋的瘾君子在舞台上叫嚷着，双手在空中挥舞。“叶科卡塔，快快出发！”

En: Yekokataa, the zone of ecological catastrophe? That's too morbid, Egg. Got anything else?
Zh: “叶科卡塔，生态重灾区？这也太变态了，凡客。还有别的吗？”

En: HARD CORE! A witless, victorious smile adorns his face. "HARD CORE TO THE MEGA!"
Zh: “硬核！”他的脸上挂着一种愚蠢的胜利微笑。“硬核最强！”

En: I wouldn't. I wouldn't build a club and I wouldn't name it either.
Zh: “我不会的，我不会建立一间俱乐部，也不会给它命名。”

En: An underground place with no name? Sounds like something the crab-man would say.
Zh: “一个没有名字的秘密组织？听上去像是蟹男会说的话。”

En: We're not going with anything the crab-man would say.
Zh: “我们不会同意蟹男说的任何东西的。”

En: Noid, give me your two cents on the issue.
Zh: “诺伊德，发表一下意见吧。”

En: The Amnesia.
Zh: “失忆症。”

En: Like... the I-can't-remember-the-name-of-the-club amnesia?
Zh: “就像……我想不起来俱乐部名字了的那种失忆症吗？”

En: Why not? The crab-man has *ideas*, Acele. Ideas from another level of consciousness!
Zh: “为什么不呢？蟹男也有自己的*想法*，来自另一个意识层面的想法！”

En: I don't care, I don't like the crab-man and I don't like his ideas, his ideas are spooky. Next, please!
Zh: “我不管，我不喜欢蟹男，也不喜欢他的想法，他的想法都让人毛骨悚然的。下一个，请继续！”

En: It's not Amnesia, guys.
Zh: “可不能叫失忆症，伙计们。”

En: AMNESIA!
Zh: “失忆症！”

En: Good, we have too many opinions anyway.
Zh: “很好，反正我们的想法够多了。”

En: Andre's overthinking it, says the girl with the microphone.
Zh: “安德烈想多了，”拿着麦克的女孩说。

En: You have? Andre stops touching his hair. "Well, what's the name?"
Zh: “有吗？”安德烈不再碰他的头发了。“嗯，是什么名字呢？”

En: Disco Elysium.
Zh: “极乐迪斯科。”

En: No Truce with the Furies.
Zh: “永不和复仇女神休战。”

En: Revachol West.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖西部。”

En: No, it's beautiful. Beautiful and brave! Like we want it to be.
Zh: “不，这很美，又美又勇敢！就像我们希望的那样。”

En: Like that Dolorian word for the world, you mean? Elysium...
Zh: “你是说，就像德洛莉丝时代的世界这个词吗？极乐世界……”

En: But *Disco* Elysium... She looks unsure. "Isn't it wacky? Disco's kind of gone, isn't it? Forgotten."
Zh: “但是极乐*迪斯科*……”她有些犹豫。“不是很古怪吗？迪斯科有点过时了，不是吗？已经被人遗忘了。”

En: THE PAST IS THE FUTURE, BUT THE FUTURE IS DEAD!
Zh: “过去即为未来，但未来已死！”

En: Egg's right, they can call it 'No Truce' if it's too long. At least it stands out from the others, it *evokes* something, it's victorious.
Zh: “凡客说得对，如果嫌太长他们也可以叫它永不止战。至少它很与众不同，*唤醒*了某种东西，象征着凯旋。”

En: Sounds like money.
Zh: “听起来很赚钱的样子。”

En: What does that mean? he yells over the thumping music.
Zh: “那是什么意思？”他在砰砰的乐声中喊着。

En: Wow, it's big. A club for entire Revachol West? So big!
Zh: “哇哦，真响亮。为整个瑞瓦肖西部建立的俱乐部吗？真是响亮！”

En: SOUNDS LIKE FUTURE!
Zh: “听上去像是未来！”

En: It's settled, then. Everyone, welcome to Revachol West! He looks around the church as light beams paint the dance floor violet blue.
Zh: “那就这么定了。欢迎大家来到瑞瓦肖西部！”当光线把舞池染上紫罗兰色时，他环顾了一圈教堂。

En: It's too long. No one's going to remember it on a Friday night.
Zh: “太长了，周五的夜晚没人能记住的。”

En: NO TRUCE!
Zh: “永不止战！”

En: And yet mysterious. He looks around the church as light beams wash over the dance floor, bathing it in violet blue.
Zh: “而且还很神秘。”他环视整间教堂，与此同时，舞池正沐浴在蓝紫色的光芒之中。

En: It's settled, then. Everyone, welcome to No Truce with the Furies!
Zh: “那就这么定了。欢迎大家来到永不止战！”

En: Andre breaks into frenzied dance-like motion to celebrate the name. Someone turns up the beat.
Zh: 安德烈跳起了癫狂的舞步以庆祝这个名字，有人把音量调大了。

En: You should go with the flow. Join in on the experience.
Zh: 你应该顺势而行，体验一番。

En: Start tapping your foot.
Zh: 开始用脚打着拍子。

En: (Observe his movement.) "What are you doing, Andre?"
Zh: （观察他的动作。）“你干什么呢，安德烈？”

En: It feels good, it feels right... but what is this? What is this *thing* that Andre is doing with his limbs?!
Zh: 这感觉很好，很对……但这是什么？安德烈究竟在用他的肢体做*什么*？！

En: (I don't care.) "Cool, let's move on to the questions then."
Zh: （我不在乎。）“很好，我们开始问问题吧。”

En: I'm dancing! He performs yet another strange pattern of moves, but it doesn't look very cool or modern. Honestly, it looks kind of lame...
Zh: “我在跳舞！”他又演示了另一种奇怪的动作，但看起来并不酷，也不流行。老实说，还有点蹩脚……

En: It's settled, then. Everyone, welcome to Disco Elysium! A light beam washes over the dance floor, bathing it in violet blue.
Zh: “那就这么定了。欢迎大家来到极乐迪斯科！”舞池沐浴在紫罗兰色的光线中。

En: That soft-core gyrating is supposed to be *dancing*?
Zh: 那样软绵绵的旋转就是所谓的*跳舞*？

En: Yes, my man! He jumps up and down with glee, his moves punctuated by the stroboscopic flash of the club lights.
Zh: “是啊，我的兄弟！”他高兴地跳来跳去，动作却不时地被俱乐部的闪光灯打断。

En: We should talk about it. We should talk about your so-called 'dancing'.
Zh: “我们应该谈谈，应该谈谈你所谓的‘跳舞’。”

En: Now the only thing left to do is the name of the club. Will you do the honours, detective?
Zh: “现在唯一要做的就是给俱乐部命名了。能请你赏光吗，警探？”

En: Yes, *you* should do it, detective. The speedfreak with yellow beads around his neck is looking at you. "It would be good for the sines."
Zh: “没错，应该是*你*来，警探。”脖子上挂着黄色珠子的瘾君子看着你。“对正弦波也有好处。”

En: Noid's right. You've helped us so much, it's the right thing to do.
Zh: “正如诺伊德所说。你对我们有恩，这是我们应该做的。”

En: Finally someone who understands the plight of narcomania! Play it cool, you might have an ally here.
Zh: 总算能有人理解，麻醉剂狂的困境了！表现地冷静一点，你也许能在这里找到伙伴。

En: Narcomania... (Nod thoughtfully.) "What exactly are we talking about here?"
Zh: “麻醉剂狂……”（若有所思地点点头。）“我们到底在说什么啊？”

En: Good. This calls for an *opinion*. You're an expert in those.
Zh: 很好。这就需要一个*意见*。你是这方面的专家。

En: Interesting, sire. A bit like you then. An 'MC', for short.
Zh: 有意思，大人。还有一点像你。简写为“MC”。

En: I get it. He's a puzzle.
Zh: 我明白了。他是个谜题。

En: One of the poorest of the first world nations today -- but once a great ancient civilization. Capital: Thylakos-by-Pisantic.
Zh: 今天世界上最穷的第一世界国家——但是曾经拥有伟大的远古文明。首都：泰拉科斯-皮桑缇克。

En: And where is your posse, detective? Nothing comes to you. The world is silent.
Zh: 那你的老铁在哪里，警探？你什么也没有。这个世界都是一片寂静。

En: UNITY! DANCE!
Zh: “团结！舞蹈！”

En: You see a youngish man bleaching the tips of his hair with a toothbrush. He puts the toothbrush down and extends his hand in greeting.
Zh: 你看到一个年轻人正在用牙刷漂染头发。他放下牙刷，腾出手来打了个招呼。

En: There is definitely something futuristic about his hair, aggressively so. You get the sense that *this* is what the future will look like...
Zh: 他的头发相当有未来感，很有侵略性。你有一种未来应该*如此*的感觉……

En: Imbecilic. Yes, should the future ever come, it will look deeply imbecilic. Like this guy.
Zh: 太蠢了。没错，如果未来真的来了，那一定显得相当愚蠢，就像这个家伙。

En: Shake his hand.
Zh: 与他握手。

En: Don't shake it.
Zh: 不与他握手。

En: His grip is strong, sweaty, and warm. He's trying to project and inspire confidence.
Zh: 他的手掌有力，湿润，也很温暖。他在试着展现和激发自信。

En: This is my posse: Noid....
Zh: “这是我的老铁：诺伊德……”

En: This is my posse: Noid... He pauses with his hand still hanging in the air.
Zh: “这是我的老铁：诺伊德……”他停顿了一下，手仍然悬在空中。

En: The young man with earrings looks at you suspiciously.
Zh: 戴耳环的年轻人狐疑地盯着你。

En: Egg! The tape player high above his head continues to blast what is probably *anodic* music.\n
Zh: “艾！”他把录音机举过头顶，持续播放着的可能是*阳极*音乐。

En: Egg! he yells. The tape player high above his head continues to blast strange music.
Zh: “艾！”他叫嚷着，头顶上的录音机持续播放着奇怪的音乐。

En: ...and Egg Head.
Zh: “……还有爱凡客。”

En: Together with Acele Berger -- who's out there right now, doing some *seriously* progressive sonic experimentation -- we like to think of ourselves as 'music venue organizers.'
Zh: “还有阿西尔·伯格——她正在外面做某种*正经的*前卫音波试验——我们喜欢把自己看作是‘音乐派对的组织者’。”

En: Wait, how many music venues have you organized?
Zh: “等等，你们组织了多少个音乐派对？”

En: Why are you here?
Zh: “你们怎么会在这里？”

En: We have many in the pipeline, officer.
Zh: “有很多正在准备中，警官。”

En: You see, we've been all over Jamrock North, prospecting for real estate to establish a new venue in...
Zh: “你看，我们已经跑遍了整个加姆洛克北部，寻找场地，希望能打造一个新的音乐派对……”

En: Also for talent!
Zh: “还为了人才！”

En: Yes, thank you, Egg Head. And, while there is no shortage of raw, unfettered talent spinning tapes in Jamrock, we've had rotten luck with the real estate part.
Zh: “是的，谢谢你，爱凡客。而且，加姆洛克不乏才华横溢、未经雕琢的音乐磁带，不过我们在场馆方面确实运气不佳。”

En: Place is a shithole.
Zh: “这地方就是个粪坑。”

En: Which brings me to the problem of occupied ecclesiastical property. I bet you've noticed the derelict hive of *narcomania* on the coast?
Zh: “这就让我想到了被占领的教会财产的问题。我敢说你一定注意到海岸上*麻醉剂狂*的废弃窝点了吧？”

En: There's no need. The place is pretty bad.
Zh: “没必要，这地方相当糟糕。”

En: Next time, watch yourself.
Zh: “下次，注意一点。”

En: I apologize for my friend Noid's potty-mouth. *I* realize this is not how you speak to a police officer. He has authority issues.
Zh: “我为我的朋友诺伊德的臭嘴道歉。*我*知道这不是跟警察说话的正确方式。他对当局有些抵触。”

En: I will, law-man. The young man nods and drops a drill chuck into a plastic container.
Zh: “我会的，执法官。”年轻人点了点头，把钻夹头扔进了一个塑料容器。

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么？”

En: An attempt to pander to your perceived conservative sensibilities. No person his age would ever use a word like *narcomania* with a straight face. Don't fall for it!
Zh: 他在试图迎合你的保守程度。在他这个年纪，没有人会板着脸使用*麻醉剂狂*这样的字眼，别上当了！

En: Enough histrionics. What are you talking about?
Zh: “别再装模作样的了。你在说什么？”

En: I'm talking about the church. And I'm not exaggerating! Even a place of spiritual refuge can become a magnet for all sorts of *dopeheads* and *burnouts* if left unattended...
Zh: “我说的是教堂。而且我一点也没在夸张！哪怕是个心灵避难所，如果无人看管，也会吸引各种各样的*混混*和*瘾君子*的。”

En: Dopeheads!
Zh: “瘾君子！”

En: Burnouts! He angrily spits on a screw, then starts cleaning it.
Zh: “混混！”他气愤地往一枚螺钉上吐了口唾沫，然后做起了清洁。

En: Well, I'm sad to say, that's exactly what happened. Sad because we were just about to put Martinaise on the map with one of the maddest dance clubs in Jamrock -- no, strike that -- in Revachol...
Zh: “我很遗憾，但事情确实如此。遗憾是因为我们正打算让马丁内斯以拥有全加姆洛克——不，不对——全瑞瓦肖最疯狂的舞蹈俱乐部的方式出名呢……”

En: Strike that -- the world!
Zh: “不对——全世界！”

En: And sadder yet because the dopeheads and burnouts holed up in there are *the worst* kind.
Zh: “更遗憾的是，那里面的混混和瘾君子都是*最糟糕*的那种。”

En: What kind?
Zh: “哪种？”

En: The *spooky* kind.
Zh: “令人*毛骨悚然*的那种。”

En: What exactly do you mean by *spooky*?
Zh: “*毛骨悚然*具体指的是？”

En: 'Spookiness' is not a matter for police investigation.
Zh: “*闹鬼事件*并不属于警方的调查范围。”

En: You know, officer, you really should judge that for yourself. But keep in mind, their spookiness is the kind that keeps us from restoring this church into a community centre. And a place of spiritual refuge.
Zh: “警官，你真的应该自己去判断一下。但请记住，正是那些令人毛骨悚然的东西让我们无法将这座教堂重建为社区中心，一处心灵避难所。”

En: Also, they don't heat or clean the building. Shit's gonna collapse.
Zh: “此外，他们没有给房子供暖或者清理，那玩意迟早会塌掉的。”

En: People just wanna spin tapes without them spookin' it up! Place has bad sines! No one can dance like that.
Zh: “大家只是想在不被惊吓的情况下放些磁带！那地方的正弦波很不好！那样没人愿意跳舞的。”

En: I was hoping you would be the judge of that, officer. All I can say is, their spookiness is the kind that keeps us from restoring this church into a community centre. And a place of spiritual refuge.
Zh: “我希望你能亲自去判断一下，警官。我只能说，正是那些令人毛骨悚然的东西让我们无法将这座教堂重建为社区中心，一处心灵避难所。”

En: Thank you, Egg Head...
Zh: “谢谢你，爱凡客……”

En: You mentioned some kind Ecclesiastes own the church. Who are these Ecclesiastes?
Zh: “你刚提到说某种传道书是教堂的主人。这些传道书是谁？”

En: We'll look into it. Tell me more. (Accept the task.)
Zh: “我会去调查的。再多跟我说一点。”（接受任务。）

En: I'll look into it. Tell me more. (Get the task.)
Zh: “我会去调查的。再多跟我说一点。”（接受任务。）

En: I'll make up my mind later. I have questions for you first. (Not now.)
Zh: “我之后会考虑的，但我想先问你几个问题。”（现在不行。）

En: The police have more important things to do right now, kid. (Not now.)
Zh: “警察目前有更重要的事情要做呢，孩子。”（现在不行。）

En: So you're gonna look into it, right? He turns to you. "It *should* be a police matter -- getting them out. Whatever spooky stuff they're doing, I'm sure it's not what the Ecclesiastes meant their property for."
Zh: 他转向你：“所以你会去调查的，对吗？这*应该*是警方事务——驱逐他们。不管他们在搞什么鬼把戏，我敢说传道书不会希望他们的财产被这样使用。”

En: C'mon, man, at least *think* about it first. It's our lives at stake here, our *dreams!* I'm sure you'll see things differently once you've let me explain it... You guys like questions, right? Cops *love* questions, ask me some! You'll see!
Zh: “拜托老兄，至少*考虑*一下吧。这关系到我们的生命，我们的*梦想*啊！我相信如果你听了我的解释，就会改变看法了……你们喜欢问问题，对吧？警察*热爱*问问题，那就问我吧！你就明白了！”

En: Yeah! His comrade nods his huge head along to the bass. "You got questions -- we got answers!"
Zh: “是啊！”他的同伴有节奏地点了点他巨大的头。“你们有问题——我们有答案！”

En: *Alright*, man! He claps his hands, enthusiastically...
Zh: “*太好了*，老兄！”他热情地拍手……

En: ...with the sort of glee that's meant to imply you've already taken on the case.
Zh: ……这种喜悦意味着你已经接手这个案子了。

En: Alright, man! He claps his hands enthusiastically.
Zh: “太好了，老兄！”他热情地拍手。

En: Andre is obviously very happy you took him seriously. The whole tent is. The boys exchange giddy looks.
Zh: 安德烈显然很高兴你能把他当一回事，整个帐篷里的人都是，男孩们交换着愉悦的眼神。

En: That's the spirit!
Zh: “就是这种劲头！”

En: Nope. [Leave.]
Zh: “不了。”[离开。]

En: Okay, maybe just a few questions...
Zh: “好吧，那就问几个问题……”

En: I bet he's got a shit-load of questions.
Zh: “我打赌他肯定有一大堆问题要问。”

En: He's right. All those sweet questions waiting to be answered... you can choose to let them go, though. There will be plenty of questions to ask at another time. Or even from other people.
Zh: 他说得对，那么多疑惑都有待回答……但你也可以选择让他们离开。这么多问题都可以留到下一次，甚至找其他人再问。

En: Acele told me Noid put the padlock on the door. Why?
Zh: “阿西尔告诉我说诺伊德把门锁上了。为什么？”

En: Did you put the padlock on the door?\n
Zh: “你在门上挂了一把锁吗？”

En: What's the status of the church? I haven't gotten inside the building yet.
Zh: “教堂的状况如何？我还没进去过呢。”

En: Totally. There isn't a trace of doubt in his voice.
Zh: “没错。”他的语气里没有一丝怀疑。

En: The Perikarnassian Church is about *love*! Anodic music is about *love*! I got love for my Perikarnassian posse, *love* is the relay out of death! WE DANCE!!! He violently shakes his fist to the rhythm of the beat.
Zh: “佩里卡纳西斯教堂是有*爱*的！阳极音乐同样很有*爱*！我获得了佩里卡纳西斯老铁团伙们的爱，*爱*是可以超越死亡的力量！我们跳舞！！！”他随着节奏猛烈地挥舞着拳头。

En: The Perikarnassian Church is about *love*! Anodic music is about *love*! I got love for my Perikarnassian posse, *love* is the relay out of death! WE DANCE!!! He violently shakes the tape player, as if to see if he can break it.
Zh: “佩里卡纳西斯教堂是有*爱*的！阳极音乐同样很有*爱*！我获得了佩里卡纳西斯老铁团的爱，*爱*是可以超越死亡的力量！我们跳舞！！！”他使劲地摇晃着录音机，好像在测试能否把它弄坏。

En: You're *totally* wrong about that. He shakes his head.
Zh: “你*大错特错*。”他摇了摇头。

En: Love is HARD CORE!
Zh: “爱是硬核！”

En: Unity.
Zh: “团结。”

En: UNITY!
Zh: “团结！”

En: Make some noise for my Insulindian posse! He turns the volume up, then looks at you with a knowing nod. As if it's obvious you will now break into dance.
Zh: “为我的伊苏林迪老铁团来点噪音啊！”他把音量调大，然后会意地点头看着你，仿佛你显然已经按捺不住要开始跳舞了一样。

En: You feel it. The anodes and the cathodes coursing through you -- your big toe starts tapping along to the bass, as if testing the waters...
Zh: 你感觉到了，阳极与阴极贯穿你的身体——你的大脚趾开始随着低音打着节拍，就像在试水一样……

En: I don't quite understand what you're talking about. What's a *posse*?
Zh: “我没明白你在说什么。什么是*老铁*？”

En: It sounds like you're just saying random things: love, posse, make noise.
Zh: “你听起来只是在随机说一些事情：爱、老铁、制造噪音。”

En: I guess love *can* be pretty hard core...
Zh: “我想爱也*可以*很硬核……”

En: Yeah, let it out! Let the disco happen. Bring the disco into this world! Get this church shit on and then dance there motherfucker!
Zh: 是啊，释放出来吧！开始迪斯科吧。把迪斯科带入这个世界！让这座教堂活起来，然后在那里尽情舞蹈吧，狗杂种！

En: You're right, they do! he says, without the slightest hint of irony.
Zh: “说得对，就是这样！”他没有一丝讽刺地说着。

En: On second thought -- no. This is too much. No sane organization would want this level of absurdity going on in their church. (Proceed.)
Zh: “转念一想——不，这样太过分了。任何理智的组织都不希望他们的教堂如此荒唐。”（继续。）

En: No. This is too much. No sane organization would want this level of absurdity in their church. (Proceed.)
Zh: “不，这样太过分了。任何理智的组织都不希望他们的教堂如此荒唐。”（继续。）

En: Your posse's like your people, man! Like you got your cop posse -- you look out for each other and you party together. That's a posse!
Zh: “老铁就是指你的同伴，老兄！就像你有警察老铁一样——你们相互照顾，一起狂欢。这就是老铁！”

En: Are we? He looks at you mysteriously.
Zh: “是吗？”他神秘地看着你。

En: Two men and a woman stand on the beach, under a row of houses long abandoned to war damage. The words "Feld Electrical" have peeled off the turn-of-the-century waterfront development. One of the men carries a small boy on his shoulders. The boy looks up at the faded logo.
Zh: 两个男人和一个女人站在海滩上，上面是一排长期被战争摧残的房子。“菲尔德电气”的字样已经从世纪之交的滨水开发区上剥落了。其中一个男人肩上扛着一个小男孩。男孩抬起头看着褪色的标识。

En: The woman says to him: "Good to see you here, special consultant. We're worried about the lieutenant-yefreitor."
Zh: 那个女人对他说：“很高兴在这里见到你，特别顾问。我们都很担心荣誉警督。”

En: Why are you worried about the lieutenant-yefreitor? asks the boy. Then a wave comes crashing in.
Zh: “为什么你们要担心荣誉警督？”男孩问到，然后一个浪头打了过来。

En: You hear a wave crashing outside, not far. It's a big one. The rumble dissipates into the bassline...
Zh: 你听到不远处有海浪打来，来势凶猛，轰隆声消失在领海基线……

En: Yeaahh... The one with the large head really enjoys it when his friend gets mysterious.
Zh: “是啊……”那个大脑袋显然很喜欢他的朋友神秘起来的样子。

En: Oh yeah, it can!
Zh: “哦耶，当然可以！”

En: He's coming around. He nods at his friend, then turns to you with a mischievous grin: "You're *getting* it."
Zh: “他来了。”他朝朋友点点头，然后带着狡黠的笑容转向你：“你马上就能*明白*了。”

En: I didn't want to say it, but it *was* pretty lame of you to imply otherwise. Anyway, you got more questions?
Zh: “我本不想这么说的，但你用别的话来暗示我*真是*太差劲了。无论如何，你还有问题吗？”

En: YEAAGH! Yekokataa -- the place to be!
Zh: “耶！！叶科卡塔——快快出发！”

En: Do it for the masses, do it for the crew. His friend forms a fist with a screwdriver still in his hand. Approvingly so.
Zh: “为了大家，为了全体人员。”他的朋友握紧拳头，手里还拿着一把螺丝刀，十分支持的样子。

En: He seems ecstatic that you share his vision of Perikarnassianism.
Zh: 他似乎很高兴你能同意他关于佩里卡纳西斯主义的观点。

En: That's pretty down-beat. I think I speak for all of us when I say we expected the law to be more open to the idea of love, unity, and the Perikarnassian posse.
Zh: “那可真是太令人沮丧了。当我说我们期望法律能对爱、团结和佩里卡纳西斯老铁团体更加开放一些的时候，我以为自己是为大家说的。”

En: That's just the way it is.
Zh: “事情就是如此。”

En: I don't understand a single thing you're saying.
Zh: “你说的话我一句也听不懂。”

En: I'm sorry the law doesn't share your vision.
Zh: “抱歉，法律并不同意你的观点。”

En: Yeah, he shrugs with melancholy. "I know you don't. So, you got more questions?"
Zh: “是啊。”他忧郁地耸耸肩。“我知道你不同意，还有别的问题吗？”

En: Yeah, he shrugs with melancholy. "I guess it is. So, you got more questions?"
Zh: “是啊。”他忧郁地耸耸肩。“我想也是，还有别的问题吗？”

En: Well... He pauses for a moment. "We're sorry too. So, you got more questions?"
Zh: “这个嘛……”他停顿了一会了。“我们也很抱歉，还有别的问题吗？”

En: No words. Just dance.
Zh: 没有语言，只是跳舞。

En: Aesthetic of anodic music.
Zh: 阳极音乐美学。

En: Higher quality audio.
Zh: 高质量音频。

En: Dance fever incoming.
Zh: 狂热舞蹈。

En: Standard Anti-Wirrâl Die.
Zh: 标准反威勒尔骰子。

En: Got shot in the hip.
Zh: 臀部中枪。

En: Found the melody.
Zh: 找到旋律。

En: Found the beat.
Zh: 找到节拍。

En: Maybe everything isn't quite as you've been told? Take a moment to analyse.
Zh: 也许事情并不是你听到的那样？花点时间分析一下。

En: Acele was high.
Zh: 阿西尔嗑嗨了。

En: Soona said something's amiss.
Zh: 苏娜说有些地方出错了。

En: Confronted him about Soona's hint.
Zh: 质问他苏娜的提示。

En: Unexplained ether smell.
Zh: 尚未解释的乙醚气味。

En: Unexplained Nosaphed.
Zh: 解释不清的鼻通灵。

En: Unexplained starting fluid.
Zh: 解释不清的启动液。

En: Headphones were sold.
Zh: 耳机被卖掉了。

En: Convinced you're a 'Smart Cop'.
Zh: 确信自己是个*聪明警察*。

En: Narcomania is a plague!
Zh: 毒枭就是个麻烦！

En: That's it for now. [Leave.]
Zh: “先这样吧。”[离开。]

En: As always -- we'll be right here. Waiting patiently for the news. Yeah!
Zh: “像往常一样——我们会待在这里。耐心地等待消息。哦耶！”

En: Be careful in there, officer. And tell us how it goes, yeah? We'll be here.
Zh: “在那里要小心点，警官。记得告诉我们是怎么回事，好吗？我们会待在这里的。”

En: He doesn't look resigned at all. Probably counting on your curiosity to get the best of you sooner or later.
Zh: 他看起来一点也不认命，或许在指望着你迟早有一天会屈从于自己的好奇心。

En: I've been meaning to ask you, what's with the hair?
Zh: “我一直都很想问，你的头发怎么了？”

En: It's to express my individuality.
Zh: “这是为了表达我的个性。”

En: Is that a bald spot? It's hard to tell for sure with the fused-together spikes, but it looks like he's balding.
Zh: 那是斑秃吗？很难从那些尖尖的头发上得到确切的答案，但他看上去像是在掉头发。

En: I don't know, I'm a bit of a dopehead myself...
Zh: “我不知道，我自己也嗑糊涂了……”

En: Are they foreigners? I bet they're foreigners.
Zh: “他们是外国人吗？我打赌他们是外国人。”

En: Oh, if it was just like that, he rushes to reassure you, "it would be fine. But they're also *spooky*."
Zh: “哦，如果是那样的话，”他冲过来向你保证。“那就没事了。但他们还很令人*毛骨悚然*。”

En: Could be, we don't know for sure. But we do know they are *spooky*.
Zh: “有可能，我们也不确定。但我们知道他们很令人*毛骨悚然*。”

En: I won't stand for narcomaniacs of any kind. No *narcomaniacs* on my watch. (Shake your head gravely.)
Zh: “我不会容忍任何形式的麻醉剂狂，在我的管辖范围内不允许出现*麻醉剂狂*。”（严肃地摇头。）

En: Yes, yes. And the worst part is -- they're also *spooky*.
Zh: “是的，是的。最糟糕的是——他们还很令人*毛骨悚然*。”

En: I feel like you may be laying this on a bit thick. What's really so bad about these 'dopeheads' and 'burnouts'?
Zh: “我觉得你可能有点言过其实了，那些*混混*和*瘾君子*有什么好怕的？”

En: They're *spooky*.
Zh: “他们令人*毛骨悚然*。”

En: I don't know, I indulge a little too sometimes...
Zh: “不好说，我有时候也会放纵一下……”

En: Oh, that's all fine, of course he rushes to reassure you. "But these people -- they're also *spooky*."
Zh: “哦，没问题，当然”他冲过来向你保证，“但那些人——他们还令人*毛骨悚然*。”

En: If you do decide to look into the matter of the church, you know where to find us. He flashes you a confident smile.
Zh: 他给了你一个自信的微笑。“如果你决定去调查教堂了，你知道哪里可以找到我们。”

En: Oh! Oh! You should have the key. We had to padlock the door to the church to keep more vagrants from wandering in... Noid, give the officer the key!
Zh: “哦，哦！你应该拿上钥匙。我们不得不把教堂的门用挂锁锁起来，以防更多的流浪汉混进去……诺伊德，把钥匙交给警官！”

En: ...or because I'm balding, yes. I want to fuse the remains of my hair together, before it leaves me.
Zh: “……或者是因为我谢顶了，是的。我想在剩下的头发离开我之前，把它们黏在一起。”

En: Of course it is, otherwise I'd just be another poor guy with no education and no money. General-issue, man. Now I'm all that -- *and* I have radical spikes.
Zh: “当然了，否则我就只是另一个没文化没有钱的穷人了。这很普遍，老兄。现在的我是这样的——*还有*激进的尖刺。”

En: I wanna show my hair: I don't give a fuck how old you think I am. I'm twenty!
Zh: “我想展示我的头发：我他妈才不管你觉得我有多大，我就是二十岁！”

En: How old *are* you, Andre?
Zh: “你*究竟*多大了，安德烈？”

En: Not twenty.
Zh: “不是二十。”

En: Yes it is, man. Absolutely.
Zh: “没错，老兄。绝对的。”

En: He looks at the molten toothbrush. "Maybe it was a bad idea... anyway."
Zh: 他看着脱毛的牙刷。“或许这是个坏主意……无所谓了。”

En: That's pretty down-beat. I think I speak for all of us when I say we expected the law to be more open to the idea of love, unity, and the Perikarnassian posse. Especially since you're the one who helped us get inside the church. He looks around.
Zh: “那可真是太令人沮丧了。当我说我们期望法律能对爱、团结和佩里卡纳西斯老铁团更加开放一些的时候，我以为自己是为大家说的。特别是因为有了你我们才能进入教堂。”他四下看了看。

En: I didn't see the other spooker, just some machines.
Zh: “我没见到另一个幽灵，只有一些机器。”

En: Soona agreed with your nightclub plan, but I promised to help her with her project first. It's going to take some time.
Zh: “苏娜同意了你们的夜店计划，但我答应要先帮她完成她的计划，这需要一些时间。”

En: Actually, he told me he wouldn't mind the nightclub at all.
Zh: “实际上，他告诉我他一点也不介意开夜店的事。”

En: Alright. Do what you gotta do, man. We trust you. He looks at his posse, then back at you, smiling. "If you need any help, let us know."
Zh: “好吧，做你该做的事吧，老兄，我们相信你。”他看了看自己的老铁们，然后笑着看向你。“如果你需要任何帮助，请告诉我们。”

En: Nothing. I just walked around a bit. There was no one inside.
Zh: “没什么，我就是随便走了走，里面一个人都没有。”

En: Hmm... but you *did* see the machines, right? And the spooky water basins set up in a ritualistic circle in the middle of the church?
Zh: “嗯……但你*确实*看到那些机器了，对吗？那在教堂中间摆成了一个仪式圈的那些令人毛骨悚然的水盆呢？”

En: I tell you, they're in there... You just have to give it another go.
Zh: “我跟你说，他们就在那儿……只要你再去一次。”

En: Oh man, the crab-man, you mean! Who is he, what did you think?
Zh: “哦老兄，你是指蟹男吗！他是谁？你有什么想法？”

En: Hmm... But she's in there somewhere, *has* to be. Have you tried investigating the machines? He tilts his head, one hand touching the spikes in his hair. "Anyway, do let us know if you see her..."
Zh: “嗯……但她就在那里的某个地方，*一定*没错。你检查那些机器了吗？”他歪着脑袋，一只手摸着尖尖的头发。“总而言之，如果你见到了她，务必告诉我们……”

En: About the church door...
Zh: “关于教堂的门……”

En: The one with the large head is still looking at you, nodding his head, waiting for your body to start moving... his expectation is fierce...
Zh: 那个大头青年还在盯着你，轻点着头，等待你的身体开始动作……他的期望很强烈……

En: Do it. Do it for art.
Zh: 来吧。为了艺术。

En: Going to the island can wait. This is super important.
Zh: 上岛可以等。这个超级重要。

En: That's only because you're constantly hung over from said 'high-grade narcotics'. Getting clean takes *months*.
Zh: 那只不过是因为你经常沉醉于所谓的‘优质麻醉剂’。戒掉可能要花*几个月*的时间。

En: Just dance for hours, one with the night!
Zh: 只要跳上几个小时，伴着夜色舞蹈！

En: Are pretty much the same, but instead of falling asleep at the 12th hour they go on for *24 hours straight*.
Zh: 差不多是一样的，不过他们不是在第12个小时就睡着了，而是持续*整整24个小时*。

En: Exxcccccitement bubbles up in you -- maybe you should blast some of these drugs right here in this tent?!
Zh: 兴奋在你体内沸腾——也许你应该搞点毒品吸吸，就在这个帐篷里？！

En: Talk? What is there to talk about if you can express yourself with *moves*?
Zh: 说话？如果你能通过*行动*来表达自己，为什么还要说话呢？

En: Yes, my man! He jumps up and down with glee, his moves punctuated by the stroboscopic flash of the club lights.
Zh: “是的，我的兄弟！”他高兴地上蹿下跳，他的动作不时会被频频闪烁的俱乐部灯光打断。

En: Going to the island is your fate, this is an ACT OF FREE WILL. Say yes to CHOICE!
Zh: 上岛是你的命运，这是自由意志产生的行为。对选择说是！

En: He's right. All those sweet questions waiting to be answered... you can choose to let them go, though. There will be plenty of questions to ask at another time. Or even from other people -- from people on the *island*.
Zh: 他说得对。所有那些甜蜜的问题可以等待回答……不过，你可以选择让它们离开。下次还会有很多可以问的问题。或者甚至是其他人——来自*岛上*的其他人。

En: Report back to me when you've done so, alright? We're counting on you!
Zh: “你完事之后回来告诉我们一声，好吗？我们就指望你了！”

En: I don't have time for this. My leg hurts from running around, and I have to get to the island... (Look at the tent flap.)
Zh: “我没这个时间。到处跑的话我会腿疼，而且我必须到岛上去……”（看着帐篷门帘。）

En: Come on, man! Just talk to her -- I'm sure there's a workaround. Pretty please? We can run the club without bothering her.
Zh: “拜托，老兄！只要跟她聊聊——我肯定还有替代方法。求求你了？我们可以在不打扰她的情况下运营俱乐部。”
