En: Remember -- the island isn't going anywhere. It's not like it's going to *sail away* or *disappear*. Take it easy!
Zh: 记住——那座岛哪里也不会去。又不是说它会*扬帆远航*或者*消失*。别紧张。

En: The Return.
Zh: “归来。”

En: The Return? What's that?!
Zh: “归来？那是什么？！”

En: The Return. I like the sound of it.
Zh: “归来，我喜欢这个名字。”

En: Guys, we can't do The Return. There are like tons of places called The Return already. There's this communist joint in Jamrock that my friends like to frequent, and then there's this really famous radio show, you know the one...
Zh: “伙计们，我们不能叫归来。已经不知道有多少地方叫归来这个名字了。加姆洛克有一个康米主义者的娱乐场所，我朋友经常出入那里，而且还有一个特别出名的广播节目，你知道的……”

En: Okay, okay, we're not doing The Return. Andre turns to you and shrugs. "Sorry, cop-man. Got anything else?"
Zh: “好吧，好吧，我们不叫归来。”安德烈转向你，耸了耸肩。“抱歉，警察老兄。还有别的吗？”

En: The lieutenant isn't saying anything -- he's watching you, observing.
Zh: 警督什么也没说——他在看你，观察你。

En: *And* short. *And* memorable!
Zh: “*而且*还短。*而且*让人难忘！”

En: No -- it's THE RETURN and that's it. (Name the club.)
Zh: “不——就叫归来，就这样了。”（为俱乐部命名。）

En: Okay, I think I have a few other ideas... (Choose another name.)
Zh: “好吧，我想我还有别的主意……”（换一个名字。）

En: Of course, he had to smuggle in 'furies' somehow... the lieutenant mumbles to himself.
Zh: “当然，他可能要在*复仇女神*里挣扎一下了……”警督自言自语到。

En: It's settled, then. Everyone, welcome to THE RETURN! He looks around the church as light beams paint the dance floor violet blue.
Zh: “那就定了。大家，欢迎来到归来！”他看了看教堂周围，光线将舞池涂成了蓝紫色。

En: YEAGH, TIME TO MAKE THE FINAL CHOICE!
Zh: “哦耶，是时候做出最终决定了！”

En: YEAGH! A RETURN TO *LE RETOUR*!
Zh: “哦耶！归来，*回到原地*！”

En: I refuse to throw her out, but I can try convincing her. (Proceed with the quest.)
Zh: “我不打算把她赶出去，但是我可以试着说服她。”（继续任务。）

En: The wind brings some snow in from the broken gallery. It wraps you in its embrace, cold and luminous. You should return to the mural soon, to the one that said: Tomorrow is just a whisper away.
Zh: 风夹着雪花，从破败的走廊里吹了进来。它把你拥进自己的怀抱，冰冷而明亮。你应该很快就会回到那幅壁画旁，它的上面写着：明日近在眼前。

En: The one with the large head is still looking at you, nodding his head, waiting for your body to start moving...
Zh: 那个大脑袋还在看你，点着头，等待你的身体舞动起来……

En: You feel like you could go for a little disco when -- or if -- they get this club going. You've got it in you.
Zh: 你觉得自己也可以来点迪斯科，等到——或者说如果——他们能把俱乐部运营起来的话。你有这个本事。

En: The one with the large head is still looking at you, nodding his head, waiting for your body to start moving... his expectation is fierce...
Zh: 那个大脑袋还在看你，点着头，等待你的身体舞动起来……他的期待非常强烈……

En: Yeah let it out! Let the disco happen. Bring the disco into this world!
Zh: 耶，释放吧！迪斯科起来吧。让这个世界见识一下迪斯科！

En: You can't be scared of moving a lil bit, are you?! Get the grind on!
Zh: 稍微动几下吓不到你，对吧？！跳起来吧！

En: Yeah. It's all set up, man! Can you already imagine a thousand people in here?! Ten thousand?! He waves his hands in an unbelievably lame, non-hard-core manner.
Zh: “是啊，一切都安排好了，老兄！你能想象有一千人？！一万人在这里吗？！”他以一种令人难以置信的蹩脚，非常不硬核的方式挥动着双手。

En: I know Noid has his sines, but what's the deal with Egg Head there?
Zh: “我知道诺伊德有一套正弦波的理论，但爱凡客是什么情况？”

En: What's the deal with Egg Head? I want to talk to him.
Zh: “爱凡客是什么情况？我想和他谈谈。”

En: He's a quiet man, mostly communicates through music... and by being a master of ceremonies.
Zh: “他是个安静的人，主要通过音乐……和司仪的身份跟人交流。”

En: How do *you* communicate with him?
Zh: “那*你*是怎么跟他交流的？”

En: Well... he shrugs, "...he's just kinda here, I don't really know how to *communicate* with him."
Zh: “这个嘛……”他耸耸肩，“……他就只是待在这里，我也不太知道如何跟他交流。”

En: Uh, what's a master of ceremonies?
Zh: “呃，司仪是什么？”

En: You know, a host. A declaimer of slogans. He's a performer. Gets the people going.
Zh: “你知道的，一个主持人，喊喊口号什么的。他很有表演天赋。能把大家带动起来。”

En: Have you ever *really* talked to him?
Zh: “你跟他有过*真正的*谈话吗？”

En: Yeah, sometimes... when I, like, stumble and find my way into his centre. You have to hear a lot of 'hard core to the mega' first, though.
Zh: “嗯，有时候会……当我，比如说，我绊了一跤，然后闯进他的地盘的时候。不过，你必须先听很多句*硬核最强*才行。”

En: Oh, that makes sense. Another question then.
Zh: “哦，有道理。还有一个问题。”

En: Oh, the Founding Party. I do know them... but can you refresh my memory?
Zh: “哦，立国党。我确实知道他们……但你能帮我回忆一下吗？”

En: Audio waves thump against your rib cage. The speaker set-up makes everything sound much better... but there's a noticeable lack of something.
Zh: 音波撞击着你的胸腔，扬声器的装配使一切听上去更好了些……但还有一处明显的不足。

En: Audio waves thump against your rib cage. The speaker set-up makes everything sound much better... but the *BEAT* is still so weak.
Zh: 音波撞击着你的胸腔，扬声器的装配使一切听上去更好了些……但*节奏*仍然很弱。

En: Oh, hey, man! It's good to see you. He dances over to you in an inexplicably soft-core manner. It's embarrassing.
Zh: “嘿，老兄！很高兴见到你。”他以一种令人费解的温和方式朝你舞动过来，非常尴尬。

En: What if... you re-routed the terrifying audio assault that the lead programmer's microphone setup picks up through this compressor?
Zh: 如果……你能通过这台压缩机重新改道首席程序员的麦克设置接收到的可怕音频冲击。

En: I'm here to talk about the church again.
Zh: “我又来谈教堂的事了。”

En: About the church... I checked it out.
Zh: “关于那座教堂……我去查看过了。”

En: And...? He tenses up. "What happened?"
Zh: “然后呢……？”他紧张起来。“发生什么事儿？”

En: I saw a shadow-clad being climbing the beams. A man-beast!
Zh: “我看见一道黑影爬到了横梁上，一头人形野兽！”

En: Wow! What happened next?
Zh: “哇哦！接下来呢？”

En: I ran away -- it was terrifying.
Zh: “我就跑了——太吓人了。”

En: Uh... c'mon, that was just the crab-man. You're a brave cop, maybe you should go back and talk to him. Figure out the situation, see what he wants.
Zh: “呃……拜托，那只是蟹男啊。你是个勇敢的警察，或许你应该回去跟他谈谈。弄清楚状况，看看他想要什么。”

En: I... came here to *report* what I saw. The crab-man.
Zh: “我就……过来*报告*我的见闻了，蟹男。”

En: But you didn't talk to him? He hums to himself. "I think you should go back -- figure out the situation, see what he wants."
Zh: “但你没跟他说话？”他哼了一声。“我觉得你应该回去——弄清楚状况，看看他想要什么。”

En: Actually, I want to talk about something else.
Zh: “实际上，我想谈点别的。”

En: This time, I talked to the crab-man.
Zh: “这次，我和蟹男说话了。”

En: I talked to the crab-man.
Zh: “我跟蟹男说话了。”

En: I talked to the shadow-clad being climbing the beams -- a man living on the ceiling!
Zh: “我跟那个爬上横梁的黑影谈了谈——一个住在屋顶上的人！”

En: I talked to Soona again.
Zh: “我又跟苏娜交谈起来。”

En: I was using the mainframe when Soona, the former lead programmer of Fortress Accident, appeared.
Zh: “我正在用主机的时候，要塞事故的前任首席程序员苏娜出现了。”

En: Oh man! Who is he, what did you think?
Zh: “哦老兄！他是谁，你是怎么想的？”

En: Seemed okay, to be honest. Very spiritual.
Zh: “老实话，好像还好。非常有灵性。”

En: He keeps himself physically active, thinks spiritual thoughts and doesn't drink. Who am I to evict such a person?
Zh: “他让自己的身体保持活跃，思考精神上的问题，而且也不喝酒。我又有什么资格驱逐这样一个人呢？”

En: Of course he's a problem -- he's a *crab-man*!
Zh: “他当然是个麻烦——他可是个*蟹男*啊！”

En: As far as I can tell he's not going to leave. He'll climb around up there, and guys, you'll never catch him.
Zh: “据我所知他是不会离开的。他很会爬上爬下，伙计们，你们永远抓不住他的。”

En: You're just going to have to live with the crab-man.
Zh: “我觉得你们只能跟蟹男生活在一起了。”

En: And? What did she say?
Zh: “然后呢？她说了什么？”

En: A programmer? That's odd. What was she like? Did you ask her about the nightclub?
Zh: “一个程序员？真是怪了。她长什么样子？你问她夜店的事了吗？”

En: A shame. He sighs. "What can we do now? Do you see a way out of this jam -- and into a laser-lit future of dance and unity?"
Zh: “真遗憾。”他叹了口气。“我们现在该怎么办呢？你有没有看见一条摆脱困境的道路——通往一个被激光点燃的、舞蹈与团结的未来？”

En: I kicked the programmer out, she won't bother you anymore. Time to dance!
Zh: “我把那个程序员赶出去了，她不会再打扰你们了。是时候跳舞了！”

En: Really? He smiles, happier than he's ever been before. "We are grateful, cop-man. You're a true ally and the augury of a new era of *ANODIC DANCE MUSIC*."
Zh: “真的吗？”他从未如此开心地笑着。“我们都很感激，警官。你是个真正的盟友，并且预示着一个*阳极舞曲*新时代的到来。”

En: Hm. He sounds disappointed. "But do let us know if something changes in the church..."
Zh: “哼。”他听起来有些失望。“但如果教堂有什么变化，请务必让我们知道……”

En: Maybe... uh... I guess we'll figure something out. Okay, but what about the other spooker -- the one in grandma's clothes? Did you see her?
Zh: “也许吧……呃……我想我们总会有办法的。好吧，那另一个幽灵呢——穿老太太衣服的那个？你看见她了吗？”

En: I'm making progress in the church, but it's slow going. There's a hitch in Soona's research.
Zh: “教堂方面，我取得了一些进展，不过很缓慢。苏娜的研究遇到了麻烦。”

En: She's trying to catch an anomaly using a special microphone set up, but she can't hear the audio coming through her headphones.
Zh: “她试图用一种特殊的麦克风格捕捉异常情况，但她听不到耳机里的声音。”

En: What kind of a hitch?
Zh: “什么样的麻烦？”

En: An audio shortfall, you say? The other speedfreak points his thumb toward the speaker in the back. "Guess what, we've got speakers with *massive* low end. There's a good chance it can do it."
Zh: “你是说音频缺失吗？”另一个瘾君子用拇指指着后方的扬声器。“你猜怎么着，我们已经有了*大规模*低阶音箱。它很可能派上用场。”

En: I think we have found a solution.
Zh: “看来我们找到解决方案了。”

En: Good news! I managed to convince Soona. She's okay with you guys moving in, but on one condition -- she needs your speakers for her project.
Zh: “好消息！我设法说服了苏娜，她同意你们搬进去，但有一个条件——她的项目需要借用你们的扬声器。”

En: She made it very clear that she won't leave until her own project is finished.
Zh: “她非常明确地表示，在项目完成之前她是不会离开的。”

En: No, I *won't* evict her. We have to come up with a different solution.
Zh: “不，我*不会*驱逐她的。我们得另外想个办法。”

En: Yeah, I *can't* evict her. I tried, but she quoted the Wayfarer Act, and she's actually correct about it, so...
Zh: “是啊，我*无法*驱逐她。我试过了，但她引用了过路人法案，而且她说的没错，所以……”

En: We can figure something out, right, guys? Work out a deal. We can run the club without bothering her.
Zh: “我们会想出办法的，对吧，伙计们？达成某种协议，我们可以在不打扰她的情况下经营俱乐部。”

En: The lines in the dark, exist, CO-EXIST!
Zh: “黑暗中的线条，存在，共存！”

En: Wonderful! He nods enthusiastically, then leans in and whispers: "If co-existing fails, you can always muscle her out, right? If... it's all okay with you. What do you think?"
Zh: “好极了！”他热情地点点头，然后凑过来小声说：“如果共存没有用，你总能武力请她出去，对吧？如果……你不介意的话。你觉得如何？”

En: Perhaps if you dropped the drug lab, it might make it easier to convince her.
Zh: “如果你放弃毒品实验室计划的话，或许会更容易说服她。”

En: He looks around the tent. "Aww man, but the drug lab was like an *integral* part of getting the club going."
Zh: 他环顾整间帐篷。“哎呀，可这座毒品实验室是维持夜总会运作*不可或缺*的部分。”

En: Excellent! Good luck, my friend. He smiles wide, like a replica of his friend with the large head.
Zh: 他笑得很开心，就像他的大脑袋朋友的翻版。“好极了！祝你好运，我的朋友。”

En: Alright, I'll see how it goes. (Proceed with the quest.)
Zh: “好吧，我看看我能做什么。”（继续任务。）

En: Yes? What's the deal?
Zh: “是吗？怎么回事？”

En: Yeah, he can do his climbing thing in the tower. And the programmer... does she like anodic dance music?
Zh: “是啊，他可以在塔里爬来爬去。而那个程序员……她喜欢阳极舞曲吗？”

En: She *absolutely* does not. Really truly despises it.
Zh: “她*非常*不喜欢，极其不屑。”

En: (Lie.) "She gets down. I mean, who doesn't? Just listen to that crazy pulse!"
Zh: （说谎。）“她很喜欢。我是说，谁不会呢？听听那些疯狂的脉冲！”

En: At least crab-man seems like an *advanced* being. He's hard. He'll understand.
Zh: “至少蟹男感觉是个*先进的*物种。他很坚强。他会理解的。”

En: And you can't just evict her?
Zh: “你就不能把她赶走吗？”

En: If that's okay with her... We only wanna get in the church and spread the joy and ecstasy of music.
Zh: “如果她不介意的话……我们只想进入教堂，散播音乐的欢乐。”

En: She did not like it, and the drug lab didn't really help either.
Zh: “她不喜欢这个，毒品实验室更是起不到什么作用。”

En: What a pity! That's my favourite thing in the world. He drops a hammer back into a toolbox. "And she doesn't like it at all."
Zh: “真是遗憾！这是我在这个世界上最喜欢的东西了！”他把锤子扔回工具箱。“而且她竟然*一点*都不喜欢。”

En: For me it was my favourite part. Chemistry is great. Besides, imagine all the drugs we could do if we had our own drug lab.
Zh: “对我来说这是我最喜欢的部分了。化学是如此伟大，此外想象一下，如果我们有自己的毒品实验室，就能做出所有毒品了。”

En: She said you guys haven't been completely honest with me about your plans.
Zh: “她说你们没有告诉我全部的计划。”

En: C'mon, man. Who will you trust, a spooky programmer or us? We just wanna make the world a better place.
Zh: “拜托，老兄。你会相信谁，一个幽灵程序员还是我们？我们只想把这个世界变成一个更好的地方。”

En: A courageous cop-man such as thyself surely did not flee in fear from a mere mortal!
Zh: 像你这般勇敢的警察当然不会因为恐惧就逃避一个凡人！

En: You won't believe it. I stood my ground, bravely staring him down, but suddenly -- fire started shooting out of his eyes! It was out of this world.
Zh: “你不会相信的！我坚持着自己的立场，勇敢地盯着他，但突然——他的眼睛里喷射出了火焰！真是太了不起了！”

En: You're right, I don't believe the last part... sounds like one of the cheap *literature fantastique* novels. Always full of otherworldly sorceries and hokey stuff.
Zh: “你说得对，我不相信最后的部分……听着就像是廉价的*奇幻文学*小说，总是充斥着超自然的魔法和矫揉造作的东西。”

En: That must have been the crab-man. It's okay if you got spooked and ran away. He hums to himself. "I think you should go talk to him -- figure out the situation, see what he wants."
Zh: “那一定是蟹男，就算你被吓跑了也没什么。”他哼了一声。“我觉得你应该回去——弄清楚状况，看看他想要什么。”

En: Really? Huh... interesting. What's he doing in the church?
Zh: “真的吗？嗯……有意思。他在教堂里做什么？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: No matter, the paranoid young man mumbles gruffly. "Is he going to be a problem?"
Zh: “没关系，”那个偏执的年轻人粗声粗气地嘟囔着。“他会成为我们的麻烦吗？”

En: Yeah, Noid is right... let's get back to the point. What are we going to *do* about him?
Zh: “是啊，诺伊德说得对……我们言归正传吧。你打算拿他*怎么办*？”

En: I guess it's not a *massive* problem, now that I think of it.
Zh: “现在想想，我想这不算什么*大*问题。”

En: Everyone is welcome -- to dance till the morning light! YEAGH!
Zh: “欢迎所有人——不停舞蹈，直到天明！哦耶！”

En: So...? He rubs his chilled hands together.
Zh: “所以呢……？”他搓了搓冰冷的双手。

En: It's impossible to catch someone who climbs walls like a freaking spider-crab.
Zh: “想要抓到能像蜘蛛蟹一样爬墙的人是不可能的。”

En: But he's likely up to no good. Andre shakes his head. "He's a crab-man, man... a menace to society."
Zh: “但他很有可能不安好心。”安德烈摇摇头。“他是蟹男，老兄……对社会也是个威胁。”

En: I doubt that. Besides, I'm pretty sure it's impossible to catch him climbing up there.
Zh: “我表示怀疑。再说了，我敢说想要爬上去抓住他是不可能的。”

En: I dunno, man... doesn't it feel like a major hindrance to you? He rubs his jaw. "A spooky guy climbing around when all the guests are trying to have nice friendly hyper-time?"
Zh: “我不知道，老兄……不觉得对你来说很大的障碍吗？”他揉着下巴。“所有客人想度过一段愉快又融洽的亢奋时光的时候，还有一个幽灵样的家伙爬来爬去？”

En: Right? There's something really odd about him... what's he doing in the church anyway?
Zh: “对吧？他真的很不对劲……他究竟在教堂里干什么呢？”

En: Feel the love! GET DOWN AND FEEL IT! The stuffy tent muffles the last two words. The command fails to impress.
Zh: “感受爱！全身心去感受！”闷热的帐篷把最后两个字闷住了。这条指令没有给人留下深刻的印象。

En: Okay... maybe I'm kinda feeling it.
Zh: “好吧……或许我感觉到了。”

En: You'll get there, believe me... when we've got our gear set up. Things will be flowin' and pumpin'...
Zh: “你会懂的，相信我……当我们准备好装备，一切就会水涨船高了……”

En: The love? No, I'm really not feeling it at all.
Zh: “爱？不，真的一点感觉都没有。”

En: Anyway, now that it's settled... how did she seem? I mean, disposition-wise, about the dance club idea? 'Ja' oder 'Nein'? Rockin' it or droppin' it?
Zh: “无论如何，既然已经解决了……她看上去怎么样？我是说，在性情方面，对舞蹈俱乐部的想法？‘行’还是‘不行’？支持还是反对？”

En: She did not like the anodic dance club idea.
Zh: “她不喜欢阳极舞蹈俱乐部的主意。”

En: Drugs and anodic music, I mean. And she doesn't like either one of them. Some people are just *born* retrograde.
Zh: “我是说，毒品和阳极音乐。而且她两样都不喜欢。有些人*生来*就退化了。”

En: What a pity! Those are my two favourite things in the world. He drops a bundle of screws into his toolbox.
Zh: “真遗憾！在这个世界上，我最喜欢那两样东西了。”他把一包螺钉丢进工具箱。

En: You crazy motherfucker.
Zh: “你真是个疯狂的狗杂种。”

En: These guys will never catch him. You will never catch him. There's nothing to do.
Zh: 这些家伙永远抓不到他。你也抓不到他。无能为力。

En: Don't worry, I don't think he really gives a damn about you or anyone else.
Zh: “别担心，我不觉得他会在乎你或者其他人。”

En: A half-hearted sell of something which does not seem worth buying.
Zh: 不走心的出售看起来并不值得购买。

En: Look at you, honour-man.
Zh: “看看你，光荣的兄弟。”

En: No, Noid. He's right... maybe we've approached it the wrong way after all. I'm sure there's a workaround. We can make a deal not to bother her.
Zh: “不，诺伊德。他是对的……或许我们的方法从一开始就错了。我相信会有办法的，我们可以达成某种协议，不打扰到她。”

En: I *could* go for another try. Bring down the hammer of the law.
Zh: “我*可以*再去试一次，带着法律的铁锤。”

En: Hell yeah. He picks up a small hammer and holds it out, ritualistically.
Zh: “太对了。”他拿起一支小锤子，仪式性地举了起来。

En: No, Noid. No hammer. I'm sure there's a workaround. We can, like, make a deal not to bother her? You know, co-exist.
Zh: “不，诺伊德。不要锤子。我肯定还有替代方案。我们可以，比如说，做笔交易，答应不打扰她？你知道的，和平共存。”

En: No worries. We'll figure it out. The man nods enthusiastically, then leans in and whispers: "If co-existing fails, you can always muscle her out, right? If... it's all okay with you. What do you think?"
Zh: “别担心。我们会解决的。”男人热情地点点头，然后凑过来小声说：“如果共存没有用，你总能用武力请她出去，对吧？如果……你觉得可以的话。你怎么看？”

En: Egg head cannot believe what you just said. It makes him pump the jam a *little* slower for a moment, but then he returns to the full swing of it.
Zh: 爱凡客不敢相信你刚才说的话。这让他的演奏稍微慢了那么*一点*，不过然后他又如火如荼地干了起来。

En: We've got some serious range on this baby. He nods. "It'll blast her shoes off, believe me."
Zh: 他点点头。“我们必须严肃对待她。否则会把她的鞋子吓掉的，相信我。”

En: I'm not really so sure it's about the lack of bass, but I'll see what Soona thinks.
Zh: “我不太确定是不是因为低音不够，但我会去问问苏娜的想法的。”

En: We are grateful, cop-man. You're an augury of a new era of *ANODIC DANCE MUSIC*. The speedfreak smiles, happier than he's ever been before.
Zh: 这个瘾君子从未如此开心地笑着。“我们都很感激，警官。你是*阳极舞曲*新时代的预兆。”

En: You're going to have to share space for a couple of weeks until she gets her research finished.
Zh: “在她完成研究之前，你们得和她共享那个地方几个星期。”

En: That's fine, we can manage. He grins, excited.
Zh: “没关系，我们同意。”他兴奋地笑着。

En: I'm glad I could help.
Zh: “很高兴能帮上忙。”

En: We appreciate it. He grins. "It's going to take us a bit to move our stuff inside. A couple of hours, maybe. Come check back later!" Andre waves to the other speedfreaks. "Let's get moving!"
Zh: “感激不尽。”他咧嘴一笑。“我们得花点时间才能把行李搬进去。可能需要几个小时。等我们搞定再来吧！”安德烈向其他瘾君子挥了挥手。“咱们开始搬吧！”

En: The man smiles mysteriously -- choosing to let the beat speak for itself.
Zh: 男人神秘兮兮地笑了——他选择让节拍代替自己的话语。

En: No words, enjoy the beat. (Nod your head.)
Zh: 不用言语，享受节奏。（随着音乐点头。）

En: Feels good.
Zh: 感觉很好。

En: I mean, anodic music is great and all, but drugs -- they make you feel really good. When did you last feel really, really good?
Zh: “我的意思是，阳极音乐是很棒，但是毒品——它们真的能让你感觉非常好。你上一次有这种非常、非常好的感觉是什么时候？”

En: Hey, who needs drugs when you've got the mysteries of *Al Gul* to discover?
Zh: “嗨，当你要探寻*阿尔古*的秘密的时候，谁还需要毒品呢？”

En: Hey, who needs drugs when you've got a bottle of beer or two?
Zh: “嗨，要是手里有一两瓶啤酒的话，谁还需要毒品呢？”

En: You're right, I only ever feel good when I'm blasting some high-grade narcotics. Then I feel *great*!
Zh: “你说得没错，只要在我嗑优质毒品的时候，我才会感觉到好。然后我会感觉*棒极了*！”

En: Never. I've never felt good in my entire life.
Zh: “从来没有。我这辈子从来没有感觉好过。”

En: I don't have time to feel good, I'm a very busy officer of the law.
Zh: “我没有时间去感觉良好，我是个非常忙碌的执法官。”

En: No, but you see, no one likes *drunk* people, drunk people get into fights and try to hit on everyone. Now, people on *speed*, however...
Zh: “不，但是你看，没人喜欢*喝酒*的人，醉酒的人总会招惹事端，想要攻击所有人。不过现在，大家都会嗑*思必得*……”

En: See?
Zh: “看到了？”

En: But that's exactly what drugs are for. Exactly. Time to expand your mind!
Zh: “但这就是毒品的用处啊。就是这样。是时候扩展一下你的思维了！”

En: Pull out that stick, law-man. You'd absolutely *love* drugs. They'll make you feel transcendent.
Zh: “得了，放下矜持吧，执法官。你绝对会*爱上*毒品的，它们能让你获得至高无上的感觉。”

En: Yeah -- it's a matter of occupied ecclesiastical property. I bet you've noticed the derelict hive of *narcomania* on the coast?
Zh: “是啊——这是侵占教会财产的问题。我敢说你一定注意到海边那片*毒枭*的废弃窝点了吧？”

En: Was there something you wanted? Your friend Acele said there was a problem with the church.
Zh: “那里有你想要的东西吗？你的朋友阿西尔说教堂有点问题。”

En: Was there something you wanted?
Zh: “那里有你想要的东西吗？”

En: Hello, I'm Andre. It's a pleasure to meet you.
Zh: “你好，我是安德烈，很高兴认识你。”

En: That means they haven't set up a single one yet.
Zh: 这意味着他们一个都没有搞起来。

En: He leans back a little, watching you with a steady, serious gaze, letting you imagine just how bad those 'dopeheads' and 'burnouts' really are.
Zh: 他往后靠了靠，用坚定、严肃的眼神看着你，任你想象那些*瘾君子*和*混混*到底有多糟糕。

En: Ecstatic vibrations! Totally transcendent! And I've finished setting up the new compressor too! He looks at the imposing black box in the corner that's churning out the sound.
Zh: “狂喜振动！非常卓越！我也已经安装好了新的压缩机！”他看着角落里那个正在发出声响的夺目的黑匣子。

En: Hi again. Have you changed your mind? You changed your mind, right? The tips of his hair are sharp and white. The bleach has consumed almost all of the toothbrush on the mirror in front of him.
Zh: “又见面了。你改变主意了吗？你一定改变主意了，对吗？”他的发梢又尖又白，漂白剂几乎把他面前镜子上的牙刷溶解光了。

En: Oh, the God of Dance is back! I'm dancing with the God of Dance! He dances over to you, moving in a slightly less lame manner.
Zh: “哦，舞蹈之神回来了！我在与舞蹈之神共舞！”他朝你舞动过来，动作显得不那么笨拙了。

En: This is gonna bring Revachol together.
Zh: “这会让瑞瓦肖重整旗鼓的。”

En: I should start making the fires now.
Zh: “我得开始生火了。”

En: This place is wa-aaaay *under* the underground.
Zh: “这个地方远比秘密组织还要*秘密*。”

En: With a full house, it's gonna be a mad-house. I love it.
Zh: “这里爆满时会变成一间疯人院。我就喜欢这样。”

En: I can already see it, crème de la crème of Revachol...
Zh: “我已经看到了，瑞瓦肖最精华之处……”

En: Everybody letting it *all* hang out -- elite entertainment zone.
Zh: “每个人都在*尽情*玩乐——名流娱乐场所。”

En: All of Revachol is gonna flock in here.
Zh: “整个瑞瓦肖的人都会涌进来。”

En: Mhm?
The speedfreak nods to you.
Zh: 那个瘾君子朝你点点头。

En: A respectful nod. You proved something here.
Zh: 一个充满敬意点头，你证明了自己。

En: Only if he promises to look into the spookers in the church. He looks at you gravely.
Zh: “除非他答应调查教堂里的幽灵。”他严肃地看着你。

En: Officer, I apologize... Noid doesn't always understand the joy of giving for the sake of giving. I think it may have something to do with his childhood.
Zh: “警官，我很抱歉……诺伊德总是不能理解单纯给予的快乐。我想这可能与他的童年有关。”

En: Okay, just give me the key. (Get the task.)
Zh: “好吧，把钥匙给我就行了。”（接受任务。）

En: You can't coerce me into taking this on. I don't need the key. (Not now.)
Zh: “你不能强迫我接收委托，我不需要钥匙。”（现在不行。）

En: Well, if you ever decide to change your mind, then we're here. He taps the wooden crate he's sitting on. "Just ask me about the door again."
Zh: “好吧，如果你决定改变主意了，我们就在这里。”他轻敲着屁股下面的木箱。“再问我一次大门的事情就行了。”

En: Brilliant! He claps his hands. The air in the tent feels lighter. "Noid, the key!"
Zh: “太好了！”他拍了拍手，帐篷里的气氛也轻快起来。“诺伊德，钥匙！”

En: A pincer manoeuvre.
Zh: 钳形攻势。

En: Dammit, take it, don't you just want to see what's *in* there?
Zh: 该死，接受它吧，你不想看看*里面*有什么吗？

En: Catch it.
Zh: 抓住它。

En: THE CAGE OF THE RAGE, ROUGH BLAZE!
Zh: “愤怒的牢笼，狂暴的火焰！”

En: Egg means the situation is monitored. The man nods grimly. "It's 'relatively' safe. No one has died yet."
Zh: “爱凡客的意思是事态已经得到了控制。”男人严肃地点点头。“这样‘相对’安全一些。还没死人呢。”

En: Sure, man. Tell us what you wanna know, let's do it!
Zh: “当然，老兄。你想知道什么，告诉我们，我们开始吧！”

En: Glimpses? Who are these people, secret agents? Stealth operatives?
Zh: 瞥见？这些是什么人，间谍吗？秘密特工？

En: This machinery is of the deeply mystical variety.
Zh: 这种机器是非常神秘的那一种。

En: For this man even regular silence is awful enough. But that was... something greater.
Zh: 对于这个男人来说，就连最普通的沉默都够可怕的了。但那个是……更大的东西。

En: The woman -- red hair maybe? A lorry-woman?
Zh: “那个女人——也许是红头发？一个女货車司机？”

En: Sure, why not! Yeah!
Zh: “当然，为什么不呢！是啊！”

En: But not really, the other one adds. "Brown hair. Old. Heavy, dark sines."
Zh: “但也不全是，”另一个人补充到。“棕色头发。很老。厚重，黑暗的正弦波。”

En: So brown and older? Ruby might have dyed her hair? Though it seems like a stretch at this point.
Zh: 棕色头发，更老一些？露比可能染头发了？不过目前似乎是条线索。

En: Ruby's gone. The woman is someone else.
Zh: 露比走了。这个女人是别人。

En: So which one is it?
Zh: “所以到底是哪一个？”

En: What Noid said!
Zh: “诺伊德说的那个！”

En: You have to see for yourself. These people are not reliable.
Zh: 你必须亲自去看看。这些人不可靠。

En: Odd. Crabs are usually marine creatures. And not known for climbing walls.
Zh: 奇怪。螃蟹一般是水生动物。而且也不是以爬墙出名的。

En: There probably is no crab-man. Don't let them draw you in with this nonsense.
Zh: 可能根本没有什么螃蟹人。别被他的废话带偏了。

En: The implications of this are... too numerous to consider. Proceed with caution. Learn all you can before entering that dark building.
Zh: 这就暗示着……要考虑的实在是太多了。一定要谨慎行事。在进入那栋黑暗的建筑之前，一定要尽全力了解相关信息。

En: Why not! Let's go with that.
Zh: 为什么不呢！我们就这么办吧。

En: Sure. What?
Zh: “当然了。什么？”

En: It's like he's lying to you, my liege. But he's slippery enough that there's nothing for you to grab hold of.
Zh: 他像是在欺骗您，大人。但是他又足够狡猾，你抓不到任何把柄。

En: A shrug is good enough for us! Why say it when you can *shrug* it.
Zh: 耸耸肩对于我们来说就足够了！当你可以*耸肩*的时候，为什么还要说话呢。

En: Keep on it, sire.
Zh: 继续坚持，大人。

En: You're not *completely* satisfied yet.
Zh: 你还没有*完全*满意。

En: He nods enthusiastically. No doubt a *little* relieved.
Zh: 他热情地点点头。无疑*稍微*放松了一些。

En: So he hasn't even been in there lately? Is he afraid?
Zh: 所以他最近没有进去过？他是在害怕吗？

En: Must be. Even Cuno knew that.
Zh: 肯定是。那个就连坤诺都知道。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I changed my mind about the key. I'll look into the spookers for you. Just give it to me. (Get the task.)
Zh: “我改变主意了。我会帮你们调查幽灵。把钥匙给我吧。”（接受任务。）

En: Oh, so you met her? Good, good.
Zh: “哦，那你见过她了？很好，很好。”

En: I did ask Noid to install a measure against more drifters wandering in. It's a temporary fix. Just something to contain the situation.
Zh: “我确实让诺伊德安装一项防护措施，不让更多流浪者进去。这只是暂时的解决办法，为了控制全局。”

En: Yes. I asked Noid to install a measure against more drifters wandering in. It's a temporary fix. Just something to contain the situation.
Zh: “是的，我让诺伊德安装一项防护措施，不让更多流浪者进去。这只是暂时的解决办法，为了控制全局。”

En: I asked Noid to install a measure against more drifters wandering in. A padlock. It's a temporary fix. Just something to contain the situation.
Zh: “我让诺伊德安装了一项防护措施，不让更多流浪者进去。这把挂锁，着只是个临时方案，暂且控制一下局势。我只能匆忙制作。并不是什么杰作……”

En: I had to do it in a hurry. Not my best work... But it should hold for a while.
Zh: “我必须得尽快完成。虽然它不是我最好的作品……但也能撑上一段时间。”

En: He's not as glad as he would like you to think. There's concern in his voice.
Zh: 他并不像他希望你认为的那样高兴，反而声音中充满了关切。

En: *Ka-ching!* The key hits your palm. The speedfreaks nod to you approvingly.
Zh: *哗啦！*钥匙打在了你的手掌上，瘾君子们赞许地朝你点点头。

En: (With your hand on your eye, pick up the keys.) "I'm getting too old for this. Where were we?"
Zh: （把手放在眼睛上，拿起钥匙。）“我做这个有点太老啦，我们说到哪儿了？”

En: You sense the trajectory of the little piece of metal and plastic. The object makes a small ringing sound as it approaches -- just the tiniest chime to your left.
Zh: 你能感觉到小块金属与塑料的轨迹。当物体靠近的时候，它发出了一阵轻微的嗡嗡声——在你左侧，最微弱的鸣响。

En: I need the key.
Zh: “我需要钥匙。”

En: What about the key?
Zh: “钥匙呢？”

En: Right. Other questions. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。其他问题。”（总结。）

En: How long have those people been locked in there?
Zh: “那些人被关在里面多久了？”

En: It feels good to be the smart cop, doesn't it? That's a good cop to be. Has a nice ring to it. "Smart Cop." You wouldn't want to be "Stupid Cop," now would you? But still -- maybe he's just sucking up to you?
Zh: 当个聪明的警察感觉很好，不是吗？你真是个好警察，听起来不错吧。“聪明警察。”你不会想当“愚蠢警察”吧？不过——或许他只是在拍你的马屁呢？

En: Of course. Noid, give the officer the key.
Zh: “当然了。诺伊德，把钥匙给警官。”

En: Not long. Like a week maybe? He shrugs.
Zh: “不长，大概一个星期吧？”他耸了耸肩。

En: Alright. The speedfreak dips into his belt pack and produces a yellow key. He then makes a sudden, cool-infused move, tossing it in your general direction.
Zh: “好吧。”这个瘾君子把手伸进自己的腰包，掏出一把黄色钥匙。紧接着，他做了一个出乎意料的动作，把钥匙抛向了你。

En: Bask in the glory.
Zh: 沐浴在荣耀中。

En: How can you be sure they haven't starved to death?
Zh: “你怎么能确定他们没有饿死呢？”

En: This is cool. Taking initiative like this is proper citizen behaviour and I endorse it.
Zh: “不错，像这样采取主动才是正确的公民行为，我对此表示赞同。”

En: Be the Cool Cop. Catch the key as it flies toward you!
Zh: 做个酷炫警探。当钥匙飞向你的时候，一把抓住它！

En: Dresscode: Extreme performance.
Zh: 着装要求：极致表演。

En: We were talking about... the padlock I think?
Zh: “我们是在说……那把挂锁吧？”

En: Let it fall to the ground, then pick it up. Like a normal adult person.
Zh: 让它落到地上，然后再捡起来，就像一个正常的成年人。

En: I'm super sure they're alive. I mean, c'mon! I'm at least 90%... maybe 85% sure they're still alive.
Zh: “我非常确定他们还活着。我的意思是，拜托！我至少有90%……或者85%的把握他们还活着。”

En: Thank you, we just thought it was the right thing to do.
Zh: “谢谢你，我们只是认为这么做是正确的。”

En: He looks like he's genuinely sorry he didn't throw them better.
Zh: 他看上去是真的很后悔没能扔好。

En: The word *august* used as an adjective -- that's how cool it feels to be old and cool in the eyes of the young. Let's face it, you're near death yet bathed in the golden rays of stately grandeur. Like a late summer's day. Like a Daddy-O...
Zh: *威严*用作形容词——在年轻人的眼睛里，变得又老又酷就是这种感觉。让我们面对现实吧，你虽然濒临死亡，不过还是沐浴在富丽堂皇的金光之中。就像夏末的日子。就像一个情圣……

En: I hope that settles it... or wait...
Zh: “希望这能解决问题……或者再等等……”

En: Somewhere in the ruinous past that led you here there was something called 'exams'. You may have learned the term 'involuntary manslaughter' there.
Zh: 在崩坏的过去中有一种叫做*测试*的东西，让你沦落至此。你可能是在那里学会了*过失杀人*这个词。

En: Noid watches you pick up the key with a smirk.
Zh: 诺伊德看着你傻笑着拿起了钥匙。

En: The key... He cautiously hands you the yellow keyring.
Zh: “钥匙……”他小心翼翼地把黄色钥匙圈递给了你。

En: Ouch! Goddamn asshole... what is WRONG with you?! Can't you see I'm in PAIN now?!
Zh: “哎哟！该死的混蛋……你有病啊？！知不知道你把我打疼了！”

En: My bad.
Zh: “我的错。”

En: He nods attentively, ready to answer the questions of 'one smart cop.'
Zh: 他聚精会神地点了点头，准备回答*一个聪明警察*的问题。

En: Andre, do you know what 'involuntary manslaughter' means?
Zh: “安德烈，你知道*过失杀人*是什么意思吗？”

En: I don't need your pity money. Now where were we? (Don't take the money.)
Zh: “我不需要你的施舍，我们说到哪儿了？”（不要收钱。）

En: Eight-five percent is not good enough when you're dealing with another person's physical well-being.
Zh: “涉及到别人的健康状况时，百分之八十五应视为身体欠佳。”

En: Eighty-five percent is good enough. No one lives forever, baby!
Zh: “百分之八十五已经够好了。人生苦短啊，宝贝儿！”

En: That's the least you could do. (Take the money.)
Zh: “这是你最起码应该做的。”（收下钱。）

En: It's as if time has frozen somehow. You think you can *sense* the key moving in the air. Yeah, this is gonna be *way* cool.
Zh: 仿佛时间凝固了一般，你认为自己能够*感受*到钥匙在空中划过。没错，这肯定会*超级*酷。

En: Don't ruin the cool by overdoing it. Raise your hand in front of your face with minimum effort.
Zh: 不要过分夸张，破坏了这份酷炫。把手伸到你的脸前，尽量不要太刻意。

En: Wow! Looks like there's quite a lot there...
Zh: 哇哦！看来那里有很多……

En: Oh, okay. He nods his bleached, spiked head. "I won't do that anymore."
Zh: “哦，好吧。”他点了点自己漂白了的尖脑袋。“我不会再这么做了。”

En: That's enough glory. At least for now. [Leave.]
Zh: 已经足够荣耀了，至少现在是这样。[离开。]

En: Don't even look at them. You're *that* cool. [Leave.]
Zh: 甚至不要去看他们，你就是*那么*酷。[离开。]

En: Yes I do, I listen to Channel 8 all the time. I know about crime stuff and I assure you, officer, this is not what's happening here. I'm *at least* 80% sure they're alive. I mean, c'mon -- most people aren't ever that alive in their entire lives!
Zh: “我知道，我一直有听8频道。我知道犯罪相关的事情，但我向你保证，警官，情况绝不像那样。我*至少*有80%的把握他们还活着。我的意思是，拜托——大多数人在他们的一生中都没有如此鲜活过！”

En: Exactly! That's *exactly* what we thought!
Zh: “没错！这*正是*我们所想的！”

En: Okay, I'm sorry.
Zh: “好吧，我很抱歉。”

En: Thanks, man. (Play it cool.)
Zh: “谢了，老兄。”（保持冷静。）

En: Of course you are! It's too late to be a Tequila *Sunrise* now... there will be no sunrise, not for you.
Zh: 当然啊！现在当个龙舌兰*日出*也太晚了……不会再有什么日出了，对你来说。

En: Feels nice. It would feel a whole lot less nice had you dropped them, or worse -- got hit in the eye with them. Good thing that didn't happen!
Zh: 感觉不错。如果你把它们掉到地上，那感觉可就不太好，甚至更糟——说不定会被它们打中眼睛。幸好没有发生！

En: Are you sure? It's probably too late to be a Tequila *Sunrise* now.
Zh: 你确定吗？现在做个龙舌兰*日出*可能已经太迟了。

En: Just felt right. Why, do you want to be a Tequila *Sunrise?* It's a little too late for that...
Zh: 只是感觉很对。怎么，你想成为龙舌兰*日出*吗？现在有点为时太晚了……

En: What does that even mean?
Zh: “那到底是什么意思？”

En: Like a Tequila Sunset.
Zh: 就像龙舌兰日落。

En: Yeah, I catch your drift.
Zh: “是啊，我明白你的意思了。”

En: I really am sorry, man -- just take this, okay? He pulls out some black paper from his belt-pack.
Zh: “我真的很抱歉，老兄，拿着它好吗？”他从腰包里掏出几张黑纸。

En: I'm sorry, too. I guess it wasn't very hard core of us to just lock them in like that.
Zh: “我也很抱歉。我想就那么把他们关起来并不符合我们硬核的理念。”

En: I'm sorry, I'm sorry... (Bend your face in *mockery* of his useless contrition.) "You almost *eye-murdered* me -- a cop! That's use of LETHAL FORCE!"
Zh: “抱歉，我很抱歉……”（埋头*嘲笑*他毫无意义的忏悔。）“你差点*眼睛谋杀*了我——一个警察！这是滥用致命暴力！”

En: Man up! It's nothing. Pick the goddamn key up, put it in your pocket, and move on.
Zh: 男人一点！这不算什么。把那该死的钥匙捡起来放进口袋，然后就算了吧。

En: No, none of this Tequila Sunset business. It's gotten me to death's door. There has to be more in this world...
Zh: 不，这跟龙舌兰日落无关。它让我在鬼门关走了一遭。这个世界上肯定还有更多……

En: Damn right I am Tequila Sunset. *Arriva*!
Zh: 我他妈就是龙舌兰日落。*登场*！

En: Where did that come from? Why am I a *Tequila Sunset*?
Zh: 这究竟是什么说法？为什么我是*龙舌兰日落*？

En: Man, I'm super sorry. That was totally my bad, I got overexcited. Threw them too hard. I'm sorry.
Zh: “老兄，真是太抱歉了。这全都怪我，是我激动过头，扔得太用力了。很抱歉。”

En: Sounds like nonsense.
Zh: “听起来像是废话。”

En: I get by. Now where was I with that padlock...
Zh: “我勉强接受吧。挂锁的事我们说到哪儿了……”

En: You can stop buttering me up now, thank you.
Zh: “你可以不用再拍我马屁了，谢谢。”

En: You're right. It *is* nonsense. Total garbage. I knew you'd see through it, you're one smart cop!
Zh: “你说的对，这*就是*废话，毫无意义。我就知道你会明白的，你是个聪明的警察！”

En: I don't know... He pauses to think. "What does anything mean, really?"
Zh: “我不知道……”他停下来思考着。“说起来，一切究竟有什么意义呢？”

En: I knew you'd get it. You're one smart cop.
Zh: “我就知道你会明白的，你是个聪明的警察。”

En: *Oh yeah...* He looks at his friend with an expression of profound understanding.
Zh: “*噢耶……*”他意味深长地望着自己的朋友。

En: Me too... I shouldn't have that kind of power over another person. I'm not the one with the violence monopoly here -- you are. I'm glad that key is out of my hands now.
Zh: “我也是……我不应该对他人使用暴力。但我不是这里的暴力滥用者——你才是。我很高兴现在钥匙不在我手里了。”

En: Truth is, I don't really know. None of us do. I don't even know how many there are... all we've seen are glimpses.
Zh: “老实说，我也不太清楚，没有人知道。我甚至不知道具体有多少……我们只是稍微瞥了几眼。”

En: You haven't even seen them and you want the police involved?
Zh: “你都没见过他们，就想让警察介入了？”

En: Don't let all that 'technology' fool you. He makes little quotation marks with his fingers when he says *technology*. "Where do you think drugs come from?"
Zh: “别被那些*科技*忽悠了。”当他说*科技*这个词的时候，用自己的手指打了一个小小的引号。“你以为毒品是从哪里来的？”

En: Well... he leans in for emphasis, "there's also *the machinery*..."
Zh: “这个嘛……”他倾身强调着，“还有*机器*呢……”

En: Better safe than sorry. Anything more you can tell me?
Zh: “安全总比遗憾要好。你还有什么能告诉我的吗？”

En: One of them has wires running into bowls of water. Wires. Into *water*. Never seen anything like it.
Zh: “其中一个的电线直接伸入了盛水的碗里。电线，在*水里*，从没见过这种事。”

En: Andre, tell him about the feeling!
Zh: “安德烈，告诉他那个感觉！”

En: Oh, and it felt like there was some *thing* in there with us, watching us from the dark...
Zh: “哦，感觉好像有什么*东西*跟我们在一起，从黑暗中看着我们似的……”

En: No! The other one.
Zh: “不是！另一个。”

En: Uhm, which other one? I'm not as in tune with my emotions as you are, Egg.
Zh: “呃，哪一个？我的直觉并不像你那么灵敏，爱凡客。”

En: Felt like silence! Awful silence...
Zh: “感觉很寂静！可怕的寂静……”

En: But you haven't physically *seen* anyone?
Zh: “但你肉眼并没有*看到*任何人？”

En: I don't see a single thing wrong with that argument.
Zh: “我看不出这种说法有什么不对。”

En: Not exactly. We've just seen someone who we think is a woman go in and out of the church. A couple of times. And we felt someone... or some *thing*... eyeing us inside. But... that's kinda it.
Zh: “不完全是。我们最近看到一个好像是女性的人出入了教堂好几次。而且我们还感觉到有人……或者什么*东西*……在里面看着我们。但是……也就这样了。”

En: He means like one of those rich-boy private radio stations, where you can listen to people getting killed on. Then jerk off to it... sick shit.
Zh: “他的意思是，就像那些富家子弟的私人电台一样，你可以听到人们被杀害的声音。然后对着它打飞机……变态玩意。”

En: Alright, let's talk about something else.
Zh: “好吧，我们说点别的吧。”

En: So how can you be sure they're *burnouts* and *dopeheads* if you haven't even seen them?
Zh: “如果你都没见过他们，怎么能确定他们都是*混混*和*瘾君子*呢？”

En: Can you tell me more about this machinery?
Zh: “你能再跟我说说那台机器的情况吗？”

En: We're getting derailed here. In short, you can talk to Noid about the machines. He'll tell you more.
Zh: “我们已经离题了。简而言之，你可以跟诺伊德谈谈机器的事。他能跟你说得更多。”

En: Well, honestly, I can't. But I am.
Zh: “说实话，我不能确信，但我就觉得他们是。”

En: With a small surge of PEA, alertness fills you. As if to say: This is a dark corner, look behind it!
Zh: 随着一小波苯基乙胺冲击，你整个人都警觉起来。就好像在说：这是一个黑暗的角落，快看看它背后！

En: Wow! You can't. But you do. I should add weaponised idiocy to my own repertoire.
Zh: “哇哦！你不可以。但是你还是做了。我应该把愚蠢武装化加入我自己的指令系统中去。

En: This is a below feeble attempt at avoidance. Basically, he is attempting to weaponise idiocy.
Zh: 这是一次无力的逃避尝试。基本上，他在尝试把愚蠢武器化。

En: Jacked up to some *snuff station*?
Zh: “对着某个*鼻烟电台*打飞机？”

En: I'm 70% sure they're substance users. Don't let the 'technology' fool you. He makes little quotation marks with his fingers when he says *technology*.
Zh: 他点头。“我有70%的把握他们是药物滥用者，可别被*科技*忽悠了。”当他说*科技*这个词的时候，用自己的手指打了一个小小的引号。

En: I'll talk to Noid then.
Zh: “那我去跟诺伊德谈谈。”

En: The gesture is super-futuristic. Goes well with his spiked imbecile-hair.
Zh: 这个姿势极其超未来主义，跟他那头尖尖的低能发型很相配。

En: You should talk to Noid about that. I just got a distinct *burnout* and *dopehead* sine from them. Probably jacked up to some snuff station too. Probably, very likely.
Zh: “这你要去跟诺伊德说，我只是从他们身上感到了一种明显的*混混*和*瘾君子*的正弦波。他们可能还会对着某些鼻烟电台打飞机。大概吧，很有可能。”

En: Hey now! He furrows his brow. "I'm 70% sure they're substance abusers."
Zh: “嘿！”他皱起眉头。“我有70%的把握他们是药物滥用者的。”

En: Yes, he leans in for emphasis, "there's also *the machinery*..."
Zh: “是的。”他倾身强调着，“还有*机器*呢……”

En: He appears sincere enough, sire. You could of course be wrong.
Zh: 似乎他的诚意已经足够了，大人。您可能真的错了。

En: When I first scouted the place, back in February, it was abandoned. Empty. Took some time getting the crew together, so about two weeks ago we came here hoping to set the stuff up. Suddenly there are all these strange *machines* lying around in there.
Zh: “今年二月，我第一次发现这个地方的时候，它就已经被遗弃了，空荡荡的。我们花了一点时间把大家集合在一起，所以大约两周前我们来到这里，打算把一切都布置好。突然里面就堆满了那些奇怪的*机器*。”

En: Not that we would know *anything* about that. Noid just likes to relay stuff.
Zh: “这并不意味着我们真的知道那是*怎么回事*。诺伊德只是热衷于转发各种消息。”

En: Yes? What?
Zh: “是的？怎么了？”

En: I see you brought your own water.
Zh: “我看到你自己带水来了。”

En: All right. Enough of this. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。这个就差不多了。”（总结。）

En: I get the Nosaphed -- but what about the engine starting fluid?
Zh: “鼻通灵我明白了——但是引擎启动液是怎么回事？”

En: What's that engine starting fluid for? (Point to the bottle of "Coupris L'Ange.")
Zh: “引擎启动液是干什么用的？”（指向“库普瑞斯昂热”的瓶子。）

En: Yeah, yeah! Good to have, bitch to carry. When I first scouted the place I did some reconnaissance. I'm not sure the church even has running water.
Zh: “是啊！幸好带来了，虽然很重。我第一次发现这个地方的时候就做了勘测，我不能确定教堂究竟有没有自来水。”

En: And it's distilled too.
Zh: “而且还蒸馏过了。”

En: About another thing...
Zh: “还有一件事……”

En: The engine starting fluid? It's for starting engines.
Zh: “引擎启动液？那不是用来发动引擎的吗。”

En: It's for starting engines, man.
Zh: “是用来启动引擎的，老兄。”

En: Nope.
Oh? He doesn't know what to say.
Zh: “哦？”他不知道该说些什么了。

En: Wait. I also smell ether. Why?
Zh: “等等。我还闻到乙醚的味道。为什么？”

En: Is the amiable speedfreak becoming a bit cross with you? Sounds like it.
Zh: 这个和蔼可亲的瘾君子是不是有点生你的气了？听上去有点像。

En: There was another thing...
Zh: “还有一件事……”

En: Oh, okay, I guess that makes sense. Tell me about another thing.
Zh: “好的吧，我想这能说得通。再告诉我一件事吧。”

En: Hate to tell you, but it reeks of sweat in here.
Zh: “我本不想说的，但这里弥漫着一股汗味。”

En: It's the one they sell at the fuel station.
Zh: “就是他们在加油站卖的那种。”

En: It does, doesn't it?
Zh: “没错，不是吗？”

En: Told you we have a smell problem. He picks up a piece of telephone cord and inspects it.
Zh: “跟你说过我们的嗅觉有点小问题。”他拿起一根电话线，仔细检查了起来。

En: It's mixed with a peculiar chemical scent, like laundry detergent...
Zh: “还夹杂着一种特殊的化学味道，比如洗衣粉……”

En: He sniffs the air, then shrugs.
Zh: 他嗅着空气，然后耸了耸肩。

En: Ether? I don't smell ether. Do you, Noid?
Zh: “乙醚？我没闻到啊，你呢，诺伊德？”

En: Good. Now, about another thing...
Zh: “很好。其实还有一件事……”

En: Relax. How's that extra high ether content working out for you? Does it do the trick?
Zh: “放松。超高的乙醚含量对你有什么好处？奏效了吗？”

En:
How's that extra high ether content working out for you? Does it do the trick?	“超高的乙醚含量对你有什么好处？奏效了吗？”
0x0100005A0000992B	Dialogue Text	ICE / ANDRE TENT CONTENT	You	What engines do you start with it?"
Zh: “你用它来启动什么引擎？”

En: Good. Now unto the next thing.
Zh: “很好，接下来还有一件事。”

En: At least not under the influence of stimulants. That doesn't rule out: hallucinogens, benzos, some depressants... how do you know all this?
Zh: 至少没有受到兴奋剂的影响，但并不排除：迷幻剂、苯丙、某些镇静剂……你是怎么知道这些的？

En: I guess it does.
Zh: “我想是的。”

En: What's with the god damn Romangorod Hearings? It's cold, the fluid's for the generator. He points toward the machine.
Zh: “这该死的罗曼哥罗德听证会是怎么回事？天气那么冷，启动液是为了发电机准备的。”他指着机器说。

En: The whatever, I wanted to know about another thing.
Zh: “别管那个了，我还想问点别的。”

En: It doesn't take a forensic scientist to guess it's drug-related. They look and act like the kind of guys who've done their fair share.
Zh: 无需法医就能判断出这一定与毒品有关，他们的外表和行为都像极了参与进来的人。

En: The what now? He leans in to hear you better.
Zh: “又怎么了？”他凑过来以便听得更清楚。

En: What's with all the *Nosaphed*?
Zh: “*鼻通灵*究竟是怎么回事？”

En: Uhm... sure. He picks one up from the corner and hands it to you. "Here you go, officer -- the Nosa! Blast away!"
Zh: “呃……当然。”他从角落里拿起一个递给你。“拿着吧，警官——通灵！引爆吧！”

En: You know, maybe that slight scent of ether I felt before is somehow connected to *ethyl* ether?
Zh: “知道吗，也许我之前闻到的那股轻微的乙醚味在某种程度上就与*二*乙醚有关？”

En: Super. (Nod, then let the silence linger.) "And what is ethyl ether anyway?"
Zh: “好极了。”（点头，然后沉默一会儿。）“而且二乙醚到底是什么东西？”

En: You sound fine to me.
Zh: “在我听来你还不错。”

En: No idea, man.
Zh: “我不知道，老兄。”

En: However, their breathing is regular, their jaws stay put, and their pupils aren't dilated. So -- not under the influence right now.
Zh: 无论如何，他们的呼吸有规律，下巴稳定，瞳孔也没有扩大。所以——现在并没有受到影响。

En: Unlike the girl outside, however, the boys' breathing is regular, their jaws stay put, and their pupils aren't dilated. So they're not under the influence *currently*.
Zh: 然而，不像外面那个女孩，小伙子们的呼吸很规律，下巴稳定，瞳孔也没有扩大。所以他们*现在*并没有受到影响。

En: Did you know ethyl ether is used as a light anaesthetic? And a solvent. And an explosive. And for manufacturing amphetamine.
Zh: 你知道二乙醚被当成轻度麻醉剂用的吗？或者是溶剂、爆炸物，还能用来制造安非他命。

En: I have a major sinus infection. Stuffy nose. We all do. Shit's all blasted up. Winter. Can't even breathe.
Zh: “我有严重的鼻窦感染，鼻塞，我们都有。被那狗屎害惨了。大冬天的，甚至没法呼吸。”

En: Yes! He nods energetically. "That's all Nosaphed's doing. Without the Nosa I'd be drowning in shit right now."
Zh: “是的！”他用力地点点头。“多亏了鼻通灵。如果没有，我已经在那玩意里面窒息了。”

En: Nosaphed *is* the shit!
Zh: “鼻通灵*就是*那玩意！”

En: Can I have some? I have some nose problems too.
Zh: “能给我一些吗？我的鼻子也有点问题。”

En: (Point at the bottles of nasal spray in the corner.) "The 'Nosaphed Ultra.' You have a lot of it lying around."
Zh: （指向角落里的几瓶鼻喷剂。）“‘超级灵鼻通灵’，你们有好多啊。”

En: Oh! The old 'Ultra'... we.... uhm...
He's like an actor looking to the souffleur for his line.
Zh: 他就像个在寻找提词器的演员。

En: The young man with ribcage-shaped suspenders looks at you standing there -- with a goofy grin on your face.
Zh: 你一傻笑的站在那儿，穿着胸腔形状的背带裤的年轻男人看着你。

En: Blam! Straight in the eye. Straight in the old eye-orb. In *the lookin' ball*!
Zh: 砰！直击眼睛，直击眼球，直中*视觉之球*！

En: He is shifting in his spot uncomfortably, still feeling sorry for the mishap.
Zh: 他不安地在原地踱来踱去，仍然在为那件不幸感到难过。

En: A stabbing pain. Tears stream uncontrollably from your right eye.
Zh: 一阵刺痛，眼泪无法控制地从你的右眼中流下。

En: What was that about some *thing* watching you?
Zh: “有*东西*在看着你们又是怎么回事？”

En: Like... you aren't *alone*, you know?
Zh: “比如说……感觉你不是*一个人*，你懂得吧？”

En: It wasn't quite *human* -- if you know what I mean.
Zh: “那不太像是*人类*——如果你明白我的意思的话。”

En: *Not human*? As in a ghost? *Do* you know what he means?
Zh: *不像人类*？幽灵吗？*你*明白他的意思吗？

En: Non-human means a predator.
Zh: 非人类意味着捕食者。

En: It was this dark shape climbing upside down along the ceiling... like some kind of *crab-man*.
Zh: “是那个顺着天花板倒挂着爬行的黑乎乎的东西……就像某种*螃蟹人*一样。”

En: You should ask her about it -- but be nice. Don't tell her you don't believe in the crab.
Zh: “你应该去问问她——但是客气一点。别说你不相信螃蟹。”

En: Yeah, you know. The way it was climbing up and around the ceiling. Like a crab.
Zh: “是啊，你知道的。它在天花板上爬上爬下的。像一只螃蟹。”

En: A crab-man?
Zh: “一个螃蟹人？”

En: Are you *sure* there was a crab-man?
Zh: “你确定有*螃蟹人*的吗？”

En: Ambush behaviour. (Nod.) "Crab-man."
Zh: “伏击行为。”（点点头。）“螃蟹人。”

En: Yeah, totally. I mean... I didn't *personally* see it -- Acele was alone that time, but I believe her. If she comes out running and says there's a crab in there, there's a crab in there.
Zh: “是啊，完全就是那样。我是说……我并没有*亲眼*看到——那时候只有阿西尔一个人，但是我相信她。如果她跑出来说那里有一只螃蟹，那就是有一只螃蟹。”

En: It was stalking Acele. Exhibiting ambush behaviour.
Zh: 另一个人表示赞同。“它在跟踪阿西尔。表现出了伏击行为。

En: CRAB-MAN!
Zh: “蟹男！”

En: Good idea.
Zh: “好主意。”

En: Dance! Aren't you going to *dance*?!
Zh: “跳舞！你不打算*跳舞*吗？”

En: No. Recording. She stares at you, then briskly shakes her head and puts her headphones back on.
Zh: 她盯着你，然后轻快地摇了摇头，戴上耳机。“不了。我在录音。”

En: Andre shrugs.
Zh: 安德烈耸了耸肩。

En: I understand, detective. I hope we can continue on an amiable path, right? No more misunderstandings, no more lies. He smiles cautiously. "Before you go, is there anything else you need?"
Zh: “我明白的，警探。希望我们能继续友好相处下去，好吗？不再有误解和谎言。”他小心翼翼地赔笑着。“在你离开之前，还有什么需要吗？”

En: You're absolutely beat. Muscles relaxed and feet like noodles underneath.
Zh: 你完全脱力了，肌肉松弛，脚软得如同面条。

En: I *WOULD*.
Mmmhm. (Rub your chin.) What's the *gist* of it.
Zh: 嗯。（摸摸你的下巴。）它的*要点*是什么。

En: Mmmhm. And then there's the narcomania.
Zh: 嗯。然后还有麻醉剂狂。

En: Tough stuff. They're not kidding.
Zh: 坚强的东西。他们不是在开玩笑。

En: Nothing...  Let's call this incident crime prevention. But I have my eye on you.
Zh: “没什么……我们姑且把它称为预防犯罪吧，但我会一直盯着你的。”

En: Nothing jumps out to you. Nothing out of the ordinary.
Zh: 你什么也没看出来，眼前没什么不寻常的事。

En: Really? Because I could swear something's not adding up here.
Zh: 真的嘛？因为你能发誓这里有些不太对劲。

En: How about you get out and breathe -- the air in the tent is too thick for you to think clearly.
Zh: 不如你走出去，换口气——帐篷里的空气太浓密，你能办法清醒地思考。

En: Hmm, but Soona said they haven't been honest with me...
Zh: 嗯，但是苏娜说他们没有跟我说实话……

En: Haven't they? Or maybe it's that mean programmer herself who's been lying to you...
Zh: 没有吗？或者是那个程序员在跟你撒谎……

En: This is it. Judgement time!
Zh: 就是它了。判决时间到！

En: This is your chance -- to show up for art.
Zh: 你的机会来了——展示你的艺术。

En: *And* you've been recently shot in the leg.
Zh: *而且*你的大腿最近刚刚中弹。

En: Your aching leg doesn't want you to imagine *anything*, however. It just wants to lie down and rest.
Zh: 然而，你的腿很疼，不想让你想象*任何事情*。它指向躺下来休息。

En: I hope you're not trying to dance with that leg of yours, the lieutenant says, worried. "Healing from a gunshot wound takes time and patience. Be careful."
Zh: “希望你没有打算拖着那条腿跳舞，”警督担忧地说到。“枪伤恢复是需要时间和耐性的。小心点。”

En: WHAT'S HAPPENING?! The lieutenant is forced to yell over the futuristic music blasting from the speakers. "HOW THE HELL ARE YOU DANCING WITH THAT BROKEN LEG OF YOURS?"
Zh: “出什么事了？”警督被迫大喊出声，盖住了扬声器里传出的未来主义音乐。“你都伤了一条腿了，还想要跳舞？”

En: Part of him is amazed you're both able to just *dance* after all these horrifying events in Martinaise.
Zh: 他有一些吃惊，在马丁内斯发生那么多恐怖事件之后，你们俩居然还能*跳舞*。

En: It's his leg. Did you know he was *shot* just a few days ago? He shouldn't be dancing like a madman, he should be in bed, *resting*.
Zh: “他的腿有问题。你知道他前几天*中枪*了吗？他不该像个疯子一样跳舞的，他应该躺在床上，*好好休息*。”

En: The lieutenant sounds genuinely worried about you -- and furious with himself, for not stopping you from running around.
Zh: 听起来警督是真心担心你——而且还为自己感到愤怒，因为没能阻止你到处乱跑。

En: Nah, man, don't worry, he's just dehydrated. Happens all the time in night clubs. He'll recover.
Zh: “没事，老兄，别担心，他只是有些脱水。夜店经常发生这种事。他会恢复过来的。”

En: Your broken hip reminds itself again with a dull, but persistent *ache*.
Zh: 伴随着一阵迟钝，但却执着的*疼痛*，你中枪的臀部又开始秀自己的存在了。

En: It's not merely the dehydration -- it's your broken leg, reminding itself again with a dull but persistent ache.
Zh: 其实并不只是脱水——是你那条受伤的腿，用一种迟钝但又执着的疼痛提醒着自己的存在。

En: It's not merely the dehydration -- it's your broken leg, reminding itself again with a dull but persistent ache.
Zh: 其实并不只是脱水——是你那条受伤的腿，用一种迟钝但又执着的疼痛提醒着自己的存在。

En: It's not the beat, he was *shot*, the lieutenant says, irritated.
Zh: “不是节拍，他*中枪*了，”警督生气地说到。

En: Has anything like this happened to you before? Like... a *seizure*?
Zh: “你之前也发生过这种事吗。比如说……*突然发作*？”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "Alright, party-boy, can you stand?"
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“好吧，派对小子，你能站稳吗？”

En: You close your eyes and vacate your skull, leaving your brain to wonder: where did that little fluttering light go?
Zh: 你闭上眼睛，清空了思绪，任你的大脑恣意想象：那道颤动的光到哪儿去了？

En: You close your eyes and dream of the shapes your body should form to bring this strange music into life. For now, such ferocity of motion is beyond you...
Zh: 你闭上眼睛，想象着你身体应该是何种模样才能给这首奇怪的音乐赋予生命。目前，这种猛烈的运动超出了你的能力范围……

En: Puts a grin on your face just to think about it. Even a failed attempt gets the juices flowing and repairs some of the damage done by battles lost. If you up the dose and TRULY DANCE, who knows what will happen?
Zh: 咧开嘴巴，想象一下。即便是一次失败的尝试也能让血液奔腾，并修复一些战斗伤害。如果你加大剂量，真正跳起舞来，谁知道会发生什么呢？

En: ...but just *imagine* the moves you could pull to this futuristic beat!
Zh: ……那就*想象*一下你能在那未来的节奏中跳出什么样的舞步吧！

En: Somehow you manage to hear him sigh.
Zh: 不知怎地，你听到了他的叹息。

En: Hard-Cop!
Zh: “硬核警探！”

En: SALVATION!
Zh: “拯救！”

En: HYPER! HYPER!
Zh: “超强超强！”

En: TOTAL DARKNESS! You sink down the darkest fathoms of your own personal deep, vertebrae by vertebrae -- THROUGH THE UNFORMED SKULLS OF YOUR SPINE.
Zh: 完全的黑暗！你陷入自己内心最深沉的黑暗之中，脊椎一节一节地陷入——透过你的脊柱尚未成形的头骨。

En: HERE IT WILL BEGIN.
Zh: 将从这里开始。

En: What was that about *unformed skulls*?
Zh: “尚未成形的头骨*是怎么回事？”

En: I wouldn't go *that* far...
Zh: “我不会陷*那么*深的……”

En: Bust a move.
Zh: 纵情舞蹈。

En: Okay, this is too imbecilic. Excuse me.
Zh: “好吧，这实在太蠢了，抱歉。”

En: Don't bust a move.
Zh: 不要纵情舞蹈。

En: Don't worry, man. I'll remember them.
Zh: “别担心，老兄，我会记住它们的。”

En: Hope I remember those moves, man!
Zh: “希望我能记住那些舞步，老兄。”

En: Yes. My mission here is complete. [Leave.]
Zh: “是的。我已经完成了在此的使命。”[离开。]

En: Above my hair, man!
Zh: “我头发上面，老兄！”

En: The what now? The lieutenant suddenly stops. "The sound above your *what*?"
Zh: “什么东西？”警督突然停了下来。“你*什么*上面的声音？”

En: Open your eyes and dance like you've never danced before.
Zh: 睁开眼睛，像你从未舞动过一样跳舞。

En: Right ecstatic!
Zh: “彻底狂欢！”

En: Alright!
Zh: “太好了！”

En: Where did the church go?
Zh: 教堂去哪儿了？

En: WHO FUCKING CARES?!
Zh: “谁他妈在乎？！”

En: The lieutenant crosses his arms with a bemused look.
Zh: 警督困惑地抱起双臂。

En: Good.
Zh: 很好。

En: I'm scared.
Zh: 我很害怕。

En: I hate waking life. I hate this world. I hate and fear everything. All I want is to dance... Please god, just let me dance!
Zh: 我讨厌清醒的生活，我讨厌这个世界，我讨厌并害怕着一切。我只想跳舞……神啊，请让我跳舞吧！

En: I said: Cut a god damn rug, lieutenant! BOOGIE-WOOGIE!
Zh: “我说：快他妈摇摆起来吧，警督！来段布吉伍吉舞！”

En: GOD?
Zh: 神？

En: It doesn't look like he'd *give a shit* either.
Zh: 看起来他也不*在乎*。

En: GOOD. YOU SHOULD BE.
Zh: 很好，你本该如此。

En: The lieutenant begins to heel-kick the church floor with such intensity, it's reasonable to fear he'll kick a hole right through it, causing the floor to crack and the pillars to collapse, bringing the church roof down on all of you.
Zh: 警督开始用脚后跟狠狠踢着教堂的地板，有理由担心他可能会把地板踢出一个洞，导致地板裂开，柱子坍塌，让教堂的屋顶砸向你们所有人。

En: No. NOT GOOD.
Zh: 不，不太好。

En: Sheesh, okay... He backs up with his hands raised in the air, observing the criss-cross of your feet...
Zh: “天哪，好吧……”他后退一步，双手举在空中，观察着你纵横交错的舞步……

En: No really, why can't I hear anything?
Zh: 不，说真的，我怎么什么都听不到？

En: This is a pivotal moment. Try not to piss yourself.
Zh: 这是个关键时刻，小心别尿裤子了。

En: WHERE IS YOUR GOD?
Zh: 你的神明在哪里？

En: Now check *this* shit out!
Zh: “现在来看看*这个*吧！”

En: ORGASMIC!!!
Zh: 极度兴奋！！！

En: Good for you! He looks around the church hall. "Rock on, then..."
Zh: “真不错！”他环顾着教堂大厅。“那就摇滚起来吧……”

En: That all you got? I did 15 years in the Juvenile Crime Unit. I can *do* age-inappropriate.
Zh: “就这些？我在青少年犯罪小组工作了15年，*应付*与年龄不符的事绰绰有余。”

En: Oh, c'mon, Harry...\n
Zh: “哦，拜托，哈里……”

En: Oh, c'mon...
That's it! Don't let the lieutenant leave without getting his hard core on!
Zh: 就是这样！不要让警督在尚未硬核起来之前离开！

En: Respects you.
Zh: 尊敬你。

En: Emergency on the dancefloor!
Zh: 舞池中的突发事件！

En: Outrank him.
Zh: 级别比他高。

En: Dancing -- so infectious.
Zh: 舞蹈——极具传染性。

En: Oh yeah? I did 15 years in the Juvenile Crime Unit. I can *do* age-inappropriate.
Zh: “哦是吗？我在青少年犯罪小组工作了15年，*应付*与年龄不符的事绰绰有余。”

En: Where did the music go?
Zh: 音乐去哪儿了？

En: Oh, don't worry, *the music* is still there. It's *you* who's gone.
Zh: 哦，别担心，*音乐*仍然在播放，消失的其实是*你*。

En: Concentrate on hearing.
Zh: 集中精力倾听。

En: Hard core, *shmard core*... I did 15 years in the Juvenile Crime Unit. I can *do* age-inappropriate.
Zh: “硬核，*超硬核*……我在青少年犯罪小组工作了15年，*应付*与年龄不符的事绰绰有余。”

En: Hey! I called you to dance!
Zh: “嘿！我是叫你来跳舞的！”

En: It comes to you like a blue whale. You sense it stir the tranquillity of the deep -- a single note of bass passing through. Then it's gone. Back to *el silencio*.
Zh: 它像头蓝鲸一样扑向你，你感受到它搅动了深海的平静——一声简单的低音划过，随即消失，归于*静谧*之中。

En: Nothing. Just the immaculate silence of your spinal fluid. Electrified.
Zh: 没什么，唯有你的脊髓保持着完美的沉寂，被震惊了。

En: Say nothing, but push the footwork extra hard.
Zh: 沉默不语，只是加倍努力。

En: Psst. I'm gonna let you in on a little secret. Every vertebrae in your spine is an unformed skull ready to pop up and replace the old one. Like shark teeth. The one you're currently in has a little brain forming in it. Waiting for its turn...
Zh: 喂。让我告诉你一个小秘密。你脊椎上的每一节脊椎都是一个尚未成形的头骨，随时准备跳出来取代旧的那个。就像鲨鱼的牙齿。你现在这个里面还形成了一个小小的大脑。等着轮到它的那天……

En: Oh my god, is that true?
Zh: 我的天哪，真的吗？

En: Yes. It's all true -- in the *SPINAL CORD*.
Zh: 是的。全都是真的——就在*脊髓*之中。

En: Hard core will never die -- but you will!
Zh: “硬核永远不会消亡——但是你会！”

En: ...to rule the world.
Zh: ……统治世界。

En: You talk a big talk, lieutenant! Let's see some moves!
Zh: “尽管吹牛吧，警督！让我看看你的舞步！”

En: We ain't going anywhere before we *tear shit up*!
Zh: “在我们*把一切搞砸*之前，哪都不会去的！”

En: It's not really an emergency, Kim! I lied!
Zh: “并没有什么突发事件，金！我说谎了！”

En: What do you want?
Zh: 你想怎么样？

En: What is happening?
Zh: 发生什么事了？

En: I have become Dance, the destroyer of worlds.
Zh: “我变成了舞蹈，世界的毁灭者。”

En: I'm sorry, Kim, I thought it would be funny to call a Code 31! I didn't even know it was a real code...
Zh: “抱歉金，我以为呼叫代号31会很有趣！我甚至都不知道那是个真正的代号……”

En: Then a breath of cold sea air fills the church as the lieutenant opens the door and steps out.
Zh: 警督打开门走出去时，一阵冰冷的海风涌入了教堂。

En: Yeah... The lieutenant snorts. "Whatever."
Zh: “嗯……”警督嗤之以鼻。“随便吧。”

En: What?
Zh: “怎么？”

En: Yes, officer in need of assistance... ON THE DANCE FLOOR!
Zh: “没错，请求支援……冲向舞池！”

En: I'm under cover!
Zh: “我是卧底！”

En: From what I can see, it's about to BUST A MOVE.
Zh: 在我看来，它准备纵情舞蹈了。

En: As it ought to be. Where is it?
Zh: 即便如此，它在哪？

En: IN THE SPINAL CORD!
Zh: 就在脊髓之中！

En: Now imagine if we could do that, right? But with, like, a *thousand* people?
Zh: “现在想象一下如果我们能做到，好吗？但是跟，大概，*一千*人一起呢？”

En: I'm sorry! I know this looks crazy, but it feels too good to stop!
Zh: “我很抱歉！我知道这看起来很疯狂，但感觉好到停不下来！”

En: The lieutenant taps his foot and looks at his wristwatch. He doesn't seem pleased with this waste of time.
Zh: 警督用脚打着拍子，看了一眼手表，似乎很不喜欢这种浪费时间的行为。

En: I'm ready.
Zh: 我准备好了。

En: Good. Because from what I can see, it's about to BUST A MOVE.
Zh: 很好。因为在我看来，它准备纵情舞蹈了。

En: Nah, it's not *Ubi folk*, it's hard core!
Zh: “得了，才不是*尤比民族*，是硬核！”

En: Who are you?
Zh: 你是谁？

En: I am the spinal cord!
Zh: 我是脊椎！

En: Foolhardy! Do you even *know* what's happening on the surface? Maybe a thousand years have passed? Or maybe you started spazzing out like two seconds ago?
Zh: 有勇无谋！你究竟知不*知道*外界发生了什么？也许都过去一千年了？或者你两秒钟之前才开始昏头的？

En: You might be imagining it but it feels like Egg Head turned the volume down. Such is his respect.
Zh: 这可能是你的想象，但感觉爱凡客似乎调低了音量以示敬意。

En: Help, Kim, I think I'm turning into some kind of hyper-star!
Zh: “救命啊，金，我感觉自己要化身为超级巨星了！”

En: With your eyes still closed, the first thing you feel, all the way back in the pivoting darkness of your own torso, is warmth. You have become a triumph of rhythmo-plastics -- somewhere in a smelly wooden church on the coast of Revachol. The wounds from the war you've waged on your body are healing, twist by twist, turn by turn.
Zh: 你的眼睛始终紧闭，一路回到自己躯干里的无限黑暗之中，你的第一感觉便是温暖。你已然成为了节奏塑胶韵律的胜利者——在瑞瓦肖海滨的一座散发着臭味的木制教堂里。你身上的战争创伤正在愈合，一道接着一道，一个接着一个。

En: End of human development. Mission. Complete.
Zh: “人类发展的终结，任务，完成。”

En: You must have touched upon an entirely new way of moving the human body! Every motion is pumping your brain full of endorphins... Open your eyes to the pioneering glory of HARD STYLE!
Zh: 你一定是触及了某种全新的扭动人体的方法！每一个动作都会让你的大脑充满了内啡肽……为了硬核风格的开拓性荣光张开眼睛吧！

En: As you open your eyes, you should scream "HYPER! HYPER!" (It seems appropriate.)
Zh: 当你睁开眼睛的时候，应该大喊“超强超强！”（感觉很适合。）

En: Okay, you psychopath... He adjusts his spectacles, then pops his collar as high as it will go. "I see what you're doing there. It's jacked-up footwork, plus some... Is that Ubi folk dancing?"
Zh: “好吧，你这个疯子……”他调整了一下眼镜，然后把衣领尽可能得往上拉。“我很清楚你在做什么。加强版的步法，加上一些……那是尤比民族舞蹈吗？”

En: Oh my god... The speedfreaks' would-be leader stands slack-jawed in disbelief at the hard-coreness on display. He whispers to himself: "No way..."
Zh: “我的天……”瘾君子们未来的指挥站在那里，对其展现出来的硬核程度目瞪口呆，难以置信。他低声对自己说：“不可能……”

En: Nothing. It was nothing. (Leave him be.)
Zh: “没什么，这没什么大不了的。”（放任他。）

En: No one cares. The move will bust itself. Resistance only makes the move more futuristic.
Zh: 没人在乎，舞步会自然迸发出来，反抗只会让这一行动更具未来感。

En: I *did* say there was an emergency on the dance floor, did I not? The emergency?! NOT ENOUGH KIM!
Zh: “我*确实*说过舞池里有突发事件，不是吗？突然事件？！含金量不足！”

En: Man... now... now, man... now... The would-be leader stutters with excitement.
Zh: “老兄……现在……现在，老兄……现在……”这位未来的指挥激动得结结巴巴。

En: Guys, I'm out of juice... (Stop.)
Zh: “伙计们，我没劲儿了……”（停下。）

En: I said: Get your goddamn groove on! BOOGIE-WOOGIE!
Zh: “我说：你快他妈的律动起来！布吉伍吉舞！”

En: A Code 31 emergency? Really?
Zh: “31号紧急事件？真的吗？”

En: Get my *what* on? The lieutenant leans closer, unable to make out your words over the pumping beats.
Zh: “让我*什么*起来？”警督凑得更近了，在跃动的节拍中他无法听请你的话。

En: *I'm* not *doing* anything. The music made its mind up a long time ago -- I'm just *implementing*!
Zh: “*我*什么都没*做*，这音乐很久以前就决定了——我只是*奉命行事*！”

En: What's going on here? The lieutenant looks at you and the speedfreaks grinding around in the church, a group of unhinged lunatics. "Delinquents," he grumbles.
Zh: “这是怎么回事？”警督看着你和一群在教堂里吵闹的疯子，一群精神错乱的疯子。“小流氓，”他抱怨着。

En: What? The lieutenant leans closer, unable to make out your words over the pumping beats. "Cut a *what*?"
Zh: “什么？”警督凑得更近了，在跃动的节拍中他无法听请你的话。“摇*什么*？”

En: WHAT'S HAPPENING?! The lieutenant is forced to yell over the futuristic music blasting from the speakers.
Zh: “怎么回事？！”警督被迫在扬声器里播放的未来派音乐中大喊着。

En: You have become a flawless interlocking mechanism. A flesh-and-bone approximation of the throb coming from the speaker set-up of the one called Egg Head's. Entirely, rigidly imbecilic. Without pity or fear.
Zh: 你已经成为了一个完美的连锁装置，这样有血有肉的悸动来自于一个叫做爱凡客的人的扬声器装配。严格意义上完全的愚笨，没有一丝怜悯和恐惧。

En: Free from self-awareness. No deliberation, only -- and I mean *only* -- execution.
Zh: 摆脱自我意识，没有深思熟虑，只有——我是说*只有*——执行。

En: The lieutenant squeezes the bridge of his nose. The lights reflect off his glasses...
Zh: 警督紧紧地捏着鼻梁，灯光反射到他的镜片上……

En: Yes way.
Zh: “可能。”

En: THIS IS REALLY HAPPENING.
Zh: “这真的发生了。”

En: We're done.
Zh: “就到此为止吧。”

En: Yes, if anything, the lieutenant feels tired. Like a man who's heard the same record one time too many.
Zh: 是的，如果有什么的话，那就是警督觉得累了。就像一个人听了太多遍相同的唱片一样。

En: Dance? Yellow? He pauses to look at you with tired eyes. "*Monkey fucker*?"
Zh: “跳舞？黄色？”他停下来用疲惫的眼神看着你。“*傻逼猴子*？”

En: With his reel-to-reel mixer blasting the anthem of a future that will never come, the young man observes your moves for a second...
Zh: 他的混音器播放了一首永远不会到来的未来之歌，这个年轻人飞速看了一眼你的舞步……

En: ... then blasts into the same hideous pattern, yelling: "AAAAAAHHHH!"
Zh: ……紧接着他又以同样可怕的方式咆哮起来：“啊啊啊啊啊！”

En: He's obviously having trouble adjusting to this new reality.
Zh: 显然，他很难适应新的现实。

En: He throws a screwdriver and a bunch of drill chucks into the corner and explodes into dance. What he lacks in sharpness, he more than makes up for in violent enthusiasm.
Zh: 他将一把螺丝刀和一堆钻头扔到角落，而后跳起舞来。他虽然缺乏敏锐的观察力，但他的狂热足以弥补一切了。

En: Then a breath of cold sea air fills the church as the lieutenant opens the door and steps out.
Zh: 警督打开门走出去时，一阵冰冷的海风涌入了教堂。

En: DANCE! (Point at Andre.) "It's *The Law*."
Zh: “跳舞！”（指向安德烈。）“*法律*规定的。”

En: Kim, get in here! (Call for the lieutenant to join you.)
Zh: “金，快过来！”（叫警督加入你。）

En: Dance? he repeats. "*Monkey* fucker?"
Zh: “跳舞？”他重复道。“傻逼*猴子*？”

En: (Stop.) "I don't know what got into me. I'm so sorry. I just lost control for a moment."
Zh: （停下。）“我不知道自己怎么了，真抱歉，我只是一时失控了。”

En: He looks down, then at you.
Zh: 他低下头，然后看着你。

En: Dance, you yellow monkey fucker!
Zh: “跳舞啊，你这黄色傻逼猴子！”

En: Is there anything more I can do? I want to break the limit.
Zh: 我还能做什么吗？我想打破极限。

En: The young man immediately bounces up and down, then assumes the same dance pattern, embellishing it with some sort of waving motion.
Zh: 那个年轻人立刻上蹿下跳起来，然后摆出同样的舞步，用某种挥手的动作加以修饰。

En: You TOTALLY do! Andre waves his hands around his head, perhaps to imply that he's *out of his mind*...
Zh: “完全要啊！”安德烈双手举过头顶挥舞着，或许在暗示自己已经*疯了*……

En: The lieutenant squeezes the bridge of his nose. The lights reflect off his glasses, giving him the appearance of a surgeon in the middle of a tense procedure...
Zh: 警督紧紧地捏着鼻梁，灯光反射到他的镜片上，让他看上去像是个正在紧张手术过程中的外科医生……

En: You feel as if turning on the hyper-drive will be a point of no return. Feels almost melancholy... Are you sure you have the entire posse along for this?
Zh: 你感觉如果开启了超光速推进装置就再也无法回头了，有点让人悲伤……你确定所有人都到齐了吗？

En: The authority of The Law is clearly unquestionable.
Zh: 法律的权威不容置疑。

En: This is clearly a Code 31 emergency.
Zh: 这明显是31号紧急事件。

En: He's having trouble adjusting to the new reality before him.
Zh: 他很难适应眼前的新现实。

En: It's all a *joke* to you isn't it?! Chain of command -- joke! Future of dance -- joke! Well, no more! Cut a rug, lieutenant!
Zh: “这对你来说就是个*笑话*，不是吗？！指挥链——笑话！未来舞蹈——笑话！好吧，再也不会了！翩翩起舞吧，警督！”

En: Together! WHERE ARE YOU?!
Zh: “一起来！你在哪儿？！”

En: No, wait...
Zh: 不，等等……

En: I assure you, I am *not*. Now get your groove on, lieutenant!
Zh: “我向你保证，我*没有*。现在律动起来吧，警督！”

En: No! Stop immediately. Stop dancing and apologize.
Zh: 不！立刻停下！停止跳舞并且道歉。

En: Okay, okay... (Leave him be.)
Zh: “好吧，好吧……”（放任他。）

En: Did that just cheer him up a bit? Could be... It feels like the lieutenant cracked a little smile there.
Zh: 这让他高兴一点了吗？可能吧……警督似乎挤出了一丝微笑。

En: Are you kidding me?!
Zh: “你在开什么玩笑？！”

En: This would be a good time to add some nerve damage to the mix. Pull *THE EXPRESSION* too!
Zh: 这是个增添一些神经损伤的好机会，别忘了*那个表情*！

En: Excuse me, *what*?
Zh: “抱歉，*什么*？”

En: (Say to yourself.) "On second thought, maybe I don't need to involve the lieutenant in this... pioneering dance-off."
Zh: （自言自语。）“再想一想，或许我不需要把警督牵扯进……这场开拓性舞蹈表演中去。”

En: Code 31! Code 31!
I said, get in here and DANCE!
Zh: “我说，快过来跳舞！”

En: Here we go again!!!
Hard core fills the air!
Zh: “空气中充满了硬核！”

En: Lieutenant! I am only going to pull rank on you one time. And that time is NOW!
Zh: “警督！我只会对你摆着一次架子，就是现在！”

En: (Point to the floor.) "ON THE DANCE FLOOR, NOW! Dance or face a disciplinary hearing!"
Zh: （指向地板。）“现在就到舞池里来！要么跳舞，要么去面对纪律听证会吧！”

En: The sound above my hair!
Zh: “我头发上面的声音！”

En: Dance, monkey fucker!!
Zh: “跳舞啊，傻逼猴子！！”

En: How about: "Gather around, kids!"
Zh: 不如：“集合，孩子们！”

En: A number of things don't add up. Let's take a look!
Zh: 很多事情都说不通。我们来看看！

En: Indeed. So one of them came upon the abandoned church. They want to turn it into a club for dance music, but agents of narcomania have overrun it. You shudder to think of all the narco they must have *already* consumed in there! Narco is bad.
Zh: 确实。所以他们中的一个人偶然邂逅了这座被遗弃的教堂。他们想把它改造成一间舞曲俱乐部，但麻醉剂的代理人已经占领了它。你一想到他们已经在里面消耗了那么多毒品就不寒而栗！毒品非常不好。

En: The gist of it is: They want to turn the church into a club, but a suspicious element has overtaken the building. It's very important to understand what the *gist* of things is -- always consult the gist before making up your mind. This is going well.
Zh: 要点是：他们想把教堂变成一间俱乐部，但一些可疑的元素占据了这座建筑。搞清楚事情的*要点*非常重要——做决定前一定要参考要点，现在的进展很顺利。

En: Well, first of all: you're a smart cop. And a smart cop like you would understand if something wasn't quite right, so this should be easy!
Zh: 首先：你是个聪明的警察。如果有什么不对劲，像你这样聪明的警察一定能判断出来的。所以这应该相当容易！

En: Okay, so the speedfreaks want to start a club for dance music. That much checks out. Youths like music. You feel as though you might have liked music more when you were young too... but you digress.
Zh: 好吧，所以瘾君子们想要成立一间舞曲俱乐部。这也说得通，年轻人们都喜欢音乐。你感觉自己年轻时可能也很喜欢音乐……但你跑题了。

En: Plus -- and it has to be considered -- you can't invent the future of dance music in this smelly old tent. Imagine if you had the church! That settles it -- analysis complete, their story checks out.
Zh: 此外——这是必须要考虑的——这个散发着臭味的旧帐篷里是无法创造出未来舞曲的。想象一下，如果你拥有一座教堂！这就说得通了——分析完成，他们的故事验证了。

En: Of course, not everything is 100% clear here but when is it ever? Congratulations, kids. Your story *sort of* checks out.
Zh: 当然了，现在事情并不是100%清晰，但有什么事情是呢？恭喜你，孩子，你的故事*有几分*说得通了。

En: This will be nothing.
Zh: 这将什么都不是。

En: Okay kids, now gather around...
Zh: “好了孩子们，现在集合……”

En: Some time in the past -- I'm not sure when and where, but betrayal was involved -- I fell sick and became the shadow you see now. But before that, I have reason to believe I was a police detective.
Zh: “过去某个时候——我不确定是何时何地，但其中涉及了背叛——我生了场病，变成了你们现在看到的幽灵。但在那之前，我有理由相信自己是一名警探。”

En: Some time in the past -- I'm not sure where and how -- I fell sick and became the shadow you see now. But before that, I have reason to believe I was a police detective.
Zh: “过去某个时候——我不确定具体原因和地点——我生了场病，变成了你们现在看到的幽灵。但在那之前，我有理由相信自己是一名警探。”

En: I got bad news for you, Andre. Things don't add up.
Zh: “我有坏消息要告诉你，安德烈，事情有些不合理。”

En: The one with the large head seems very enthusiastic about whatever you have planned.
Zh: 大脑袋的那个人看起来对你的计划非常热情。

En: The young speedfreak puts down a busted capacitor and looks at you.
Zh: 那个年轻的瘾君子放下坏掉的电容器，看着你。

En: He senses something is wrong.
Zh: 他感觉有些不对劲。

En: Their would-be leader is less amused.
Zh: 他们未来的指挥可就没那么开心了。

En: I was good enough in this job to be awarded the rank of lieutenant-yefreitor. I could have been captain. Imagine that?
Zh: “我在工作中的优秀表现足以被现授予荣誉警督的头衔了。我本来可以做警长的，能想象吗？”

En: But you still are...
Zh: “但你依然是警察……”

En: We need a win. He looks at you with wide eyes. "I *promise* you this will be a win. We won't cook speed in there, we'll do it clean, we'll do it true, we'll do it sober and *real* and beautiful. This will be a victory for the light."
Zh: “我们需要一场胜利。”他睁大眼睛看你。“我向你*保证*这将是一场胜利。我们不会在那儿制毒，会保证清白。我们会真诚地、清醒地、*真正*漂亮地办事。这会是光明正大的胜利。”

En: What happened? Egg Head looks serious, suddenly.
Zh: “出什么事了？”爱凡客突然间看起来很严肃。

En: There *needs* to be a club for anodic music in there. Everyone hates each other. Everyone hates it, it's all just drugs and we're slaves and I *can't...* We are running out of time.
Zh: “这里*需要*一间阳极音乐俱乐部。每个人都相互憎恨，每个人都讨厌这里，到处都是毒品，而我们都是奴隶，*我不能……*我们没时间了。”

En: Disco happened.
Zh: “偶然遇到了迪斯科。”

En: It smelled so impossibly sweet...
Zh: “闻起来甜得令人难以置信……”

En: He looks almost as old as you.
Zh: 他看上去几乎跟你一样大。

En: I did. *I* happened to myself.
Zh: “是我。*我*自己发生了变化。”

En: Life tore me a new asshole.
Zh: “生活让我变成了一个混蛋。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: I was even entrusted with a firearm, believe it or not. (Tap on your holster.) "A real firelance..."
Zh: “信不信由你，甚至还有人向我托付了一把枪。（轻拍你的枪套。）“一件真正的火器……”

En: Silence. He watches your hand anxiously.
Zh: 沉默，他焦虑地看着你的手。

En: What things?
Zh: “什么事情？”

En: He senses something is wrong.
Zh: 他感觉有些不对劲。

En: But the sine was *way* off too. I couldn't feel the love at all...
Zh: “但是正弦波有*很大*的偏差。我根本感受不到爱……”

En: Thank you for your kind words, but everyone in here sees I'm a disgrace to the uniform.
Zh: “谢谢你的美言，但这里的每个人都认为我让自己的这套制服蒙羞了。”

En: I've been trying to say we need the next step in dance music to happen *fast*...
Zh: “我一直在说，我们需要让舞曲的下一步来得更*快*些……”

En: He looks at his friend: "Shut it."
Zh: 他看着他的朋友：“闭嘴。”

En: That's a real downer, he shakes his large head.
Zh: “真扫兴，”他摇晃着那个大脑袋。

En: Now, obviously, that might as well have been a *thousand* years ago. But there's still some detective left in me.
Zh: “现在，很显然，那很可能是*一千年*前的事了。但我还是有一些警探潜能的。”

En: He doesn't know what to say.
Zh: 他不知道该说些什么。

En: Like I told you, spooky assholes moved in while I was getting all this stuff together. A month ago the place was empty and now it's all spooked up.
Zh: “正如我告诉过你的，当我整理装备的时候几个令人毛骨悚然的混蛋搬进来了。一个月以前这地方还是空的，现在开始闹鬼了。”

En: The young speedfreak is silent.
Zh: 那个年轻人的瘾君子沉默不语。

En: What? I *have*... I've said that!
Zh: “什么？我*已经*……我已经说过了！”

En: I've heard enough.
Zh: “我听得够多了。”

En: And why did you need me?
Zh: “那你为什么要扯上我？”

En: Your girl Acele is high as a kite out there. It must have been hard for you boys to keep sober for this meeting.
Zh: “你的女朋友阿西尔已经在外面嗑嗨了，你们这些家伙在这次会面中一定很难保持清醒吧。”

En: You're sober. Was it hard for you to keep sober for this meeting?
Zh: “你很清醒，你在这次会面中保持清醒很困难吗？”

En: We don't need drugs to be hard core!
Zh: “我们不需要毒品来保持硬核！”

En: Shut the fuck up, Egg!
Zh: “你他妈闭嘴，凡客！”

En: Maybe not today, Egg, but you need drugs to get through the days when you're not expecting me.
Zh: “也许不是今天，凡客，但我认为你需要毒品才能熬过那些不能指望我的日子。”

En: I know I wouldn't be as hard core without drugs.
Zh: “我知道没有毒品我是硬核不起来的。”

En: Uhm... He doesn't knows how to comment on what you just said.
Zh: “嗯……”他不知道对你刚才说的话该作何评价。

En: Climb down from the equestrian monument, cop-man. Consciousness is new to the universe. We all have our ways to ease the shock.
Zh: “别摆出一副高高在上的样子，警官。觉悟对大家来说都很新鲜，我们都有缓解冲击的方法。”

En: This isn't the makings of a club, it's a tent full of laboratory equipment. For manufacturing drugs.
Zh: “这不是俱乐部用的材料，这是个装满了实验室设备的帐篷。用来制毒的。”

En: So, does Acele need drugs more than you?
Zh: “那么，阿西尔比你更需要毒品吗？”

En: I've heard enough.
Zh: “我听得够多了。”

En: We know she has a problem, man. We're working on it. She didn't exactly have a smooth adolescence.
Zh: “我们知道她的问题，老兄。我们正在努力呢，她的青春期并不顺利。”

En: Underneath, he is furious she ruined it for them. This means trouble for their female friend.
Zh: 在内心深处，他对她坏了他们的好事感到愤怒，这对他们的女性朋友来说意味着麻烦。

En: Whatever you think is right, sir. But please... we were only trying to make a living.
Zh: “做你认为正确的事，长官。但是拜托……我们只想讨个生活。”

En: We'll do whatever you think is right, sir. But please... we were only trying to make a living.
Zh: “我们会做你认为正确的事情，长官。但是拜托……我们只想讨个生活。”

En: Sir, you promised you'd be lenient...
Zh: “长官，你答应过要宽大处理的……”

En: So... what now?
Zh: “那……现在怎么办？”

En: Sell some fucking speed, okay? Produce and sell speed.
Zh: “卖点毒品，行吗？生产销售点毒品。”

En: I have no idea how you arrived at that conclusion, but it's *wrong!* Look, we even have speakers! He points at the speaker.
Zh: “我不知道你是怎么得出这个结论的，但你*错了*！看呐，我们甚至还有扬声器呢！”他指着扬声器。

En: Bottom line is: I know. (Proceed.)
Zh: “底线是：我知道。”（继续。）

En: What exactly is it you know? He sounds confrontational.
Zh: “你究竟知道什么了？”听上去他是要跟你对着干了。

En: One speaker. They have *one* speaker.
Zh: 一个扬声器，他们有*一个*扬声器。

En: Where is his friend? Did he lose his friend?
Zh: “他的朋友在哪儿？他的朋友不见了吗？”

En: (I like it -- move hand closer to holster.) "So it's *not* punishable by death?"
Zh: （我喜欢——将手移动至枪套处。）“所以*不会*被判处死刑了？”

En: What do you mean *friend*?
Zh: “你说的*朋友*是什么意思？”

En: The other speaker. You have only one.
Zh: “另一个扬声器，你只有一个扬声器。”

En: It's a one-speaker system! It's monodynamic. You wouldn't know the first thing about sound reproduction in anodic music! Other speaker... Pffft!
Zh: “这就是单一扬声器的系统，是单声道动力的！你根本不了解阳极音乐的放声系统！另一个扬声器……嘁！”

En: This may be the brain damage talking, but you've definitely never heard of  *monodynamic* or *one-speaker systems*.
Zh: 这可能是大脑损伤的原因，但你绝对从未听说过*单一扬声器系统*或者*单声道动力*。

En: No, man! They're spooky alright. It's just that they would also probably call the police if we started cooking speed in there.
Zh: “不，老兄！他们真的很让人毛骨悚然。只是如果我们开始在里面制毒，他们可能会报警的。”

En: I know you pawned them. Likely for lab equipment and drug ingredients.
Zh: “我知道你把它们当掉了，说不定是为了换实验室设备和毒品原料。”

En: I'm sorry, but there is no lab equipment. And no drug ingredients.
Zh: “抱歉，但这里没有实验室设备，也没有毒品原料。”

En: What do you know about spinning tape? Nothing!
Zh: “你对播放带了解多少？一无所知！”

En: You have no headphones. Wouldn't Acele need her headphones to *spin tape*?
Zh: “你没有耳机。难道阿西尔不需要戴着耳机*播放磁带*吗？”

En: That Nosaphed is here for its active ingredient.
Zh: “鼻通灵出现在这里是因为它的活性成分。”

En: They're not really *spooky*, are they?
Zh: “他们不是真的让人*毛骨悚然*，是吧？”

En: So, what happened?
Zh: “所以，发生什么事了？”

En: Likely pseudoephedrine, almost exactly the shape of ephedrine. Ephedrine makes you happy and so does pseudoephedrine.
Zh: 很可能是伪麻黄碱，一种与麻黄碱的成分几乎完全一致的东西。麻黄碱能让你快乐，伪麻黄碱也一样。

En: He said it was for his nose. What more do you want?
Zh: “他说过是给鼻子用的。你还想怎么样？”

En: The distilled water -- cornerstone of a clean lab.
Zh: “蒸馏水——这是构建一间纯洁的实验室的基石。”

En: And of all cellular-based life. What's your point, law-bringer?
Zh: “还有所有细胞基生物的基石。你想说什么，法律大使？”

En: Get our fucking foot in the door.
Zh: “他妈的是为了让我们能入门。”

En: The ethyl ether from Coupris L'Ange -- a solvent. Good for getting acting agent out of a solution.
Zh: “你们从库普瑞斯昂热中得到的乙醚——那是一种溶剂。很适合从溶液中提取活性成分。”

En: The ether in the air -- a useful solvent. Good for getting acting agent out of a solution.
Zh: “空气中的乙醚——是一种有用的溶剂，能够完美地从溶液中提取出活性成分。”

En: Just like.... before we do we need to earn a bit of dough, do some real work! Get things going, you know.
Zh: “就是……在那之前，我们得先挣点钱，做些真正的工作！知道吗，让一切运转起来。”

En: Make up your mind, first it's the sweat, then it's the ether... He smiles nervously.
Zh: “想想清楚吧，先是汗水，然后又是乙醚……”他紧张地笑着。

En: No shit... He sounds tired.
Zh: “不用……”他听起来很疲惫。

En: There is no need for me to pile on any more, is there? (Proceed.)
Zh: “我没必要再说下去了，是吗？”（继续。）

En: So lying to a police officer is *not* punishable by death?
Zh: “所以对警察撒谎*不会*被判处死刑吗？”

En: Preposterous?
Zh: “荒谬？”

En: Okay! He sounds very happy.
Zh: “好吧！”他起来很高兴。

En: I meant to say: Not true.
Zh: “我是想说：不是这样的。”

En: To get our foot in the door.
Zh: “是为了让我们能入门。”

En: Or else I'll blow your fucking brains out.
Zh: “否则我就打爆你的脑袋。”

En: Not blowing your brains out.
Zh: “不要把你的脑袋打爆。”

En: So turning a police officer into an unwitting accomplice in a crime is *not* punishable by death?
Zh: “所以，让一名警官成为一个不知情的共犯，难道*不会*被判处死刑吗？”

En: That's... He waves his hands. "C'mon, that's..."
Zh: “那是……”他摇摇手。“拜托，那真是……”

En: Against the law?
Zh: “违法？”

En: I meant to say: Not true.
Zh: “我是想说：不是这样的。”

En: In short: you tried to use a police detective to set up a drug lab.
Zh: “简而言之：你试图利用一名警探，帮你建成一个毒品实验室。”

En: It's not! He looks at your hand near your holster. His eyes fill with fear.
Zh: “不是这样的！”他看着你放在枪套旁边的手，眼睛里充满了恐惧。

En: So *this* is what power feels like. Do you like it?
Zh: *这就是*权力的感觉，你喜欢吗？

En: (I can do without it. Put your hand down.) "So it's *not* punishable by death?"
Zh: （我没有它也行，把手放下。）“所以*不会*被判处死刑了？”

En: No! It's not *true* is what I meant!
Zh: “不！我是想说不是*这样*的！”

En: Punishable by summary execution?
Zh: “该被就地正法？”

En: It's not! His eyes are wide with fear.
Zh: “不是的！”他吓得睁大了眼睛。

En: We just... need to turn it into a speed lab before.
Zh: “我们只是……先需要把它变成一间思必得实验室。”

En: You know, to get our foot in the door.
Zh: “你知道的，让我们能入门。”

En: WAIT!!!
(Lower the gun.) "All right. Speak."
Zh: （放下枪。）“好吧。说吧。”

En: Okay, man, okay... He's shaking. "Don't do anything rash! We didn't mean to fuck you over."
Zh: “好吧老兄，好吧……”他颤抖着。“不要冲动！我们不是故意要骗你的。”

En: The 9mm Villiers feels surprisingly light in your hand. Almost toy-like. You imagine death, sorrow and the dramatic re-imagining of Andre's face burst out of its muzzle, and it feels almost preposterous...
Zh: 9毫米的维利耶在你手中意外的轻巧。几乎就像玩具。你想象着死亡，悲伤，又戏剧性地重新想象了安德烈的脸从枪口中迸发出来，感觉几乎有些可笑……”

En: You can hear the ice cracking underneath you. Outside, the last century waterside development is crumbling in the wind, a grapeshot row of falling houses on the pier. And so is Rue Saint-Gérôme and Main Street. The Old Cinema is sinking underneath Villalobos...
Zh: 你能听到脚下冰面正在开裂。帐篷外面，上世纪残留的水畔建筑在风中摇摇欲坠，码头边散落着一排倒塌的房屋。圣杰罗姆路和主干道旁亦是如此。古老的电影院正逐渐沉入维拉洛博斯地下……

En: We do this law-man style. First you tell me everything. Then I pass judgement.
Zh: “我们公事公办吧，你先把一切都告诉我，我再做出判断。”

En: Or else? There is an audible clank as he lets a wrench drop into his toolbox.
Zh: “否则呢？”当他把扳手放进工具箱时，发出了叮当一声。

En: It was just impossible -- look at us! How are we going to pull a club out of our ass?! We need starter capital, that's all... We were *totally* going to turn the church into the wickedest club for anodic music in, like, ever...
Zh: “只是可能性真的太低了——看看我们吧！我们该如何自己弄出一间俱乐部来？！我们需要启动资金，仅此而已……我们*绝对*打算把教堂变成有史以来最牛逼的阳极音乐俱乐部的……”

En: Okay, man, okay.... You can see his hands shaking a little.
Zh: “好吧老兄，好吧……”你能看出他的手在微微颤抖。

En: This is NOT happening. Officer, please... Without his smile Egg Head looks heartbroken and older than you thought.
Zh: “不能这样。警官，拜托你……”没有了笑容的爱凡客看上去非常伤心，而且比你想象中的更加苍老。

En: Or else I'll call back-up and haul you all off to the pen. For starters.
Zh: “否则我会先呼叫支援，把你们都关起来再说。”

En: Not calling back-up and hauling you all off to the pen -- for starters.
Zh: “首先——我可以呼叫增援，然后把你们关起来。”

En: This is so fucked up... Egg Head mumbles, looking for the *stop* button on the tape player. For a moment you can hear the wind howl outside.
Zh: “这真是糟透了……”爱凡客咕哝着，寻找着录音机上的*停止*键。一时间你能听到外面的风在呼啸。

En: You tell me what's really going on and we'll work from there. I can be lenient.
Zh: “你跟我坦白，那我们一切都好谈，我可以做宽大处理。”

En: What do you mean by *lenient*?
Zh: “你说的*宽大处理*指的是？”

En: We just gotta turn it into a speed lab *first*.
Zh: “只是我们*先得*把这里变成一间毒品实验室。”

En: So what are we going to do with you? (Proceed.)
Zh: “那我们该拿你怎么办呢？”（继续。）

En: Or my cut will be 100%. Haven't you heard -- I'm the dirtiest cop this side of the river.
Zh: “或者给我100%的分成。你没听说吗——我是河这边最腐败的警察。”

En: Haven't you heard? I'm the dirtiest cop this side of the river. I'll make life *hard* for you, using every connection I've got.
Zh: “你没听说吗？我是河这边最腐败的警察。我会利用一切关系让你的生活*饱受折磨*的。”

En: Okay, man, okay. He raises his hands.
Zh: “好吧老兄，好吧。”他举起双手。

En: Things are just so, so hard for an entrepreneur in this city right now! It's not like we lied when we said we wanna turn the church into the wickedest club in East Revachol...
Zh: “这个城市的企业家真是太难了！我们说要把教堂变成东瑞瓦肖最牛逼的俱乐部的时候又不是在撒谎……”

En: Because we do, we totally do!
Zh: “因为我们可以，绝对可以！”

En: In the fucking world, man!
Zh: “全世界范围内，老兄！”

En: What do you mean 'do'?
Zh: “你说的*怎么办*是什么意思？”

En: There's resignation in his voice. He's almost ready to drop the act. It wouldn't take a lot of pushing.
Zh: 他的语气中满是无可奈何，他几乎要放弃了，不必再多费功夫。

En: The optimal way to go about this would be indifference. It begins by you telling him you don't care about any of this.
Zh: 最佳的解决方式就是置之不理。可以从告诉他你根本不在乎这些开始。

En: He thinks for a moment, then opens his mouth, but closes it again. Then finally raises his hands:
Zh: 他想了一会儿，张开嘴却又闭上了，最后他举起了手：

En: Things are just way too hard for an entrepreneur in this city. It's not like we're *not* gonna turn the church into the wickedest club in East Revachol...
Zh: “这个城市的企业家真是太难了，我们又不是*不*打算把教堂变成东瑞瓦肖最牛逼的俱乐部了……”

En: Because we are! We totally are!
Zh: “因为我们是有的，绝对是！”

En: I don't really care, I just wanted to crack the case. Do what you want and I'll do what I want.
Zh: “我真的不在乎，我只想破案而已。去做你想做的吧，我也会做我想做的事的。”

En: *Really...?* He sounds relieved.
Zh: “*真的吗……？*”他听上去松了一口气。

En: Yes, I did. I'm that kind of person. Now tell me... (Move on to other questions.)
Zh: “是的，没错，我就是那种人。现在告诉我……”（继续下一个问题。）

En: Of course not *really*. I'm a cop! Now tell me what's going on, immediately!
Zh: “当然不是*真的*，我是个警察！现在跟我坦白吧，马上！”

En: Really-really. I wanted to ask you about something else.
Zh: “真的，真的。我想问你点别的事。”

En: We'll see. Now speak.
Zh: “看情况。现在说吧。”

En: You wanted to just ask me... he's a bit perplexed now. "About *something else*?"
Zh: “你只想问我几个问题……”他现在有些困惑了。“有关*别的问题*？”

En: (Shrug.) "Or you can tell me the plan for the church really was."
Zh: （耸肩。）“或者你可以告诉我，改建教堂的计划是真的。”

En: What's harder core than a little bit of...
Zh: “还有什么比一点点……更加硬核的呢？”

En: Who's the one in charge? You are!
Zh: 谁是负责人？是你！

En: Soona hinted that there's something else going on. That I'm being *naive*.
Zh: “苏娜暗示说还有别的事。说我太*天真*了。”

En: Hey man, who knows what she's on about. He scoffs. "I get it, she doesn't want us in the church. She's got something against us."
Zh: “嘿，谁知道她在盘算什么呢？”他嗤之以鼻。“我明白，她不想让我们进教堂。她对我们有意见。”

En: Who doesn't like to dance? *She* doesn't like to dance!
Zh: “谁会不喜欢跳舞呢？她*不*喜欢跳舞！”

En: (Time for heavy leverage. Pull out your gun.) "Summary execution."
Zh: （是时候加大筹码了，拔出你的枪。）“就地正法。”

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: Dresscode: Ceramic knight.
Zh: 着装要求：陶瓷骑士。

En: Dresscode: Pseudo-SKULL.
Zh: 着装要求：伪骷髅头。

En: Your hand moves, smooth and lightning-fast, to the holster -- you whip out the gun.
Zh: 你的手动了，平稳又迅速，伸向了枪套——你把枪掏了出来。

En: You grab hold of the grip and pull hard, but it won't budge. The gun is caught on the seam inside your jacket.
Zh: 你抓住枪把用力抽出，它却纹丝不动，手枪卡在你的夹克里了。

En: It still feels as if this didn't *quite* go as it should have, but...
Zh: 还是感觉*并没有*往应该的方向发展，但是……

En: The disdain is palpable. His eyes pierce you like lightning, as he lets go of the wallet.
Zh: 这种鄙夷是显而易见的。当他松开钱包的时候，眼睛如同闪电一样穿透了你。

En: No fucking way.
Zh: “他妈的没门。”

En: Give me your cash. (Ask for a bribe first.)
Zh: “我要现金。”（先索求贿赂。）

En: C'mon man... He looks surprised. "Really?"
Zh: “拜托老兄……”他看上去惊讶极了。“说真的？”

En: Noid, give him the wallet.
Zh: “诺伊德，把钱包给他。”

En: The young man doesn't move. His earrings rattle from the tension. With his jaw clenched, he says:
Zh: 这个年轻人纹丝未动，耳环却因为紧张而嘎吱作响，他咬紧牙关说：

En: Yes. Fucking. Way. The would-be leader points at his friend. His finger shakes in the air.
Zh: “是的，他妈的，有门。”这位未来的指挥指着他的朋友，手指在空中颤抖着。
