En: Without a word the other speedfreak pulls a red wallet out of the toolbox and hands it to you.
Zh: 另一个瘾君子一言不发地从工具箱里掏出一个红色的钱包递给你。

En: Now give me the rest. (Ask for another bribe.)
Zh: “现在把剩下的也给我。”（再次索贿。）

En: Pack up and report to Precinct 41. (Arrest them.)
Zh: “收拾好东西去41分局报到吧。”（逮捕他们。）

En: (Ignore the question.) "First I need to see all of your identification documents."
Zh: （忽略问题。）“首先我要看看你们所有的身份证明文件。”

En: Get lost, I don't want to see you again. (Evict them.)
Zh: “快滚吧，我不想再见到你们。”（驱逐他们。）

En: Pack it up, Egg. The young man closes his toolbox.
Zh: “收拾一下，凡客。”年轻人合上了自己的工具箱。

En: Fuck, man, I thought we were cool... He looks beat.
Zh: “操，我还以为咱们关系不错呢……”他沮丧不已。

En: Fuck, man, you said you'd be lenient... Andre looks beat.
Zh: “妈的，老兄，你说过会宽大处理的……”安德烈看上去很疲惫。

En: The would-be leader looks beat. "Okay," he nods.
Zh: 这位未来的指挥看上去疲惫不已。“好吧，”他点了点头。

En: Fuck, man, I thought we were cool... He looks beat. "What happens at the station?"
Zh: “操，我还以为咱们关系不错呢……”他沮丧不已。“警察局会怎么处理？”

En: Fuck, man, you said you'd be lenient... He looks beat. "What happens at the station?"
Zh: “妈的，老兄，你说过会宽大处理的……”他看上去很疲惫。“到了警局要做什么？”

En: He looks beat. "Okay," he nods. "What happens at the station?"
Zh: 他看上去疲惫不已。“好吧，”他点了点头。“到了警局要做什么？”

En: You should be glad. I'm not even arresting you.
Zh: “你应该高兴才对，我甚至都没有逮捕你们。”

En: I'll go talk to Soona again, maybe there's a way to persuade her.
Zh: “我再去找苏娜谈谈，或许有办法说服她。”

En: Pull harder.
Zh: 更加用力。

En: Nope. It's stuck in there good.
Zh: 不，它彻底卡住了。

En: God dammit, can't get... (Keep tugging.) "...fucking gun... out!"
Zh: “妈的，抽不……”（继续抽。）“……该死的抢……抽不出来！”

En: Come on, don't do this to me, baby, it's my time to be cool...
Zh: “拜托，别这样对我宝贝，现在是我的闪光时刻了……”

En: Whoa-whoa, hold on! There's no need for this! A bead of sweat runs down the side of Andre's head.
Zh: “哇，哇，等等！没必要这样！”一颗汗珠顺着安德烈的脑袋滑落。

En: This is so fucked up...
Zh: “真是糟透了……”

En: I'm plenty calm. I think it's about time you told me everything.
Zh: “我很冷静，我想是时候你把一切都告诉我了。”

En: Remember, I can still punch you in the face.
Zh: “记住，我还是可以揍你一拳的。”

En: Law-man, be real careful and sensible about what you're about to do. Noid is slowly shifting his hand toward a hammer.
Zh: “执法官，慎重理智地考虑一下你接下来的举动。”诺伊德慢慢把手伸向一柄锤子。

En: So there was never going to be a club in there?
Zh: “所以那里永远都不会有俱乐部了？”

En: YEAHH! The young man's smile widens to inhuman proportions. His teeth beam in the floodlight.
Zh: “耶！”那个年轻人的笑容变得异常灿烂，他的牙齿在探照灯下闪闪发亮。

En: He looks almost innocentic, with that Harmon-Wowshi player raised up high. Could this be the certainty a spirit-of-the-world feels?
Zh: 他看上去几乎是天真无暇的，高高举起那台哈蒙沃什播放器。这会是世界精神的必然感受吗？

En: No, Andre, it's *harder* now. This Hard Cop has come to show us how much the fish is and the fish is always *so much* more... We all know there was never going to be a club for anodic music with the speed lab. Now it has a fighting chance!
Zh: “不，安德烈，现在更*硬核*了。这位硬核警探向我们展示了鱼的价值，而鱼总是会*多*很多……我们都知道从来都不会有一间配备毒物实验室的阳极音乐俱乐部，现在有了一个难得的机会。”

En: It's impossible now.
Zh: “现在不可能了。”

En: I knew it... The would-be leader drops his spiked head between his knees...
Zh: “我知道的……”这位未来的指挥把他的尖脑袋垂到两膝之间……

En: Preach!
Zh: “说教！”

En: What's that about a fish?
Zh: “什么鱼不鱼的？”

En: We don't have any more money! You robbed us blind.
Zh: “我们没有更多钱了！你已经把我们搜刮干净了！”

En: The would-be leader pulls out a card from his jacket pocket and hands it you. The name reads: 'Pete Andre.'
Zh: 这位未来的指挥从他的夹克口袋里掏出一张卡片递给你，上面写道：“皮特·安德烈。”

En: The case will be reviewed. You might get prosecuted, or not. We'll see.
Zh: “这宗案件会被复审的，你们可能会被起诉，也可能不会。到时候再看。”

En: His friend hands you his papers. The name reads: 'Karl Holtzmann.'
Zh: 他的朋友把自己的文档递给你，上面写道：“卡尔·霍兹曼。”

En: Close to tears, the one with the large head hands you a card. The name reads: "Germaine van der Wijk."
Zh: 那个大脑袋的人快要哭出来了，他递给你一张卡片，上面写道：“杰曼尼·范·德·维克。”

En: And your name was Germaine?
Zh: “你的名字是杰曼尼？”

En: Fine... I guess we're truly done. He sighs.
Zh: “好吧……我想这是真的结束了。”他叹了口气。

En: Mark their names down on your paperwork and hand each their form.
Zh: 把他们的名字写在你的文书报告中，然后把表格交给他们。

En: Germaine. The one with the large head looks crushed handing you his paper. The name reads: "Germaine van der Wijk."
Zh: “杰曼尼。”那个大脑袋的人把自己的文档递给你的时候看上去快要崩溃了，上面写道：“杰曼尼·范·德·维克。”

En: No, please! The one with the large head presses STOP on the tape player. In the silence, you can hear the wind howl outside.
Zh: “不要，求你了！”那个大脑袋的人按下了录音机的停止键。在一片寂静中，你能够听到外面的风声。

En: There *needs* to be a club for anodic music in there. Everyone hates each other. Everybody hates it here, it's all just drugs and we're slaves and I *can't*... We are running out of time! Without his smile he looks heartbroken. And older than you thought he was.
Zh: “这里*需要*一间阳极音乐俱乐部。每个人都相互憎恨，每个人都讨厌这里，到处都是毒品，而我们都是奴隶，*我不能……*我们没时间了。”没有了笑容的他看上去非常伤心，而且比你想象中的更加苍老。

En: We need a win. I promise this will be a win! We won't cook speed in there, we'll do it clean, we'll do it true. We'll do it sober and *real* and beautiful. This will be a victory for the light.
Zh: “我们需要一场胜利，我向你*保证*这将是一场胜利！我们不会在那儿制毒，会保证清白。我们会真诚地、清醒地、*真实*漂亮地办事。这会是光明正大的胜利。”

En: This will be nothing.
Zh: 这将什么都不是。

En: He looks almost as old as you.
Zh: 他看上去几乎跟你一样大。

En: You can hear the ice cracking underneath you. Outside on the pier the last century development crumbles in the wind, a grapeshot row of falling houses. And so does Rue Saint-Gérôme and Main Street. The Old Cinema is sinking underneath Villalobos...
Zh: 你能听到脚下冰面正在开裂。帐篷外的码头边，上世纪残留的建筑在风中摇摇欲坠，还散落着一排倒塌的房屋。圣杰罗姆路和主干道旁亦是如此。古老的电影院正逐渐沉入维拉洛博斯地下……

En: The dynamic motion of your flailing body is bordering on the extreme. You're going off the charts...
Zh: 你大幅摆动的身体已近乎动态极限，你要打破纪录了……

En: Turn on the hyper-drive!
Zh: 开启超光速推进装置！

En: Speed addict.
Zh: 毒瘾者。

En: Dresscode: Interisolar business class.
Zh: 着装要求：跨岛商务舱。

En: Soona is in.
Zh: 苏娜进来了。

En: Andre's dancing harder.
Zh: 安德烈跳得很用力。

En: Got Kim dancing.
Zh: 喊金跳舞。

En: Kim left. A bummer.
Zh: 金离开了，真是扫兴。

En: Wearing Carabineer jacket.
Zh: 穿着登山夹克。

En: FIND ME ON THE BEACH.
Zh: 在海滩上找我

En: Talked to her on intercom.
Zh: 通过对讲机与她通话。

En: Dresscode: Insane silk dragon.
Zh: 着装要求：丝绸狂龙。

En: On the coast of the Martinaise Inlet, in a small weather-beaten stave church built 380 years ago by settlers from the Occident, most likely to guard against an anomaly at its centre, an officer of the RCM is contorting his body into idiotically rigid shapes, as he invents the future of dance music... It's the *hardest* anyone has ever danced.
Zh: 马丁内斯的海岸上有一座小教堂，自380年前由奥西登移民建成之后便历经了风吹雨打，很可能是为了防范其中心的异常情况。一位RCM的警官正在开创舞曲的未来时，把自己的身体扭曲成各种白痴般的僵硬形状……这是所有人所跳过的最*困难*的舞蹈。

En: Music pounds through the hall of an old stave church on the coast of Martinaise. Several figures are lost in the beats, their bodies pulled this way and that by sound. One of them, an officer of the RCM, suddenly stops. He appears disoriented, then starts shaking -- violently, no longer in rhythm with the music.
Zh: 音乐在马丁内斯海岸上的一座古老的木质教堂大厅里回荡着。有几个人都在节拍中迷失了自我，他们的身体随着声音扭来扭去。其中一位RCM的警官突然停了下来，他看似有些神志不清，然后开始猛烈地——颤抖，跳脱出了音乐的节奏。

En: Acele, aren't you going to dance?
Zh: 你还没来得及重复你的问题，她就插嘴了。“阿西尔，你不去跳舞吗？”

En: (Turn to Acele.) "What are you doing?!"
Zh: （转向阿西尔。）“你在干什么？！”

En: This is where the beat plunged. It will resurface soon. Brace for glory.
Zh: 这里就是节拍开始跳水的地方，它很快就能重新浮出水面。迎接荣耀。

En: The lead programmer throws the other young woman a knowing glance before turning her attention back to her own work.
Zh: 首席程序员朝另一位年轻女士投去会意的一瞥，然后将自己的注意力转回到工作上。

En: Keep the lab, but I want a cut of your operation. (Proceed with both, become a partner.)
Zh: “留下实验室吧，但我要从中分一杯羹。”（两者兼备，成为合作伙伴。）

En: Okay. That's cool with us, right? He looks around.
Zh: “好吧，我们可以接受，对吧？”他四下看了看。

En: Yeah! His friend nods his huge peroxide blond head.
Zh: “是啊！”他的朋友点了点自己巨大的漂白了的脑袋。

En: 50% it is. (Agree with the deal.)
Zh: “那就50%吧。”（同意这笔交易。）

En: C'mon man, you're killing us... Andre runs his hands through the spikes of his hair.
Zh: “拜托老兄，你这是要我们的命啊……”安德烈用手捋过自己的头发尖。

En: It's 75% or I'm taking you to the holding pen right now.
Zh: “要么我拿75%，要么我现在就带你们去拘留室。”

En: Ten percent.

Don't be offensive, Noid. We could use a partner on the other side. It's safer with someone watching over the operation, keeping the... khm... he clears his throat, "other cops out of our soup. How does 35% sound?"
Zh: “别这么莽撞，诺伊德，我们需要一个来自对面的伙伴。有人全程监督还能更保险一点……咳……”他清了清嗓子。“其他警察就不要考虑了，35%如何？”

En: You could probably justify taking 70%. The risks are greater for you as a police officer.
Zh: 你拿走70%大概更合理一些。毕竟作为一名警察，你面临的风险更大。

En: You have more control than they do, the agreement should reflect that. You should push for 60%.
Zh: 你比他们有更多的控制权，协议中应该反映出这一点，你应该争取60%。

En: Deal.
Zh: “成交。”

En: 70%. Nothing below that.
Zh: “70%，不能再低了。”

En: Why should I profit so handsomely? I don't own the means of production, I don't wear the top hat. 25% is enough, towarzysz.
Zh: “我为什么要赚那么多钱呢？我不掌握生产方法，也不在幕后操作。25%已经足够了，朋友们。”

En: You deliver us the church and keep the law away from it and we'll give you your share, alright? Be sure to return every couple of months for your pay.
Zh: “你把教堂交我们，让执法人员不要插手，我们就可以把属于你的那份给你，好吗？记得每两个月回来一次领钱。”

En: Okay? He looks around. There are no objections. "I want you to know there's no bad blood here."
Zh: “可以吗？”他环顾四周，没有反对意见。“我想让你知道这里没有嫌隙。”

En: 70%. I'm a police officer. I could be risking thirty years of service for this. Actually -- I should take 75%.
Zh: “70%。我是个警察，我可能要为此赌上三十年的服役经验。实际上——我应该拿75%。”

En: Fuck this. Take us in or kill us. We're not bowing any longer.
Zh: “去你的吧。要么把我们抓起来，要么杀了我们，我们不会再妥协了。”

En: 60%. I hold all the cards here, kids. I can lock you up and say this was all an act.
Zh: “60%。孩子们，所有的牌都在我手上。我可以把你们锁起来，然后说这只是公事公办。”

En: C'mon man, you're killing us... Andre runs his hands through the spikes of his hair.
Zh: “拜托老兄，你这是要我们的命啊……”安德烈用手捋过自己的头发尖。

En: C'mon man, you're killing us... Andre runs his hands through the spikes of his hair.
Zh: “拜托老兄，你这是要我们的命啊……”安德烈用手捋过自己的头发尖。

En: 55%. I want the control stock in this or I simply won't be motivated. (Shrug.)
Zh: “55%。我需要掌握控股权，否则我就是没有动力。”（耸肩。）

En: He looks to his friend, then at you. "Sorry," he nods. "I'm with Noid on this. Take us in or do it for 50%."
Zh: 他先看了看他的朋友，然后又看看你。“抱歉，”他点点头。“我同意诺伊德的观点，要么把我们抓起来，要么拿走50%。”

En: No. 50%. We do this even or not at all.
Zh: “不行，50%。我们要么平分，要么干脆不要这么做了。”

En: That's fair of you. The young man nods, approvingly. "Towarzysz hard core."
Zh: “你真是太好了。”年轻人赞许地点了点头。“硬核朋友。”

En: Ask for a cut. It's the right time to become business partners.
Zh: 要求分一杯羹，现在是成为商业伙伴的好机会。

En: Hang on, I thought I'd get some money quickly?
Zh: “等等，我以为很快就能拿到钱了？”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Come now, it'll take weeks to get everything set up, start making the produce, and deliver it around to the needy. This is a lengthy process.
Zh: “拜托，把一切都准备好，开始制造，然后分发给有需要的人，得花好几个星期的时间。这是个漫长的过程。”

En: It's okay, Egg. It's all okay... He's still shaking from the tension.
Zh: “没事的，爱凡客。不是什么大事……”他仍然紧张得浑身发抖。

En: My partner is outside and he's quite the gunslinger. Trust me, I've seen him in action.
Zh: “我的搭档就在外面，而他是个相当厉害的神枪手。相信我，我见过他行动的样子。”

En: My partner is still outside and he's a no-nonsense guy.
Zh: “我的搭档就在外面，而他是个相当严肃的家伙。”

En: There's always your backup outside... stay firm. You've got the upper hand.
Zh: 你还有你的支援……站在外面。你占了上风。

En: If they try something, just knock their teeth out!
Zh: 如果他们有什么小动作，就把他们的牙齿敲掉！

En: The lieutenant stands behind a white table, cleaning his gun. It would truly come in handy in a tense situation like this one.
Zh: 警督站在一张白色的桌子后面擦枪，在这种紧张的情况下绝对能派上用场。

En: You're only making things worse. The jacket is going to rip if you continue.
Zh: 这只会让事情变得更糟，再这样下去，你的夹克就要裂开了。

En: Better keep those hands where they are, Noid.
Zh: “你的手最好老实点，诺伊德。”

En: (Show both hands to the guys.) "Alright, I'm not going to do anything harsh... yet."
Zh: （展示双手。）“行了，我暂时……不会做什么残酷的事情。”

En: Noid! Andre hisses. "No need to escalate things. It's okay, let's all calm down."
Zh: “诺伊德！”安德烈嘘了一声。“没必要让事态升级，没事的，大家都冷静点。”

En: It's all cool... we don't need to escalate things. Let's all calm down.
Zh: “没事的……我们没必要让事态升级，大家都冷静点。”

En: Sorry, boiadeiro, the moment has passed. You just won't look cool anymore.
Zh: 抱歉啦，牛仔，这个时刻已经过去了，你看上去一点都不酷了。

En: (Let go of gun.) "You better start talking right now."
Zh: （松开手枪。）“你最好现在就开口。”

En: Got it! There's no need to get anyone else involved. He wipes the sweat off his head. "Okay, man, listen..."
Zh: “明白了！没必要把其他人也牵扯进来。”他抹去了头上的汗水。“好吧老兄，听着……”

En: I get it! Okay, man... He's still shaking a bit...
Zh: “我明白了！好吧，老兄……”他仍然有些颤抖……

En: (Let go of gun.) "Man, how did I mess this up so bad."
Zh: （松开手枪。）“天，我怎么会搞得这么糟糕呢。”

En: You made your point, alright? We don't need to escalate things... let's just calm down.
Zh: “你已经说的很明白了，好吗？我们没必要让事态升级……大家都冷静点。”

En: All in all, you really can't tell what that mean programmer grandma was talking about.
Zh: 总而言之，你真的不明白那个程序员奶奶在说什么。

En: Soona said you've been lying about your plans. Your answer was very unconvincing.
Zh: “苏娜说你们一直在计划的事上说谎。你的回答非常没有说服力。”

En: He furrows his brow. "Fuck, man, it's difficult to get along with some people, but we're trying to make an effort. We are on a *mission* here."
Zh: 他皱起眉头。“他妈的，老兄，有些人相处起来很困难，但我们正在努力。我们在这里还有自己的*使命*。”

En: (Point gun at Andre.) "Yes? I'm listening."
Zh: （用枪指着安德烈。）“是吗？我在听。”

En: (Admire the gun in your hand.) "Tell me, why should I do that?"
Zh: （欣赏你手中的枪。）“告诉我，我为什么要那么做呢？”

En: Time for a threat. He can't handle the pressure.
Zh: 威胁时间到了。他无法承受压力。

En: As if you've done it before.
Zh: 就好像你以前也这么做过一样。

En: You can't just... you can't... He's mumbling half-incoherently. "We haven't..." Suddenly, he gets interrupted by a voice:
Zh: “你不能就这么……你不能……”他有些语无伦次地嘟囔着。“我们还没有……”突然，他被一个声音打断了：

En: Noid surveys the situation with his face pale and blank. His eyebrow is twitching.
Zh: 诺伊德面色苍白，茫然地审视着当下的事态。他的眉毛颤动着。

En: (Holster the gun.) "There we go. A few threats will get things moving quickly. Time to talk."
Zh: （收回枪。）“看到了吧，一点威胁能让事情进展迅速，是时候开口了。”

En: From here on it'll be straight all the way!
Zh: “从现在开始，不得再有隐瞒！”

En: I have to look into the spooker situation before I can decide what to do with you. (Decide later.)
Zh: “我得先去调查一下幽灵的情况，然后才能决定如何处理你们。”（稍后决定。）

En: And then crabman and the programmer lady moved in while you were gathering your shit.
Zh: “然后你们在筹备那些屁事的时候，蟹男和女程序员搬进来了。”

En: Exactly! It was going fine -- a month ago the place was empty, then suddenly it's all spooked up.
Zh: “没错！原本一切都很顺利的——一个月前那地方还是空的，突然间就开始闹鬼了。”

En: I've heard enough.
Zh: “我听得够多了。”

En: I have to check out the church before I can decide what to do with you. (Decide later.)
Zh: “我得先去教堂看看，然后才能决定如何处理你们。”（稍后决定。）

En: We can continue on an amiable path, right? No more misunderstandings, no more lies. He nods, smiling cautiously. "Before you go, is there anything else you need?"
Zh: “我们还可以继续友好交流的，对吗？不再有误解，不再有谎言。”他点点头，谨慎地微笑着。“在你离开之前，还有什么需要吗？”

En: It wouldn't change a thing. It's too late. For everything. You're arrested. (Arrest them.)
Zh: “不会有任何改变的，一切都已经太迟了。你们被捕了。”（逮捕他们。）

En: Wait, does anyone feel that?
Zh: “等等，有人感觉到了吗？”

En: No... He looks like he's at least 45 years old. "What does that even mean?"
Zh: “不……”他看上去至少有45岁了。“那究竟是什么意思？”

En: The law is the law. If you avoid the Call we will consider you fugitives and hunt you down. (Arrest them.)
Zh: “法律就是法律。如果你们对传唤不予理会，我们就会将你们视为逃犯，实施追捕。”（逮捕他们。）

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Man, that speech was beautiful. I think I've changed my mind. (Don't arrest them.)
Zh: “老兄，你的发言真是太美好了。我想我改变主意了。”（不逮捕他们。）

En: Thank you...
Zh: “谢谢你……”

En: The young man gulps like he's trying not to cry.
Zh: 年轻人哽咽着，仿佛在强忍着哭泣。

En: Pack it up, Egg. He closes his toolbox.
Zh: “收拾一下，凡客。”他合上了自己的工具箱。

En: I guess that's that.
Zh: “我想就是这样了。”

En: But first I need to see all of your identification documents.
Zh: “但首先我得检查一下你们所有人的身份证明文件。”

En: There *needs* to be a club for anodic music in there. Everyone hates each other. Everybody hates it here, it's all just drugs and we're slaves and I *can't*... We are running out of time! Without his smile Egg Head looks heartbroken. And older than you thought he was.
Zh: “这里*需要*一间阳极音乐俱乐部。每个人都相互憎恨，每个人都讨厌这里，到处都是毒品，而我们都是奴隶，*我不能……*我们没时间了。”没有了笑容的爱凡客看上去非常伤心，而且比你想象中的更加苍老。

En: No, please! Egg Head presses STOP on the tape player. In the silence, you can hear the wind howl outside.
Zh: “不要，求你了！”爱凡客按下了录音机的停止键。在一片寂静中，你能够听到外面的风声。

En: We need a win. I promise this will be a win! We won't cook speed in there, we'll do it clean, we'll do it true. We'll do it sober and *real* and beautiful. This will be a victory for the light.
Zh: “我们需要一场胜利，我向你*保证*这将是一场胜利！我们不会在那儿制毒，会保证清白。我们会真诚地、清醒地、*真实*漂亮地办事。这会是光明正大的胜利。”

En: This will be nothing.
Zh: 这将什么都不是。

En: He looks almost as old as you.
Zh: 他看上去几乎跟你一样大。

En: You can hear the ice cracking underneath you. Outside on the pier the last century development crumbles in the wind, a grapeshot row of falling houses. And so does Rue Saint-Gérôme and Main Street. The Old Cinema is sinking underneath Villalobos...
Zh: 你能听到脚下冰面正在开裂。帐篷外的码头边，上世纪残留的建筑在风中摇摇欲坠，还散落着一排倒塌的房屋。圣杰罗姆路和主干道旁亦是如此。古老的电影院正逐渐沉入维拉洛博斯地下……

En: Man, that speech was beautiful. I think I've changed my mind. (Don't evict them.)
Zh: “老兄，你的发言真是太美好了。我想我改变主意了。”（不驱逐他们。）

En: The young man gulps like he's trying not to cry.
Zh: 年轻人哽咽着，仿佛在强忍着哭泣。

En: Wait, does anyone feel that?
Zh: “等等，有人感觉到了吗？”

En: No... He looks like he's at least 45 years old. "What does that even mean?"
Zh: “不……”他看上去至少有45岁了。“那究竟是什么意思？”

En: No debate. Get lost, I'm not letting some druggies take over a church. (Evict them.)
Zh: “别再狡辩了，快滚吧，我不会让几个瘾君子占据教堂的。”（驱逐他们。）

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Thank you...
Zh: “谢谢你……”

En: It wouldn't change a thing. It's too late. For everything. Just pack your bags and go. (Evict them.)
Zh: “不会有任何改变的，一切都已经太迟了。收拾好东西快走吧。”（驱逐他们。）

En: You're getting off easy. I'm letting you live.
Zh: “这次让你们轻易脱身了，我饶了你们一命。”

En: He glares at you. "Yes. *Thank you*, officer."
Zh: 他瞪着你。“是啊，*谢谢你*，警官。”

En: He doesn't even look at you.
Zh: 他甚至没有看你一眼。

En: Okay. We're all cool now, right? He sounds hopeful. "What's the next step? What's gonna happen now?"
Zh: “好吧。现在我们都没事了，对吧？”他的语气充满了希望。“下一步要怎么做？现在会怎么样？”

En: Other than squeezing every drop out of us.
Zh: “而不是把我们榨干。”

En: Other than all our money.
Zh: “除了我们的钱。”

En: Hush, Noid.
Zh: “嘘，诺伊德。”

En: Let's do this clean. No speed lab, just a club for anodic music. (Proceed with the club.)
Zh: “让我们清白一点，忘记毒品实验室，只有阳极音乐俱乐部。”（继续俱乐部计划。）

En: It wouldn't work without the lab. Do what you have to do to keep the club alive. (Proceed with both.)
Zh: “没有实验室是不行的，尽你们所能让俱乐部继续运作下去吧。”（两者兼备。）

En: It wouldn't work without the lab. Do what you have to do to keep the club alive. (Proceed with both.)
Zh: “没有实验室是不行的，尽你们所能让俱乐部继续运作下去吧。”（两者兼备。）

En: We need a win, Andre. He looks at you. "I promise this will be a win! We won't cook speed in there, we'll do it clean, we'll do it true. We'll do it sober and *real* and beautiful. This will be a victory for the light!"
Zh: “我们需要一场胜利，安德烈。”他看着你。“我保证这将是一场胜利！我们不会在那儿制毒，会保证清白。我们会真诚地、清醒地、*真实*漂亮地办事。这会是光明正大的胜利。

En: There *needs* to be a club for anodic music in there. NEEDS TO! Everyone hates each other. Everybody hates it here, it's all just drugs and we're slaves and I *can't... we are running out of time!
Zh: “这里*需要*一间阳极音乐俱乐部，必须！每个人都相互憎恨，每个人都讨厌这里，到处都是毒品，而我们都是奴隶，*我不能……*我们没时间了！”

En: This will be nothing.
Zh: 这将什么都不是。

En: You can hear the ice crack underneath you. Outside the last century waterfront development is crumbling in the wind, a grapeshot row of falling houses. And so is Rue Saint-Gérôme and all the houses on Main Row. The Old Cinema is sinking underneath Villalobos...
Zh: 你能听到脚下冰面开裂的声音。帐篷外的码头边，上世纪残留的水岸开发区在风中摇摇欲坠，还散落着一排倒塌的房屋。圣杰罗姆路和主干道旁的建筑亦是如此。古老的电影院正逐渐沉入维拉洛博斯地下……

En: Okay, we'll try to do it without the drugs. He raises his head from between his knees.
Zh: “好吧，我们尽量不嗑药。”他从两膝之间抬起头。

En: Wait, does anyone feel that?
Zh: “等等，有人感觉到了吗？”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Right. Let's call this incident crime prevention, but I have my eye on you.
Zh: “很好。我们姑且把它称为预防犯罪吧，但我会一直盯着你的。”

En: There *needs* to be a club for anodic music in there. NEEDS TO! So we won't cook speed in there, we'll do it clean, we'll do it true. We'll do it sober and *real* and beautiful.
Zh: “这里*需要*一间阳极音乐俱乐部，必须！所以我们不会在那儿制毒，会保证清白。我们会真诚地、清醒地、*真实*漂亮地办事。这会是光明正大的胜利。”

En: We'll do a straight up club in there, spinning the maddest reels and nothing but, I swear to god! Okay, Egg?
Zh: “我们要在那里建一间周末狂欢俱乐部，只播放最疯狂的里尔舞曲，我对天发誓！可以吗，凡客？”

En: YEAHH! The young man's smile widens to inhuman proportions. His teeth beam in the floodlight.
Zh: “耶！”那个年轻人的笑容变得异常灿烂，他的牙齿在探照灯下闪闪发亮。

En: He looks almost innocentic, with that Harmon-Wowshi player raised up high. Could this be the certainty a spirit-of-the-world feels?
Zh: 他看上去几乎是天真无暇的，高高举起那台哈蒙沃什播放器。这会是世界精神的必然感受吗？

En: Respectable. I'm really feeling the sines right about now.
Zh: “值得尊敬，我现在真的感受到正弦波了。”

En: It'll be tough, but we'll figure it out.
Zh: “会很艰难，但我们会找到办法的。”

En: I'm sure you'll figure something out. We can manage it with her if we get inside...
Zh: “我相信你会想出办法的。如果我们能进去，就有办法对付她了……”

En: It's probably not going to be easy, the spookers in the church are *not* going to be happy about this.
Zh: “可能不会太容易，教堂里的幽灵对此可*不会*太高兴的。”

En: But you can just evict them, right? You're the law, man. You've got this. He seems to be getting jittery with excitement.
Zh: “但你可以直接驱逐他们，对吗？你就是法律啊，老兄，你能搞定的。”他似乎激动得有些神经质了。

En: Keep us updated about the situation. He thrusts his toothbrush towards you. "Maybe we can help somehow."
Zh: “随时告诉我们最新的进展。”他拿着自己的牙刷向你刺来。“或许我们能帮上忙。”

En: I knew from the get go we could count on you. He looks at his friends. "We can make this work -- the dance club will have a future!"
Zh: “我从一开始就知道我们能指望你。”他看着自己的朋友们。“我们可以的——舞蹈俱乐部会有未来的！”

En: No time to wait!
Zh: 没时间等待了！

En: The beats that had you all fired up just a moment ago now seem overwhelming, nauseating. They shift and multiply like pieces of coloured glass in a kaleidoscope.
Zh: 之前让你们兴奋起来的节奏现在似乎有些过火，令人作呕了。它们如同万花筒里的彩色玻璃一般移动和增殖着。

En: You're gripped by panic, alongside a dizzying feeling of deja vu. Then everything goes silent.
Zh: 你被恐慌所笼罩，同时还有一种令人晕眩的既视感。然后一切归于沉寂。

En: What happens next?
Zh: 接下来会如何？

En: Then consciousness will slowly begin to return. It won't be fun.
Zh: 然后意识将会慢慢回归，不会有多少趣味了。

En: I feel like this has happened before.
Zh: 我觉得这种事以前就发生过。

En: Not sure, but it's definitely not a normal dance-floor experience. There's something seriously wrong with you, brah.
Zh: 还不确定，但这绝对不是正常的舞池体验。你有严重的问题，兄弟。

En: What's happening to me?
Zh: 我是怎么了？

En: You'll lay here in complete darkness for a while, far away from everything and everyone.
Zh: 你会在这完全的黑暗中躺一阵子，远离所有人和事。

En: Could be. It was almost like the utter unpleasantness of the experience was breaking through the barrier of your recent memory loss...
Zh: 或许吧。这几乎就像是这段完全不愉快的经历打破了你最近记忆丧失的壁垒……

En: But you already know -- it rarely is.
Zh: 但你已经知道了——这种可能性不大。

En: Waking up, you see the lieutenant talking to the young people, a worried look on his face.
Zh: 醒来后，你看见警督正和一个年轻人谈话，脸上挂着忧虑的表情。

En: Oh, there you are, man. Don't worry, I've seen this happen before to people partying too hard. You're dehydrated and all.
Zh: “哦，你醒啦老兄。别担心，我以前见过这种狂欢得太过头的情况，你脱水了。”

En: I've seen this thing happen before to people partying this hard...
Zh: “我以前见过这种狂欢得太过头的情况……”

En: It looks pretty dangerous, though.
Zh: “不过看起来相当危险。”

En: Nah, man, don't worry, he's mainly dehydrated. It's a regular party reaction, yeah.
Zh: “行了老兄，别担心了。他就是脱水了而已，是种常见的派对反应，没错。”

En: What happened? Are you feeling alright?
Zh: “怎么了？你还好吗？”

En: It was intense, but I'm pulling myself together. Boy, I'm all out of juice. (Get up.)
Zh: “真是太刺激了，但我正在努力振作。天呐，我一点劲儿都没有了。”（起身。）

En: Where's Kim?
Zh: “金去哪儿了？”

En: Dunno what happened between you guys, but it was not cool. You should go out and talk to him.
Zh: “不知道你们之间发生了什么，但这非常不妙。你应该出去和他谈谈。”

En: Oh, there you are, man. Don't worry, I've seen this happen before to people partying too hard. You're dehydrated and all. Can you get up?
Zh: “哦，你醒啦老兄。别担心，我以前见过这种狂欢得太过头的情况，你脱水了。还能站起来吗？”

En: The young woman lifts her headphones up slightly and raises her chin, looking at you expectantly.
Zh: 那位年轻女士微微抬起耳机，扬起下巴期待地望着你。

En: No. Recording. She turns to Soona, then back to you. She briskly shakes her head and puts her headphones back on.
Zh: 她转向苏娜，又转向你，然后轻快地摇了摇头，戴上耳机。“不了。我在录音。”

En: She's still at her mainframe, pressing buttons, reading printouts, but she's started nodding her head along to the music.
Zh: 她仍然坐在主机前，按着按钮，浏览打印出来的东西，但她已经开始随着音乐点头了。

En: Khk, khk! (With *THE EXPRESSION* primed on your face, aim at the lieutenant's feet with your finger pistols.)
Zh: “咳咳，咳咳。”（挂着*那副表情*，用手指瞄准警督的脚。）

En: With *THE EXPRESSION* primed on your face, dance toward the lieutenant.
Zh: 挂着*那副表情*，向警督跳去。

En: Dance, you yellow monkeyfucker! (Start firing!)
Zh: “跳舞啊，你这黄色傻逼猴子！”（开枪！）

En: Dance, you yellow monkey fucker!
Zh: “跳舞啊，你这黄色傻逼猴子！”

En: I should get help.
Zh: 我需要寻求帮助。

En: What are you going to do, call the lazareth again? And if you were to go to a regular hospital... Do you know how long it would take to figure out what's really wrong with you? The interviews, the poking and prodding that you'll undergo?
Zh: 你打算怎么办，再给检疫所打电话吗？如果你去了正规医院……你知道要花多长时间才能弄清楚你究竟出了什么问题吗？那些询问，还要被人戳来戳去的？

En: Do you know how long it will take to figure out what's really wrong with you? The interviews, the poking and prodding that you'll undergo?
Zh: 你知道要花多长时间才能弄清楚你究竟出了什么问题吗？那些询问，还要被人戳来戳去的？

En: All this excitement of dancing and dancing harder and even fainting *real* hard is better than dragging your feet from office to office, examination room to examination room, all mercilessly white, bright, and smelling of antiseptic.
Zh: 跳舞、用力地跳舞乃至*跳到*昏厥都比被从一间办公室拖到另一间办公室，一间诊室拖到另一间诊室，到处都是明亮的惨白与抗菌剂的味道要好。

En: Besides, you've got a case to solve right now, and you won't get to solve it if you turn yourself into a lab rat. Whatever's wrong with you can wait.
Zh: 另外你现在还有个案子要解决呢，如果你把自己变成了一只实验室小白鼠，你绝没办法解决它了。无论你有什么问题，都可以再等等。

En: How long was I out for?
Zh: “我昏迷多久了？”

En: I think I had a seizure...
Zh: “我想我是癫痫发作了……”

En: Maybe your memory loss was caused by something like this.
Zh: 或许你的失忆就是由这种事情引起的。

En: Maybe this is what happened to me when I lost my memory...
Zh: “也许这就是我失忆的时候发生过的事……”

En: Give him a moment. He comes over with a cup of water and puts it to your lips. "See, this is why you need distilled water."
Zh: “给他一点时间。”他端着一杯水放到你的唇边。“看到没，这就是你需要蒸馏水的原因。”

En: Give him a moment. He comes over with a cup of water and puts it to your lips. "You've gotta drink water, man."
Zh: “给他一点时间。”他端着一杯水放到你的唇边。“你得喝点水，老兄。”

En: He bounces up and down, pointing at you. "Look, he's awake!"
Zh: 他一边跳动一边指着你。“瞧，他醒了！”

En: It felt familiar.
Zh: “这感觉很熟悉。”

En: Naw, you just raved too hard. The beat gets so hard, you know, and you were rolling super hard on it...
Zh: “不，你就是嗨过头了。节奏太强劲了，明白吗，而你又跳得超级嗨……”

En: When exactly did you lose consciousness? When you started shaking or when you were already on the ground?
Zh: “你到底是什么时候失去知觉的？你开始颤抖的时候，还是躺到地上的时候？”

En: Thirty minutes or so. She raises an amused eyebrow.
Zh: 她饶有兴致地挑起眉毛，回应道：“大约30分钟。”

En: Has anything like this happened to you before?
Zh: “你以前遇到过这种事吗？”

En: With the moves you've got on you -- I don't doubt it!
Zh: “你这样的舞步——我毫不怀疑！”

En: He ignores the young man. "It very well could be that something similar happened the night you lost your memory. Can you stand?"
Zh: 他没有理睬那个年轻人。“很可能在你失忆的那晚也发生了类似的事情。你能站得住吗？”

En: He nods. "That seems likely. Can you stand?"
Zh: 他点了点头。“有这个可能，你能站得住吗？”

En: I AM LA REVACHOLIÈRE.
Zh: 我是瑞瓦肖之神。

En: What do you mean, *you are the city*?
Zh: *你是这座城市*，什么意思？

En: How are you talking to me?
Zh: 你是怎么跟我说话的？

En: I AM THE CITY.
Zh: 我就是这座城市。

En: I AM A FRAGMENT OF THE WORLD SPIRIT, THE GENIUS LOCI OF REVACHOL.
Zh: 我是世界精神的碎片，瑞瓦肖的守护神。

En: MY HEART IS THE WIND CORRIDOR. THE BOTTOM OF MY AIR IS RED. I HAVE A HUNDRED THOUSAND LUMINOUS ARMS.
Zh: 我的心是风之回廊，我身下的天空是红色，我有十万条发光的手臂。

En: COME MORNING, I CARRY INDUSTRIAL DUST AND LET IT SETTLE ON TREE LEAVES. I SHAKE THE DUST FROM THOSE LEAVES AND ONTO YOUR COAT.
Zh: 清晨我带来工业的尘埃，让它们落在树叶上。我把树叶上的灰尘抖落到你的外套上。

En: YOU ARE AN OFFICER OF THE CITIZENS MILITIA. *AGENTES IN REBUS*, WHEN YOU WEAR YOUR COAT, YOU WEAR MY SOUL.
Zh: 你是公民武装的警官，*字谜的代理人*，当你穿上外套，便穿上了我的灵魂。

En: What are you afraid of?
Zh: 你在害怕什么？

En: But who am *I*? Why are you talking to me?
Zh: 但*我*是谁？你为什么在跟我说话？

En: DEATH -- IT IS TERRIFYING. I NEED YOU TO PROTECT ME FROM DEATH. I CANNOT PERISH. LOOK AT ME. I CANNOT END.
Zh: 死亡——着实非常可怕，我需要你来保证我的生命。我不能消亡，看着我，我不能就这样终结。

En: She's speaking fast because she is afraid.
Zh: 她出于恐惧，说得飞快。

En: All this eloquence -- it's in service of something. She's afraid.
Zh: 这一系列修辞——都是出于某种目的的，她很害怕。

En: Beneath the grandness, there is fear.
Zh: 在伟大的背后，是恐惧。

En: THE MODULATIONS OF MY VOICE ARE NOTED DOWN WITH THERMOMETERS AND BAROMETERS. YOU FEEL ME IN YOUR NOSTRILS, ON THE LITTLE HAIRS ON THE BACK OF YOUR NECK.
Zh: 我声音的调转被温度计和气压计记录，你用你的鼻孔，与脖子后面的汗毛感受着我。

En: THE MODULATIONS OF MY VOICE ARE NOTED DOWN WITH THERMOMETERS AND BAROMETERS. YOU FEEL ME IN YOUR NOSTRILS, ON THE LITTLE HAIRS ON THE BACK OF YOUR NECK.
Zh: 我声音的调转被温度计和气压计记录，你用你的鼻孔，与脖子后面的汗毛感受着我。

En: I ALSO RESIDE IN YOUR LUNGS AND VESTIGIAL ORGANS. EVERYWHERE THERE IS SPACE.
Zh: 我同样存在于你的肺与退化的器官之中，各种空间里。

En: IN 22 YEARS, THE FIRST SHOT WILL BE FIRED. NOT A SHOT FROM A GUN -- AN ATOMIC DEVICE THAT WILL LEVEL ALL OF ME. ALL OF ME.
Zh: 22年后，第一枪将会打响。并非来自于枪炮——一种原子装置将会把我，我的一切夷为平地。

En: But... what can I do about it?
Zh: 可是……我能做什么呢？

En: YOU ARE AN OFFICER OF THE CITIZENS MILITIA. YOU MOVE THROUGH MY STREETS FREELY IN MOTOR CARRIAGES AND ON FOOT. YOU HAVE ACCESS TO THE HIDDEN PLACES. YOU ALSO CIRCULATE AMONG THOSE WHO ARE HIDDEN.
Zh: 你是公民武装的警官，你坐着車或者步行，自由地穿行在我的街道之间。你能够进入隐秘的地方，同样也能游走于隐秘的人群之中。

En: YOU MOVE THROUGH MY STREETS FREELY IN MOTOR CARRIAGES AND ON FOOT. YOU HAVE ACCESS TO THE HIDDEN PLACES. YOU ALSO CIRCULATE AMONG THOSE WHO ARE HIDDEN.
Zh: 你坐着車或者步行，自由地穿行在我的街道之间。你能够进入隐秘的地方，同样也能游走于隐秘的人群之中。

En: My partner... I think I insulted him just now, and I'm not sure that he'll forgive me.
Zh: 我的搭档……我想我刚刚侮辱了他，我不确定他是否会原谅我。

En: It did look like you... short-circuited, she says, flipping through printouts.
Zh: “你确实像是……短路了一样，”她一边说着，一边翻看着打印出来的文件。

En: Yup, sounds like a seizure. And you probably had a more massive one a few days back.
Zh: 没错，看上去很像是癫痫发作。而且几天前你很可能经历了更严重的一次。

En: It's not your moves that are at fault, per se. You almost certainly had a seizure just now -- and a much more massive one a few days ago, when you lost your memory.
Zh: 本质上来说错的不是你的舞步，你刚才一定是癫痫发作了——并且前几天还有一次更严重的，在你失忆的时候。

En: But all kinds of things could be wrong with you at this point -- you've known that much all along. Meanwhile, you still have a killer to catch.
Zh: 但这种时候你可能会出现各种各样的问题——你早就意料到了。与此同时，你还有一个杀手要抓呢。

En: You've got to press on with this case, though. Just -- take better care of yourself. Don't mix with adolescent drug addicts, maybe?
Zh: 不过你得抓紧查案了，只是——要好好照顾自己，或许别再跟青少年瘾君子们混在一起了？

En: YOU MUST MAKE IT RIGHT AT ONCE. UNITY IS PARAMOUNT. AND REMEMBER...
Zh: 你必须马上改正过来，团结是重中之重。记住……

En: An officer of the RCM is lying on the floor of a small church with his eyes rolled back and his tongue lolling out. Several others are standing around him. He slowly comes to.
Zh: 一名RCM警官躺在一座小教堂的地板上，翻着白眼，吐出了舌头。还有几个人站在他周围，他慢慢地苏醒过来。

En: What is this strange feeling I keep having? This cold... even now.
Zh: 这种奇怪的感觉是什么？这种寒冷……即便是现在。

En: What's happening to me? What is this strange feeling that comes...
Zh: 我是怎么了？这种奇怪的感觉是什么……

En: I NEED YOU. YOU CAN KEEP ME ON THIS EARTH. BE VIGILANT. I LOVE YOU.
Zh: 我需要你，你在地球上的时候可以拥有我。保持警惕，我爱你。

En: (Get up.)
Zh: （起身。）

En: Had a good rest there?
Zh: “休息得好吗？”

En: Had a good rest there?
Zh: “休息得好吗？”

En: I bet you did! Those were *some* advanced moves, man.
Zh: “我信你！你那些舞步可*真不赖*，老兄”

En: That's all great, but we should really get going. We've spent enough time doing aerobic exercise for today.
Zh: “很不错，但我们真得走了，今天的有氧运动我们已经做得够多了。”

En: Keep the thought to yourself.
Zh: 不要把这个想法告诉别人。

En: I'VE SEEN YOU, I'VE SEEN YOU! I'VE SEEN YOU WITH HER -- AND I'VE SEEN YOU WITHOUT HER. I'VE SEEN YOU ON THE CRESCENT OF THE HILL.
Zh: 我见过你，我见过你！我见过你跟她在一起——也见过你独自一人的时候。我曾在新月山见过你。

En: I knew it. I knew the end of the world was coming!
Zh: 我就知道。我就知道世界末日要降临了！

En: I won't live that long.
Zh: 我活不了那么久的。

En: I spoke to the City of Revachol!
Zh: “我跟瑞瓦肖的灵魂谈过了！”

En: I don't know about good...
Zh: “我说不清是好还是不好……”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Fuck yeah!
Zh: “太他妈对了！”

En: Relax. People pass out while partying hard all the time. You're just dehydrated.
Zh: “放轻松。总会有人狂欢过头昏过去，你只是脱水了而已。”

En: So... He looks at you with mild concern. "We should get going maybe?"
Zh: “那么……”他略带关切地看着你。“我们是不是可以离开了？”

En: That was great, but I'm all out of juice now, guys. Besides, I should really talk to my partner. (Get up.)
Zh: “那真是太好了，但我一点劲都没有了伙计们。而且我真的得去跟我的搭档谈谈了。”（起身。）

En: That was great, but I'm all out of juice now. Besides, I really need to get to the island now. (Get up.)
Zh: “那真是太好了，但我现在一点劲都没有了。再说，现在我真要上岛了。”（起身。）

En: That was great, but I'm all out of juice now. (Get up.)
Zh: “那真是太好了，但我现在一点劲都没有了。”（起身。）

En: That's all great, but we should really get going. We've spent enough time doing aerobic exercise for today.
Zh: “很不错，但我们真得走了，今天的有氧运动我们已经做得够多了。”

En: I CANNOT PERISH WITH IT, OFFICER OF THE CITIZENS MILITIA. YOU CANNOT LET ME.
Zh: 我不能与它一同消亡，公民武装的警官。你不能弃我不顾。

En: BY THE WAY, APOLOGIZE TO YOUR PARTNER AT ONCE. UNITY AMONG THE RANKS IS PARAMOUNT.
Zh: 顺便说一句，立刻去向你的搭档道歉，不同等级的团结是重中之重。

En: Nice try, son, but this is not something you should be attempting to do with your bare hands.
Zh: 想的挺美，孩子，但这并不是你赤手空拳就能做到的事。

En: The padlock passes through a staple that's been hastily attached to the wood. Closer inspection reveals that one of the screws is not a screw at all, but a nail. The work has been done recently and is unprofessional, to say the least.
Zh: 挂锁穿过一根钉在木头上的螺钉，仔细检查后发现其中一根螺钉根本不是什么螺钉，而是一根铁钉。这是最近才挂上去的，至少可以判断手法非常不专业。

En: Look at the sticker.
Zh: 查看贴纸。

En: Inspect the staple.
Zh: 检查螺钉。

En: You see a yellow circle with two X's and a big curve below them that looks like a mouth. You're pretty sure you haven't seen it before, but what the symbol depicts is clear enough: a smiling dead guy. The curve makes it smile and the X's make it dead.
Zh: 你看到一个黄色圆圈，上面有两个X，下面有一条大曲线，看起来像是一张嘴。你很确定自己之前从未见过它，但这个符号所描绘的东西已经足够清晰了:一个面带微笑的死者。曲线让它微笑，X让它死亡。

En: There is something blindingly *modern* about this symbol. Its modernness puts to shame everything you've seen before.
Zh: 这个标志有某种极具*现代*气息的东西。它的现代程度使你之前见过的一切都相形见绌起来。

En: Have you seen this symbol before? (Point to it.)
Zh: “你以前见过这个符号吗？”（指向它。）

En: Take another look at the boring padlock. For absurdity's sake.
Zh: 再看一眼那把无聊的挂锁，看在荒谬的份上。

En: Take another look at the padlock.
Zh: 再看一眼挂锁。

En: He takes off his glasses and uses a blue handkerchief to thoroughly wipe them clean before inspecting the sticker. Then he looks up, pauses, and replies...
Zh: 他摘下眼镜，用一块蓝色手帕把眼镜彻底擦干净，才检查起了贴纸。然后他抬起头，停顿了一下后回答道……

En: What does it look like to you?
Zh: “你认为如何？”

En: Looks like a dead man smiling. Suggests... junior delinquency.
Zh: “看上去像是个在微笑的死人，意味着……青少年过失行为。”

En: I haven't seen that sticker before. And I am not a youth.
Zh: “我从没见过这个标志。而且我也不是年轻人。”

En: I agree -- it's very modern. But does the cheery guy not know he's dead, or does the dead guy not care that he is? What is the source of the irony here?
Zh: “我同意——它相当现代。但这个快活的人是不知道自己已经死去？还是这个死去的人不在乎自己已经死去了呢？里面的讽刺源头是什么？”

En: That level of conceptual thinking is not part of my skill set.
Zh: “这种程度的概念性思考不在我的技能范畴之内。”

En: Should you want to get through, it might be easier to just pry the whole thing off.
Zh: 如果你想进去的话，直接把它撬开可能会更容易些。

En: Kim, do you happen to have a prybar?
Zh: “金，你身边有撬棍吗？”

En: He takes a step back and looks at the church looming overhead. "You really want us in there... I do have tools in the Kineema."
Zh: 他往后退了一步，望着头顶上若隐若现的教堂。“看来你是真的很想进去……我的锐影里面的确有些工具。”

En: The lieutenant takes a step back and looks at the church looming overhead. "Yes, there's a toolbox in the Kineema..."
Zh: 警督往后退了一步，望着头顶上若隐若现的教堂。“是的，锐影里面有个工具箱……”

En: There is a touch of guilt in his voice.
Zh: 他的声音里有一丝内疚。

En: Should we start with that kick drum coming from the ice? I heard a sound, before.
Zh: “我们应该从冰上传来的鼓声入手吗？我之前就听到一个声音了。”

En: Where do you think we should start?
Zh: “你认为我们应该从哪里入手？”

En: Yes. The pulsing bass... He raises his hand to his ear. "A sure sign of junior delinquency -- somewhere east of here."
Zh: “是的。低音脉冲……”他把手举至耳边。“青少年过失行为的明确符号——就在这里往东去的某个地方。”

En: Can you hear the pulsing bass underneath the wind? He raises his hand to his ear. "A sure sign of junior delinquency -- somewhere east of here."
Zh: “你能听见风中传来的低音脉冲吗？”他把手举至耳边。“青少年过失行为的明确符号——就在这里往东去的某个地方。”

En: Apply some official force on the padlock.
Zh: 对挂锁上施力。

En: Objects Must Fall.
Zh: 物体必将下落。

En: Tool not equipped.
Zh: 未装备工具。

En: I don't know... you *don't*? Or maybe we'll find a new one. I don't carry more than one prybar with me... He looks up at the bell-tower.
Zh: “我不知道……你*不*知道？或者我们可以找个新的。我只能带上一根撬棍……”他抬头望向钟塔。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: What is suggestive of junior delinquency here?
Zh: 这里有什么地方暗示了它是青少年过失行为吗？

En: He feels sorry for you. For him as a lieutenant it would be demeaning to have someone recite terms from the officer's exam.
Zh: 他很是为你难过。对于身为警督的他来说，让别人叙述警官考试中的术语相当有失身份。

En: These acts aren't called *crimes* as they would be for adults. Crimes committed by minors are called 'delinquent acts'. This was part of your officer's exams. He puts the handkerchief in his coat pocket.
Zh: “这些行为不同于成年人那样被称为*犯罪*，未成年人犯下罪行被称为‘过失行为’。这都是你警官考试的一部分。”他把手帕放回外衣口袋。

En: For Revachol ZoC, the Moralintern defines junior delinquents as minors between the ages of 10 and 16 who have committed an act in violation of the law...
Zh: “对于瑞瓦肖SAR，国际道德伦理委员会将少年犯定义为10到16岁之间有违法行为的未成年人……”

En: Okay, what is *junior delinquency*?
Zh: “好吧，什么是*青少年过失行为*？”

En: Try to peel off the sticker without ripping it.
Zh: 试着完好得将贴纸揭下来。

En: You dried your hands.
Zh: 你擦干了双手。

En: Your hands are clammy from the sea air. The piece of sticker keeps slipping away...
Zh: 你的双手因为海边的空气变得潮湿，那张贴纸不断地滑落……

En: There's nothing like the sound of a sticker unpeeling. Now it's stuck to your thumb.
Zh: 没有什么比揭开贴纸的声音更美妙了，现在它黏在你的拇指上了。

En: Shake it off your thumb and throw it in the wind.
Zh: 把它从拇指上甩下来，扔到风里去。

En: Put the sticker *on* your ledger -- right on the cover!
Zh: 把贴纸贴在你的手册*上*——就在封面上！

En: Put the sticker *in* your ledger -- after the last entry, where it belongs.
Zh: 把贴纸贴在你的手册*里*——最后一项条目之后，正是它的位置。

En: Do you think our suspect is hiding inside? He raises his sight to the darkened windows overhead.
Zh: “你认为我们的嫌犯藏在里面吗？”他抬头望着头顶上昏暗的窗户。

En: Run your hand over a beam.
Zh: 用手拂过梁柱。

En: Back up and look at the door.
Zh: 后退一步，看着那扇门。

En: The surface is smooth from the wind, but moist to the touch.
Zh: 其表面被风吹得很光滑，但摸起来很潮湿。

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: What makes it so modern?
Zh: 它为何会如此现代？

En: It's the contrast between the cheery, chemical yellow and the rigor mortis. As if the cheery guy didn't know he was dead or the dead guy didn't care that he was. Either way, you get the sense the forces of *future* are at work here.
Zh: 是愉悦、化学黄色与死后僵硬的反差。就好像这个快活的人不知道自己已经死去了，或者这个死去的家伙不在乎自己的死亡。无论如何，你能感到这其中有*未来*的力量在发挥作用。

En: But maybe we should circle the building and look for another way in first. The building has seen enough mistreatment.
Zh: “但我们或许可以先绕着大楼走一圈，找找别的入口。这座建筑已经遭了太多罪了。”

En: I do.
Zh: “是的。”

En: People do look to churches for shelter, but an abandoned thing like this... I'm not so sure.
Zh: “人们确实会去教堂寻求庇护，但像这样被遗弃的教堂……我不太确定。”

En: Kim, could I have that handkerchief? My hands are wet.
Zh: “金，能把那块手帕借我吗？我的手湿了。”

En: Fuck this! (Hit the lock.)
Zh: “去他的吧！”（击打挂锁。）

En: Gracefully let the lock go.
Zh: 优雅地放下锁。

En: The lieutenant hands you a blue handkerchief. He looks a little dismayed when he gets it back crumpled. However, it should be easier to peel the sticker off now.
Zh: 警督递给你一块蓝色手帕。当他拿回皱巴巴手帕的时候，看起来有点失望。不过，现在应该更容易揭下贴纸了。

En: That hurt. The lieutenant looks up at the roof of the church, ignoring your little tantrum.
Zh: 那一下肯定很疼。警督抬头望着教堂的屋顶，没有理会你的小脾气。

En: The lock clanks against the door, cold and heavy.
Zh: 门上的锁当啷一声撞到门上，冰冷又沉重。

En: (Turn to the lieutenant.) "This is where Mr. Prybar comes in handy."
Zh: （转向警督。）“这就是撬棍先生出场的时候了。”

En: He takes a step back. "Maybe we should circle the building first and look for another way. The building has seen enough mistreatment."
Zh: 他后退了一步。“我们或许可以先绕着大楼走一圈，找找别的入口。这座建筑已经遭了太多罪了。”

En: Voilà! Looks very modern.
Zh: 瞧瞧！看起来太现代了。

En: Au revoir, sticker!
Zh: 再见啦，贴纸！

En: Looks like today was a gold star day!
Zh: 看来今天是个好日子！

En: Padlock, padlock, padlock!
Zh: 挂锁，挂锁，挂锁！

En: Admit defeat and leave the lock alone.
Zh: 承认失败，不再碰那把锁。

En: I'm not as *sturdy* as you are. I have even less leverage. He doesn't even try to sound convincing.
Zh: “我不像你那么*彪悍*，甚至起不了多少作用。”他甚至没有尝试让自己显得有说服力一些。

En: You could give me a hand, you know?
Zh: “你可以帮我一把的，知道吗？”

En: If you really think we should get in there, we need to find some other way...
Zh: “如果你真的认为我们应该进去，就得想出其它办法……”

En: The padlock hits the wood like a knocker, with a resounding thud. Your cold hand hurts from the impact. Congratulations!
Zh: 挂锁像门环一样撞在木门上，发出响亮的撞击声。你冻僵的手也弄疼了，真是恭喜！

En: A great whoosh of air rushes into the dark innards of the church, as though rushing to fill a great vacuum...
Zh: 一股巨大的气流冲进了教堂的黑暗之中，仿佛要填补一个巨大的真空……

En: Feels like electricity and a very small piece of nothingness.
Zh: 感觉就像电流和一点点的虚无。

En: Feels exceptional. Three hundred tons of pine wood fit together seamlessly. It's old too, cut and carved many centuries ago.
Zh: 感觉很不寻常。300吨的松木严丝合缝地拼接在一起。它也很古老了，是很多个世纪以前雕刻而成的。

En: It'll be easier to break the staple than the lock. Also -- that sticker is interesting, somehow...
Zh: 弄断订书钉要比弄断锁容易得多。还有——不知道为什么，那张贴纸挺有意思的……

En: These forces of future have chosen to depict something that reminds you of... you.
Zh: 这些未来的力量选择描绘一种东西，让你想起了……你。

En: Thus, it must be made by youths.
Zh: 所以说，这个标志一定是一个年轻人制作的。

En: You're part of the future-brigade now. And so is your formerly humdrum ledger. Neon, baby!
Zh: 现在你也是未来部队的一部分了。你以前那本无聊的手册也是一样。霓虹灯，宝贝！

En: There's something on the sea ice there.
Zh: 那边的海冰上面有什么东西。

En: Your muscles do not know. This shouldn't happen. You're sure the wood looked weak.
Zh: 你的肌肉不知道。这种事不应该发生。你肯定木头看起来很脆弱。

En: Tools break, officer. Try to control yourself.
Zh: “工具坏了，警官。试着控制一下自己。”

En: For example, see how calm I am, although you broke my favourite prybar.
Zh: 比如说，看看我有多淡定，虽然你把我最心爱的撬棍弄坏了。

En: The church does not reply. The wood now seems less rotten, the screws (and one nail) almost shimmer with perseverance.
Zh: 教堂没有回应。现在木头似乎没那么腐朽了，螺丝（还有一只钉子）几乎还在坚持不懈地闪耀。

En: I thought this was a lousy way to enter a place of worship that's already in disrepair. I guess the church agreed.
Zh: “我觉得用这种方法进入一个年久失修的宗教场所非常糟糕。我猜教堂也会同意的。”

En: Pull on the doors...
Zh: 拉开门……

En: You enter the church.
Zh: 你进入教堂。

En: Heavy wooden doors more than twice your height stand shut in front of you. The rectangular, sea-worn ornamentation appears in stark contrast to the padlock carelessly drilled into the wood.
Zh: 两倍于你身高的厚重木门紧闭于你的面前。这个长方形的、被海水侵蚀过的装饰物，与被漫不经心地镶在木头上的挂锁形成了鲜明的对比。

En: Rattle the doors to see if they open.
Zh: 用力摇门，看看是否能打开。

En: Inspect the carpentry.
Zh: 检查木头做工。

En: Take a closer look at the padlock.
Zh: 仔细检查挂锁。

En: I don't think that's going to work...
Zh: “我觉得这样行不通……”

En: No, I'm just curious.
Zh: “不，我只是有点好奇。”

En: She might be. It's worth checking out anyway.
Zh: “或许吧，无论如何都值得一试。”

En: The lieutenant nods silently.
Zh: 警督无声地点了点头。

En: What is this feeling?
Zh: “这是什么感觉？”

En: Back up and look at the door.
Zh: 后退一步，看着那扇门。

En: THERE IS A HOLE IN MY HEART.
Zh: 我的心上有一个洞。

En: The lieutenant looks at the padlock. He didn't hear you asking, you were quiet enough.
Zh: 警督看着挂锁，他没听到你的问题，你太小声了。

En: The doors remain unchanged -- closed with a padlock.
Zh: 大门纹丝不动——被一把挂锁锁住了。

En: The carving on the door is blocklike and angular, like the church itself. Two large beams shoot downwards, sinking into the wood before they reach the threshold.
Zh: 门上的雕刻呈块状，棱角分明，正如教堂本身。两根大梁扎入地下，在到达门槛之前便没入了木头之中。

En: High above, the wind wraps the church in its rush. Cold and wet from the ocean bay, it parts around the massive keel-shaped roof. Like a test tunnel washing both sides, the way it has done for three-hundred-and-forty years. The wind keeps its distance. So should you.
Zh: 云霄之上，狂风把教堂包裹得严严实实。海湾传来的寒冷和潮湿，沿着巨大的龙骨状屋顶分开了。宛如一个风洞洗刷着两侧，340年以来，一直都是同样的方式。狂风都保持着一定距离，你也应该这么做。

En: This cheap-looking padlock is sturdily built. It shackles together a hasp and a staple screwed into the wooden door.
Zh: 这把廉价的挂锁异常坚固，它禁锢了一个搭扣和一根钉在木门上的螺钉。

En: This cheap-looking padlock is sturdily built. It shackles together a hasp and a staple screwed into the wooden door. The lock is adorned with a yellow sticker.
Zh: 这把廉价的挂锁异常坚固，它禁锢了一个搭扣和一根钉在木门上的螺钉，上面还装饰着一张黄色的贴纸。

En: Back off.
Zh: 退后。

En: Open the padlock with the key.
Zh: 用钥匙打开挂锁。

En: The lock turns easily. You hear a click as the shackle pops open.
Zh: 锁轻易就打开了，你能够听见如同镣铐被打开时的咔哒声。

En: Let's go. The lieutenant nods at you.
Zh: “我们走吧。”警督朝你点点头。

En: Nothing happens, only the sound of the padlock rattling against the door.
Zh: 什么也没有发生，只有挂锁撞门的声音。

En: But maybe we should look for another way in first... The building has seen enough mistreatment.
Zh: “但我们或许可以先找找别的入口……这座建筑已经遭了太多罪了。”

En: He takes a step back. "Maybe we should look for another way in first... The church has seen enough mistreatment."
Zh: 他退后一步。“也许我们应该先找找别的入口……这座教堂已经受过不少虐待了。”

En: There is a touch of something in his voice. Hard to say what.
Zh: 他的声音里有一种东西。很难说是什么。

En: How about we go and check out that tent again? He nods at the shabby piece of canvas set up on the ice next to the church.
Zh: “我们再去那个帐篷看看如何？”他朝着教堂旁边的冰面上的破旧帆布点了点头。

En: Besides, we should really get to the island. He looks at the sea.
Zh: “再说了，我们真应该上岛了。”他望向大海。

En: ... in the heart of the city.
Zh: ……在这座城市的心脏。

En: The pathetic old wood, rusty screws (and one nail) stand no chance against the forces of *leverage*. From deep within, a bellow rips...
Zh: 可怜的朽木、生锈的螺钉（还有一枚铁钉）根本无法抵挡*杠杆*的力量，从内部撕开了一道裂痕……

En: A minute or so of mauling away at the lock has led to no success. The Kvalsund in your hand has given you... zero mechanical advantage.
Zh: 一分多钟的对于挂锁的蹂躏并没有成功，你手中的库瓦桑德……并没有给你任何机械增益。

En: A minute or so of mauling away at the lock has led to no success. The prybar in your hand has given you... zero mechanical advantage.
Zh: 一分多钟的对于挂锁的蹂躏并没有成功，你手中的撬棍……并没有给你任何机械增益。

En: You jam the prybar between the lock and the staple and apply force -- your biceps bulge from the strain. The metal creaks and squeals...
Zh: 你把撬棍卡在挂锁和螺钉之间，然后发力——你的肱二头肌因此膨胀起来，金属嘎吱作响……

En: Welcome to Revachol, lock!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖，挂锁！”

En: RAAARRRRRG!
Zh: “呃啊啊啊！”

En: Suddenly, the prybar snaps. Metal shavings fly everywhere. The upper half, between the lock and the staple, falls to the ground with a *thunk*.
Zh: 突然，撬棍断了，金属碎片四处飞溅。挂锁与螺钉之间的上半部分*呯*得一声掉在了地上。

En: The fuck? How is this even *possible*?
Zh: “什么鬼！？这怎么*可能*？”

En: I broke the prybar. Where do I get a new one? (Proceed.)
Zh: “我把撬棍弄坏了。该去哪里招新的呢？”（继续。）

En: Should we start with that kick drum coming from the ice?
Zh: “我们应该从冰上传来的鼓声入手吗？”

En: Where do you think we should start?
Zh: “你认为我们应该从哪里入手？”

En: Yes. The pulsing bass... He raises his hand to his ear. "An indication of junior delinquency."\n
Zh: “是的，低音脉冲……”他把手举至耳边。“青少年过失行为的征兆。”他转身向门口走去。

En: Can you hear the pulsing bass underneath the east wind? He raises his hand to his ear. "A sure sign of junior delinquency."
Zh: “你能听见东风中的低音脉冲吗？”他把手举至耳边。“明显是青少年过失行为的征兆。”

En: It does seem rather irrelevant to our case. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。“这似乎跟我们的案子毫无无关系。”

En: I'm actually not that interested in the church right now.
Zh: “其实我现在对教堂不是很感兴趣了。”

En: Look at the broken tool in your hand.
Zh: 看着你手中损坏的工具。

En: The broken edge of the snapped prybar is smooth. Took the tip right off...
Zh: 折断的撬棍裂口非常光滑。尖端直接没有了……

En: A bad omen. Beware of this church.
Zh: 一个不祥之兆。小心这座教堂。

En: This was surely just a freak accident.
Zh: 这肯定只是一起不寻常的事故。

En: As you do, you hear and the echo of the Doomed Commercial Area. Its black halls and dusty machines. Then the feeling passes.
Zh: 与此同时，你竖起耳朵倾听，那是来自在劫难逃商业区的回响。那漆黑的走廊和尘封的机械。这种感觉转瞬即逝。

En: ....as though you're supposed to be sharing some tremendous, evangelical secret...
Zh: ……就好像你们打算分享一些惊人的教会秘闻似的……

En: Hard core.
Zh: “硬核。”

En: But is it? I mean, really? He tilts his head to the other side, like an owl.
Zh: “但是这样吗？我是说，真的吗？”他像猫头鹰一般把脑袋歪到一边。

En: THE QUESTION IS, WHAT IS THE QUESTION?!
Zh: “问题是，问题是什么？！”

En: I was thinking that too!
Zh: “我也在想这个！”

En: No, but seriously... I'm a bit worried it isn't. He frowns, then starts bobbing his head back and forth once more.
Zh: “不，但说真的……我有点担心不是。”他皱着眉头，然后开始来回摆动他的脑袋。

En: There's nothing wrong with it. I'm still in love with the hard core. He turns pensive all of a sudden.
Zh: “这没有什么不对，我仍然深爱着硬核。”他突然开始沉思起来。

En: Don't be alarmed! Everything is okay. He isn't actually *worried*. Everything is still super hard core! What he probably means is -- it could be even more so!
Zh: 别慌！一切都很好，他并不是真的在*担心*。一切还是超级硬核！他的意思大概是——甚至还可以更硬核！

En: Isn't something holding it back? From being hyper? He thinks for a moment, then his expression clears...
Zh: “是有什么东西在阻止它吗？除了嗑嗨了之外？”他思索了一会儿，然后他的表情变得明朗了……

En: You said you were worried. What do you think is wrong with the music?
Zh: “你说你很担心，是觉得音乐有什么问题吗？”

En: Are you a thought reader?
Zh: “你能读心吗？”

En: Is your real name *Germaine*?
Zh: “你的真名是*杰曼尼*吗？”

En: NO NATION, BUT TRANCE NATION! NO WAR, BUT CLASS WAR!
Zh: “没有国家，只有模糊的国界！没有战争，只有阶级战争！”

En: Does that mean you're a thought reader?
Zh: “意思是你会读心吗？”

En: So you're not a thought reader, you're a communist?
Zh: “所以你不能读心，而是个康米主义者吗？”

En: He's not a communist. That's just something he likes to yell. He picked it up from a tape-jockey at The Paliseum... *she* was a communist though.
Zh: “他不是什么康米主义者，只是喜欢喊这些东西罢了。他是从帕里瑟姆的一个音乐节目主持人那里学来的……不过，*她*倒是个康米主义者。”

En: YEAH! WITH A REBEL YELL!
Zh: “耶！反叛的呐喊！”

En: But why aren't you a communist? Communism is the truth.
Zh: “但你为什么不是康米主义者呢？康米即是真理。”

En: I can be a communist! He nods. "If you want that -- do you want me to be a communist?!"
Zh: “我可以成为一个康米主义者！”他点点头。“如果你是这么希望的话——你想让我成为一名康米主义者吗？！”

En: Please don't turn him into a communist.
Zh: “请不要把他变成一个康米主义者。”

En: Oh yeah, turn him into a communist, c'mon! It would be super!
Zh: “噢耶，把他变成康米主义者吧，来吧！那可就太棒了！”

En: Be a communist, Egg Head. The future needs your help.
Zh: “成为康米主义者吧，爱凡客，未来需要你的力量。”

En: Don't be a communist... wait, what am I saying? Of course be a communist!
Zh: “不要成为康米主义者……等等，我在说什么呢？当然要成为康米主义者啊！”

En: Don't be a lunatic. Of course he isn't. Germaine here just yells random things. Odds are, sooner or later one of them will come off as thought reading.
Zh: “别发疯了，他当然不会，杰曼尼只是喜欢乱喊乱叫而已。不过迟早有一天，这会成为读心的。”

En: Yeah! REVACHOL IMPERATIVE!
Zh: “耶！瑞瓦肖势在必行！”

En: Unless you were thinking 'REVACHOL IMPERATIVE' right now... Anyway, I've had a similar thing happen with Egg's yelling, I know what you mean.
Zh: “除非你现在想的是*瑞瓦肖势在必行*……总而言之，我也遇到过类似的情况，所以我明白你的意思。”

En: (Lie.) "But I *was* thinking 'Revachol imperative'!"
Zh: （说谎。）“但我想的*就是**瑞瓦肖势在必行*！”

En: So it's the world that's the thought reader?
Zh: “那么，能够读心的是这个世界吗？”

En: So, it's not this one. It's the world that's the thought reader in this equation?
Zh: 那么，不是眼前的这个，而是这个世界在读心？

En: THE WORLD IS A COLD SINK.
Zh: 世界就是个冰冷的水池。

En: YOU ARE IN ITS ARMS.
Zh: 你身处它的怀抱中。

En: The young man picks up a level and inspects it against the floodlight. The water sparkles in the small measuring tube. "Yes," he says after a while. "I think it's correct to say that."
Zh: 年轻人拿起水平仪，对着探照灯检查起来，水在小小的测量管里闪闪发光。“是的，”过了一会儿他说着。“我想这么说是正确的。”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess he is a communist now.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！！！”他叫嚷着，你想他现在是个康米主义者了。

En: That's fucked up, man. Lying like that. And a cop too...
Zh: “那可糟糕了，老兄。谎话连篇，而且还是个警察……”

En: Tell me something else, Egg Head...
Zh: “再告诉我一件事吧，爱凡客……”

En: Communism rules the nation! He looks at you with an almost impossibly wide, shining grin, looking to see if you approve.
Zh: “康米主义统治国家！”他带着无与伦比的灿烂笑容看着你，看你是否认同。

En: Capital rules the nation! He looks at you with an almost impossibly wide, shining grin, looking to see if you approve.
Zh: “资本统治国家！”他带着无与伦比的灿烂笑容看着你，看你是否认同。

En: The nation rules the nation! He looks at you with an almost impossibly wide, shining grin, looking to see if you approve.
Zh: “国家统治国家！”他带着无与伦比的灿烂笑容看着你，看你是否认同。

En: It's time to compromise! He looks at you with an almost impossibly wide, shining grin, looking to see if you approve.
Zh: “是时候妥协了！”他带着无与伦比的灿烂笑容看着你，看你是否认同。

En: Yeaaargh!
Zh: “嗷耶！”

En: Don't be a communist. That path is *too* hard core.
Zh: “不要做康米主义者，那条路*太过*硬核了。”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess he won't become a communist after all.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！！！”他叫嚷着，你想他终究不会成为康米主义者的。

En: You're right, I wasn't thinking that.
Zh: “你说得对，我没有想那个。”

En: Hard Core Superstar!
Zh: “硬核巨星！”

En: COMMUNISM FOREVER!!
Zh: “康米主义万岁！！”

En: CAPITAL FOREVER!!
Zh: “资本万岁！！”

En: WELCOME TO REVACHOL!!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！！”

En: Normal! Stable! Normal! Stable!
Zh: “正常！稳定！正常！稳定！”

En: Yeagh!
Zh: “嗷耶！”

En: Right on, comrade luminary!
Zh: “对极了，光明同志！”

En: Almost there. Could use just a bit more work...
Zh: “差不多了，可以再努力一点……”

En: You're wrong. Communism doesn't rule the nation.
Zh: “你错了，康米主义并不能统治国家。”

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: The young man drops his oversized head in shame. He is truly sorry he couldn't come up with a proletarian yell that would satisfy The Great Leader.
Zh: 这位青年羞愧地垂下了那过大的脑袋。他没想出一句能够让伟大领袖满意的无产阶级口号，这令他感到惭愧。

En: HERE COMES THE SUN!
Zh: “太阳降临！”

En: The worker must MOVE YOUR ASS!
Zh: “工人们必须行动起来！”

En: Superb!
Zh: “好极了！”

En: No, that's still not it.
Zh: “不，还是不行。”

En: You could swear you feel the bright future of the working classes reflected off Germaine Egg Head's giant smile and right onto your face! Revolution makes your toes curl in anticipation.
Zh: 你发誓自己能够感觉到工人阶级的光明未来从杰曼尼·爱凡客的巨大微笑里映射到你的脸上！革命让你的脚趾都期待地弯曲起来。

En: It's like it's only *ultra*.
Zh: “好像只是有点*极致*而已。”

En: I think it's super hard core, but you're right, it's not *hyper* hard core.
Zh: “我觉得已经超凡硬核了，但你是对的，不是*超级*硬核。”

En: I am The Law and an expert in these matters. Let me be the judge of what it is: hyper, super, or ultra hard core.
Zh: “我就是法律，我就是这方面的专家。让我来判断它是：超级、超凡还是极致硬核。”

En: It's hyper hard core.
Zh: “这是超级硬核。”

En: It's super hard core.
Zh: “这是超凡硬核。”

En: The young man looks at you reverently, as if observing the judgement inside your head. Growing, taking form.
Zh: 那个年轻人虔诚地望着你，仿佛在观察你的脑袋中判断、增加、减少的方式。

En: If anything, it sounds a bit *proto*. Like it's not fully formed yet. You might be a moribund alcoholic and a failed cop, but you are pretty certain a thing cannot be both *proto* and *hard core*.
Zh: 如果有什么的话，那就是它听起来有点像*原型*。就好像它尚未完全成形。你或许是一个濒死的酒鬼，一个失败的警察，但你很肯定一样东西不可能既*原型*又*硬核*。

En: It's only ultra hard core.
Zh: “这只是极致硬核。”

En: It's proto -- not hard core at all.
Zh: “这太原始了——一点都不硬核。”

En: How much is the fish?
Zh: “这条鱼多少钱？”

En: I could settle it for you, but not right now. Tell me something else, Egg Head.
Zh: “我可以帮你解决，但不是现在。爱凡客，再告诉我一件事。”

En: His friend drops his loitering act and turns toward you two. He, too, waits for your judgement with undivided attention.\n
Zh: 他的朋友收起了吊儿郎当的态度，一本正经地面向你们。他也在等待着你全神贯注地做出判断。

En: In the background, his friend drops a heavy electric drill. He, too, waits for your judgement with undivided attention.
Zh: 身后，他的朋友放下了一个沉重的电钻，他也在一心一意地等待这你的判断。

En: This is a serious matter. Stop talking about fish. Fish has got nothing to do with this.
Zh: “这是很严肃的问题，别再说鱼的事了，这跟鱼一点关系也没有。”

En: Psst... You can hear the would-be leader whisper to his friend: "Don't let him know he doesn't understand the fish thing."
Zh: “啧……”你能听到这位未来的指挥对他的朋友耳语道：“别让他知道他不懂鱼的事。”

En: We can all hear it's not hyper hard core. If figures of authority keep telling us things are hyper, when they're clearly not -- how are we ever supposed to evolve to hyperdom?
Zh: “我们都知道这不是超级硬核了。如果权威人士不断地这么告诉我们，而事实显然并非如此——我们又怎能进步为超级王国呢？”

En: It is, isn't it?! He nods in agreement. "I knew it!"
Zh: “就是这样，不是吗？！”他点头表示赞同。“我就知道！”

En: Fuck yeah, I knew it!
Zh: “他妈的，我就知道！”

En: You can't. But you do.
Zh: “你不可以，但还是这么做了。”

En: I don't really have an answer for this. Tell me something else, Egg Head.
Zh: “我并不知道答案，爱凡客，再告诉我一件事。”

En: The young man smiles and nods. Slowly he picks up two hammers at once.
Zh: 年轻人微笑着点点头，同时慢慢地拿起了两柄锤子。

En: THE R, THE C, THE M, THE THEOLOGY! AROOO!
Zh: “R，C，M，神学！嗷！”

En: RCM? That's the acronym for Revachol Citizens Militia -- you! Does everything this man yells mean something? Is it truly *internally coherent*?
Zh: RCM？那是瑞瓦肖公民武装的简称——是你！这个人叫嚷的每句话都是有意义的吗？*内部相容*是真的吗？

En: Okay, I've changed my mind.
Zh: “好吧，我改变主意了。”

En: Uhuh? Egg Head raises his fist even higher in anticipation.\n
Zh: “嗯哼？”爱凡客满怀期待地把拳头举的更高了。

En: Uhuh? The young man raises the tape player higher in anticipation.
Zh: “嗯哼？”年轻人满怀期待地把录音机举得更高了。

En: WHOAH! Culture Cop!
Zh: “哇哦！文化警察！”

En: For a moment you almost think he's going to put the tape player down. But that would be ludicrous.
Zh: 有那么一瞬间你几乎以为他要把录音机放下来了，但那未免也太可笑了。

En: I think you might be right! But how could it *become* hard core then? I know it in my heart, but cannot think it in my head. If this is not hard core, how could anything be...
Zh: “我想你是对的！但它要如何*变得*硬核呢？我心里明白，脑袋却想不清楚。如果这都不是硬核，那其它的又算什么呢……”

En: He *seems* sure, but you are left with the nagging doubt you might have overestimated the hard coreness of that jam...
Zh: 他*似乎是*确定了。但你却留下了挥之不去的疑虑，自己可能高估了这段演奏的硬核程度……

En: But how could it *become* harder-core? I know the answer in my heart, but cannot think it in my head. If this is not hyper, how could anything be...
Zh: “但它要如何*变得*硬核呢？我心里明白，脑袋却想不清楚。如果这都不算超凡，那其它的又算什么呢……”

En: Wait! I just remembered something! I'm the police!
Zh: “等等！我刚刚记起来了！我是警察！”

En: Try to think if anything could make it... harder-core?
Zh: 试着想想，有没有什么东西可以让它……硬核起来？

En: Uh-uh? The young man is bursting with anticipation!
Zh: “嗯哼？”这个年轻人充满了期待！

En: And I have a job to do instead of this absurd idiocy! Goodbye, I'm off to the island. [Leave.]
Zh: “我还有正经工作要做，而不是荒谬地犯傻！再见，我要上岛了。”[离开。]

En: And I have a job to do instead of this absurd idiocy! Goodbye. [Leave.]
Zh: “我还有正经工作要做，而不是荒谬地犯傻！再见。”[离开。]

En: Nothing. Me being the police isn't going to help us.
Zh: “没什么。我是警察也帮不上什么忙。”

En: Actually, it's more likely to *hinder* us.
Zh: “实际上，它更有可能会*妨碍*我们。”

En: Oh...
I can't help you with this right now. I need something else, something *extra*...
Zh: “我现在帮不了你。我需要点别的，某种*特别*的东西……”

En: YEAGH! KILL ALL RICH PEOPLE! (He's not even the slightest bit disappointed.)
Zh: “嗷耶！杀光所有富人！”（他甚至没有半点失望。）

En: YEAGH! CAPITAL IS SALVATION! (He's not even the slightest bit disappointed.)
Zh: “嗷耶！资本才是救赎！”（他甚至没有半点失望。）

En: YEAGH! REVACHOL IS FOR REVACHOLIANS! (He's not even the slightest bit disappointed.)
Zh: “哦耶！瑞瓦肖属于瑞瓦肖人民！”（他没有半点失望的情绪。）

En: YEAGH! I DON'T KNOW WHAT I'M DOING! (He's not even the slightest bit disappointed.)
Zh: “嗷耶！我不知道自己在做什么！”（他甚至没有半点失望。）

En: YEAGHH! (He's not even the slightest bit disappointed.)
Zh: “嗷耶！”（他甚至没有半点失望。）

En: As your mind works, the beat recedes from your ears. You hear your own blood pulsing through your head, nourishing your thoughts with oxygen. The rhythm is familiar...
Zh: 你的大脑开始运作时，耳边的节奏消失了。你听到血液在自己的脑袋中奔腾，用氧气滋养着你的思想，那节奏很耳熟……

En: The abstract shapes swelling in the foreground have done so in vain. This is a core matter. The answer, in the double-kick that moves the millilitres through your mind. The dark thud is the source of all rhythm, the inspiration behind mathematics...
Zh: 你眼前膨胀起来的抽象形状皆是虚无。这是个核心问题，而答案，在你的头脑中不断跌宕。黑暗的撞击声是所有节奏的源泉，数学背后的灵感……

En: Whaaaat?! He looks at you with customary amazement. "Guys, there's something happening in his head!"
Zh: “什么？”他带着惯有的惊奇望着你。“伙计们，他脑子里发生了某种事情！”

En: Think even harder.
Zh: 更努力思考。

En: But you're not. This is almost certainly a matter that surpasses the limits of reason.
Zh: 但你没有。几乎可以肯定的是，这是一个超越理性极限的问题。

En: Feel the movement of blood through your head.
Zh: 感受血液在你头脑中流动。

En: Oh yeah! He's DOING it!
Zh: “噢耶！他正在做了！”

En: The hard core.
Zh: 硬核。

En: My imagination fails me.
Zh: “我的想象力枯竭了。”

En: I know, so does mine! He laughs and shakes his head.
Zh: “我知道，我的也是！”他笑着摇了摇头。

En: Did someone say *disco*?
Zh: “有人说*迪斯科*了吗？”

En: No, that would be silly. Your body isn't for thinking. It's for robust, wholesome things. Things like discus, javelin, and 400 metre hurdles. Mmm... discus.
Zh: 不，那也太蠢了。你的身体不是用来思考的，而是为了强健自己。比如铁饼、标枪或400米跨栏，哼……铁饼。

En: Maybe your body can tell you what Arno van Eyck's jam is missing? To make it... harder core.
Zh: 或许你的身体可以告诉你阿诺·凡·艾克的谈演奏缺了点什么？让它……更加硬核。

En: The aesthetic of anodic music.
Zh: 阳极音乐美学。

En: The beat of your heart.
Zh: 你的心跳。

En: Horrific necktie wants to try again!
Zh: 恐怖领带想要再试一次！

En: What was that sound?
Zh: 那是什么声音？

En: Expert on anodic music.
Zh: 阳极音乐专家。

En: Sometimes I just feel like anodic music is in its infancy, you know? For example, take this Arno van Eyck jam I've been pumping for the last month... and will continue pumping for the rest of '51...
Zh: “有时候我觉得阳极音乐还处于婴儿阶段，你知道吗？比如说，上个月我一直在听这个阿诺·凡·艾克的即兴演奏……51年剩下来的日子还会继续听……”

En: Sounds suspiciously like a *question*. I thought the question was: What is the question?
Zh: “听起来似乎是个*疑问*，我以为问题是：问题是什么呢？”

En: No, this is THE ANSWER!
Zh: “不，这就是答案！”

En: Alright. Goodbye, Egg Head. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，再见了，爱凡客。”[离开。]

En: Alright! HERE COMES THE NIGHT!
Zh: “好吧！黑夜降临！”

En: D'accord hard core! Germaine Egg-Head.
Zh: “确实硬核！杰曼尼·爱凡客！”

En: Hard-Cop! You know about *him* too... He moves his mouth, but sound doesn't come out. Egg Head's eyes are the size of saucers. Looks like you've rendered him speechless.
Zh: “硬核警探！你也知道*他*……”他的嘴唇动了动，但没有发出声音；爱凡客的眼睛瞪得有调味碟那么大。看来你让他哑口无言了。

En: You know about him... He moves his mouth, but sound doesn't come out. His eyes are the size of saucers. Looks like you've rendered him speechless.
Zh: “你知道他……”他的嘴唇动了动，但没有发出声音；他的眼睛瞪得有调味碟那么大。看来你让他哑口无言了。

En: Who is this van Eyck"? Did he make this music?"
Zh: “这位*凡·艾克*是谁？是他创作了这首曲子吗？”

En: So this is the famous van Eyck I'm hearing?
Zh: “那我现在听到的就是大名鼎鼎的凡·艾克的曲子吗？”

En: Uhm, basically, yes it is.
Zh: “嗯，基本上是这样的。”

En: You know van Eyck?
Zh: “你知道凡·艾克？”

En: He's overcome with some mysterious emotion. It's not merely adoration for what a Hard Cop you are either. It's more. Could it be *gratitude*?
Zh: 他被某种神秘的情感控制了。这不仅是对你这个如此硬核的警察的崇拜，还有更多。会是*感激*吗？

En: Yeah, I'm a major Eyck-head.
Zh: “没错，我是艾克的狂热粉丝。”

En: Your friend Acele mentioned him.
Zh: “你的朋友阿西尔提起过他。”

En: No, it was just a turn of phrase.
Zh: “不，就是这么一说。”

En: Even dancing to this beat before, you knew it in your lungs where the pressure wasn't bursting. And in your heart that was alone and in your solar plexus where the hits should have landed... so does every chordate animal:
Zh: 伴随这一节奏起舞的经历唤醒了你五脏六腑的记忆。你的肺知晓这压力不够炸裂。你的心脏知晓那种孤独。你的太阳神经丛知晓鼓点应落在何处……世间所有脊索动物都是如此：

En: You know it in your lungs where the pressure should vibrate, in your heart that's alone, and in your solar plexus where the hits should land... so does every chordate animal...
Zh: 你的肺知道气压会在那里振动，你的心知道它的孤独，而你的太阳神经丛知道舞步要踩在哪里……每个脊索动物都是如此……

En: Back to the heavyweight jam! says the young man from behind his mixing deck. Lung-shaped trees grow on his silver belt buckle.
Zh: “再来点重量级的演奏！”混音台后面的年轻人说到，他银色的皮带扣上映着肺状的树芽。

En: Back to the heavyweight jam! says the young man with the tape player and the large boiadeiro boots. Lung-shaped trees sprout on his silver belt buckle.
Zh: “再来点重量级的演奏！”那个拿着录音机，脚踩巨大的布雅迪罗靴子的年轻人说到，他银色的皮带扣上映着肺状的树芽。

En: The young man behind the mixer deck acknowledges your return. When he looks at you, he squints as though you were the setting sun...
Zh: 混音台后面的年轻人注意到了你的回归。他眯起眼睛看你，仿佛你像夕阳一样……

En: The young man with the tape recorder acknowledges your return. When he looks at you, he squints as though you were the setting sun...
Zh: 那个拿着录音机的年轻人注意到了你的回归。他眯起眼睛看你，仿佛你像夕阳一样……

En: You're just gonna keep saying it's *hard core*, aren't you?
Zh: “你就只会不停地说这很*硬核*，对吗？”

En: I don't know what to say to that.
Zh: “我不知道该说些什么了。”

En: Skibadee, skibadanger! I am the rearranger!
Zh: “希巴笛，希巴笛的节拍！我是混音之王！”

En: Could there have been a *right* way out of this garden of forking paths, you think?
Zh: 你认为，有没有一条*正道*能走出这个小径分岔的花园呢？

En: YeaaaaaaGH!
Zh: “耶耶耶耶耶嗷耶！”

En: Yeaaaah!
Zh: “耶耶耶耶！”

En: You can *yeah* and say random things all day long, but the track still doesn't sound all that *hard* to me.
Zh: “你可以*耶*或者胡言乱语一整天，但这些歌对我来说仍然没什么*难度*。”

En: Lakierski materialski!
Zh: “喷漆材料！”

En: Spinning out lyrics since the day I was born.
Zh: “从我出生的那天起就开始写歌词。”

En: And the amount of lyrics I got is against the law!
Zh: “我写的歌词数量都多到违法了！”

En: The Y to the E to the A to the A to the A to the A to the A to the G to the H to the exclamation mark...
Zh: “耶——噫——欸——欸——欸——欸——欸——啊——嗷——再加一个感叹号……”

En: YeaaaaaGH!
Zh: “耶耶耶耶耶嗷耶！”

En: He missed one 'A'!
Zh: 他漏了一个耶！

En: You missed an 'A' there. I yelled mine with six 'A'-s, so you have to yell it with six too.
Zh: “你漏掉了一个‘耶’。我喊了六个‘耶’，所以你也得喊六个耶。”

En: You missed an 'A' there, but that's okay. I'm a pretty lenient cop.
Zh: “你漏掉了一个‘耶’。但是没关系，我是个相当宽容的警察。”

En: It's nice to be important, but it's more important to be nice!
Zh: “重要固然很好，但更重要的是要友好！”

En: I'm the hard rhymer, the track attacker, the mic enforcer, the *six* checker! I am the law!
Zh: “我是硬核韵律者，乐曲进攻者，麦克执行者，*第六*检查者！我就是法律！”

En: Alright!
Zh: “好吧！”

En: HERE COMES THE NIGHT!
Zh: “黑夜降临！”

En: I was wondering if you knew who killed the mercenary hanging behind the Whirling-in-Rags hostel?
Zh: “我在想，你知不知道是谁杀了那个雇佣兵？就是被吊在褴褛飞旋旅店后院的那个。”

En: Good morning, yeah! One, two, three! Yekokataa, the place to be!
Zh: “早上好啊，耶！一，二，三！叶科卡塔，快快出发！“

En: Please tell me, what exactly are you doing?
Zh: “请告诉我你究竟在做什么？”

En: Good morning! Yeaaaaaah! Pump it up, pump it up, pump it up! he shouts, apparently unaware of the time of day.
Zh: “早上好！耶！一起来，一起来，一起来！”他叫嚷着，显然没有意识到现在的时间。

En: Good morning! Yeaaaaaah! Pump it up, pump it up, pump it up!
Zh: “早上好！耶！一起来，一起来，一起来！”

En: I am beginning to think this really doesn't have anything to do with the case.
Zh: “我开始认为这真的与本案无关了。”

En: You said that before. Why is this *Yekokataa* the place to be? What does that mean?
Zh: “你以前也这么说过。为什么要出发去*叶科卡塔*？这是什么意思？”

En: But Yekokataa is a catastrophe zone. A desolate wasteland. It can't be the place to be?
Zh: “但叶科卡塔是个重灾区，一处荒凉的废墟。不可能要去那儿吧？”

En: Why is this *Yekokataa* the place to be? What does that mean?
Zh: “为什么要出发去*叶科卡塔*？这是什么意思？”

En: It's the message so listen and you will see. No illusion, the spirit is what you feel!
Zh: “仔细听，你就会明白要传达的讯息了。没有幻觉，你感受到的是灵魂！”

En: Yekokataa is a hard core place!
Zh: “叶科卡塔是个硬核的地方！”

En: Yekokataa is an abbreviation from the Graadian term "Zone of Ecological Catastrophe," an agricultural megaproject in the extreme south east of the Graadian isola. It involved cutting-edge approaches to irrigation and a completely new type of fertilizer.
Zh: “叶科卡塔”是格拉德语“生态灾难地区”的缩写，那是格拉德洲东南边境地带的巨型农业项目。该项目涉及到多种先进的灌溉方式，以及一种全新的肥料。

En: An intricate system of irrigation networks pockmarking the earth, intermittent seas of phosphorous mud, ripped tarpaulin fluttering in the wind... A pair of molten rubber boots also comes to mind. All in all -- a truly hard core place.
Zh: 错综复杂的灌溉网络系统在大地上纵横交错，时断时续的磷质泥浆海洋，撕破的油布在风中飘扬……还有一双熔化的橡胶靴子。总而言之——一个真正硬核的地方。

En: ALL CORE! ALL RIGHT! YEAH!
Zh: “真正的核心！太棒了！耶！”

En: I'm the party boy, it's my job!
Zh: “我是派对男孩，这是我的工作！”

En: What is a party boy?
Zh: “派对男孩是什么？”

En: HARD CORE PARTY 25/7 BEYOND THE WINTER'S ORBIT STYLE!
Zh: “硬核派对25/7超越冬区风格！”

En: I think I'm also a party boy.
Zh: “我想我也是个派对男孩。”

En: There is a place far away in Katla, beyond a certain latitude known as *winter's orbit*, where there are 25 hours in a day. It is a tremendously cold place, abandoned to drunks and failed rock stars. Full of eternite, depression and half finished ski flying hills. The Suru live there.
Zh: 遥远的卡特拉有一个地方，越过被称为*冬季轨道*的特定纬度之后，那里的一天有25个小时。那是一片极寒之地，在惨遭遗弃之后仅存酒鬼和惨败的摇滚明星。那里遍布亘石，萧条与只建了一半的跳台飞跃滑雪荒山。苏鲁人居于此地。

En: Gotta get the people going!
Zh: “要让人们行动起来！”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Two on a track, watch your back, watch out for the heart attack!
Zh: “音乐之中，小心你的背后，小心心脏病发作！”

En: I see.
Zh: “我明白了。”

En: Yeah, request! I want everybody as close to the stage as possible!
Zh: “没错，请求！我希望每个人都能尽可能的靠近舞台！”

En: That would have been good, if I had asked you a question. But I didn't. Now it's just idiotic.
Zh: “如果我问了你问题，那就再好不过了。但我没有，这样就有点白痴了。”

En: Just answer the question!
Zh: “回答问题就好！”

En: But there was a question...
Zh: “但是有个问题……”

En: But there was no question...
Zh: “但并没有问题……”

En: This is hard core.
Zh: “这就是硬核。”

En: Hard core!
Zh: “硬核！”

En: We're close! says the young man standing behind the mixer deck, pumping his fist in the air. Lung-shaped trees grow on his silver belt buckle.
Zh: “就快了！”那个站在混音台后面，挥舞着拳头的年轻人说到，他银色的皮带扣上映着肺状的树芽。

En: We're close! says the young man with the tape player and the large boiadeiro boots. Lung-shaped trees sprout on his silver belt buckle.
Zh: “就快了！”那个拿着录音机，脚踩巨大的布雅迪罗靴子的年轻人说到，他银色的皮带扣上映着肺状的树芽。

En: True, hard, full core!
Zh: “没错，完全的，硬核！”

En: I don't know what to say exactly...
Zh: “我不知道该说些什么了……”

En: Feels like you should reply with the very pinnacle of idiocy here, so that things get totally transcendent. But you haven't gotten there yet, so you don't know what to say.
Zh: 感觉你应该用最愚蠢的方式回应，这样事情就会变得非常完美了。但你还没到那一步，所以你不知道该说些什么了。

En: Under the radar, OVER THE TOP!
Zh: “雷达之下，巅峰之上！”

En: Why are there lungs on your belt buckle?
Zh: “为什么你的皮带扣上有肺？”

En: Needs more bass.
Zh: “需要更多低音。”

En: What?! The young man makes a sudden move, like he's about to turn the volume down. But that would be ridiculous.
Zh: “什么？！”这个年轻人突然动了一下，仿佛要把音量调低了，但这是异想天开了。

En: Wow, okay! We should start with the melody. But where would we get that stuff from?
Zh: “哇哦，好吧！我们应该从旋律开始，但我们要从哪里弄来这些东西呢？”

En: I don't know. I was thinking *you* would know...
Zh: “我不知道，我还以为*你*知道呢……”

En: Nowhere. I'm never ever getting to the island if I'm gonna become some sort of Anodic Cop, too. I've got enough sidecases already.
Zh: “哪儿都没有。如果我也变成什么阳极警探的话，我就永远没办法上岛了。我手上已经有很多二级任务了。”

En: Nowhere. I'm not gonna become some sort of Anodic Cop too, I've got enough copotypes already.
Zh: “哪儿都没有。我也不会变成什么阳极警察，我已经有够多警察类型了。”

En: Oh... For a moment he doesn't know what to say.
Zh: “哦……”他一时不知该说什么好。

En: Okay, I get it, man. The party goes on!
Zh: “好吧，我明白了，老兄。派对继续！”

En: I'm sorry, I don't know anything about anodic music, I'm just the party boy. I get the people going and say it's hard core...
Zh: “抱歉，我对阳极音乐一无所知，我只是个派对男孩。我让人们说这很硬核……”

En: He feels ashamed he can't be of more service to the future of dance music.
Zh: 他为自己不能对舞曲的未来作出更大贡献而感到羞愧。

En: There is a hawthorn tree on Rue de Saint-Ghislaine, right next to the canal. A reel of magnetic tape is caught in its branches, like bronze ribbons blowing in the wind...
Zh: 圣吉莱纳路上有一棵山楂树，紧挨着运河。一卷磁带缠绕在它的树枝上，仿佛青铜丝带在风中飘扬着……

En: Rue de Saint-Ghislaine... I've been there.
Zh: “圣吉莱纳路……我去过那里。”

En: Before I go on, where is Rue de Saint-Ghislaine?
Zh: “在我继续下个话题之前，圣吉莱纳路在什么地方？”

En: It feels cold.
Zh: “感觉很冷。”

En: Shake it off.
Zh: 摆脱这种感觉。

En: Oh, I know! I know this! I can tell you where it is!
Zh: “哦，我知道！我知道这个！我可以告诉你它在哪儿！”

En: Saint-G is the boulevard before the canal bridge. The one that takes you to the Whirling-in-Rags and the Industrial Harbour. It's got the lanterns and the---
Zh: “圣杰罗姆路就是运河大桥前面那条路，通往褴褛飞旋和工业港口，有路灯和……”

En: I knew that! I could have said that!
Zh: “我知道！我本来可以说的！”

En: And the mosaic sidewalk.
Zh: “和马赛克人行道。”

En: Maybe you should DANCE again, huh? That would be mega! It was mega, the way you did that!
Zh: “也许你应该再跳起来，嗯？那样会超级棒的！你的舞姿真的太棒了！”

En: Does it? He looks around, looking for the cold.
Zh: “是吗？”他环顾四周，寻找寒冷的源头。

En: The large-headed youth has closed his eyes, lost in the music. Sensing you, he opens them...
Zh: 那个大脑袋青年闭上了眼睛，沉浸在音乐之中。他感觉到了你，张开了双臂……

En: A dopamine surge accompanies the words. It feels like electricity flowing down your scalp, dissipating into your neck. Feels good, like a spark of life in that moribund sponge you call your body.
Zh: 这些话伴随着多巴胺激增。那感觉就像电流从你的头皮直灌而下，流入你的脖颈。感觉很好，像是给你如同破烂海绵般的身体注入了生命之光。

En: Okay, I'll look into it. In an official capacity. It's up to the police to make the beats go harder. (Take the task.)
Zh: “好的，我会去调查的，以官方的身份。让节奏更有力是警方的责任。”（接受任务。）

En: Alright, I'll see if I come up with something on my own. A citizen investigation -- before the island. (Take the task.)
Zh: “好吧，我看看自己能不能想出什么办法。在上岛之前——来一次公民调查。”（接受任务。）

En: Alright, I'll see if I come up with something on my own. A citizen investigation. (Take the task.)
Zh: “我去看看我能不能以个人身份想出什么办法。就把这当成一个民事调查。”（接受任务。）

En: No, I really don't have time for this. (Don't take the task.)
Zh: “不，我没有时间。”（拒绝任务。）

En: The young man falls silent with appreciation. He even tries to contain his smile, as if it could hinder your investigation.
Zh: 年轻人感激地沉默着，他甚至在抑制自己的笑容，仿佛这会妨碍到你的调查一样。

En: Egg, the music?
Zh: “爱凡客，音乐？”

En: Like that guy from the pawnshop, what was his name? The Bird's Nest Roy.
Zh: 就像那个当铺老板，他叫什么名字？鸟巢罗伊。

En: Could it be? Maybe for him -- you only have a chapter or two left in you. Last of the penultimate, more like...
Zh: “会吗？或许是对他来说——你只剩下一两章内容了。更像是，倒数第二章……

En: Whooh, thank god -- adapters noticeably degrade the sound quality.
Zh: 哇哦，感谢老天——适配器明显降低了声音的质量。

En: Here's an idea -- say what you said before. The exact same thing! OR. Or, wait! Say something that's only marginally different! It will yield a drastically different outcome.
Zh: 有个办法——把你以前说过的话再复述一遍！或者，或者，等等！说一点稍微不同的东西！这将产生截然不同的结果。

En: Your cop training did not prepare you for this. What to do?
Zh: 你没有接受过这方面的警察训练。怎么办？

En: A great excitement is bubbling to the surface within him. This is *big*.
Zh: 他心底涌出一股巨大的喜悦。这是件*大事*。

En: A young man with peroxide blond hair stands behind a mixer deck, nodding along to the music. He looks at you with a knowing smile and says...\n
Zh: 一个漂染了金发的年轻人站在一个调音台背后，随着音乐节拍点头。带着会意的微笑看着你说……

En: A young man with peroxide blond hair holds up a Harmon-Wowshi tape player, nodding along to the music. He looks at you with a knowing smile and says...
Zh: 一个漂染了金发的年轻人正举着一台哈蒙沃什录音机，随着音乐节拍点头。他带着会意的微笑看着你说……

En: Is it?
Zh: “是吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: HARD CORE TO THE MEGA!
Zh: “硬核最强！”

En: Still say nothing.
Zh: 继续沉默。

En: But is it? I mean, really?
Zh: “但是这样吗？我是说，真的硬核吗？”

En: HAAAAAAARD COOORE!!! AAAAAAAIGHH!!! He lets out an agonized roar over the feeble-ish, obviously not too hard core beat below.
Zh: “硬核！！！！嗷啊啊啊啊！！！”他有气无力地发出了一声痛苦的咆哮，显然不够硬核。

En: HARD CORE!
Zh: “硬核！”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: INTERNALLY COHERENT!
Zh: “内部相容！”

En: Still say nothing.
Zh: 继续沉默。

En: IT'S HAAAAAAAAARD CORE!
Zh: “就是硬核！”

En: SO HARD CORE!
Zh: “太硬核了！”

En: No, but seriously... I'm a little worried it isn't.
Zh: “不，但说真的……我有点担心不是。”

En: YEAAGHH!
Zh: “嗷耶！”

En: This young man adds a capital *G* before the *H* in his *YEAGHs* and *AAGHs*. This produces a guttural, *Gottwaldian* accent and makes him sound more animal, more *in it*.
Zh: 这个年轻人在喊出*啊*和*耶*之前都*嗷*了一声。这样就能发出自带喉音的*戈特伍德*口音，让他的吼叫听上去更加狂野，更加*投入*。

En: Or maybe it's not Gottwaldian, maybe it's *Oranjese* -- probably an homage to Oranje, where Arno van Eyck is from, judging by his name. Could you be listening to an Arno van Eyck creation *right now*?
Zh: 或许并不是戈特伍德，可能是*奥兰治*——大概是在向阿诺·凡·艾克的故乡奥兰治致敬，从他的名字来看。你*现在*听到的会是阿诺·凡·艾克的作品吗？

En: Is it, though? He stops dead in his tracks, tilting his head to the side.
Zh: “是吗？”他猛地停了下来，歪着脑袋。

En: The skin on your back is crawling. For a second you can't even hear the music anymore.
Zh: 你背后的皮肤紧绷起来，有那么一秒钟，你甚至听不到音乐了。

En: There is a hawthorn tree on Rue de Saint-Ghislaine, right next to the canal.
Zh: 圣吉莱纳路上有一棵山楂树，紧挨着运河。

En: IT IS!
Zh: “是的！”

En: I was just thinking that a moment ago!
Zh: “我刚才还在想呢！”

En: ONE MIND! ONE SPIRIT! THERE IS NO OTHER WORLD -- THERE IS NO OTHER LIFE!
Zh: “一个思想！一个灵魂！没有第二个世界——没有第二条生命！”

En: The question is, what is the question?
Zh: “问题是，问题是什么？”

En: I am the mic enforcer, I am the chicks checker, YEAGH!
Zh: “我是麦克执行者，我是小鸡检查员，嗷耶！”

En: *El silencio*.
Zh: *沉默*。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Lungs are for love!
Zh: “肺代表爱！”

En: Good to know.
Zh: “很高兴能知道。”

En: Why would lungs be for love?
Zh: “为什么肺能代表爱？”

En: When Dolores Dei was anointed innocence, her lungs started glowing through her body, for the world loved her and she loved it back! YEAGH!
Zh: “当德洛莉丝·黛受膏为无罪者时，她的肺脏发出了透过身体的光芒，因为整个世界都爱她，而她以爱回报了这个世界！耶！”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: I think so.
Zh: “我想是的。”

En: That doesn't sound like a thing that would happen.
Zh: “这听起来不太可能会发生。”

En: But that's what everybody saw. Explain it as you please, but it doesn't really do to just deny it ever happened.
Zh: “但大家都看到了。随便你怎么解释，但你不能否认它曾经发生过。”

En: Oh. Well, it really is him. It's an honest to god van Eyck beat. Well, actually a reworking of a Pepi Popikarnassos track, but let's not get too deep into that whole mess.
Zh: “哦好吧，真的是他，这绝对是凡·艾克的节奏。好吧，实际上是对佩皮·波皮卡纳索斯的旋律的重新编排，但我们就别深究了。”

En: Good, good.
Zh: “很好，很好。”

En: Wow!
Zh: “哇哦！”

En: The skinny wraith looks at you with some disbelief.
Zh: 那个瘦骨嶙峋的幽魂用怀疑的目光看着你。

En: This is Arno van Eyck! ARE YOU READY?
Zh: “这就是阿诺·凡·艾克！你准备好了吗？”

En: Oh yeah. That makes perfect sense.
Zh: “哦耶，这样就说得通了。”

En: LOVE! he suddenly yells and the world seems to stop. "In a woman's lungs! Lonely as I am, I'm not afraid! This strange, damaged feeling grows on and on, 'cause I've never loved someone like you before!"
Zh: “爱！”他突然叫嚷起来，然后世界仿佛都静止了，“在一个女人的肺里！孤独如我，也不畏惧！这种奇异的、破碎的感觉愈发强烈，因为我从未爱过像你这样的人！”

En: Bourgeois love from a bourgeois god-queen towards a world getting rapidly more bourgeois, that's lungs for you.
Zh: “一个资产阶级的女王给予一个急速资产阶级化的世界的资产阶级之爱，这就是你的肺。”

En: Objectively, it was good for its time. And they sort of screwed it up after she passed, like they always do.
Zh: “客观地说，当时是很好。然而她去世后，他们就如往常一样把事情搞砸了。”

En: Don't listen to that retrograde class warrior, Egg.
Zh: “别听那个倒退者的话，爱凡客。”

En: L'amour, la compassion, l'autodiscipline.
Zh: “仁爱、慈悲、自律。”

En: Thank you for the story.
Zh: “谢谢你的故事。”

En: Rhymes that keep their secrets will unfold behind the clouds!
Zh: “隐藏着他们秘密的韵律会在云后揭开！”

En: Why wouldn't they be? Are the lungs not the place where you hold the breath of your soul?
Zh: 怎么不是呢？肺难道不是你的灵魂呼吸的地方吗？

En: Well. The lungs are where you hold the breath of your soul, aren't they?
Zh: “这个嘛，肺是你的灵魂呼吸的地方，不是吗？”

En: Heiligeili!
Zh: “极乐世界！”

En: And a melody. A good melody is what makes the song really *stick*, so that you can't get it out of your head anymore. (Point at your head.)
Zh: “还有旋律。一首好旋律才是让歌曲真正*持久*的秘诀，这样你就不会再把它抛之脑后了。”（指向你的脑袋。）

En: So am I! SO AM I! He begins to shake his head so everyone would understand.
Zh: “我也是！我也是！”他开始摇头好让每个人都明白。

En: Oh! Andre almost falls over backward from the realization. "Is *that* why they call you Egg Head, because..."
Zh: “哦！”安德烈意识到这一点后差点摔倒。“他们就是因为*这个*才叫你爱凡客，因为……”

En: Eyck-Head to the mega! The K became the G! The boy became the man!
Zh: “艾克粉头最强！普通人变成了粉头！男孩长成了男人！”

En: THE ADVENT!
Zh: “降临了！”

En: There's a record store in Jamrock, but that's a bit far away... Maybe some local shopkeeper or handyman can help you fix it?
Zh: “加姆洛克有一家唱片店，但离这里有点距离……或许当地的店家或者杂工能帮你修一下？”

En: Maybe Arno van Eyck lives around here and just threw a part of his song away, because he thought it was shit?
Zh: “也许阿诺·凡·艾克就住在这附近，只是把他的部分歌曲扔掉了，因为他觉得很烂？”

En: Intriguing. The way I see it... van Eyck based his remix on some famous original piece. Like, a folk song? Something local. Seems you found an initial part with the main melody.
Zh: “很有趣，在我看来……凡·艾克的混音作品是根据一些名篇改变的，比如民歌，本地小调之类的。你似乎是找到了主旋律的开头部分。”

En: I think it's just happenstance. Chaos in action. Contingencies of our limited existence. That and Egg Head's fantastic talent. He nods to his friend behind the turntables.
Zh: “我认为这只是个巧合，混乱的行为，有限存在的偶然事件。就像爱凡客的天赋一样。”他朝唱机转盘后面的朋友点了点头。

En: Andre's got it. Sounds like a local song re-mixed.
Zh: “安德烈说对了，听起来就像是本地歌曲的重置版本。”

En: I agree with Noid, it's just luck -- *and* Egg Head's incredible mixing skills.
Zh: “我同意诺伊德，运气好而已——*以及*爱凡客惊人的混音技巧。”

En: But it's all blocked with that stupid traffic jam right now! Anyway...
Zh: “可现在那该死的交通把一切都堵塞了！无论如何……”

En: Basically what you need to find here is a tape with some banging music on it, so that Egg Head could use it to remix van Eyck's jam.
Zh: “基本上，你需要的就是一盘节奏强劲的音乐磁带，这样爱凡客就能用它跟凡·艾克的作品重新混音了。”

En: Yeah. Maybe that streethawker across the pawnshop has got some tapes to sell? That's just an idea.
Zh: “是啊。也许当铺对面那个街头小贩有磁带卖？只是有个想法。”

En: Anyway... That's all yours to figure out, cop-man.
Zh: “总而言之……这都是你要解决的问题了，警官。”

En: COP-MAN! yells Egg Head.
Zh: “警官！”爱凡客大叫着。

En: What about the bass? Do you have any ideas for that? Andre looks back at you.
Zh: “那低音呢？你有什么想法吗？”安德烈看着你。

En: What if... we used that crazy sound assault from Soona's experiment, but contained, tamed it -- made it pulse? (Proceed with task.)
Zh: “不如……我们就用苏娜实验性的疯狂冲击声吧，但要调和一下——让它跃动起来。”（继续任务。）

En: Honestly, nothing springs to my mind right now. But I'll see if I can come up with a solution down the lane. (Proceed with task.)
