Zh: “老实说，我现在什么也想不起来。但我会试试能否想出一个解决方案的。”（继续任务。）

En: I think it's good enough the way it is. Adding that melody has already been a *massive* improvement. It's pretty hard core now. (Finish task.)
Zh: “我认为这样就够了，加上那段旋律已经是个*巨大的*改善了。现在已经很硬核了。”（结束任务。）

En: Yeah, I remember -- you said it needs more bass!
Zh: “没错，我想起来了——你说还需要更多低音！”

En: Ooh. Ooh! Egg Head's puzzled face turns into a wicked grin. "But how?!"
Zh: “哦，哦！”他困惑的脸挂上了邪恶的笑容。“但要如何做呢？”

En: What do we have around here to achieve some sort of... *parallel processing*...
Zh: 我们现在正在进行某种……*并行处理*……

En: What about that compressor Andre was setting up... to achieve some sort of *parallel processing*.
Zh: 那安德烈正在安装的压缩机……正在进行某种*并行处理*……

En: You're *THE WARRIOR*! THE WARRIOR OF DANCE MUSIC!
Zh: “你是*战士*！舞曲的战士！”

En: Don't be too hard on yourself if you don't figure it out. I think the jam's already pretty ultra.
Zh: “如果你没能弄明白，也别太苛责自己了。我想这首作品已经相当厉害了。”

En: But it could be hyper, HYPER HARD CORE!
Zh: “但它还能更加，更加硬核！”

En: HARD CORE! Not exactly the hyper that I was hoping for, but... it's definitely ULTRA!
Zh: “硬核！不完全是我希望的那种超凡，但……这绝对是极致的！”

En: I think you did an amazing job here, detective. I would have never thought that van Eyck could be improved on -- but you did it, you made it even better than before.
Zh: “我认为你非常出色，警探先生。我从未想过凡·艾克还能继续精进——但你做到了，比之前的还要完美。”

En: Yeah, plus it's good to know your limits.
Zh: “没错，另外能知道自己的极限也很好。”

En: NO LIMITS ON THE DANCEFLOOR!
Zh: “舞池之中没有限制！”

En: Chill, my man. You'll get there -- eventually.
Zh: 冷静点，老兄。你可以的——最后肯定可以。

En: Oh no, please become one... (He didn't even notice you mentioning some 'island'.) "You'll miss out on so much good content otherwise. It's like being the greatest cop on earth!"
Zh: “哦，不，请变成一个……”（他甚至都没有注意到你提到了某个‘小岛’。）“不然你会错过很多好内容的。感觉就像是变成这个世界上最棒的警察！”

En: Wait, they want you to run all the way back to Martinaise with that leg of yours, just to get some 'tape'? Ouch.
Zh: 等等，他们希望你用自己的两条腿一路跑回马丁内斯，就为了找盘‘磁带’？好痛。

En: It's your health -- you're not well enough to run around just days after you've been shot.
Zh: 这是你的健康问题——你的状态不太好，没办法在中枪几天之后就到处跑。

En: I think you might know the answer...
Zh: 我想你也许知道答案……

En: You can't just leave it without a bass track!
Zh: 你不能在没找到低音音轨的情况下就这么离开！

En: Noid's right, Egg Head's technical talent is the key.
Zh: 诺伊德是对的，爱凡客的天才技术就是关键。

En: Something *mysterious* is going on here...
Zh: 这里有些很*神秘*的事情……

En: Yeagh, re-mix time! His voice booms through the church as he takes the tape and attaches it to the empty reel slot. "Tape goes here -- into deck B." He clicks a switch, the tape starts spinning...
Zh: “哦耶，混音时间到！”他的声音响彻整个教堂，他拿起磁带，把它放进空的卷轴槽上。“磁带放这里——B面。”他按下开关，磁带开始旋转……

En: Hear that? He grins with glee. "Sounds like the missing part of van Eyck's jam!"
Zh: “听见了吗？”他高兴地笑了。“听起来就像凡·艾克作品不见的一部分！”

En: Whooh, hear that?! He wipes his brow. "The sines match perfectly!"
Zh: “哇哦，听见了吗？！”他擦了擦眉头。“正弦波能够完美匹配！”

En: Noid's right, Egg Head's technical talent is the key.
Zh: 诺伊德是对的，爱凡客的天才技术就是关键。

En: No, this is definitely part of the same song. Something cut from it. It fits too well.
Zh: 不，这绝对是同一首歌的一部分。某段剪辑。实在是太适合了。

En: Something *mysterious* is going on here...
Zh: 这里有些很*神秘*的事情……

En: Hey... Acele looks up from her microphone. "What did you do to Egg Head, cop-man? Did you *break* him?"
Zh: “嘿……”阿西尔从麦克上抬起头。“你对爱凡客做了什么，警官？你把他*弄坏*了吗？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Uh, no, actually I found it tangled up in a hawthorn tree...
Zh: “呃，不是，其实我是在一棵山楂树上找到的……”

En: Listen, I'm just going to show it to you... Ready?
Zh: “听着，我正准备给你看呢……准备好了吗？”

En: Ready.
Zh: “好了。”

En: I'm... not sure.
Zh: “我……不确定。”

En: Just wait and see. (Nod at Egg Head.)
Zh: “等着瞧好了吧。”（朝爱凡客点头。）

En: ARE YOU READY, POSSE?!
Zh: “老铁们都准备好了吗？！”

En: Give him a thumbs up.
Zh: 朝他竖起大拇指。

En: I was born ready, Egg!
Zh: “我生下来就准备好了，爱凡客！”

En: Noid straightens his back, ready for the beat...
Zh: 诺伊德挺直腰板，等待着节奏……

En: Dolorian church, the place to be! Make some noise, my church people! Large speakers are set up behind the young man, blasting a familiar song.
Zh: “德洛莉丝教堂，快出发吧！发出声音，我的教友们！”年轻人的身后摆放了一个大扬声器，正放着一首熟悉的歌曲。

En: It's good you're not a communist anyway. You should be an ultraliberal.
Zh: “还好你不是康米主义者，你应该成为一名极端自由主义者。”

En: Communists are pansies. Can't fight invaders with internationalist babble. You need the strength of a pure nation.
Zh: “康米主义者就是一群娘炮，国际主义空谈是无法对抗侵略者的，你们需要一个国家的力量。”

En: Best not to be a communist. Having extreme views on issues is detrimental to understanding all sides.
Zh: “还是不要成为康米主义者为好，持有极端观点并不利于全面理解问题。”

En: I can be an ultra! He nods. "If you want that -- do you want me to be an ultra?!"
Zh: “我可以成为极端分子！”他点点头。“如果你是这么希望的话——你希望我成为一个极端分子吗？！”

En: Please don't turn him into an ultraliberal.
Zh: “请不要把他变成一个极端自由主义者。”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess Egg Head is an ultraliberal now.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！”他叫嚷着，你想爱凡客现在是个极端自由主义者了。

En: Oh geez, please don't do that. There's enough exploitation in the world.
Zh: “天哪，请不要那么做。世界上的剥削已经足够多了。”

En: Don't be an ultraliberal. That path is *too* hard core.
Zh: “不要做极端自由主义者，那条路*太过*硬核了。”

En: Be an ultraliberal, Egg Head. Capital needs your help.
Zh: “成为极端自由主义者吧，爱凡客，资本需要你的帮助。”

En: Don't be an ultra... wait, what am I saying? Of course be an ultraliberal!
Zh: “不要成为极端自由主义者……等等，我在说什么呢？当然要成为极端自由主义者啊！”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess Egg Head won't become an ultraliberal after all.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！！！”他叫嚷着，你想他终究不会成为极端自由主义者的。

En: I can do that! He nods. "If you want that -- I can be a conduit for the mystical nationhood!"
Zh: “我可以的！”他点点头。“如果你是这么希望的话——我可以成为那个神秘国度的渠道！”

En: Please don't turn him into a fascist.
Zh: “请不要把他变成一个法西斯主义者。”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess Egg Head is a fascist now.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！！！”他叫嚷着，你想爱凡客现在是个法西斯主义者了。

En: Oh yeah, sure, this is *exactly* what we need.
Zh: “哦太好了，这*正是*我们需要的。”

En: Don't be a fascist. That path is *too* hard core.
Zh: “不要做法西斯主义者，那条路*太过*硬核了。”

En: Be a fascist, Egg Head. Our future needs your help.
Zh: “成为法西斯主义者吧，爱凡客，我们的未来需要你的帮助。”

En: Don't be a fascist... wait, what am I saying? Of course be a fascist!
Zh: “不要成为法西斯主义者……等等，我在说什么呢？当然要成为法西斯主义者啊！”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess Egg Head won't become a fascist after all.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！！！”他叫嚷着，你想他终究不会成为法西斯主义者的。

En: Oh sure, I can do that! He nods. "If you want that -- I can avoid taking a stand!"
Zh: “当然，我可以的！”他点点头。“如果你是这么希望的话——我可以不再表明立场！”

En: Please don't turn him into a moralist.
Zh: “请不要把他变成一个道德主义者。”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess Egg Head is a moralist now.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！！！”他叫嚷着，你想爱凡客现在是个道德主义者了。

En: I don't... even know what to say to that.
Zh: “我甚至……不知道要如何开口了。”

En: Don't be a moralist. That path requires a sensible examination of all nuances unattainable to most people.
Zh: “不要成为道德主义者，那条路需要对大多数人无法觉察的细微差别进行理性审视。”

En: Be a moralist, Egg Head. The balance needs your help.
Zh: “成为道德主义者吧，爱凡客，平衡需要你的帮助。”

En: Don't be a moralist... wait, what am I saying? You should consider your choice carefully and rationally.
Zh: “不要成为道德主义者……等等，我在说什么呢？你应该小心理性地考虑你的选择。”

En: AIIIIIGH!!! he yells. You guess Egg Head won't become a moralist after all.
Zh: “嗷啊啊啊！！！”他叫嚷着，你想爱凡客终究不会成为道德主义者的。

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: You're wrong. Capital doesn't rule the nation.
Zh: “你错了，资本并不能统治国家。”

En: The young man drops his oversized head in shame. He is truly sorry he couldn't come up with a profitable market strategy.
Zh: 那个年轻人羞愧地低下了他那过大的脑袋。他真的很难过自己未能想出一个有利可图的市场策略。

En: THE MARKET REGULATES ITSELF!
Zh: “资产是会自我调节的！”

En: No, that's still not it.
Zh: “不，还是不行。”

En: Superb!
Zh: “好极了！”

En: Almost there. Could use just a bit more consolidation, a tad less regulation...
Zh: “差不多了，可以再多一些整合，少一些监管……”

En: EAT THE POOR!
Zh: “消除贫困！”

En: Right on, Lord Raubritter!
Zh: “正是，瑞伯特尔勋爵！”

En: You swear you feel the profit margins reflecting off Germaine Egg Head's giant smile.
Zh: 你发誓自己能感觉到从杰曼尼·爱凡客的巨大笑容里反映出的利润率。

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: You're wrong. The nation can't bend all to its will.
Zh: “你错了，国家是不能完全屈从于自己的意志的。”

En: The young man drops his oversized head in shame. He is truly sorry he couldn't come up with a heart-felt yell that would satisfy The Great Leader.
Zh: 这位青年羞愧地垂下了那过大的脑袋。他没想出一句感人肺腑，能够让伟大领袖满意的口号，这令他感到惭愧。

En: WELCOME TO REVACHOL!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: No, that's still not it.
Zh: “不，还是不行。”

En: Welcome to Revachol!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: Almost there. Could use just a bit more heart...
Zh: “差不多了，可以再发自内心一些……”

En: WELCOME TO REVACHOL! WELCOME TO REVACHOL! MYSTICAL NATIONHOOD!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！神秘的国度！”

En: Welcome to Revachol!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: The bright future of Revachol reflects off Germaine Egg Head's giant smile. A world of Revacholian domination, where its shores are bereft of ill influence.
Zh: 杰曼尼·爱凡客巨大的笑容中折射出瑞瓦肖光明的未来，一个完全由瑞瓦肖人主宰的世界，这里的海岸并没有被邪恶侵蚀。

En: INCREMENTAL CHANGE!
Zh: “增量变化！”

En: Appropriate.
Zh: “很恰当。”

En: Almost there. Could use just a bit more... something...
Zh: “差不多了，可以再多……一些……”

En: I'm swiftly moving toward a solution which pleases nobody!
Zh: “我正迅速地迈向一个谁也不喜欢的方案！”

En: Obviously. One shouldn't get too hasty with their decision-making.
Zh: “显然，人不应该草率地做决定。”

En: You feel Germaine Egg Head's smile is too enthusiastic, but it'll have to do, for now.
Zh: 你感觉杰曼尼·爱凡客的笑容过于热情，但现在也只能这样了。

En: Yeah...
He stands on stage behind a table, nodding along to the music and waving his hand in the air. In front of him -- the audio mixer, one reel spinning.
Zh: 他站在桌子后面的舞台上随着音乐点头，并在空中挥着手。在他面前的——是一台正在运转的混音器。

En: A hawthorn tree on Rue de Saint-Ghislaine. Tangled in its branches, something bronze flutters in the wind...
Zh: 圣吉莱纳路上有一棵山楂树。它的树枝上缠绕着某种东西，某种青铜色的东西在风中飘扬……

En: Good morning! YEEAAAHHH! He waves his hand in the air. "Harder core!" The words echo magnificently throughout the nave.
Zh: “早上好！耶耶耶！”他挥了挥手。“硬核！”这些话在中殿恢弘地回荡起来。

En: Vibrations thump through your blue soul. The music sounds much better in the church.
Zh: 振动敲打着你的忧郁的灵魂，这音乐在教堂里听起来好听多了。

En: For a moment you almost think he's going to put his hand down. But that would be ludicrous.
Zh: 有那么一瞬间你几乎以为他要把手放下来了，但那未免也太可笑了。

En: But there's something else that hasn't failed you...
Zh: 但还有一样东西没有让你失望……

En: Oh no, please become one... you'll miss out on so much good content otherwise. It's like being the greatest cop on earth!
Zh: “哦不，成为他吧……否则你会错过很多精彩的，这就好像是成为世界上最伟大的警察一样！”

En: Please. Please? The young man smiles at you widely, bright and innocent as the summer sun.
Zh: “拜托，拜托？”那个年轻人对你灿烂地笑着，如同夏日的太阳一般无罪。

En: His pleaful smile is disarming, but you can withstand it's glorious assault, if you just put your heart into it.
Zh: 他恳求的笑容能够让人卸下心防。但只要你的意志坚定，就能抵挡住这般灿烂的攻击。

En: You don't have time for this nonsense. You've got safe boring things to do.
Zh: 你没时间管这些破事，你还有更加安全无趣的事情要做呢。

En: How do you like it in the church?
Zh: “你觉得在教堂里怎么样？”

En: Yeeaaaagh! Back on the case, no disgrace! He pumps his fist in the air. "Bring it down to party place!"
Zh: “嗷耶！继续破案，不要丢人！”他扬起拳头。“带来派对现场！”

En: THE FIRST PAGE OF THE SECOND CHAPTER! His voice echoes all around the grand hall.
Zh: “第二章第一页！”他的声音在大堂里回响。

En: I got the tape fixed. Maybe we can use it now. (Give him the fixed hawthrone tree tape.)
Zh: “爱凡客！我把磁带修好了。也许现在能用了。”（将修好的山楂树磁带给他。）

En: I found this reel of tape, maybe you can use it to hard-up Eyck's jam. (Give him the fixed hawthrone tree tape.)
Zh: “我找到了这卷磁带，或许你可以把它跟凡·艾克的演奏重新合成一下。”（将修好的山楂树磁带给他。）

En: Hey, I have a tape with me, maybe you can use it to improve van Eyck's jam.
Zh: “嘿，我这里有一盘带子，或许你可以用它来提升凡·艾克的即兴演奏。”

En: But... it needs to be played, on the turntable. And it's broken. Seeing this abuse of music brings a twinge of sadness to his face.
Zh: “可是……它得在唱机转盘上播放。而且它坏掉了。”目睹音乐被如此粗暴地对待，他的脸上流露出悲伤。

En: Wait, what do you mean?
Zh: “等等，你是什么意思？”

En: Alright, where can I get it repaired?
Zh: “好吧，哪里能修它呢？”

En: Look around! You gotta help it, help it survive. Can't leave the tune like this. He hands back the bundle of magnetic tape.
Zh: “四处找找吧！你必须得帮它，拯救它，不能放着不管。”他把那捆磁带递了回来。

En: Tape goes around spool. I spin the world 'round, with my mic in the ground! Yeeaaaahhh!
Zh: “磁带绕着卷轴转，我的麦克在地上，让世界旋转！耶！”

En: Tape, yeah! He grins. "Spin the tape until the space escape, yeah!"
Zh: “磁带，哦耶！”他咧嘴一笑。“转动磁带，直到空间逃亡，哦耶！”

En: I got you this banging Megamix. (Give him the Great Doorgunner Megamix.)
Zh: “我给你带了个炸裂的精选集。”（给他门枪手精选集。）

En: What about this one? (Give him the Smallest Church in Saint-Saëns.)
Zh: “这个呢？”（给他圣桑小教堂。）

En: Aiiight! He snatches the tape from your hand and attaches it to the empty reel slot. One hand on his headphones, he listens to the audio. Then, shaking his head, he says:
Zh: “好的！”他从你手里抢过带子，把它接到空的卷轴槽上。他一只手搭在耳机上听着音乐，然后摇着头说：

En: Righteous! He takes the tape and attaches it to the empty reel slot. Hand over ear, he listens, then shakes his head:
Zh: “好极了！”他接过带子，把它接到空的卷轴槽上。他一只手捂住耳朵听着音乐，然后摇着头说：

En: No. No, no, this is gonna make people scared! He hands the tape back to you. "Keep it positive, keep the *love* in the house!"
Zh: “不，不，不，这会把人们吓跑的！”他把带子还给了你。“积极一点，让房间里充满*爱*！”

En: No. No, can't do, this is gonna make people sad! This isn't sad-core. He hands the tape back to you.
Zh: “不，不，不行，这会让人们伤心的！这不叫硬核。”他把磁带还给了你。

En: I found this over at downtown Martinaise. (Give him the bundle of magnetic tape.)
Zh: “我是在马丁内斯市中心找到的这个。”（把那团磁交给他。）

En: Okay, never mind.
Zh: “好吧，没关系。”

En: Yeagh, re-mix time! His voice booms through the church as he takes the tape and attaches it to the empty reel slot. "Tape goes here -- into deck B." He clicks a switch, the tape starts spinning...
Zh: “哦耶，混音时间到！”他的声音响彻整个教堂，他拿起磁带，把它放进空的卷轴槽上。“磁带放这里——B面。”他按下开关，磁带开始旋转……

En: A hand on his ear, he listens to the audio through his headphones, and shouts...
Zh: 他一只手捂住耳朵，用耳机听着音乐，然后大叫着：

En: Wow... His face lights up with delight. "Did you get this from Arno himself?"
Zh: “哇哦……”他高兴地满脸通红。“这是你从阿诺本人那里拿到的吗？”

En: Whooh, hear that?! He wipes his brow. "The sines match perfectly!"
Zh: “哇，你听到了吗？”他擦了擦额头。“正弦波完全匹配上了！”

En: The sines match perfectly!
Zh: “正弦波完全匹配上了！”

En: No, this is definitely part of the same song. Something cut from it. It fits too well.
Zh: 不，这绝对是同一首歌的一部分。某段剪辑。实在是太适合了。

En: What is this? Acele looks up from her contact mic, stunned. "It sounds *so good*. The way melody and bass flow together... how did you make it so perfect?"
Zh: “这是什么？”阿西尔震惊地从她的接触麦克风上抬起头。“听起来*太棒了*，旋律与低音相互交融……你们是如何做到如此完美的？”

En: What is this? Acele looks up from her contact mic. "It's *good*. How did you guys do that?"
Zh: “这是什么？”阿西尔从她的接触麦克风上抬起头。“真是*不错*，你们是怎么做到的？”

En: Do what? I just found a random tape and brought it to Egg Head. He's just incredibly good at remixing.
Zh: “做什么？我只是随手找到了一盘磁带交给爱凡客，而他正好非常擅长混音。”

En: You're right, it's uncanny how well it all goes together. Something else must be going on here...
Zh: “你说得对，这一切配合得如此之好真是太不可思议了，其中一定另有隐情……”

En: Yes, but what if van Eyck based his remix on some forgotten local melody -- like a folk song? And you just found the original piece that inspired him to create this jam. That would explain why it fits so well.
Zh: “没错，但如果凡·艾克是基于一段不为人知的本地小调做出的混音带呢——民歌之类的？而你只是找到了激发他创作灵感的原版作品，这就能解释为何它们如此契合了。”

En: Nah, to me it sounds like classic van Eyck, I don't think he needs any 'inspiration' from folk songs. Maybe he lives in Martinaise and just threw away a part of his song because he thought it wasn't good enough?
Zh: “得了，在我听来这就是经典的凡·艾克作品，我不认为他需要从民歌中获得任何*灵感*。也许他就住在马丁内斯，然后随手扔掉了一部分他认为不够好的作品？”

En: I think it's just happenstance. Chaos in action. Contingencies of our limited existence. That and Egg Head's fantastic talent. He nods to his friend behind the turntables.
Zh: “我认为这只是个巧合，混乱的行为，有限存在的偶然事件。就像爱凡客的天赋一样。”他朝唱机转盘后面的朋友点了点头。

En: Andre's got it. Sounds like a local folk song re-mixed.
Zh: “安德烈说对了，听起来就像是本地民歌的重置版本。”

En: Acele's right, van Eyck must live around here. It's definitely his creation.
Zh: “阿西尔说得对，凡·艾克一定就住在附近，这绝对是他的作品。”

En: Be how it may -- if it fits, it fits! He pumps his fist in the air. "Bring up the volume!"
Zh: “无论如何——适合就适合！”他扬起拳头。“提高音量！”

En: I've thought about the discarded melody and came to the conclusion that van Eyck has lived around here.
Zh: “我思考了一下那段被丢弃的旋律，得出的结论是凡·艾克就住在附近。”

En: J'adore! He turns a knob on his mixer. "God is close by, but maybe he doesn't have good enough ears. Let me turn it up, so we can lure him here."
Zh: “真我！”他转动着混音器的旋钮。“神就在这附近，但或许他的耳朵不太好。我来提高音量，好把他引到这儿来。”

En: THIS IS BEAUTY, THIS IS LIFE!
Zh: “这就是美，这就是生活！”

En: The audio onslaught can be tamed. Connect the dots.
Zh: 音频的冲击可以被调和，连接这些点。

En: Andre's compression algorithm.
Zh: 安德烈的压缩算法。

En: Fiddled with some knobs.
Zh: 摆弄些旋钮。

En: Lied about secret audio tech.
Zh: 撒谎拥有秘密音频技术。

En: Solved Arno Van Eyck.
Zh: 解决了阿诺·凡·艾克。

En: Egg, can you please route Soona's signal through your speakers?
Zh: “爱凡客，能请你把苏娜的信号通过你的扬声器传出来吗？”

En: Whoa, that was a crazy sound we heard before.
Zh: “哇哦，我们之前听到的声音真是疯狂。”

En: To the mega! Yeagh! He gets serious. "I can route it through auxilliary -- what kind of a cable does she use? 3.5 or 4.5?"
Zh: “当然可以！嗷耶！”他认真起来。“我可以用辅助器——她用是那根线？3.5还是4.5的？”

En: Yeah! It was awesome, and scary. Very hard core! His voice booms through the chamber, then gets silent.
Zh: “没错！酷炫又可怕，非常硬核！他的声音响彻整个房间，然后沉寂下来。”

En: Too hard core, in fact. He brushes his hand through his hair. "I couldn't control it. At all!"
Zh: “实际上，不行。”他用手梳理着头发。“我完全无法控制它。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么了？”

En: It sucks up all the air in the mix until its the only thing left! Starts compressing itself and everything around it. Completely fills up the headspace. Extreme!
Zh: “它把混音器里抽成了真空，然后开始压缩自身与周围的一切。完全填满了顶部空间，绝了！”

En: What if this is the answer to van Eyck's jam? We could use the sound for the bass...
Zh: “如果这就是凡·艾克作品的答案呢？我们这个用这个声音作为低音部……”

En: Inside your chest, the heart beats still with the after-effects of the sound. Imagine if you could harness that power... making it pulse.
Zh: 在你的胸腔，心脏随着声音的余韵震动着。想象一下如果你能驾驭这股力量……让它跳动起来。

En: Ooh. Ooh! His puzzled face turns into a wicked grin. "But how?!"
Zh: “哦，哦！”他困惑的脸挂上了邪恶的笑容。“但要如何做呢？”

En: What about the compressor the one with the spiky hair was setting up... to achieve some sort of *parallel processing*...
Zh: 那个留着尖尖头发的人正在安装的压缩机呢……正在进行某种*并行处理*……

En: What do we have around here to achieve some sort of... *parallel processing*...
Zh: 我们现在正在进行某种……*并行处理*……

En: SIDE-CHAIN THE BEAT!
Zh: 旁链节拍！

En: *Bzoot-bzoot-bzoot* goes the track in the background, never-ending. You can't grasp what's missing. But you can try to make something up anyway.
Zh: *嘭-嘭-嘭*的背景音没有宁日，你无法捕捉到错过的东西，但你可以试着编造一些出来。

En: *Side-chain*? What does it even mean?
Zh: *旁链*？那究竟是什么意思？

En: Is there anything we can do about it?
Zh: “我们能做点什么吗？”

En: Ooh. His mind is seemingly flipping through the options. "I don't know. Maybe *someone* can do something with this. I imagine this is the sound that the future could make."
Zh: “哦哦。”他似乎正在头脑中翻阅着诸多选项。“不知道啊。说不定*有人*能用这个做出什么。按我的想象，这就是未来会发出的声音。”

En: Whooh, thump-thump-thump, maestro! The speedfreak waves his hand in the air.
Zh: “嚯，砰—砰—砰，大师！”瘾君子挥着手。

En: Listen, you can use the compressor to select between which track it's compressing, either the auxiliary signal or the main input from the tape. Make it alternate between the signals.
Zh: 听着，你可以用压缩机来选择压缩中的音轨，无论是辅助信号或者磁带作为主输入。让它在信号之间交替。

En: The compressor controls the gain based on the level of the signal on the aux side-chain input. It will allow maintaining a loud sound without peaks that fill up all the headspace.
Zh: 压缩机根据辅助旁链输入信号的水平来控制增益。这样就能允许保持高音，无需填补所有顶部空间的峰值。

En: Okay. Egg, you need to start side-chaining it. (Explain the concept to him.)
Zh: “够了，爱凡客，你得开始旁链了。”（向他解释这个概念。）

En: I don't know the technical term, but it needs less *bzoot-bzoot*.
Zh: “我不知道专业术语，但它不需要那么多*嘭-嘭*。”

En: Side-chaining it, you said? He turns down the music, his hands moving deftly across the mixer, setting up the necessary controls...
Zh: “开始旁链，你是说？”他把音乐声调小，双手灵巧地在混音器上移动着，设置各种控制键……

En: Then he puts on his headphones and his eyes go wide, wider than they've ever gone on drugs. He starts jumping up and down with bliss, in total silence -- still listening to his headphones.
Zh: 然后他戴上耳机，双眼大睁，比吸毒的时候还要扩散。他开始在一片寂静中兴奋地上蹿下跳——还在听耳机里的音乐。

En: What in the world is going on? Acele looks on, amazed at the display. "The way melody and bass flow together... it's unnatural."
Zh: “到底发生什么事了？”阿西尔在一旁看着，被眼前的景象惊呆了。“旋律与低音交融的感觉……很不自然。”

En: What in the world is going on? Acele looks on, amazed at the display.
Zh: “到底发生什么事了？”阿西尔在一旁看着，被眼前的景象惊呆了。

En: We have... tamed the sound. Made it our own.
Zh: “我们……调和了声音，让它成为了我们自己的东西。”

En: The hard core aesthetic just got more bassy.
Zh: “硬核美学的低音加剧了。”

En: God damn it, you hear Andre say to himself over the thumping beat, "this dance club idea might just work out."
Zh: “该死的，”你听到安德烈在沉重的节拍中自言自语，“舞蹈俱乐部的主意还真有可能成功。”

En: This is wild, the sines are off the charts.
Zh: “这太疯狂了，正弦波已经破表了。”

En: Hear that? He grins with glee. "Sounds like the missing part of van Eyck's jam!"
Zh: “听到了吗？”他高兴地笑着。“听上去就像是凡·艾克的作品遗失的部分！”

En: THIS ONE IS FOR THE ANODIC GENERATION!
Zh: “这是为了阳极一代准备的！”

En: Your body is taking a beating from the low frequencies crashing over you. It's making you feel... alive again.
Zh: 你的身体正承受着低频率的冲撞，这让你感觉……重获新生。

En: INTRODUCING THE ULTIMATE SOUND!
Zh: “终极效果登场！”

En: DOLORIAN CHURCH -- THE PLACE TO BE! Egg's losing himself in the sound. "Pump it, pump it!"
Zh: “德洛莉丝教堂——快快出发！”爱凡客在音乐中迷失了自己。“一起来！一起来！”

En: I agree with Noid, it's just luck -- *and* Egg Head's incredible mixing skills.
Zh: “我同意诺伊德，运气好而已——*以及*爱凡客惊人的混音技巧。”

En: The Horn of Apocalypse. It draweth near.
Zh: “末日号角，快要降临了。”

En: The audio assault is glorious. The speedfreak dances on the stage, intensely waving his hand in the air.
Zh: 音频的冲击极其恢弘，这个瘾君子在舞台上手舞足蹈，热情地挥舞着双手。

En: This is it... this is a new era. The fabric of the world has been irrevocably altered.
Zh: 就是这样……这是个崭新的时代，世界的架构已经不可逆转地改变了。

En: Who will be the innocence of hard-core anodic dance music?
Zh: 谁会成为硬核阳极舞曲的无罪者？

En: This is it... this is a new era. The fabric of the world has been irrevocably altered.
Zh: 就是这样……这是个崭新的时代，世界的架构已经不可逆转地改变了。

En: Who will be the innocence of hyper-hard-core anodic dance music?
Zh: 谁会成为超硬核阳极舞曲的无罪者？

En: Great, someone got through to him. Okay, let's get it all set up. Can we turn the music off, please?
Zh: “太好了，有人搞定他了。好的，大家都做好准备，我们能把音乐关掉吗？”

En: The Arno will be back! But we're doing *something else* for one moment. He turns to you. "Alright, go tell her that Egg Head is ready to rave to her *tunes* -- and then I'll turn off the music!"
Zh: “阿诺即将归来！但我们目前还在做*别的*事情。”他转向你。“好吧，去告诉她，爱凡客已经准备好跟随她的*好音乐*狂欢了——然后我会把音乐关掉！”

En: YEAAAGH! Welcome to the Gloaming! He grins.
Zh: “嗷耶！欢迎来到黄昏！”他笑着说。

En: The other reel deck is empty. Cables run hither and thither. On one side, you see an auxiliary line-in with the number 4.5 written next to it.
Zh: 另一个卷轴台空着，缆线到处都是，另外一边，你看到一条辅助线上面写着数字4.5。

En: It's 3.5, no doubt about it. It's on the ground. (Point to it.)
Zh: “毫无疑问这是3.5，在地板上。”（指着它。）

En: Oh... oh... she uses 3.5... An uncomfortable pause follows. "Yeah, the auxiliary line-in is 4.5 mm. These two don't mix."
Zh: “哦……哦……她用的是3.5……”紧接着是一阵令人不安的沉默。“是啊，辅助线是4.5毫米的。这两者不兼容。”

En: Oh no. The lieutenant closes his eyes. "We're going to be in this church *forever*."
Zh: “哦不。”警督闭上了眼睛。“我们要*永远*呆在这座教堂里了。”

En: Oh god, you're going to be in this church forever. FOREVER.
Zh: 哦天哪，你要永远呆在这座教堂里了，永远。

En: Don't worry, I have an *adapter* for it right here! He searches for the cable on the ground and picks it up, looking at the jack.
Zh: “别担心，我这儿有个*适配器*！”他找到地上的缆线拿起来，看着插座。

En: Hang on -- this *is* a 4.5! We're all good, people! With a grin he sticks the plug into the auxiliary line-in. You hear a satisfying click.
Zh: “等等——这*就是*4.5的。没事了，大家！”他咧嘴一下笑，把插头插进了辅助线路。你听到了令人满意的咔哒声。

En: Everybody, everybody! Don't panic, I'm going to turn off the Arno for *just a sec*, the young man shouts as he clicks a switch on the mixer, "for a *special scheduled event*..."
Zh: “大家，大家！别慌，我*马上*就把阿诺关掉，”年轻人边喊边按下了混音器的按键。“为了一个*特别安排的活动*……”

En: Sounds like a horrible future.
Zh: “听上去未来堪忧。”

En: Sounds like the future could be even more hard core than I ever imagined.
Zh: “听上去未来可能比我想象的还要硬核。”

En: Can't escape the sound, can't escape the future!
Zh: “无法逃避声音，无法逃出未来！”

En: YEEEAAAGH!
Zh: “嗷耶耶耶！”

En: Good morning, comrade! Yeaaaaaah! He waves his hand in the air. "Harder core!" The words echo magnificently throughout the nave.
Zh: “早安，同志们！耶耶耶！”他挥着手。“更加硬核！”这些话在中殿恢弘地回荡起来。

En: Welcome to Revachol! Yeaaaaaah! He waves his hand in the air. "Harder core!" The words echo magnificently throughout the nave.
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！耶耶耶！”他挥着手。“更加硬核！”这些话在中殿恢弘地回荡起来。

En: Eat the poor! Yeaaaaaah! He waves his hand in the air. "Harder core!" The words echo magnificently throughout the nave.
Zh: “消除贫困！耶耶耶！”他挥着手。“更加硬核！”这些话在中殿恢弘地回荡起来。

En: A better deal! Yeaaaaaah! He waves his hand in the air. "Medium core!" The words echo magnificently throughout the nave.
Zh: “更好的交易！耶耶耶！”他挥着手。“中核！”这些话在中殿恢弘地回荡起来。

En: Good morning, comrade! Yeaaaaaah! he shouts, apparently unaware of the time of day.
Zh: “早安，同志们！耶耶耶！”他叫嚷着，显然没有意识到现在的时间。

En: Good morning, comrade! Yeaaaaaah!
Zh: “早安，同志们！耶耶耶！”

En: Good morning, tycoon! Yeaaaaaah! he shouts, apparently unaware of the time of day.
Zh: “早安，企业大亨！耶耶耶！”他叫嚷着，显然没有意识到现在的时间。

En: Good morning, tycoon! Yeaaaaaah!
Zh: “早安，企业大亨！耶耶耶！”

En: Welcome to Revachol! Yeaaaaaah!
Zh: “欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！耶耶耶！”

En: Incremental progress! Yeaaaaaah!
Zh: “循序渐进！耶耶耶！”

En: Or maybe it's not Gottwaldian, maybe it's *Oranjese* -- probably an homage to Oranje, where Arno van Eyck is from, judging by his name. Could you be listening to an Arno van Eyck creation *right now*?
Zh: 或许也不是戈特伍德，而是*奥兰治*——大概是在向奥兰治致敬，从他的姓名来看，阿诺·凡·艾克就来自奥兰治。你*现在*听到的会是阿诺·凡·艾克的创作吗？

En: He furrows his brow as his very large head traces the sublime invisible movement of the music in the very real air of the church...
Zh: 他皱着眉头，用他那硕大无比的脑袋，在教堂的真空中，描摹着音乐无形的崇高乐章……

En: He furrows his brow as his very large head traces the sublime invisible movement of the music in the very real air of the stuffy tent...
Zh: 他皱着眉头，用他那硕大无比的脑袋，在闷热帐篷的真空中，描摹着音乐无形的崇高乐章……

En: Now... if only we had the melody for the *full effect*!
Zh: “现在……要是我们能有*最完美*的旋律就好了！”

En: Now if only we had the beat for the *full assault*. It would be unbelievably hyper!
Zh: “要是我们现在能有*最具冲击力*的节拍就好了，那将是难以想象的狂欢！”

En: Don't listen to him, the man yells over the music. "He's making shit up. There's no top-secret cop audio tech -- there's other top-secret-spying-on-people tech most likely, but cops don't care about *music*."
Zh: “别听他的，”一个男人在音乐声中喊道。“他在胡扯，根本没有绝密的警用音频科技——或许会有某种高级间谍科技吧，但警察根本不在乎*音乐*。”

En: Isn't that what *real* artists do? Make it up as they go along?
Zh: 这难道不是*真正的*艺术家常做的吗？在前行的过程中不断弥补过去。

En: Whereof one cannot speak, one ought to remain silent.
Zh: 对于不能说的话，应当保持沉默。

En: Just make it up. No way this speedfreak can tell the difference.
Zh: 随便编个说法吧，这个瘾君子绝对分辨不出来。

En: The young man bobs his head to the music, much as before.
Zh: 年轻人跟之前一样，随着音乐摇头晃脑的。

En: You scrape the mouldy recesses of your brain for some technological sounding jargon. It'll impress the large-headed speedfreak.
Zh: 你从大脑发霉的角落里搜刮出一些听上去很专业的术语，足以给大脑袋瘾君子留下深刻印象了。

En: You should crank up the, uh, oscillator matrix.
Zh: “你应该启动，呃，振荡矩阵。”

En: Dude, you gotta reroute the sound juice through the fluxifier!
Zh: “兄弟，你得用流动器让声音改道！”

En: The fluxifier? The man's brow furrows with concern. "Where is that?"
Zh: “流动器？”那人忧虑地皱起了眉头。“在哪儿？”

En: Oscillator matrix? The man's brow furrows with concern. "Where is that?"
Zh: “振荡矩阵？”那人忧虑地皱起了眉头。“在哪儿？”

En: YEEEEAAARRGGHH. PUMP OFF THE *BZOOT-BZOOT*.
Zh: “嗷耶耶耶耶耶！一起来*嘭嘭嘭*。”

En: Oh, you don't *have* one? Maybe it's just top-secret cop-tech.
Zh: “哦，你*没有*嘛？或许那是最高机密的警用科技吧。”

En: It's nowhere. I just made it up.
Zh: “哪里都没有，是我编的。”

En: Here, let a pro show you how it's done. (Turn some knobs at random.)
Zh: “来啊，让专业人士来告诉你该怎么做。”（随意转动一些旋钮。）

En: TOP-SECRET COP TECH! MEGA COOL!
Zh: “绝密警用科技！太酷了！”

En: You gotta bring us some, Big Sheriff! The future of hard sine depends on it!
Zh: “您得给我们带点来啊，警长大爷！硬核正弦波得未来全指望它了！”

En: As you fiddle with the knobs on the mixer, the young man stares with amazement...
Zh: 当你在拨弄混音台的旋钮时，那个年轻人在一旁惊奇地看着……

En: Cop-man can ride the knobs! SO COOL. He gives you a thumbs up.
Zh: “警察能够驾驭这些按键！太酷了！”他对你竖起了大拇指。

En: Whatever it is you did, it seems to have helped slightly. Maybe now you'll be able to make sense of it?
Zh: 不论你做了什么，似乎已经有些起色了。或许现在的你能够理解了？

En: The large-headed youth gives you a sorely disappointed look...
Zh: 那个大头青年给了你一个非常失望的眼神……

En: An innocent soul, unaccustomed to acts of wanton guile.
Zh: 一个纯真的灵魂，尚未能习惯这荒唐的诡计。

En: A cruel joke to play on a brother torque dork.\n
Zh: 对扭矩呆子兄弟开的残忍玩笑。

En: A cruel joke to play on an earnest torque dork.
Zh: 对热忱的扭矩呆子开的残忍玩笑。

En: It's hard to say for sure, but the digital throbbing seems ever so slightly louder than before.
Zh: 很难确定，但是数字跳动似乎比以往更响亮了一些。

En: Wild wild I wanna see -- where's the child I used to be!
Zh: “荒野荒野请指引——从前的我何处寻！”

En: Racketeer, disappear, surging through the MEGA-sphere!
Zh: “敲诈者，无踪影，穿过巨大的球形！”

En: Faster, harder, caater!
Zh: “更快，更强，更爆炸！”

En: Die ganze Truppe fliegt mit Mach 3 in den Himmel. Jaaa!!!
Zh: “所有人到数三声跟我一起飞入天空。呀！”

En: Ziggy to the wiggy -- land of the beautiful, children of sound!
Zh: “叽里呱啦——魅力之都，声音之子！”

En: All four, back door, HARD CORE!
Zh: “四个，后门，硬核！”

En: On the verge, beat submerged, divers converge!
Zh: “濒临边界，节奏下潜，潜水员们汇聚一堂！”

En: Solar heat, feel the beat -- one track, rhythm attack -- everybody ju-uuump!
Zh: “太阳灼烧，感知心跳——单条轨迹，韵律来袭——大家跳起来！”

En: Here we go, hyper-soul, inside -- into the MEGA-heart!
Zh: “行动起来，躁动灵魂，靠近——强心脏！”

En: Who conquers the floor? That was then, this is chapter four!
Zh: “谁是舞池之王？往事过去，现在翻到第四章！”

En: Wir sind schlecht, wir sind in Gefahr, wir machen Pornos!
Zh: “我们都是坏孩子，我们都深陷险境，我们在搞黄色！”

En: Stratosphere, canoneer, music severe -- you didn't mishear, this *IS* the premier!
Zh: “平流层,大炮手,音乐发烧友——你没有听错，这*就是*第一！”

En: Re-entry, here we go! Dance floor -- HARD CORE!
Zh: “重新返回，大家一起来！舞池——硬核！”

En: Lost and found, submerged in sound, praying to the beat!
Zh: “失去而复得，潜入声音里，向节拍祈祷！”

En: Hate-gate, mate-date, no room for the fake-flake -- chicken-chaser night-cake!
Zh: “仇恨之门，伴侣约会，没有空间给小飞飞——深夜捉鸡捕手！”

En: Sandals make a man -- detour, ten-four, HARD CORE!
Zh: “凉鞋成就男人——绕道而行，消息收到，硬核！”

En: Bring it back to the source, subliminal force. No remorse.
Zh: “追根溯源，潜在能量，永不回头。”

En: What is this?
Zh: “这是什么？”

En: It looks like a makeshift bridge. The lieutenant adjusts his collar against a cool breeze. "Could be convenient."
Zh: “看起来像一座临时搭建的桥。”警督为抵御寒风而调整着衣领。“还挺方便的。”

En: We should ask that girl on the ice what's going on here. The lieutenant gestures toward the young woman next to the tent.
Zh: “去问问冰面上的那个姑娘吧，看看他们到底在干嘛。”警督指向帐篷旁边的年轻女子。

En: A pane of eternite has been planted into the snow. Two poles are holding it up.
Zh: 一块永恒之板被埋入雪中，两根杆子在支撑着它。

En: Push the eternite over.
Zh: 推倒永恒之板。

En: [Leave it as is.]
Zh: [维持原样。]

En: The pane falls into the icy snow with a soft thunk.
Zh: 随着轻柔的一声，石板落在冰冷的雪地上。

En: *Barely* holding it up. It could fall over any minute... a stronger gust of wind might be enough.
Zh: *勉强*支撑着它，随时都有可能掉下来……或许一阵强风就足够了。

En: What is this?
Zh: 这是什么？

En: When junior researcher Õlari Taal invented eternite in the un-prestigious Värtna Polytechnic Institute some thirty odd years ago, he thought it would last forever -- hence the name eternite. The only lasting thing about it turned out to be its carcinogenic effect.
Zh: 大概是三十年前，初级研究员奥拉瑞·塔尔在瓦塔那理工学院研究出了亘石，他以为这种东西能够永存——所以命名为亘石。可悲的是，唯一能够持久的只有这种材料的超高致癌效应。

En: Looks like a makeshift bridge, currently drawn up.
Zh: 看上去像一座临时搭建的桥，当前正在施工中。

En: Hmm... I heard about the assault after it had already gone down.
Zh: “嗯……我是在袭击事件解决之后才听到的。”

En: What a shame. Look at all the damage done to the church...
Zh: “真是可惜，看看教堂的损伤吧……”

En: Right. I don't really want to hear about what happened in the tent. Let's get going.
Zh: “好了，我并不想知道帐篷里都发生了什么。我们走吧。”

En: First the investigation, now this... how many more things do you have to fuck up?
Zh: 首先是调查，现在又是这个……你到底还要再搞砸多少事情？

En: Sharp pain shoots through your hip, throbbing...
Zh: 尖锐的痛楚穿过你的臀部，一跳一跳的……

En: Now -- if it were any other day, I would leave for the night. But we have more pressing matters. We need to wrap up this investigation and we need to do it *fast*.
Zh: “现在——如果是别的日子，我会今晚就离开。但是我们还有更紧迫的问题。我们需要搞定调查，而且动作要*快*。”

En: Now -- if it were any other day, I would leave for the night. But we have more pressing matters. We need to get to the island -- and we need to do it as soon as possible.
Zh: “现在——如果是别的日子，我会今晚就离开。但是我们还有更紧迫的问题。我们需要去岛上看看——而且越快越好。”

En: This case, he turns to face the sea, "is much, *much* bigger than just you and me. We're close -- let's keep it professional until the very end."
Zh: “这起案子，”他转身面向大海，“比你和我都要大的太多，*太多*。我们很接近真相了——让我们保持专业态度，直到最后。”

En: The dark shadow of the church is drawn out on the sand... two men stand there. In silence.
Zh: 教堂的黑色阴影笼罩在沙地上……两个男人站在那里。沉默不语。

En: The dark shadow of the church is drawn out on the sand... two men stand there, silent. Their talk is over.
Zh: 教堂的黑色阴影笼罩在沙地上……两个男人站在那里，沉默不语。他们的谈话结束了。

En: This case, he turns to face the sea, "is much, *much* bigger than just you or me. We're close to the end -- let's cross the finish line. Then we can part our ways."
Zh: “这起案子，”他转身面向大海，“比你和我都要大的太多，*太多*。我们已经接近终点——让我们跨越终点线。然后我们就可以分道扬镳。”

En: Now -- if it were any other day, I would excuse myself for the night. But we have more pressing matters. We need to wrap up this investigation and we need to do it *fast*.
Zh: “现在——如果是别的日子，我会今晚就离开。但是我们还有更紧迫的问题。我们需要搞定调查，而且动作要*快*。”

En: Now -- if it were any other day, I would excuse myself for the night. But we have more pressing matters. We need to get to the island -- and we need to do it as soon as possible.
Zh: “现在——如果是别的日子，我会今晚就离开。但是我们还有更紧迫的问题。我们需要去岛上看看——而且越快越好。”

En: Just a sorry stack of shit...
Zh: 只是一堆遗憾的狗屎……

En: Yeah, didn't even know they stack shit *that* sorry.
Zh: 是啊，你甚至都不知道他们把屎都堆得*那么*遗憾。

En: She said it was important that we stick together, as partners.
Zh: ”她说最重要的是我们要作为搭档团结起来。“

En: It's colder outside than you were expecting. You see the lieutenant, standing there, a small cloud of white escaping from his mouth...
Zh: 外面比你预料的更冷一些。你看见警督站在那里，一小团白色的雾气从他嘴里逃了出来……

En: He turns to you, but says nothing.
Zh: 他转向你，但什么也没说。

En: He's waiting to see what you do. For god's sake say you fucked up, please.
Zh: 他正等着你的下一步动作。看在老天爷的份上，请说你搞砸了。

En: I think I fucked up back there...
Zh: “我想我刚才搞砸了……”

En: What's your problem?
Zh: “你有什么问题吗？”

En: Hey, Kim.
Detective, he says, his voice level as a tabletop.
Zh: “警探，”他说，声音如同桌面一般平静。

En: There won't be any preamble chit-chat this time. Get to the point.
Zh: 这次不会有任何暖场式的闲聊了，言归正传吧。

En: What's *my* problem? he says, his voice rising with indignation...
Zh: “*我*有什么问题？”他说，他的声调因为愤怒而拔高了……

En: You're a real piece of work, Harry, you know that?
Zh: “你可真是了不起，哈里，你知道吗？”

En: You're a real piece of work, detective, you know that?
Zh: “你可真是了不起，警探，你知道吗？”

En: You *think*?
Okay, scratch that. I *definitely* fucked up.
Zh: “好吧，算了，我*肯定*是搞砸了。”

En: Hey, I made a mistake, but it's not all my fault, you know.
Zh: “嘿，我犯了一个错误，但那并不全是我的错，知道吗。”

En: Really? It's not your *fault* that you're *down with racism* or whatever you told that lorryman?
Zh: “是吗？*种族主义*或者你跟货車司机说的那番话也不是你的*错*吗？”

En: It's not your *fault* that you subscribed to Measurehead's inane racial theories?
Zh: “支持测颅先生愚蠢的种族主义理论也不是你的*错*吗？”

En: Yes, and?
Zh: “嗯，还有呢？”

En: 'And'?
The lieutenant stares impassively.
Zh: 警督面无表情地凝视着你。

En: What are you, a petulant child? He expects you to apologize.
Zh: 怎么，你还是个任性的孩子吗？他希望你能道歉。

En: ...and I'm sorry.
Zh: “……还有我很抱歉。”

En: I'm not apologizing, if that's what you're waiting for.
Zh: “我是不会道歉的，如果你是在等这个的话。”

En: MAKE IT RIGHT. MORE THAN YOU KNOW DEPENDS ON IT.
Zh: 重新改过，一切都取决于这个了。

En: He is your half-brother and you're driving him away. For what?
Zh: 他是你的异姓兄弟，你却要把他赶走。为了什么呢？

En: It's not your *fault* that you buy into whatever conspiracies bug-chasing cryptofascists feed you?
Zh: “听信那些追逐虫子的秘密法西斯主义者给你灌输的阴谋，也不是你的*错*吗？”

En: I don't see why you're getting all bent out of shape over a *word*.
Zh: “我不明白你为何为了一个*词*就那么生气。”

En: What do you want me to do?
Zh: “你想让我怎么做？”

En: *Apologizing* would be a good start.
Zh: “*道歉*是个很好的开始。”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "Maybe it really *isn't* your fault. You might just be an irretrievable human catastrophe..."
Zh: 警督叹了一口气。“或许这真的*不怪*你，或许你只是个无药可救的人形灾难……”

En: Let me ask you something... He leans in to inspect you.
Zh: “让我问问你……”他靠近了一些，端详着你。

En: What do you see when you look at me?
Zh: “当你看着我的时候，你看到了什么？”

En: My half-brother.
Zh: “我的异姓兄弟。”

En: A Seolite.
Zh: “一个西奥人。”

En: Just a... guy.
Zh: “就是个……普通人。”

En: A binoclard.
Zh: “一个四眼仔。”

En: A pinball wizard.
Zh: “一个弹球巫师。”

En: Well, at least you're explicit about it. Most of the time people try to be *polite*. The lieutenant doesn't even flinch. "You know what you did *not* say I am, though?"
Zh: “好吧，至少你对这种事还算坦率。大多数时候人们都会尽量保持*礼貌*。”警督丝毫没有动摇。“不过，关于我的身份，你知道你还有什么*没*说吗？”

En: Yeah, that's what most people *say*. But do you know what they *don't* say?
Zh: “是啊，大多数人都这么*说*的。但是你知道他们*没说*什么吗？”

En: That's what you're *supposed* to say, of course... But do you know what you *didn't* say?
Zh: “当然了，你*应该*会这么说……但是你知道你*没有*说什么吗？”

En: Be serious a moment, please...
Zh: “请你严肃一点儿……”

En: You know, Harry, one of your problems is that *nothing* ever seems to be your fault. Have you noticed that?
Zh: “你知道吗，哈里，你的问题之一就是*永远*不是你的错，你注意到了吗？”

En: You know, detective, one of your problems is that *nothing* ever seems to be your fault. Have you noticed that?
Zh: “你知道吗，警探，你的问题之一就是*永远*不是你的错，你注意到了吗？”

En: You definitely could have handled that better.
Zh: 你完全可以处理得更好。

En: He's right. No one *forced* you to believe that nonsense.
Zh: 他是对的，没有人*强迫*你一定要相信那些无稽之谈。

En: Those theories didn't really hold up in retrospect, did they?
Zh: “回想起来，那些理论确实站不住脚，不是吗？”

En: Okay, you're right. I fucked up back there.
Zh: “好吧，你说得对，是我把事情搞砸了。”

En: Which I guess makes *me* the idiot for hoping you could be better... I mean, *look* at yourself...
Zh: “我猜希望你能变好的*我*，反倒成了个白痴……我的意思是，*看看*你自己……”

En: Yes, look at yourself. What do you see?
Zh: 是啊，看看你自己吧，你看到了什么？

En: 'Irretrievable human catastrophe' sounds about right.
Zh: ‘无药可救的人形灾难’听起来正合适。

En: A good guy, underneath it all.
Zh: 内心深处，一个好人。

En: A goddamn superstar, that's what.
Zh: 一个超级明星，就是如此。

En: The harbinger of doom.
Zh: 厄运的预兆。

En: So that's it? You don't even *want* to change?
Zh: 就这样了？你甚至不*想*做出改变吗？

En: If that good guy never comes out, what difference does it make?
Zh: 如果那个好人总是不出现，又有什么区别呢？

En: Kind of hard to sing a duet when you're all alone on-stage, isn't it, superstar?
Zh: “如果舞台上只有你一个，想唱二重唱还是有点困难的，不是吗，大明星？”

En: I just don't understand. We had been making *real* progress toward solving the case...
Zh: “我真是不明白，我们在这个案件上已经取得了*真正的*进展……”

En: You've been spinning your wheels here for *how* long? And what do you have to show for it?
Zh: “你在这里转悠多久了？你掌握了什么证据吗？”

En: You've not just been *unhelpful* when it comes to solving the case, you've been an active *hindrance* to the investigation...
Zh: “破案过程中你不仅仅*毫无用处*，反倒是调查中的活*障碍*……”

En: But now I'm afraid I just can't trust you.
Zh: “但现在我恐怕无法再信任你了。”

En: Well, I'm done waiting for you to get your shit together.
Zh: “得了，我已经受够等待你振作起来了。”

En: Kim, I can explain...
Zh: “金，我可以解释的……”

En: I can do better.
Zh: “我可以做得更好的。”

En: So... what now? (Proceed.)
Zh: “那……然后呢？”（继续。）

En: Stop, STOP. You won't even call me by my *actual title*. It's always, 'Kim this, Kim that.' Has it even occurred to you how disrespectful that is?
Zh: “停，停，你甚至没有用我的*头衔*来称呼我。总是‘金这个，金那个’的，你有没有想过这有多不尊重人吗？”

En: But, see, you *can't*, or you just refuse to, not that the difference really matters.
Zh: “但，你要明白，你*做不到*，或者你很抵触这么做。这两者其实也没什么差别。”

En: The lieutenant goes silent for a moment.
Zh: 警督沉默了一阵子。

En: For the sake of the investigation... I'll stay.
Zh: “为了这次调查……我会留下的。”

En: So, you're giving me another shot?
Zh: “那么，你要再给我一次机会吗？”

En: No, you fucking asshole. This isn't a *game* and you're not the *hero*. You don't *get* infinite chances to do the right thing. That's not how the world works...
Zh: “不是，你这该死的混蛋。这不是*游戏*，你也不是*英雄*，是没有无限次机会让你重新改过的，世界不是这样运转的……”

En: I'm staying because *I* have to protect the investigation *from you*...
Zh: “我留下来是因为*我*必须确保调查不*被你*搞砸……”

En: Now, if you'll excuse me, I have some other matters to attend to. We'll meet again as usual in the morning. And detective...
Zh: “现在如果你不介意的话，我还有别的事情要处理。我们明早照常再见吧，还有，警探……”

En: Okay, I know you're mad, but I actually just talked to the spirit of Revachol and she told me we need to work together...
Zh: “好吧，我知道你很生气，但其实我刚刚才跟瑞瓦肖之神交流过，她说我们必须得协作……”

En: The lieutenant looks at you with a mix of pity, contempt, and alarm...
Zh: 警督看着你，眼神中带着怜悯、轻蔑与惊恐……

En: Get your shit together, detective.
Zh: “振作一点，警探。”

En: But Kim, the spirit...
Zh: “可是金，那个灵魂……”

En: I'm working on it, I just need your help...
Zh: “我正在努力呢，只是需要你的帮助……”

En: God, *no*. I'm a detective, not a charity service for shambolic degenerates who won't help themselves...
Zh: “天呐，*当然不*。我是个警探，不是那些自甘堕落的混蛋们的慈善机构……”

En: Detective, enough. I don't want to hear about your degenerate ravings. I've heard *plenty* as it is. Just stop.
Zh: “警探，够了。我不想再听你那些下流的胡言乱语了，我已经听得*够多*了，闭嘴吧。”

En: You hear that? The lieutenant's staying! Brotherhood conquers all!
Zh: 你听到了吗？警督要留下了！兄弟情能战胜一切！

En: That's great, I could still use your assistance.
Zh: “太好了，我仍然需要你的协助。”

En: Why? I don't really need you anyway.
Zh: “为什么？反正我也不是很需要你。”

En: You fucking asshole. I'm not here to be your *loyal assistant*, and I never have been...
Zh: “你这个该死的混蛋！我不是来做你*忠实助手*的，我从来都不是……”

En: Of course that's what you think, contrary to all evidence and experience. I'm not here for your sake...
Zh: “你当然是这么想的，完全与一切证据和经验相悖。我不是为了你来的……”

En: He has the upright bearing of a king's headsman, waiting to carry out the crown's sentence.
Zh: 他有着皇家刽子手的正直举止，等待着执行国王的判决。

En: He's been anticipating this moment for some time. Still, it doesn't make it any easier for him to say...
Zh: 他期待这一刻已经有一段时间了，然而，这并没有使他更容易说出口……

En: The sentence has been passed.
Zh: 判决已经被传达了。

En: The dark shadow of the church is drawn out on the sand... two men stand there, silent. Their talk is over.
Zh: 教堂的黑色阴影笼罩在沙地上……两个男人站在那里，沉默不语。他们的谈话结束了。

En: The only doom you've brought about so far is your own.
Zh: 目前为止，你带来的唯一厄运就是你自己的。

En: Revacholian.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖人。”

En: I don't suppose anyone ever questions whether you belong here. No matter how badly you fuck up, no matter what a mess you make, you never *stop* being Revacholian...
Zh: “我想应该没有人会质疑你是否属于这里。无论你闯的祸有多大，无论你把事情搞得有多糟，你永远不会*丢弃*自己瑞瓦肖人的身份……”

En: People call me all sorts of things. But you know the one thing they *never* call me?
Zh: “人们给了我各种各样的称谓，但你知道他们从来不会叫我什么吗？”

En: ... no offence.
Zh: “……无意冒犯。”

En: None taken.
Zh: “没事的。”

En: I mean, you're not wrong.
Zh: “我的意思是，你没错。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: I was born here. I grew up here. It's the only *home* I've ever known. And there's a good chance I'll die in the line of duty here, too.
Zh: “我在这里出生，在这里长大，这里是我唯一知道的*家乡*。而且有很大几率我也会在这里因公殉职。”

En: But -- to most of my countrymen, I will always be some *monkey fucker*.
Zh: “但是——对于我的大多数同胞来说，我永远都只是一只*傻逼猴子*。”

En: I don't suppose anyone ever questions whether you belong here. Whatever your faults, you're a decent-enough detective. No one will ever *doubt* that you're a real Revacholian...
Zh: “我想应该没有人会质疑你是否属于这里。不管你有什么缺点，你都是个足够体面的警探。没有人会*质疑*你是否是个真正的瑞瓦肖人……”

En: I don't suppose anyone ever questions whether you belong here. Whatever your faults, you're a solid detective. You're *obviously* Revacholian...
Zh: “我想应该没有人会质疑你是否属于这里。不管你有什么缺点，你都是个可靠的警探。你*明显*就是瑞瓦肖人……”

En: To me, you're my partner.
Zh: “对我来说，你是我的搭档。”

En: That really sucks, man.
Zh: “这真是糟透了，老兄。”

En: Wait, before I blacked out, I *talked* to *La Revacholière*.
Zh: “等等，在我晕倒之前，我跟*瑞瓦肖之神*交流*了一番。”

En: You... what?
I see... the lieutenant says, tapping his foot...
Zh: “我知道了……”警督踢踏着脚说……

En: Never mind, I was just talking crazy talk.
Zh: “算了，我就是随便说说而已。”

En: I spoke with the spirit of Revachol. Or, I guess, she spoke to *me*.
Zh: “我跟瑞瓦肖之神说过话，或者，我想，是她对*我*说过话。”

En: Suppose, for a moment, that this isn't just more of your *Remote Viewer* nonsense... what did *La Revacholière* say to you?
Zh: “咱们姑且假设这不是你又在扯那套*遥视者*的胡话……*大瑞瓦肖*对你说了什么？”

En: Suppose, for a moment, that this isn't just a pyrholidon-induced hallucination... what did *La Revacholière* say to you?
Zh: “假设，这不是由吡嚯浣酮引发的幻觉……*瑞瓦肖之神*对你说了什么？”

En: Suppose, for a moment, that this isn't more of your ghost-summoning nonsense... what did *La Revacholière* say to you?
Zh: ”假设，这不是你所谓的招魂之类的鬼话……*瑞瓦肖之神*对你说了什么？”

En: Suppose, for a moment, that you're not just chasing urban legends again... what did *La Revacholière* say to you?
Zh: “假设，这不是你又一次对都市传说的追逐……*瑞瓦肖之神*对你说了什么？”

En: Suppose I *don't* recommend you be involuntarily committed for a moment... what did *La Revacholière* say to you?
Zh: “假设我暂时*不*管你的无心之失……*瑞瓦肖之神*对你说了什么？”

En: Yes, it *does*. The lieutenant begins tapping his foot...
Zh: “是的，*确实*。”警督开始跺脚……

En: The lieutenant's not comfortable talking about himself so openly. He's eager to change the subject.
Zh: 警督不喜欢如此公开地谈论自己，他急于改变话题。

En: In any case, I have some personal matters I'd like to get in order. If you don't object, I'm going to leave the rest of the day's investigation to you...
Zh: “无论如何，我有一些私事需要处理。如果你不反对，今天剩下的调查工作就交给你了……”

En: Go ahead.
Zh: “去吧。”

En: Wait, I wasn't done...
Zh: “等等，我还没说完……”

En: Try not to break anything while I'm gone. I'll see you in the morning, detective.
Zh: “我不在的时候尽量不要破坏东西，明天早上再见，警探。”

En: For a moment the lieutenant seems to be suppressing something uncomfortable. Then he clears his throat...
Zh: 一时间，警督似乎在压抑着某种不适。然后他清了清嗓子……

En: But what about all the good times we had together?
Zh: “那我们一起经历的美好时光呢？”

En: It was just words, Kim. They don't mean anything.
Zh: “随便说说而已，金，没有任何含义。”

En: *What* good times?
Zh: “*什么*美好时光？”

En: If they don't mean anything, why do you say them?
Zh: “如果这些话毫无意义，你为什么要说出来呢？”

En: Don't fuck anything else up while I'm away.
Zh: “我不在的时候，别再把事情搞砸了。”

En: A true *Vacholiere*.
Zh: “一个真正的*瓦科莱*。”

En: The lieutenant appears caught off-guard by your response. He takes a moment to collect himself...
Zh: 警督似乎被你的回答打了个措手不及，他花了一点时间整理自己……

En: Nobody's ever said that to me before...
Zh: “从来没有人对我说过这种话……”

En: The lieutenant's not comfortable with emotional openness. He's eager to change the subject.
Zh: 警督不太喜欢袒露感情，他急于改变话题。

En: Well, good, because we *are* partners. Now, I have some personal matters I'd like to get in order. If you don't object, I'm going to leave the rest of the day's investigation to you...
Zh: “嗯，很好，因为我们*是*搭档。现在，我有一些私事需要处理。如果你不反对，今天剩下的调查工作就交给你了……”

En: The dark shadow of the church is drawn out on the sand... two men part there. In silence.
Zh: 教堂的黑色阴影笼罩在沙地上……两个男人在那里分开。沉默不语。

En: Hold on a moment. Maybe now's not the best time to tell the lieutenant that the world is ending...
Zh: 稍等一下。或许现在不是告诉警督世界即将毁灭的最佳时机……

En: Uh... she said the RCM is gonna have an important role to play...
Zh: “呃……她说RCM将会扮演一个重要角色……”

En: She said the world is going to end in 22 years.
Zh: “她说世界将在28年后毁灭。”

En: A role to play in *what*?
Zh: “扮演*什么*角色？”

En: The... future?
The end of the world.
Zh: “世界末日。”

En: What kind of *future* are we talking about here?
Zh: “是什么样的*未来*？”

En: You know... like stuff that hasn't happened yet.
Zh: “你懂的……比如还没有发生的事情。”

En: The end of the world.
Zh: “世界末日。”

En: Quit fooling around, detective. There's real urgency in the lieutenant's voice now. "What did La Revacholière tell you about the future?"
Zh: “别胡闹了，警探。”警督的语气里流露着真实的紧迫感。“瑞瓦肖之神跟你说了什么未来？”

En: She said the world is ending in 22 years.
Zh: “她说世界将在22年之内毁灭。”

En: The world... is going to end... The lieutenant considers this for a long moment...
Zh: “世界……将要毁灭……”警督思索了很长时间……

En: The lieutenant is a rational observer of reality. His worldview can't accommodate this kind of thing.
Zh: 警督是现实的理性观察者，他的世界观尚不能接收这种事。

En: Promise me something, detective: You won't tell anyone else what you just told me.
Zh: “答应我一件事，警探：你刚才告诉我的话，不可以告诉别人。”

En: But Kim, I'm the Augur of Desolation.
Zh: “可是金，我是废墟的预言者。”

En: Are you sure? It seems kinda important.
Zh: “你确定吗？这似乎很重要。”

En: That's probably wise.
Zh: “很明智。”

En: Now, I... think I need to take care of some personal matters for a while. If you don't object, I'm going to leave the rest of the day's investigation to you...
Zh: “现在，我……我想我暂时还有些私事需要处理。如果你不反对，今天剩下的调查工作就交给你了……”

En: Wait, I wasn't done...
Zh: “等等，我还没说完……”

En: Go ahead.
Zh: “去吧。”

En: Detective, I'm serious. Have you considered what will happen to the RCM's reputation if one of our own starts going around talking about the end of the world? In an official capacity?
Zh: “警探，我是认真的。你有没有想过，如果我们中的一员到处大谈特谈世界末日，RCM的声誉会怎样？还是以官方的身份？”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: Why do you think that's so?
Zh: “你为什么会这么想？”

En: Really.
Zh: “真的。”

En: We are all the same. Same eyes, same smiles, same death.
Zh: “我们都一样，同样的眼睛、同样的微笑、同样的死亡。”

En: I'm thinking we should *sell* this.
Zh: “我觉得我们应该*贩卖*这个。”

En: It should be sold, he nods, "but not by us."
Zh: “确实应该卖掉，”他点点头，“但不是被我们。”

En: I prefer not to. Both ask the wrong questions. Any spark of light from either one is accidental. Their combined movement's only concern is producing enough pig and wheat for everyone -- the end goal of humanity...
Zh: “我宁愿不要。两者都问错了问题，任何一方发出的闪光都是偶然的。他们的联合行动唯一关心的就是为每个人生产足够的猪肉和小麦——人类的终极目标……”

En: Good to know. Tell me something else.
Zh: “很好，再告诉我点别的吧。”

En: I would much rather tell you about true life than this pathetic *something else*, but you are, after all, the law of the land.
Zh: “我情愿告诉你什么是真实生活，也不想说什么可悲的*别的东西*。但你，毕竟是这片地方的法律。”

En: That's it?
Zh: “就这样？”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: It's my friend and accomplice. It makes me party harder.
Zh: “这是我的同伴和同谋，让我狂欢得更疯狂了。”

En: I can see that. And you've partied *very* hard for a *very* long time, haven't you?
Zh: “看得出来。你们已经*非常*疯狂地狂欢了*很*长一段时间了，是不是？”

En: It looks like you're trying to hang yourself with it. So large, too. So many different patterns. It's crazy man, I like it. Makes me want to forget you almost shot my friend.\n
Zh: “看上去你是想用它把自己吊死。很大，还有很多不同的图案。这也太疯狂了兄弟，我很喜欢。都让我想忘记你差点杀了我的同伴了。”

En: It looks like you're trying to hang yourself with it. So large, too. So many different patterns. It's crazy man, I like it. Makes me want to forgot you took all our money.\n
Zh: “你看起来就像是打算用这玩意上吊。尺寸夸张，还色彩斑斓。这很疯狂，朋友，我喜欢。我恨不得能把你搞没我们的钱这码事忘掉。”

En: It looks like you're trying to hang yourself with it. So large, too. So many different patterns. It's crazy man, I like it.
Zh: “看上去你是想用它把自己吊死。很大，还有很多不同的图案。这也太疯狂了兄弟，我很喜欢。”

En: No, it's just a vest of suspenders made to resemble a human rib cage. Plus some lady-clothes to piss off the sex-system. He points to your tie. "Now tell me about that hard core necktie. It's fucking primal."
Zh: “不，它只是一条做得像人类胸腔的背带裤。再加上几件让性别系统生气的女装。”他指着你的领带。“现在跟我说说这条硬核领带吧，真他妈的原始。”

En: (Look at your tie suspiciously.) "What's so hard core about it?"
Zh: （疑惑地看着你的领带。）“这有什么硬核的？”

En: They're just clothes.
Zh: “它们只是几件衣服。”

En: Are they? They look outlandish.
Zh: “是吗？它们看上去很古怪。”

En: I thought there would be more to it.
Zh: “我以为还会有更多含义呢。”

En: No, these are just boring, normal, hard core clothes. That... he points to your necktie, "*that's* outlandish."
Zh: “不，这只是些无聊、普通、硬核的衣服而已。那……”他指着你的领带，“*那个*才叫古怪呢。”

En: (Look at your tie suspiciously.) "What's so outlandish about it?"
Zh: （疑惑地看着你的领带。）“这有什么古怪的？”

En: RESPECT TO THE MAN!
Zh: “尊敬这个男人！”

En: It's just a style, you know? Normal hard style. Anyone can wear it.
Zh: “这就是一种风格，你懂的。很正常的风格，人人都能穿。”

En: You suddenly realize the patience and respect this speedfreak has shown you is partly because of your *style*. He's probably not like that with everyone.
Zh: 你突然意识到这瘾君子对你表现出的所有耐心和尊重，部分是因为你的*风格*。他可能不是对每个人都是这样的。

En: Scared? Angry? It's hard to say which this man is.
Zh: 害怕？生气？很难说这人是哪一种。

En: He's just scared.
Zh: 他只是害怕了。

En: It's not easy for him. It probably took him years to get used to Andre.
Zh: 这对他来说不太容易。他可能花了好几年时间才适应了安德烈。

En: He looks like a *well thought out* individual. The synchronization might be worth it. In the long run.
Zh: 他看起来是个*深思熟虑*的人。同步可能是值得的。从长远来看的话。

En: Interesting. He wants you to *describe* it, though he already knows what it looks like.
Zh: 有意思。他想要你*描述*它，尽管他都已经知道它的样子。

En: Got it. Tell me something else.
Zh: “明白了，再告诉我点别的吧。”

En: Good call, not exposing yourself to stuff like that.
Zh: “干的漂亮，不要让自己暴露在这种东西之下。”

En: This is a good, dangerous line of questioning. You should prod him on!
Zh: 这是一个很好、很危险的质疑。你应该刺激他一下。

En: I don't have a top ten list of things I'm most suspicious of. But if I *had* one, the left-right complex would be number one. Number two would be their sole accomplishment -- the pig/wheat paradigm.
Zh: “我并没有自己最怀疑的十件事的清单。如果我*有*，那么左右综合体就是第一号。第二个则是他们唯一的成就——猪肉/小麦范式。”

En: Tell me more about the left-right business.
Zh: “再跟我说说关于左右的事吧？”

En: What's suspicious about the production of pork and wheat?
Zh: “生产猪肉和小麦有什么可疑之处吗？”

En: Don't you mean the left-right *paradigm* and the pig/wheat *complex*?
Zh: “你说的是左右*范式*和猪肉/小麦*综合体*吗？”

En: Okay, *now* I'm ready. Let's contact Archer.
Zh: “好的，我*终于*准备好了。联系弓箭手号吧。”

En: I found a radio transceiver.
Zh: “我找到了一个无线电收发器。”

En: So your cop ways came through again. Impressive. Let's see what you got.
Zh: “看来你的条子手段又成功了。真厉害。看看你搞到了什么吧。”

En: I got this beauty down at the pawnshop. (Use the highly expensive transceiver.)
Zh: “我在当铺买到了这个宝贝。” (用昂贵的收发器)

En: Someone was using this to coordinate a drug smuggling operation. (Use the highly advanced transceiver.)
Zh: “有人用这个来协调毒品走私行动。 ”(用先进的收发器)

En: I found this in an office by the harbour. (Use the perfectly adequate transceiver.)
Zh: “我在港口边的一间办公室里发现了这个。” (用完美适配的收发器。)

En: Didn't know cops made that kind of lucre. This is a brilliant piece of equipment. Maybe even *too* brilliant. Feels out of step with our whole operation...
Zh: “我真不知道条子还能发这种横财。这设备简直绝了。甚至*过于*绝了。感觉我们的行动跟不上这个的节奏……”

En: Thought I felt a special sine coming off this one. I'll just have Egg pop in a frequency dial here, and we should be set...
Zh: “我感觉这东西正在发射一股特殊的正弦波。只要让爱凡客加装一个频率刻度盘，咱们就算准备就绪了……”

En: An ordinary white-collar transceiver joins the hard core underground. Yeah, this should do nicely...
Zh: “一个常见的白领级收发器，加入了硬核地下组织的行列。好吧，这东西应该能派上用场……”

En: He wants to keep it at a distance. He's afraid of what powers are contained therein...
Zh: 他想和这台设备保持距离。他害怕其中包含的力量……

En: Rarely has there been a more apt pairing of man and transceiver. This is a portent of great success.
Zh: 在人与收发器的组合中，很难有比这个更优异的绝配了。此乃大捷之兆。

En: A nice story to tell himself, but don't forget, it really is just a normal, run-of-the-mill transceiver unit.
Zh: 这种说法不过是在哄他自己，你可别忘了，这不过是一个普普通通，稀松平常的无线电收发装置。

En: Alright, lawfarer, we're ready to do this. I have to warn you, though, once we commit, there's no stopping until we've seen it *all the way through*. No pauses, no second-chances. This is our shot. You got it?
Zh: “好吧，执法者，咱们已经准备就绪了。但我得提前警告你，一旦行动开始，咱们就只能一口气搞定*整个过程*。没有暂停，也没有重来。这是我们绝无仅有的机会。明白了吗？”

En: So any cop-prep you've got to do, you do it now. We'll wait if we have to.
Zh: “所以说，如果你还有什么要准备的，就先去把那些搞定吧。我们可以等你。”

En: What kind of 'cop-prep' do I need to do?
Zh: “我需要做哪些‘准备’？”

En: I'm ready now. Let's do this.
Zh: “准备完毕。开始吧。”

En: Yeah, I should get some things in order first.
Zh: “对了，我还有些事应该优先处理。”

En: Wait, didn't you say we need some kind of power supply?
Zh: “等等，你不是说我们需要某种电源吗？”

En: Rest of the crew has got to stay here. He gives you a solemn nod. "Can't afford to let the beat drop right as we're getting off the ground."
Zh: 瘾君子神情严肃地向你点头示意。“其他成员需要在这里原地待命。我们可不能刚起步就掉链子。”

En: Do what you have to do. We're not going anywhere.
Zh: “去忙你的吧。我们不会离开的。”

En: How should I know? It's up to everyone to know their own needs. Whatever keeps your *body and soul* in top working condition...
Zh: “我怎么会知道？只有你知道自己到底需要什么。为了让你*全心全意*地投入于事业中，你就尽情去查漏补缺吧。”

En: Don't sweat that. Egg found something down near the waterlock. Some maniac *abandoned* a perfectly good power source...
Zh: “不用担心那种事。爱凡客在水闸附近找到了好东西。有个疯子把一台完好无损的电源*扔掉*了……”

En: That 'maniac' is obviously you, which makes the 'power source' your half-sunken Coupris 40.
Zh: 那个“疯子”显然就是你，这意味着“电源”指的是你那辆半截入水的库普瑞斯40。

En: An 'abandoned power source'? What could he be referring to?
Zh: “扔掉的电源”？他说什么呢？

En: Whatever it is, it was abandoned for *good* reason. Best to leave some stones unturned...
Zh: 无论他指的是什么，扔掉那东西的理由必定相当*充分*。有些事最好不要查到水落石出……

En: You mean the motor carriage that I crashed into the bay?
Zh: “你指的是被我开进海湾里的那辆車？”

En: What do you mean someone *abandoned* a power source?
Zh: “*扔掉*电源是什么意思？”

En: Ah, well as long as it works, I don't need to know the details.
Zh: “啊，算了。只要能用就行。我没必要了解细节。”

En: That explains why it was covered in cop colours. Whole thing's totalled, but lucky for us the engine on the back is still perfectly good...
Zh: “我说那东西怎么用了条子的涂装。整辆車都报废了，幸运的是后置发动机还完好无损……”

En: I know, it's completely mental, but someone drove a *perfectly good* Coupris MC straight into the bay. Carriage is totalled, but luckily the engine on the back is still working...
Zh: “我知道这事听起来特别疯癫，但真有人把一辆*完好*的库普瑞斯机动車直接开进了海湾里。整辆車都报废了，幸运的是后置发动机还能运转……”

En: All I have to do is run a cable from the engine up the centaur-man's memorial. It won't last forever, but it should buy us enough time to get you synced up with the Big Bad.
Zh: “我只需要把一条电缆从发动机拉到半人马的纪念碑上。虽然供电不会永久持续下去，但应该能为我们争取到足够的时间，让你能和大坏蛋的通讯频率达成同步。”

En: Right, that's your call then.
Zh: “好的，你说了算。”

En: Personally, I'm gonna make sure I've got a steady supply of PREPTIDE handy.
Zh: “就我个人而言，我要确保手头有稳定的备潮供应。”

En: *Now* he's playing your tune! Better make sure you're ready to blast while circuit-bending!
Zh: *说得好*，他这可是在投你所好了！你最好在改装电路的时候准备嗨一下！

En: The speed is to get you through the tedious *detail-oriented* parts of the work. Maybe this freak will spot you some?
Zh: 思必得能助你飞跃工作中那些冗长无趣，*注重细节*的步骤。说不定这位瘾君子能分你一点？

En: But what's this? You're all out! Maybe this freak will spot you some...
Zh: 可这是怎么回事？你已经没货了！说不定这位瘾君子能分你一点……

En: (Whisper.) "Say, Noid. My PREPTIDE supply is a little low -- think you could *hook me up*?"
Zh: （压低声音。）“听我说，诺伊德。我的备潮快没货了——能帮我*介绍*一下吗？”

En: Nah, I'm good without it.
Zh: 不，我百毒不侵。

En: Hope you don't get any little wires crossed because you lost your concentration, Mr. Clean-and-Sober.
Zh: 希望你别因为走神而搭错了线路，一尘不染先生。

En: Here, you'll like this. It's got a little straw and everything so you can slurp while you work.
Zh: “拿去，你肯定喜欢这个。这上面自带了吸管和便于你边干边吸的一切。”

En: The lieutenant politely averts his eyes as you make a drug deal in a house of worship, but make no mistake: He's *less than thrilled* about it.
Zh: 当你在教堂里交易毒品时，警督礼貌地移开了视线，但你可别误会了：他*并不待见*这种行径。

En: Fuck what the lieutenant thinks! We're off to the races, baby!
Zh: 谁他妈在乎警督的意见！咱们这就开始狂飙吧，宝贝儿！

En: HEIGH-HO, HEIGH-HO, it's off to the races we go!
Zh: 嘿呦，嘿呦，狂飙吧朋友！

En: MUCH LOVE TO MY HARD CORE ANTENNA BROTHERS!
Zh: “向我的硬核天线兄弟们致敬！”

En: Back across the waterlock. At least we're getting our *exercise* in today.
Zh: “回水闸对面吧。至少今天我们还要*锻炼*一下。”

En: You can grab that amplifier, officer. He gestures to the lieutenant. "And you start spooling up those cables, maybe we can get it all in one go..."
Zh: “你来抬那个放大器吧，警官。”他冲警督比手势示意。“你来卷起这些电缆，也许咱们可以一波搞定……”

En: Now give me a hand with these cables. It's a long walk to the roundabout...
Zh: “帮我搬一下这些电缆。走到环岛可真够远的……”

En: Hard core church! Nowhere else to go!
Zh: “硬核教堂！无处可去！”

En: Could you use your *sine-sense* to help me contact a Coalition warship?
Zh: “你能不能通过*感知正弦波*的能力帮我联系上联盟的战舰？”

En: Trying to get aligned with the Big Bad, are you? He gnaws a screw thoughtfully. "Those are some serious frequencies you want to mess with..."
Zh: “你这是想和大坏蛋结盟，对吧？”他若有所思地咬着一颗螺丝。“你打算招惹的这些频率可够正经的……”

En: Come on, Noid. I think we owe him.
Zh: “别这样，诺伊德。我觉得那是咱们欠他的。”

En: Yeah, alright. But if you're going to sync up with *them*, you're gonna need a serious wave-generator of your own. You understand what I'm saying?
Zh: “啊，好吧。但你要是想和*他们*同步，就得搞一台正经的波形信号发生器。你能理解我在说什么吧？”

En: Now that our sines are synced, can you help me contact the Coalition?
Zh: “我们的正弦波已经同步了，你能帮我联系上联盟吗？”

En: Come on, Noid. I think we owe him.
Zh: “别这样，诺伊德。我觉得那是咱们欠他的。”

En: Guess it would be pretty soft core for me to say no, wouldn't it? He gnaws a screw thoughtfully.
Zh: “我要是拒绝就显得太软核了，是吧？”他若有所思地咬着一颗螺丝。

En: What about the radio relay tower at Land's End? Is that serious enough?
Zh: “地之角的那座无线电中继塔怎么样？那个够正经吗？”

En: Not really, no.
Zh: “不，一点也不。”

En: MEGA-LOW FREQUENCY! Waves so long the ionosphere can't hold them!
Zh: “极低频！波长长到电离层都挡不住！”

En: Not even the RCM has access to that kind of equipment. The Coalition is extremely possessive of its frequency bands.
Zh: “就连RCM都无法获得那种设备。联盟对其通讯频段的独占欲极强。”

En: There's an old radio relay tower at the edge of the peninsula, right where the air waves meet the sea waves...
Zh: “这座半岛的边缘有座古老的无线电中继塔，就在无线电波与波涛的交界处……”

En: TWO WAVES, ONE SHORE. WAVE LOVE. YEAAAGH!
Zh: “两道波，一道岸。爱的波涛。耶！”

En: You should go scope it out, see what kind of condition it's in.
Zh: “你应该去那里检查一下，看看那东西的状态如何。”

En: Wait, before I go -- where is this relay tower exactly?
Zh: “等等，出发之前我还要打听一下——这座中继塔具体是在什么地方？”

En: Alright, I'll check out the tower and report back. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，我这就去检查一下那座塔，再回来告诉你们。”（结束。）

En: You do that. And let us know what you discover.
Zh: “去吧。回来和我们分享你取得的成果。”

En: It's like, up from here. You just go outside, and then turn up.
Zh: “那地方就在，这里的上边。你只要出门，往上走就行。”

En: Turn 'up'? You mean, like, north?
Zh: “往‘上’走？你指的是往北吗？”

En: Yeah, up. Do they not teach you about maps in cop school?
Zh: “对，往上。你们条子上学都不教怎么看地图的吗？”

En: Not likely. Scoped that spot a while back. It's short-wave stuff, mostly commercial. Plus it's all rusted out.
Zh: “不太可能。我不久前检查过那地方。那是个短波中继塔，基本上只有商用频率。此外，那座塔已经年久失修，锈迹斑斑。”

En: Build it ourselves! We built this nightclub, we can build anything!
Zh: “亲手造一个吧！咱们既然能建成这座夜总会，自然也能造出任何东西！”

En: Wait, *could* we build a radio transmitter ourselves?
Zh: “等等，我们*能不能*自己造一个无线电发射器？”

En: Egg is right. There's no other way.
Zh: “爱凡客说得对。没别的办法了。”

En: Yeah, maybe? The young man shrugs. "Tech-wise, radio's not the spookiest thing around. Hard part's finding an antenna *big enough* to align with those mega-low frequencies..."
Zh: 他耸了耸肩。“嗯，也许能行？从技术角度来说，无线电还不是最吓人的东西。难点在于找到*足够大的*天线和那些极低频率对齐……”

En: The radio relay tower is in bad shape. I don't think we can use it to reach Archer.
Zh: “无线电中继塔的状态不佳。我觉得那东西不能用来联络弓箭手号。”

En: This is a major setback. We're going to have to find some *other* way to generate those mega-long waves.
Zh: “这是一次重大挫折。咱们得找*其他*办法生成这些超长波。”

En: Come up with a site for a mega-longwave radio antenna.
Zh: 想出安置超长波无线电天线的地点。

En: Hard core aesthetics.
Zh: 接受了硬核审美。

En: Acquainted with local landmarks.
Zh: 熟知当地地标建筑。

En: Cavalry officer's perspective.
Zh: 骑兵军官的视角。

En: Magnetic Dice Set.
Zh: 磁性骰子套组。

En: Boring cop, boring ideas.
Zh: 无聊的警察，无聊的想法。

En: This is easy. Antennas are basically just tall things, right?
Zh: 这个简单。天线什么的不过是很高的东西，对吧？

En: We could use the steeple of *this* church. (Point up.)
Zh: “咱们可以利用*这座*教堂的尖顶。”（向上指。）

En: The young man looks straight up, following the line of your finger. A frown comes over his face.
Zh: 他顺着你指的方向抬头望去。随之皱起了眉头。

En: He's being uncharacteristically polite. He means they don't make *any* pinewood antennas. Because it's such an obviously bad idea, you see.
Zh: 他一反常态，表现得彬彬有礼。那句话的意思是世间*根本*没有松木制的天线。因为这主意显然烂到爆了，你有点自知之明吧。

En: Not likely to work. This whole church is made from pinewood. They don't make too many pinewood antennas.
Zh: “不太可行。这座教堂是由松木搭建而成的。世间可没有多少松木制的天线。”

En: Ours will be the first!
Zh: “咱们要敢为人先！”

En: Hmm, that's a good point, now that you mention it.
Zh: “嗯，听你这么一说，还真有道理。”

En: No, it won't be. There can't be a first of something that can't be.
Zh: “不，做不到的。没人抢先的原因是这不可行。”

En: He shrugs, as though the point were rather obvious.
Zh: 他耸了耸肩，好像这一点显而易见。

En: That's fine. You're just getting warmed up. Your next idea will *definitely* be better.
Zh: 无所谓。你只是在热身而已。你出的下一个主意*绝对*会比这个好。

En: Alright, brainstorming time. Remember, this is just the ideation phase. There are no *bad* ideas.
Zh: 好了，头脑风暴时间到了。记住，这只是构思阶段。没有*坏*点子。

En: Here's an idea: Maybe there's an antenna in the harbour we could use?
Zh: “我有个主意：也许港口会有咱们能用的天线？”

En: I've got it. Why don't we use the mast of a giant *boat*?
Zh: “我有主意了。咱们不妨利用一艘大*船*的桅杆吧？”

En: What, are you some kind of *aquatic cop* now?
Zh: “怎么，你又改行做*水上警察*了？”

En: I know the Wild Pines rep. She'll lend us her boat for sure.
Zh: “我认识野松公司的代表。她肯定会把她的船借给我们用。”

En: Let's just say I have an *extremely* wealthy patron.
Zh: “这么说吧，我有一位*富得流油*的赞助者。”

En: No way I'm getting on a boat owned by the Wild Pines. Everything about them gives off a real *shadowy* sine.
Zh: “我不上野松公司的贼船。那家公司的一切都散发出一股特别*阴森*的正弦波。”

En: If that's the case, though, not sure I want to get mixed up in your patron's boat business.
Zh: “就算是这样，我也不打算和你那位赞助人的船扯上关系。”

En: Oh, I get it. You're a *gigolo cop*, then. Makes sense...
Zh: “哦，我明白了。你是个*条子面首*。原来如此……”

En: Hmmm, this really didn't seem like it was going to be that hard. We'll just have to try again...
Zh: 唔，这事儿明明看起来没那么难的。咱们再试一次吧……

En: Something tells me Ms. Messier would be *less than thrilled* to hear you'd like to commandeer her boat so you can talk to a Coalition warship.
Zh: “我感觉梅西耶女士*不会乐于*听你大谈特谈征用她的船以联络联盟战舰的主意。”

En: You want to get the Union in on this plan? He gives you an incredulous look. "Is that a good idea?"
Zh: “你想让工会参与这个计划？”他满脸难以置信地盯着你。“你觉得这是个好主意？”

En: One-hundred percent. Evrart's an old friend of mine.
Zh: “百分百的好主意。艾弗拉特是我的老朋友。”

En: Hmmm, maybe not.
Zh: “嗯，要不还是算了吧。”

En: Yes, you two have grown *quite* close over the last few days. Always doing little *errands* for each other.
Zh: “是啊，你俩在最近这几天变得*相当*亲密。对彼此的*小恩小惠*从没断过。”

En: I'm not sure I'd use the word 'friendly' to describe Mr. Claire's attitude toward us.
Zh: “我不确定克莱尔先生对我们的态度能不能算是*友善*。”

En: I don't trust the fat man. Gives off a hard core würm sine, real manipulative, if you ask me.
Zh: “我不信任那个胖子。在我看来，他浑身散发着一股硬核的，蠕虫般的正弦波。”

En: Didn't think so. You gotta watch out for those guys. Give off a real *hostile* sine, if you ask me.
Zh: “我反对。你得警惕那群人。在我看来，他们散发着一股货真价实，*充满敌意*的正弦波。”

En: Your thinking about this has gotten way too rigid. Time to flip the whole *antenna paradigm* on its head.
Zh: 你关于这件事的思路实在是太僵化了。这种时候就要颠覆整个*天线的范式*。

En: Hear me out: What if we built the antenna *underground*?
Zh: “听我说：咱们把天线建到*地下*怎么样？”

En: Underground, huh? That *would* be pretty hard core...
Zh: “地下？那*必定*相当硬核……”

En: It would also be incredibly stupid. Detective, did you not take Elementary Radio Operations? I thought it was an RCM-wide requirement.
Zh: “那还必定愚不可及。警探，你不是接受过基础无线电操作培训吗？我以为那是RCM的必修课。”

En: Underground antenna! Waves so hard core only moles can hear them!
Zh: “地下天线！电波硬核到只能让鼹鼠听到！”

En: Egg makes a good point. An underground antenna's probably not the best way to contact your aerostatic.
Zh: “爱凡客言之有理。为了联络你要找的那艘静风舰，地下天线可能算不上是最佳途径。”

En: Nope, nothing. The idea factory is closed until further notice.
Zh: 没主意了，大脑空空。点子工厂停业整顿，开放时间另行通知。

En: Close your eyes. Unfold your mental map of Martinaise. It's morning. You're standing on the plaza before the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: 闭上双眼。在头脑中展开马丁内斯的地图。想象眼下正值清晨。你身处褴褛飞旋门外的广场……

En: The wind pushes a piece of tare across the cracked tiles. From the north comes the malicious laughter of two children...
Zh: 风把废品吹过一摊碎裂的地砖。北方传来两个孩子恶毒的笑声……

En: Turn to face the bay.
Zh: 转身面对海湾。

En: Turn to face the apartments.
Zh: 转身面对公寓。

En: Turn to face the traffic circle.
Zh: 转身面对环岛路。

En: Your eye follows the tiles west. You see a bench and a few twisted, pitiful trees. Then, just water...
Zh: 你的视线顺着地砖一路向西。那边有一条长椅和几棵枝干扭曲，长势可悲的树。之后就只剩水了……

En: Beyond the unkempt backyard rises a monstrous assemblage of concrete, plaster, and corrugated sheet metal...
Zh: 在一片狼藉的后院后面，耸立着一座由水泥，灰泥，金属瓦楞板构成的巨大建筑，造型骇人……

En: You turn around. The morning sun is blinding. You bring your hand to your brow and a great shadow materialises before you. A towering beast, frozen in mid-air...
Zh: 你转过身。清晨的阳光实在是晃眼。你把手搭在额头上，一道巨大的阴影呈现在面前。那是一头高耸入云的巨兽，在半空中一动不动……

En: Centaur-man is made of metal. With the right gear we could make it work.
Zh: “半人马是金属制的。只要有合适的设备，咱们就能让它派上用场。”

En: YEEEAGH! Connect the centaur to the lorries, unite the whole roundabout in hard core waves!
Zh: “耶！把半人马和卡車连起来，用硬核的电波统一整个环岛！”

En: Huh. Sounds like Egg's saying you could use some spare cables to rig up the stalled motor lorries and increase the wavelength...
Zh: “哈，爱凡客的意思是你可以通过备用的电缆把堵在一起的卡車连起来，这样就能增加波长……”

En: Effectively transforming the entire roundabout into a single, fractal antenna.
Zh: 实质上是把整个环岛变成一个分形天线。

En: Sounds like madness, but I think it could work.
Zh: “听起来很疯狂，但我觉得可行。”

En: Which would pretty much make the whole roundabout one giant antenna.
Zh: “这样基本上就是把整个环岛变成一个巨型天线。”

En: ONE ROUNDABOUT, ONE WAVELENGTH! HARD CORE!
Zh: “同一个环岛，同一个波长！硬核！”

En: About time, those other antennas were cramping my style.
Zh: “是时候了，其他天线只会扯我的后腿。”

En: We shall call it: The Herald of Destruction.
Zh: “咱们应该称之为：毁灭的先驱。”

En: As long as it gets the job done, I'm not interested in the details.
Zh: “只要能搞定就行。我不在意细节。”

En: Are you sure that's legal? I feel like I'm going to need to apologise to someone.
Zh: “你确定那样合法吗？我感觉自己得找个人赔礼道歉才行。”

En: That's... a very big antenna.
Zh: “那天线……还真够大的。”

En: It's gonna be the most hard core antenna in Martinaise, and probably all of Revachol West.
Zh: “那将成为整个马丁内斯最硬核的天线，很可能还会是整个西瑞瓦肖最硬核的。”

En: Pretty hard core, but maybe it would be better to go with something more positive?
Zh: “很硬核，但是不是应该起个更积极的名字？”

En: You're really going about this the wrong way, then. The details are the best part.
Zh: “那你可走错方向了。细节才是最精彩的。”

En: You're a lawman, lawman. Don't *you* get to decide what's legal and what isn't?
Zh: “你才是执法者，警官。合法不合法难道不是*你*说了算吗？”

En: Probably the biggest antenna in all of Revachol West. He seems very pleased with himself.
Zh: “这很可能是整个西瑞瓦肖最大的天线。”他好像对此非常满意。

En: We have the amps, we have the cables! We're almost ready! Yeeeagh!
Zh: “放大器在手，电缆也有！咱们即将准备就绪，耶！”

En: He's right. All we're missing now is the transceiver itself. Soon as you find one, we'll be all set.
Zh: “他说得对。咱们万事俱备，只欠收发器。只要你能找到，咱们就算准备就绪了。”

En: Where am I supposed to find a radio transceiver?
Zh: “我应该到哪里去找无线电收发器？”

En: (Point to Egg Head.) "Doesn't he have something we could use?"
Zh: （指向爱凡客。）“他是不是有什么咱们能用上的东西？”

En: Alright, I guess I need to find one of these transceivers then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好，那我就去找这种收发器吧。”（结束。）

En: Can't do it. We just got this place set up. Can't go risking Egg's most important equipment...
Zh: “不行。我们刚把这地方布置好。不能赌上爱凡客最重要的设备……”

En: I don't know. Buy one? Steal one? I don't know your cop ways.
Zh: “不知道。去买？去偷？我可不懂你们条子的手段。”

En: While you're gone, me and Egg will get the rest of the gear together. Meet us back here when you've got it.
Zh: “等你动身之后，我和爱凡客会把其他设备收拾好。你找到收发器就回这里找我们吧。”

En: And before you ask: No, you can't have the radio from my Kineema either.
Zh: “我知道你想说什么：不行，你别想用我那辆锐影上的设备。”

En: You know what, Kim? I'll find an even *better* radio on my own.
Zh: “怎么着，金？我凭自己也能找到*更好*的无线电。”

En: I highly doubt that. When I crank the volume on my DynaWave, it's like you're in the front row at the National Concert Hall.
Zh: “我怀疑你做不到。只要把动力波的音量开到最大，就会有坐在国家音乐厅的前排的体验。”

En: I didn't know *Speedfreaks FM* had a concert series.
Zh: “我还真不知道*瘾君子调频*还有音乐会系列节目。”

En: Alright, Kim, challenge accepted.
Zh: “行啊，金，我接受你的挑战。”

En: On second thought, this is childish. I'm just going to look for a regular radio.
Zh: “转念一想，这未免太幼稚了。我还是找个常规的无线电吧。”

En: Joke all you want, detective. Whatever radio you manage to scrounge up won't lay a *finger* on my set-up.
Zh: “随你怎么开玩笑，警探。你想方设法搞到的无线电绝对*比不上*我的设备。”

En: A cop-on-cop challenge, not bad. Let's see what you come up with... Though I have to say, cop cars and lorries usually pack the best equipment in town.
Zh: “条子之间的较量，不错嘛。看看你有多大本事吧……但我不得不承认，警車和卡車通常都配着全城最好的设备。”

En: He shrugs. "It's your transmission. A regular old transceiver should work fine, it just won't be as *hard core*, you know..."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“那是你要的信号传输。常规的旧式收发器足以搞定，只是那样不怎么*硬核*……”

En: So you had a talk with Andre, and now you want to discuss things with Noid? Good. Skin shows through the holes in the speedfreak's too-large sweater. In front of him -- an open toolbox full of carpentry tools and parts.
Zh: “所以你跟安德烈谈过了，现在又想找诺伊德了？很好。”过大的破洞毛衣露出了这个瘾君子的皮肤，在他面前——敞开的工具箱中满是木匠工具和零件。

En: It's good you talked to Andre first. Gave me time to get a reading on your *sine*. Can't really talk to people before you get a reading. He runs his hand through his hair, which is combed back in mock seriousness, and continues to fiddle with some gears.
Zh: “幸好你先跟安德烈谈过了，让我有时间去分析你的*正弦波*。分析完成之前不太方便跟人交流啊。”他用手捋了捋头发，假装一本正经地往后梳着，继续摆弄着几个齿轮。

En: You look like a woman with those earrings.
Zh: “你戴耳环的样子像个女人。”

En: Noid shakes to the beat. There are no tools in his hands at the moment. He appears to ignore you.
Zh: 诺伊德随着节奏摇摆起来。他手中暂时没有工具，看来是无视了你。

En: Okay, I'll speak to Andre. [Leave.]
Zh: “行吧，我去找安德烈。”[离开。]

En: Yes. But that's what makes it an achievement.
Zh: “没错，但这就是它成功的原因。”

En: Why are you so suspicious about everything?
Zh: “你为什么对每件事都疑神疑鬼的？”

En: It's short for *paranoid*.
Zh: “这是*偏执狂*的简称。”

En: My head is spinning. It's hard to keep up with the times.
Zh: “我开始头晕了。想要与时俱进真的太难了。”

En: It is.
Zh: “的确。”

En: Many non-Occidental cultures share a beat at their heart. Thus, they are closer to true, hard core life. There's just never been enough of them, and they had to rely on some extremely basic percussion.
Zh: “诸多非奥西登文明的心跳有着相同的节拍。因此，他们更接近真实，硬核的生活。但他们从未满足于此，所以需要借助一些极为基础的打击乐器表达自我。”

En: What if I want to speak to you first?
Zh: “如果我想先跟你谈呢？”

En: Seems awesome. (Nod in agreaance.)
Zh: “好像挺棒的。”（赞同地点点头。）

En: No need to get over-excited about it right now. Where were you?
Zh: 现在没必要对此过于兴奋。你在哪里？

En: A sticker... you mean the yellow one? Can you describe it to me?
Zh: “一张贴纸……是指黄色的那张吗？你能描述一下吗？”

En: I mean this one. (Show the sticker on your ledger.)
Zh: “我说的是这张。”（展示手册上的贴纸。）

En: Oh wow! Right on your cop ledger! You like it?
Zh: “哇哦！就贴在你的警察手册上！你喜欢吗？”

En: It's this one, are you familiar with it? (Show him the sticker inside the ledger.)
Zh: “就是这张，你认识吗？”（展示手册里的贴纸。）

En: Yeah, it's cool.
Zh: “是啊，挺不错的。”

En: It's super stupid, that's what it is. I think it reminded me of myself.
Zh: “极其愚蠢，就是这样。我认为这让我想到了自己。”

En: Just like that the speedfreak is right in your face, his eyes burning. His comrades look on worriedly.
Zh: 就像这样，这个瘾君子站在你的面前，他的眼睛在燃烧。他的同伴正忧心忡忡地看向这里。

En: It's very modern. I'd like to learn more about it.
Zh: “非常时髦，我想更了解它一点。”

En: Lord of the western plain! It is really happening, right here, the migrants are contaminating our youth!
Zh: 西部平原之王！它真的发生了，就在这里，移民在污染我们的年轻一辈！

En: Seems like my creation has found its way into the legal bureaucracy. What did you want to ask about it?
Zh: “看来我的作品已经走进法律部门了，你想问什么呢？”

En: Okay. It's a yellow circle -- the human face -- with X-es for eyes and a smile underneath. I think the X-es mean the guy is dead.
Zh: “好吧。那是张黄色圆圈——一张人脸——眼睛是两个X，嘴巴是微笑的样子。我认为X代表他已经死了。”

En: He considers this for a moment. "Yeah, that interpretation holds."
Zh: 他思考了一会儿。“是的，这种理解没错。”

En:
Why describe it, if you already know what I mean?	“如果你已经知道我在说什么了，为什么还要我描述一遍呢？”
0x0100001400002CA1	Alternate1	ICE / NOID	Noid	Maybe you're being too specific about it? Try consulting with your spinal cord... like before, when you pulled those primal dance moves. I'm sure the World was glad to see them again after all that time."
Zh: “也许你描述地有些太具体了。试着咨询一下你的脊髓吧……就像之前你跳的原始舞步一样。过了那么久之后，我肯定全世界都会很高兴再次见到它们。”

En: Maybe you're being too specific about it. Try consulting with your spinal cord.
Zh: “也许你描述地有些太具体了。试着咨询一下你的脊髓吧。”

En: So I can hear you do it. Sometimes the outside gaze helps us reflect on things.
Zh: “这样我就能听听你的看法。有时候，外界的目光能帮助我们反思。”

En: Noid, don't bother our guest with your games.
Zh: “诺伊德，别拿你的鬼把戏打扰我们的客人。”

En: That was vague. Dedication, something else, superlative...
Zh: “有点模糊。献身精神，之类的，很好的东西……”

En: Piss off, Andre, me and Mr. Cop are trying to discuss art.
Zh: “滚开安德烈，我跟警察先生正在谈论艺术呢。”

En: You shouldn't talk like that.
Zh: “你不应该那么说话的。”

En: Happy to help.
Zh: “乐意之至。”

En: Yeah, you should be more respectful.
Zh: “没错，你应该放尊重点。”

En: So you had a talk with Andre, and now you want to discuss things with Noid? He's stooped over his toolbox as usual, shuffling bolts around angrily.
Zh: “你已经跟安德烈谈过了，现在又想找诺伊德了？”他同往常一样弯着腰，怒气冲冲地搅动着工具箱中的螺钉。

En: I don't mind a little foul language here and there.
Zh: “我不介意时不时爆几句粗口。”

En: Right, noted, sorry. Anyway, what was it like?
Zh: “好的，我知道了，抱歉。总而言之，它长什么样？”

En: Cool. The ban on foulness, that's the moralists' plot to alienate us from our bodily functions so they can control us more fully...
Zh: “挺好。禁止低俗，那是道德家们的阴谋。让我们远离自己的生理功能，这样他们就能更全面的控制我们了……”

En: Thank you for clearing that up.
Zh: “谢谢你的说明。”

En: Anyhow, what was the sticker like?
Zh: “总而言之，那张贴纸长什么样子？”

En: Take care, Noid. [Leave.]
Zh: “保重，诺伊德。”[离开。]

En: Produce your ledger and show him the sticker.
Zh: 拿出你的手册，把贴纸给他看。

En: Produce your ledger and show him the sticker.
Zh: 拿出你的手册，把贴纸给他看。

En: I'm thinking maybe the smiling dead guy is a symbol for communism. It's also dead, but doesn't care.
Zh: “我在想也许那具笑着的尸体就是康米主义的象征。同样的死气沉沉，漠不关心。”

En: It's just a style, you know?
Zh: “就是一种风格，你懂的。”

En: An off-handed *sorry* if there ever was one.
Zh: 就算曾经有过道歉的话，也只是简单的一句*抱歉*。

En: The term *hard core* also denotes a strain of pornography that depicts penetration, did you know that?
Zh: “*硬核*这个词同样代表一系列描述插入的色情作品，你知道吗？”

En: Yeah!
Zh: “是啊！”

En: That's a pretty hard core coincidence, don't you think?
Zh: “那是个非常硬核的巧合，你不觉得吗？”

En: I'm not telling. Let me ask you something else.
Zh: “我不会说的，让我再问你一个问题。”

En: C'mon. Okay, then. He looks surprised and a bit disappointed. "You don't have to describe it. What did you want to know about the sticker?"
Zh: 他看起来有些惊讶和一点点失望：“拜托。那好吧，你不必描述它了。你想知道贴纸的什么事？”

En: You know what I think? I think *man*, *woman*, and *child* are arbitrary divisions which serve to bind humanity to serfdom.
Zh: “你知道我在想什么吗？我觉得*男人*、*女人*以及*儿童*都是将人类束缚于农奴制度之下的武断划分。”

En: Does this mean you are mentally ill?
Zh: “这意味着你有精神病吗？”

En:
Let's change the subject.	“我们换个话题吧。”
0x0100001400002DD4	Dialogue Text	ICE / NOID	Noid	*Mental illness* is a term the powers use to homogenize people. I think I don't reach mental illness. I am merely politically ill. A suspicious element."
Zh: “*精神疾病*是当权者用来把人们同质化的术语。我想我还没患上精神疾病，只是有些政治疾病。有些多疑。”

En: I guess one could write an entire treatise on the thing. But what for?
Zh: “我想人们可以就此写一篇论文了，但又是为了什么呢？”

En: The one with the bleached hair is not really pulling rank on his friend here. He's just trying to create the impression of himself as a reasonable, moderate leader figure.
Zh: 浅色头发那位并不是真的在作弄自己的朋友，他只是想给人留下一个讲道理的、温和的指挥的印象。

En: I think we've exhausted the subject for now.
Zh: “我想我们已经讨论得很彻底了。”

En: What are you suspicious of?
Zh: “你在怀疑什么？”

En: Oh, it'd be easier to list stuff I'm not suspicious of. I'm not suspicious of sound and colour. Mechanics and chemistry also have a trueness about them. Most anything else deceives. Wants to steal your life away.
Zh: “哦，把我不会怀疑的东西列出来会更容易些。我不会怀疑声音和颜色，力学与化学也具有相当的真实性。其余的绝大多数都是骗人的东西，想要窃取你的生命。”

En: You mentioned *true life*. What would that be like?
Zh: “你提到了*真实的生活*，那是什么样子的呢？”

En: And what are the *most* suspicious things?
Zh: “那*最*可疑的东西是什么呢？”

En: A life is true if it's free from fear and internal division among oneself. And others -- mankind has seeds of greatness in it. A germinal will come, a return to trueness. It will be hard core.
Zh: “如果没有恐惧与内心的纠葛，即为真实的生活。另外——人的心中留有伟大的种子。萌芽初生，回归本真。即为硬核。”

En: Hey, it kind of *does* look like you. He thinks for a moment. "Surely a sign of things to come. What did you want to ask about it?"
Zh: “嘿，它看上去*确实*有点像你。”他想了一会儿。“这肯定是个预兆，你想问什么呢？”

En: The original mistake was assuming that words have more being than bodies. That's what led us astray, far from our true lives. But we may yet find a way back.
Zh: “最初的错误是认为文字比肉身更具本真。这让我们误入歧途，远离了我们的真实生活。但或许我们还能找到回去的路。”

En: That's ridiculous.
Zh: “这也太荒谬了。”

En: Alright, I'll do that.
Zh: “那好吧，我会的。”

En: What's bad about cereal grain? Having enough food could be a precursor for greater things.
Zh: “麦片又有什么不好的？拥有足够的食物可能就是更伟大的事情的前兆。”

En: Yes, having food is a means to an end. But the Left never talks about the end, only the means. Caps are likewise suckers, constantly sleepless in worry.
Zh: “是的，拥有食物是达到目的的一种手段。但左翼分子从不谈论目的，只强调手段。资本主义者们也是同样的傻瓜，总是担心得睡不着觉。”

En: Talk to Andre. No point in standing here -- the sines are really out of tune. He glances at the others.
Zh: “去跟安德烈谈谈吧。没必要站在这里——正弦波完全跑调了。”他瞥了一眼其他人。

En: How would you go about *returning* to this true life?
Zh: “要如何才能*返回*真实的生活中去呢？”

En: Beats and bright lights to shatter falsehoods. Nerve impulses for the collective body. We are very much alike in basic structure. A hard enough beat would awaken everyone to a truer calling -- in unity!
Zh: “用节奏与明亮的灯光打破谎言。集合身体的神经冲动。我们有着相似的基本结构，一段足够激烈的节奏能够唤醒每个人真正的使命——融归一体！”

En: They'll come in handy for fixing the church exterior.
Zh: “它们会在修理教堂外部时派上用场的。”

En: The young man is dead serious about this.
Zh: 这个年轻人对此无比认真。

En: Was I right? Is it a dead guy smiling?
Zh: “我说的对吗？那是个死者的微笑吗？”

En: What's it supposed to be?
Zh: “它应该是什么？”
