En: So you're advocating a noise-based society?
Zh: “所以你是在倡导一个基于干扰信息的社会？”

En: These are my consumer items and my means of production. I enjoy them.
Zh: “这些都是我的消费品与生产工具，我很喜欢它们。”

En: What do you do with all that stuff?
Zh: “你用那些东西干什么？”

En: I should have known! You're taking after the kipts, that's why you're so wacky!
Zh: “我早该知道！你跟吉皮特简直一模一样，所以你才这么古怪！”

En: Enough now, Noid, you have had enough fun bothering our guest with your crazy theories. Leave him alone.
Zh: “够了，诺伊德，你疯狂的理论已经把我们的客人骚扰得够够的了。别去烦他了。”

En: I'd have a whole lot more stuff if I had a place where I could keep that stuff.
Zh: “如果我有足够的地方存放这些东西，我会拥有更多东西。”

En: Yes. You're the twenty-third person to get it right. And I've asked twenty-three people. Looks like it's a dead guy smiling to the entire human race.
Zh: “没错，你是第23位能正确理解的人，而我已经问过23个人了。看起来就像一个死者在对着整个人类族群微笑。”

En: A dead guy smiling.
Zh: “一个死者在微笑。”

En: Well, that was certainly stimulating. I want to ask you about something else now.
Zh: “这个嘛，真是相当的刺激。现在我想问你另一件事。”

En: It's a bit hypnotizing if you look at it long enough.
Zh: 如果你看的足够久，就会觉得它有点催眠。

En: His eyes flicker.
Zh: 他的目光闪烁着。

En: You have a whole lot of stuff.
Zh: “你有很多东西。”

En: Utmost dedication. Thoughts from the spinal cord. It's a potent superlative as well.
Zh: “最崇高的献身。来自脊髓的想法。它同样也是一种强有力的崇高。”

En: You're right, it's the harbinger of a new era. What would you like to know?
Zh: “你说得对，这是一个新时代的预兆。你想知道什么？”

En: Nod.
Zh: 点头。

En: Noid is a bit disappointed it's *just* cool.
Zh: 你认为它*仅是*挺好的，让诺伊德有点失望。

En: Rejection of the right-left axis, emphasis on *unity*, appreciation of some primordial mode of being -- what does that remind you of?
Zh: 拒绝左与右的倾向，强调*团结*，欣赏一些原始的存在模式——这让你想到了什么？

En: I have all kinds of screws. He holds up a handful.
Zh: “我有各种各样的螺钉。”他捧起一把。

En: Sort of like fascism then?
Zh: “有点像法西斯主义？”

En: Nationalism, militarism, racism, and emphasis on a leader character are totally absent in *hard core*. The words echo through the church majestically.
Zh: “民族主义、军国主义、种族主义以及对于领袖人物的强调统统都会被*硬核*摒弃。”这句话在教堂中庄严地回响着。

En: The contents of the young man's toolbox are organized in a neat, yet eclectic manner. He is constantly checking out the various tools and parts and rearranging them slightly...
Zh: 这个年轻人工具箱里的东西以一种整洁而兼收并蓄的方式排列着。他不断地检查着各种工具和零件，并稍作调整……

En: Fair enough. Just making an observation.
Zh: “有道理，我只是观察一下。”

En: I didn't mean it as a bad thing.
Zh: “我并不是说这是件坏事。”

En: Just stand there and observe him.
Zh: 就站在那里观察他。

En: He picks up a wrench and scratches his head with it, unaroused by fascism.
Zh: 他拿起一把扳手，用它搔了搔脑袋。对于法西斯主义表现得无动于衷。

En: It is cool. But it's also more than that. *Much* more.
Zh: “挺不错的，但还不止于此。*远*不止于此。”

En: Maybe you should just stand there, watch him rattle his toolbox. Get to know *his* 'sine.'
Zh: 或许你就应该站在那里，看着他摆弄工具箱，分析*他的*正弦波。

En: I suck at socializing, man. If you go speak to Andre, I'll have time to *tune in*. Get a reading on your *sine*. Before he's finished talking, he's already turned back to his tools.
Zh: “我非常不擅长社交，兄弟。如果你去找安德烈，我就有时间*调和*了，分析你的*正弦波*。”他话还没说完，就又回去摆弄自己的工具了。

En: Good, good. He likes what he's hearing. "What did you want to know?"
Zh: “很好，很好。”他很满意自己听到的。“你想知道什么？”

En: I saw a sticker on the padlock. Can you tell me anything about it?
Zh: “我看到挂锁上有张贴纸，你能给我说说吗？”

En: Okay... what does it mean?
Zh: “好吧……它有什么含义？”

En: And what does it mean? Why is the dead guy smiling?
Zh: “还有它有什么含义？为什么一个死者在微笑呢？”

En: He defeated History. We are living in the age of History, and in the eyes of History we are always already dead. How can we ever smile, then? Because History is a lie, and so are its deaths...
Zh: “他击败了历史。我们活在历史的时代中，而在历史眼中，我们早已经逝去。那我们如何还能微笑呢？因为历史就是一个谎言，它的死亡同样如此……”

En: The present moment and life are the hard core. The hard core expels death.
Zh: “当下以及生命即为硬核，而硬核能驱散死亡。”

En: He thinks. "Or maybe he's not dead, maybe he's just really ecstatic about the beats."
Zh: 他想到。“或许他并没有死去，也许他只是对节奏感到欣喜若狂了。”

En: Or drugged out of his mind, come to think of it.
Zh: 仔细想想，又或许他是被麻醉了。

En: He could also be drugged out of his mind.
Zh: “他也可能是被下了药，神志不清了。”

En: Or drunk, or in a clinical coma, or glad to be dead, but those versions suck.
Zh: “或是喝醉了，或是处于临床昏迷中，又或是乐意赴死，但这些说法烂透了。”

En: What makes the sticker so modern?
Zh: “为什么这张贴纸这么现代？”

En: Simplicity was brought to us by classical Solaist modernism, but that was a *tasteful*, harmonious simplicity, right?
Zh: “简约是经典的西奥现代主义带给我们的，但那是一种*雅致*、和谐的简约，不是吗？”

En: You keep mentioning *hard core*. What does it mean to you?
Zh: “你一直在说*硬核*，这对你来说意味着什么？”

En: Well, hard core is not tasteful or outwardly harmonious. It's a warning shot: 'This will be dangerous.' The echo of man's loss, haunting him.
Zh: “嗯，硬核并不雅致，其表象也并不和谐。这是一个警示：‘这会很危险。’人类失败的回声将永远萦绕在他们的心头。”

En: The term also signifies certain varieties of pornography that depict penetration, just so you know.
Zh: 这个词同样代表某些描述插入的系列色情作品，顺便告诉你一下。

En: The sticker, the clothes, the music -- same thing.
Zh: “贴纸、衣服、音乐——都一样。”

En: Egg Head actually has a better concept of the hard core. He just really likes saying 'hard core'.
Zh: “爱凡客其实对硬核有一个更好的概念，他就是喜欢说*硬核*。”

En: You come up with this stuff by yourself?
Zh: “这些东西是你自己琢磨出来的吗？”

En: Hard core! His friend shouts from behind his mix table, with a smile surpassing your own in wideness, a total moon-face, and eyes full of naive wonderment.
Zh: “硬核！”他的朋友在混音台后面叫着，脸上的笑容比你更加灿烂。完全是一张圆圆的脸，眼睛里满是天真的惊奇。

En: Not alone. Many people are thinking the same thing right now. We all see the same smiling dead guy in a couple of X-es and a circle. And a curve. The beat is the same for all.
Zh: “不是我一个人，现在很多人都在想同样的事情。我们都能在几个X，一个圆圈和一道弧线中看到微笑的死者。每个人的节奏都是一样的。”

En: Not alone. Many people are thinking the same thing right now. There's a gathering at The Paliseum. The beat is the same for all.
Zh: “不只有我一个。现在很多人都在想同样的事情。在帕里瑟姆有个集会，那里每个人的节奏都是一样的。”

En: But shouldn't the ones that are more hard core rule over the ones that are less hard core, offer them guidance?
Zh: “难道那些更加硬核的人不应该统治那些不那么硬核的人吗，为他们提供指引？”

En: Noid -- what do you think about the church?
Zh: “诺伊德——你觉得这个教堂怎么样？”

En: What do you think about this church?
Zh: “你觉得这个教堂怎么样？”

En: If some want to lead and others want to be led, I think they are welcome to it.
Zh: “如果有人想要领导，有人想要被领导，我随时欢迎他们。”

En: What's with the clothes?
Zh: “衣服是怎么回事？”

En: He shrugs. "They're hard core."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“它们很硬核。”

En: Dedicated. Hyperactive. Unified. You will have to add something of your own to understand this list of loosely formed qualities called hard core. You need your own entry. Make it!
Zh: 奉献、活跃、团结。你必须在这一连串组织松散的品质中添加自己的一些东西，来理解这个被称为硬核的品质。你需要建立自己的条目，动手吧！

En: Internalize the hard core aesthetic. Don't just nod along, really *feel* it.
Zh: 内在化硬核美学。不要只是点头附和，真正地*感受*它。

En: Hardened up your ledger.
Zh: 让你的手册更有范儿。

En: Caught the key.
Zh: 接住了钥匙。

En: Necktie is on.
Zh: 装备领带。

En: Analysed the beat.
Zh: 分析了节奏。

En: Perikarnassianism is Love.
Zh: 佩里卡纳西斯主义就是爱。

En: Aggressive. Monotonous... but also somehow sacral. Primitive, yet futuristic, like a machine man. Nothing exists, but the here and now. All are one, one purpose... All you've managed is a list. The parts don't form a whole.
Zh: 好斗、沉闷……但也是神圣的。原始，却又很未来，正如一个机器人。除了此时此刻，什么都不存在。一切归一，一个目的……你所做的只是一张列表。这些部分并不能构成一个整体。

En: The young man picks up a thin metal saw blade and nods.
Zh: 年轻人拿起一片薄薄的金属锯片，点了点头。

En: See? It's already going wrong. Sine is off. The young man picks up a thin metal saw blade and examines it.
Zh: “看到了吗？已经出问题了，正弦波失效了。”年轻人拿起一片薄薄的金属锯片，仔细地看着。

En: Go speak to Andre. I'm just the Noid, a strangely dressed young man says without looking up from his toolbox.
Zh: “去跟安德烈谈吧，我只是个偏执狂而已。”一个穿着怪异的年轻人盯着自己的工具箱，头也不抬地说。

En: Nope. Politics is an inert complex of daily corruption and inane thinkpieces. The real paradigm is economic and it concerns pig and wheat.
Zh: “不。政治就是一个由日常腐败与空洞的思想组成的惰性综合体。真正的范式应该是经济，这又与猪肉和小麦息息相关。”

En: This is where the innovation happens. It's only a theory, but I suspect they're breeding a pig/wheat hybrid. Probably in Graad.
Zh: “这就是革新发生的时刻。这只是一个想法，但我怀疑他们在培育一种猪肉/小麦的杂交品种。大概就在格拉德。”

En: It's our only shit. He shrugs. "We should make better use of not being animals -- or cereal grain -- ourselves."
Zh: “这是我们仅有的了。”他耸了耸肩。“我们应该发挥人类并非动物——并非谷物——的更好价值。”

En: What's bad about animals? Animals are cute.
Zh: “动物有什么不好的？动物很可爱啊。”

En: Nothing, animals are cool guys, I like animals too. But that doesn't mean I have to be one myself.
Zh: “没什么，动物们的确挺不错的，我也很喜欢它们。但这并不意味着我要成为它们中的一员。”

En: Yeah. It's our only shit and we fucking suck at it!
Zh: “是啊，这是我们仅剩的了，而我们他妈的烂透了！”

En: He can't help but doubt your sincerity in this matter. There is no other way -- you are a police officer.
Zh: 他不禁开始怀疑你在这件事情上的诚意了。这也别无选择——你是一名警官。

En: He gives you a long, suspicious look. "Correct."
Zh: 他向你投去了怀疑的目光。“没错。”

En: Don't assume I like the status quo, because I'm a cop.
Zh: “不要因为我是个警察，就以为我喜欢现状。”

En: Precisely! The status quo doesn't need to be loved.
Zh: “正是如此！现状根本不需要被爱！”

En: (Look him straight in the eye:) "Shit, piss, cunts, dicks, f****ts! Fuck the World!"
Zh: （直视他的眼睛：）“他妈的，该死的，婊子，混蛋，大傻逼！去他妈的世界！”

En: The young man looks startled.
Zh: 年轻人看起来很吃惊。

En: I was being sarcastic. This world has its fair share of problems, but it's not like you guys are going to solve them.
Zh: “我是在开嘲讽。这个世界有各种各样的问题，但也不是你们能够解决的。”

En: Neither will anyone else.
Zh: “其他人也不行。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: The young man doesn't say anything either. After a while he takes out a magnet and runs it over some nails in front of him.
Zh: 年轻人同样没有说话。过了一会儿，他拿出一块磁铁，对着他面前的钉子挪动起来。

En: Whatever this *true life* is, you feel it's the real centrepiece of this mythology.
Zh: 不管这个*真实生活*到底是什么，你都觉得它是这番神话的真正核心。

En: What about now? Are the sines all right now?
Zh: “现在呢？正弦波都还好吗？”

En: Na... huh. Still strongly out of sync. Stage gamma dis-alignment.
Zh: “啧……哼。仍然极其不同步，γ偏离了轴心。”

En: May I ask why? What good is being suspicious?
Zh: “我能问问原因吗？多疑有什么用呢？”

En: A reasonable question. Say, I get hurt. I want to make sure it never happens again. So I analyse the situation. Exercise caution. Caution is suspicion.
Zh: “很合理的问题。比如说我受伤了，我要确保这种事不会再发生。所以我分析了事态，审时度势。谨慎即为多疑。”

En: Oh yeah. Sure thing.
Zh: “哦耶。当然可以。”

En: Hard people. Hard republic. Hard cell. Hard party. Hard riot. Hard government. Core membership. Core secretary. Core teachings. Core fighters.
Zh: 强硬的人民、强硬的共和国、强硬的监牢、强硬的集会、强硬的暴乱。核心的成员、核心的部长、核心的教义、核心的斗士。

En: Predictable, but solid.
Zh: 能够预见，但很可靠。

En: This is taking place in Revachol, hard core rising. It could only happen here, in the bottom of the geopolitical abyss. Those are the warhorns of today. We'll be given a fresh start.
Zh: 就在瑞瓦肖，硬核已经抬头了。也只有可能在这里，地缘政治深渊的最底层。那就是今天战争的号角，我们会有一个新的开始。

En: Oh yeah it feels manly. Beautiful.
Zh: 感觉非常有男子气概，漂亮。

En: Long live the world that gave shape to hard core to complete itself! A true heir to Dolorian values. Three-and-a-half centuries and the gift still keeps on giving.
Zh: 塑造出硬核的这个世界万岁！德洛莉丝风格的真正继承者。三个半世纪过去了，这份馈赠仍然闪耀着。

En: Endless are the wonders borne out of the cultural collaboration so wisely facilitated by the free world!
Zh: 自由世界如此明智地促进了文化交融，由此产生的奇迹是无穷无尽的！

En: I've got the money, I've got the place/ You've got the figure, you've got the face/ Let's get together, we're jumping all over the world!
Zh: 我有钱，还有地/你有头，还有脸/你我联手，称霸世界！

En: JUMPING ALL OVER THE WORLD!
Zh: 称霸世界！

En: Test your limits. Surpass them. Dance till you drop. Dance till you die, if you must.
Zh: 测试你的极限。跨越它们。跳到你倒下。如果有必要的话，跳到你死去为止。

En: I am going to truly wreck myself as a machine of modern party in a final showdown between flesh and spirit!
Zh: 我要在肉体和精神的最后决战中，真正让自己成为一台现代派对机器。

En: That's a good way to go.
Zh: 这是个好办法。

En: I am the law.
Zh: 我就是法律。

En: Start the war (Hey! Hey!) Hit the floor! (Hey! Hey! ) We need more (Hey! Hey!) Army of hard core! Yea.
Zh: 发动战争（嘿！嘿！）掷地有声！（嘿！嘿！）索求更多！（嘿！嘿！）硬核战队！耶。

En: I'm thinking of wearing sports apparel as my hard core getup to maximize the mobility of limbs.
Zh: 我在考虑将运动服作为我的硬核装备，最大限度地增强四肢的灵活性。

En: Jumping around, as this sort of music requires, is actually a taxing physical activity and should be dignified with a sports suit.
Zh: 跳来跳去，如同这种音乐所要求的那样。这其实是种很费力的体力活动，应该配套运动服。

En: You might as well make it complete with a whistle, then.
Zh: 那么，不如你再配上个口哨吧。

En: I want to have fuck with you.
Zh: 我想跟你上床。

En: Say that again?
Zh: 再说一遍？

En: I want to have fuck with you!
Zh: 我想跟你上床！

En: You know it, baby!
Zh: 你懂的，宝贝！

En: Overwhelm them with moves. Try to have the most moves going on.
Zh: 用招式压倒他们，试着打出更多招式。

En: Thinking of cornering the moves market. They won't know what hit 'em.
Zh: 试着把动作市场逼入绝境。他们不会反应过来的。

En: Yeah, you should even try to put moves inside your moves.
Zh: 没错，你甚至应该试着在你的招式中套入招式。

En: SEE THE SUNSHINE/ WE AIN'T STOPPING/ KEEP ON DANCING/ TILL THE WORLD ENDS!
Zh: 看着阳光/我们不停歇/继续跳舞/直至世界尽头！

En: IF YOU FEAR IT, LET IT HAPPEN.\nKEEP ON DANCING TILL THE WORLD ENDS.
Zh: 若你害怕，就顺其自然吧！\n继续跳舞，直至世界尽头！

En: You know it feels unfair, there's a party everywhere.
Zh: 你知道这很不公平，到处都有派对。

En: That level of conceptual thinking is not part of your skill set.
Zh: 这种水平的概念思维并不是你的技能之一。

En: Your conceptualization accepts this in solemn admiration and gratitude.
Zh: 你的概念在郑重的赞赏和感激中接受了这一点。

En: I don't seem to have anything to add to the core.
Zh: 我似乎没有什么要加进核心的东西了。

En: So you've come empty-handed and pure, without burdens. A hard-core supplicant. That will have to do.
Zh: 所以你真的是空手而来，没有任何负担。一个硬核的乞求者，就这么着吧。

En: Something is off here... you feel like it should be the other way around.
Zh: 有些不对劲……你感觉应该一切应该正好相反才对。

En: It's not easy to reach a harmonic resonance of sines without some adjustment.
Zh: 如果不作适当调整，很难达到正弦的谐波共振。

En: Better not mention it.
Zh: 最好还是不要提。

En: He blinks, waiting for the next question.
Zh: 他眨眨眼，等待下一个问题。

En: You can grab that amplifier, officer. He gestures to the lieutenant. "And you start spooling up those cables, maybe we can get it all in one go..."
Zh: “你可以把扩音器拿上，警官。”他对警督做了个手势。“把那些电线卷起来的话，说不定我们可以一次就搞定……”

En: Now give me a hand with these cables. It's a long walk to the roundabout...
Zh: ”帮我弄一下电线吧。从这里到环岛还要走很久……“

En: Alright, cop-man. We've held up more than our share of this collaboration. I hope you *recognise* how much the hardcore underground came through for you.
Zh: “好吧，条子。我们已经为这次合作尽力了。希望你能*认识到*地下硬核组织为你付出了多少。”

En: Well, cop-man, we've re-paid the favour. Guess this cements the cop-hardcore alliance.
Zh: “好吧，条子，我们已经报答了你的恩情。这应该会巩固条子与硬核的联盟吧。”

En: YEEEAGH. WE ARE COPS NOW.
Zh: “耶。咱们都是警察了。”

En: Easy, Egg. We're still hardcore music venue organisers, first and foremost. But maybe this *does* make us pillars of the local community...
Zh: “别激动，爱凡客。咱们的首要身份仍然是硬核音乐会所的组织者。不过这件事*还真有可能*把咱们变成了本地社区的支柱……”

En: I'll put this tech away later. Think there's an extra can set, in case you want to grab a *souvenir* or something. Don't think anyone will miss it.
Zh: “我会把这套设备收起来。如果你想*留个念*的话，还可以随时摆出来。不过我觉得没人会想念这个的。”

En: If you want to show your appreciation, we could still use your help. Egg is the one to talk to.
Zh: “如果你想道谢的话，我们正好需要你的帮助。去找爱凡客聊聊吧。”

En: HARD CORE! MUTUAL AID!
Zh: “硬核！友爱互助！”

En: I would never let my hardcore brothers down.
Zh: “我绝不会让我的硬核兄弟们失望。”

En: It's been good working with you... gentlemen.
Zh: “和你们合作很愉快……先生们。”

En: I've got my own case to worry about, but if I have time, I'll be back.
Zh: “我还得忙自己的案子，如果我有时间，还会再回来的。”

En: Sure, I've heard that before...
Zh: “是啊，我听说过……”

En: No need to fake it, we know you're still a cop at heart...
Zh: “没必要装腔作势，我们都知道你骨子里还是条子……”

En: Sure, you have to say that now, but it's alright...
Zh: “当然，你现在是不得不说那种话，没关系……”

En: Acele's right. He's one of us now.
Zh: “阿西尔说得对。他已经是自己人了。”

En: Acele's right. He's one of us now.
Zh: “阿西尔说得对。他已经是自己人了。”

En: Good luck, guys.
Zh: “祝好运，伙计们。”

En: Try to squeeze in a promo spot while you're on-air, we've got to start putting the word out.
Zh: “我们打算在你直播期间插播一段广告，也该宣传一下了。”

En: Could you use your *sine-sense* to help me contact a Coalition warship?
Zh: “你能不能通过*感知正弦波*的能力帮我联系上联盟的战舰？”

En: Don't think so. Big Bad frequencies are extremely negative: thought suppression, dream implantation, memory revision. Pretty *out there* stuff, not sure I want to get involved with it.
Zh: “我反对。大坏蛋的频率消极至极：抑制思考，植入梦境，修改记忆。都是相当*离谱*的手段，我可不打算和那些扯上关系。”

En: There is fear there, but also curiosity. He just needs a *reason* to help you.
Zh: 他的语气流露出一股恐惧，但同时还有好奇。他只需要一个帮你的*理由*。

En: Besides, our own sines aren't even synced yet. So how am I supposed to get *you* synced up with the Big Bad? It doesn't make any sense, law-lover.
Zh: “此外，你与我的正弦波还没同步呢。这让我怎么把*你*和大坏蛋同步起来？根本没道理嘛，法律爱好者。”

En: The voice again, more urgent now: 'Nay!'
Zh: 你又一次听到了那个声音，这次显得更紧迫了：“不！”

En: Just a moment. That voice isn't human, and it's not saying 'Nay!' -- It's saying 'N-n-n-neigh!'
Zh: 稍等。那不是人类发出的声音，也不是在说“不！”——那声音是“咴儿——咴儿——！”

En: Maybe not that big, but pretty big.
Zh: “也许没那么大，但相当大。”

En: An antenna hard core enough to shake the whole world!
Zh: “一根硬核到足以撼动整个世界的天线！”

En: The statue of Fillipe III.
Zh: “菲利普三世的雕像。”

En: The horseback monument.
Zh: “马背纪念碑。”

En: From behind you, a low, spectral voice whispers: 'Nay.'
Zh: 你身手传来一声低沉，幽灵般的低语：“不。”

En: The young speedfreak gives you a suspicious look before returning to... whatever he was doing.
Zh: 年轻的瘾君子满腹狐疑地看了你一眼，然后又回头去……不知道在干嘛了。

En: You sure that's a good idea? Those are some serious frequencies you want to mess with...
Zh: “你确定这是个好主意？你打算招惹的这些频率可够正经的……”

En: Like Egg said.
Zh: “正如爱凡客所说。”

En: You'll probably wanna take a minute to get your *gear situation* sorted out.
Zh: “你可能需要一点时间去整理一下*设备状况*。”

En: His look is intensely suspicious.
Zh: 他的神情充满了强烈的怀疑。

En: This young speedfreak seems to know a lot about 'sines'... could he be the techno-tinkerer you're looking for?
Zh: 这个年轻的瘾君子似乎相当了解*正弦波*……难道他就是你要找的技术能手？

En: This young speedfreak seems to know a lot about 'sines'... could he have the technical skill you're looking for?
Zh: 这个年轻的瘾君子似乎相当了解*正弦波*……难道他具备你正在寻求的技术？

En: This young speedfreak seems to know a lot about 'sines'... could he be the techno-tinkerer you're looking for?
Zh: 这个年轻的瘾君子似乎相当了解*正弦波*……难道他就是你要找的技术能手？

En: This young speedfreak seems to know a lot about 'sines'... could he have the technical skill you're looking for?
Zh: 这个年轻的瘾君子似乎相当了解*正弦波*……难道他具备你正在寻求的技术？

En: You really should look into this totalled MC when you have a chance. Seems important...
Zh: 等你有机会，真应该去调查一下这辆报废的車。这好像相当重要……

En: At the very least, you should make sure you're wearing a good pair of gloves.
Zh: 你最起码得确保自己戴了一副不错的手套。

En: Do radiocomputers have transceivers in them?
Zh: “无线电脑里有收发器吗？”

En: Yeah, probably. Thing is you'd have to be an engineer to get it out. I'd stick with *regular radios*, if I were you.
Zh: “可能有吧。问题是只有工程师能把那东西取出来。如果是我要找的话，我更倾向于去找*常规*的无线电。”

En: I think I saw a radio in that office near the harbour...
Zh: “我好像在港口附近的办公室里见过一部无线电……”

En: Hmm, some equipment from harbour could work. Worth the leg-work, at least.
Zh: “嗯，港口确实有些设备能派上用场。至少值得跑一趟。”

En: Do you think the pawnshop will have one?
Zh: “你觉得当铺会有吗？”

En: Maybe, if you want to pay money like some kind of... money lord. Yeah, pawnshop could have one.
Zh: “可能有吧。前提是你花起钱来就像……财主一样。是的，当铺里可能会有无线电。”

En: A radio transceiver. Cool. I've got this. I've seen *plenty* of those lying around.
Zh: “无线电收发器，酷。交给我吧。我见过*不少*那种东西，简直遍地都是。”

En: Exactly. You could buy one, you could steal one...
Zh: “是啊。你可以买一台，也可以偷一台……”

En: *Lorries*, huh? You could check out the abandoned one by the roundabout...
Zh: *卡車*，嗯？你可以去检查一下环岛那边被弃置的那辆……

En: Conjuring terrible visions of the future, which you apparently don't care for.
Zh: 幻想着可怕的未来，而你显然并不在乎。

En: This is for your posse. They got to keep it up. They got to move. You are the major mic enforcer.
Zh: 这是为了你的老铁们。他们必须坚持下去。他们必须行动起来。你才是最重要的麦克风执行者。

En: Tell me about the machines you saw in the church.
Zh: “跟我说说你在教堂里看到的那些机器吧。”

En: He cringes. "Weird stuff. Specialized. There was a data processor and some sort of long-wave machinery."
Zh: 他有些畏缩。“怪里怪气的，很专业。有一台数据处理器和某种长波机器。”

En: What's wrong with the supra-natural?
Zh: “超自然怎么了？”

En: The People's Republic of Samara (SRV) is a product of Revachol's sister revolution on the isola of Graad. It's known as a severely degenerated rogue state.
Zh: 萨马拉人民共和国（SRV）是瑞瓦肖的姐妹革命在格拉德洲取得的成果。这是个臭名昭著的流氓国家。

En: Wires going into water. Gives off a spy sine, or some fucked up Samaran science sine... you know, the kind that goes head first into the supra-natural.
Zh: “铁丝浸入水中。放射出一种监视正弦波，或是某些搞砸了的萨马拉科学正弦波……你知道的，就是那种直接进入超自然状态的。”

En: I'm privy to the supra-natural arts. You a believer too? (Proceed.)
Zh: “我私下里对超自然艺术是知情的。你也是一个信仰者吗？”（继续。）

En: The supra-natural... so you think it's real, that it actually exists? (Proceed.)
Zh: “超自然……所以你认为那是真的，真实存在的？”（继续。）

En: Don't be too hard on Samara, they're all alone in the world. Half of what we know of them is just propaganda.
Zh: “别对萨马拉那么苛刻，他们是个世界孤儿。我们对他们大部分的了解只限于宣传。”

En: I know, man, I was just talking about the aesthetic.
Zh: “我明白的，兄弟，我只是在说美学。”

En: The sickest. That is perhaps *why* it should be researched.
Zh: 最恶心的那种。那可能就是*为什么*要研究它的原因。

En: Psionic powers, pale-related diseases, *pretenders* pretending to be human, folk rites, that kind of stuff.
Zh: “超能力、灰域相关的疾病、人类的*伪装者*、民俗仪式、诸如此类的。”

En: Most of it doesn't exist, but there's also stuff that isn't *allowed* to exist because the moralists think it's too *dangerous* for the plebes.
Zh: “大部分都不存在，但也有些东西的存在是不被*允许*的，因为道德学家们认为它们对平民来说太*危险*了。”

En: Nothing's wrong with it. It should definitely be researched. You can still do sick shit with it, though.
Zh: “那没有任何问题，绝对值得研究。不过你还是可以用它来做些厉害的玩意。”

En: Why are you called 'Noid'?
Zh: “你为什么叫‘诺伊德’（偏执狂）？”

En: The hard core aesthetic is esoteric. It is not meant to be discussed with the law at this moment. He picks up some sort of a widget.
Zh: 他拿起一个小工具。“硬核美学是非常深奥的，现在不是用法律来探讨这个问题的时候。”

En: Does this mean... we need sine matching?
Zh: “这是否意味着……我们需要正弦匹配？”

En: Yes, further sine-matching would do good for us. One way to achieve this would be by getting us into the church.
Zh: “是的，进一步的正弦匹配对你我都好。实现这一目标的方法之一就是让我们进入教堂。”

En: I don't understand what that means.
Zh: “我不明白那是什么意思。”

En: I can see that. Further sine-matching would do good for us. One way to achieve sine-synchronicity would be by getting us into the *church*.
Zh: “我能看出来。进一步的正弦匹配对你我都好。实现正弦同步的方法之一就是让我们进入*教堂*。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: You heard me. He examines the small metal bolt in his hand.
Zh: 他检查着手中的小金属螺栓。“你听到我说的了。”

En:
Okay, maybe I'll come back later. [Leave.]	“好吧，或许我还会再来的。”[离开。]
0x0100005A0000A18B	Dialogue Text	ICE / NOID	Noid	Huh, I see you're here again, off-sine-man." He rummages through his tools. "Did I mention getting us into the church would help?"
Zh: “哈，你又来了，正弦失衡的人。”他翻动着自己的工具。“我有说过带我们进去教堂会有帮助吗？”

En: Suspicious people are esoteric people. We don't go around spilling everything to Johnny Law. They don't call me Noid for nothing.
Zh: “多疑的人都是神秘的。我们不会随便把事情泄露给法律人员，他们喊我诺伊德不是白叫的。”

En: Yo, man. What's on your mind? He drops a bolt into his toolbox.
Zh: “嘿，兄弟，你在想什么？”他往工具箱里扔了一枚螺钉。

En: It's not easy to reach a harmonic resonance of sines without some adjustment.
Zh: 如果不作适当调整，很难达到正弦的谐波共振。

En: What did Tiago tell you about the church?
Zh: “蒂亚戈跟你说教堂的事了吗？”

En: The crab-man's been lurking here for a while. He's experienced things. Things that give off *bad sines*. As far as we can tell, the Ubis built this place about three hundred and twenty years ago as a sarcophagus.
Zh: “蟹男已经在这里蛰伏了一段时间了，他历经过会产生*负面正弦波*的重重磨难。据我们所知，这个地方是由尤比人在三百八十年前打造的石棺。”

En: What's it for?
Zh: “用来做什么的？”

En: Encasement. Confinement. Of something they were afraid of. Something new and unheard of on the isola. He looks up, into the darkness  beyond the beams. "I think that's what the crab-man is experiencing when he climbs around upstairs..."
Zh: “收容、监禁，某种他们恐惧的东西，某种在这个洲闻所未闻的新奇事物。”他抬头望着横梁后的黑暗。“我猜那就是蟹男爬上楼梯时的感受吧……”

En: Like, this is some old world shit the Ubis had heard about... and thought the best way to deal with it was to build a church. Surrounding it. To contain it.
Zh: “比如，尤比人听过的一些关于旧世界的屁话……认为最好的解决办法就是建造一座教堂，围绕着它，牵制着它。”

En: I don't get it, contain what exactly?
Zh: “我不明白，是要牵制什么？”

En: I dunno... and it's not something they properly understood either. What it does. But it's what this Soona person is looking for, and trying to measure. He nods toward the woman.
Zh: “我也不知道……可能他们也无法完全理解。但这正是那个叫苏娜的女人在寻找并试图衡量的东西。”他朝那个女人点了点头。

En: It'll be fruitless, though. She won't be able to measure it. People like that always want to measure everything, all those things they really can't.
Zh: “不过，这终将是徒劳，她是无法衡量它的。像那样的人总是想衡量一切，那些他们遥不可及的东西。”

En: The wood creaks as a gale blows by outside. Dust particles fall through the darkness, settling down on the age-bleached floor boards. The structure does not feel particularly durable.
Zh: 大风从外面刮过，木头嘎吱作响。黑暗之中的尘埃，落在褪色多年的地板上。这座建筑给人感觉并不十分耐用。

En: This building seems... less than structurally sound.
Zh: “这座建筑感觉……似乎不太牢固。”

En: No, it's pretty fucking *un*-sound if you ask me. They should have built a club for anodic music around it instead. He grins...
Zh: “不，如果你问我的话，这他妈完全站*不*住脚。他们应该在周围建一个阳极音乐俱乐部才对。”他笑着说……

En: I found a Doomed Commercial Area in Martinaise proper. Maybe it's the same thing the Ubis were trying to contain?
Zh: “我在马丁内斯当地发现了一片诅咒商业区，也许那就是尤比人想要牵制的东西？”

En: ... and that is what caused the communards to fail in defending the beachhead. Yeah, a lot of failure has gone down around here.
Zh: “……这就是公社没能保住滩头堡的原因。没错，这里发生过很多重大失败。”

En: Well, if it's without substance I guess there's nothing to worry about.
Zh: “那么，如果它没有实质的话，我想也没什么好担心的了。”

En: Do you mean there are dead bodies here?!
Zh: “你的意思是这里有尸体？！”

En: Huh, spooky. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “哼，真吓人。我们谈点别的吧。”

En: Sure. The young man cracks his knuckles.
Zh: “当然。”年轻人掰了掰手指。

En: Not like a *literal* sarcophagus. I'm just being... metaphorical.
Zh: “并非*字面上*的石棺，我只是……打个比方而已。”

En: Anodic music will *definitely* contain whatever we're dealing with. His words echo in the chamber...
Zh: “阳极音乐*绝对*能牵制我们周围的一切。”他的话回响在礼堂中……

En: What makes you think Soona's going to fail?
Zh: “你为什么觉得苏娜会失败？”

En: The young man rubs his chin, in silence, then mumbles: "... like a concentric ring spreading out... the struggling villages..."
Zh: 年轻人沉默地摩挲着下巴，嘟囔着：“……如同同心圆一般向外展开……苦苦挣扎的群落……”

En: He shrugs. "I don't know. Maybe. Maybe not. It's not a thing we can answer, cop-man. Even I have limits -- I'm a limited-psy person."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“我不知道，也许是，也许不是。这不是我们能够回答的问题，警官。即便是我也有极限——我是一个有心理极限的人。”

En: You think there's any merit to the theory?
Zh: “你觉得这个理论有什么亮点吗？”

En: Now that I think of it, it doesn't sound plausible.
Zh: “现在想想，这听上去不太可信。”

En: Maybe *you* can figure things out, cop-man. I think we got on a good level here... the sines are syncing up well.
Zh: “或许*你*能把事情弄清楚，警官。我想我们的进展还不错……正弦同步得刚好。”

En: Seems to be the trend around here, doesn't it? You can't measure shit like this... it's not like... with substance.
Zh: “这似乎是这里的趋势了，不是吗？你不能就那么衡量它……它并没有……实质。”

En: Why *are* you called Noid anyway?
Zh: “为什么你*会*被叫做诺伊德？”

En: It took us setting out for this whole enterprise to get our sines synced...
Zh: “整个计划都是为了让我们的正弦同步……”

En: Alright, I'm done talking about her.
Zh: “好吧，我不想再谈她了。”

En: You got us in, cop. I can't believe you got us in... He looks around the hall, examining the carpentry.
Zh: “你让我们进来了，警官。真不敢相信你让我们进来了……”他环视着大堂，检查着木工。

En: That's fine, man. But I've gotta warn you -- our sines are still off. It will take some time for me to get my psi on.
Zh: “没事的，兄弟。但我得警告你——我们的正弦仍然是失衡的。我得花点时间才能开启我的ψ。”

En: Between you and me -- I don't know if you've noticed this about me... I'm a little *suspicious* of authority. But you -- you really came through for the hard core underground.
Zh: “就你我之间说一句——我不知道你是否注意到了我……我对权威有点*怀疑*。但是你——你真的帮助了地下硬核组织。”

En: Yes. You really came through. For the hard core underground. He spreads his arms, looking around at the speedfreaks setting up shop. "How come?"
Zh: “是的。你真的帮了，地下硬核组织。”他张开双臂，环视着正在开业的瘾君子们。“为什么会这样？”

En: The would-be leader is cutting some futuristic shapes on the floor, sweating profusely.
Zh: 这位未来的指挥正在地板上裁切一些未来主义的形状，大汗淋漓。

En: Acele using her contact mic to listen to a tree, underwater.
Zh: 阿西尔正在用她的接触式麦克风聆听一棵树的声音，水下的树。

En: The one with the large head is blasting the dance track on repeat, while the stained glass window behind him is rattling from the bass.
Zh: 那个大头正在重复播放一段爆炸性的舞曲段落，他身后的彩绘玻璃窗被低音震得咔哒作响。

En: With wonder in his sharp eyes.
Zh: 他锐利的眼睛里满是惊奇。

En: Andre is busy cutting some slightly less lame, but still quite ungainly shapes on the church floor, sweating profusely.
Zh: 安德烈正忙着在教堂的地板上裁切一些不那么蹩脚、但是依然显得很不雅的形状，大汗淋漓的。

En: Cool. You're Noid right? Do you have a moment to talk?
Zh: “很好，你是诺伊德对吗？你有时间谈谈吗？”

En: Mega. I'll be going now. [Leave.]
Zh: “好极了，我这就走。”[离开。]

En: That's me. A friend and an ally to the hard core underground. Have a moment to talk?
Zh: “就是我。一个朋友和地下硬核组织的盟友，有时间谈谈吗？”

En: These kids got spunk. I admire that.
Zh: “这些孩子很有勇气，我很佩服。”

En: Better here than in that tent. (Shrug.) "It wasn't safe."
Zh: “在这儿总比在帐篷里好。”（耸肩。）“那里不安全。”

En: I'm genuinely into the *hard core lifestyle*. You wouldn't understand.
Zh: “我真的很喜欢那种*硬核的生活方式*，你不会明白的。”

En: I'm a corrupt cop, Kim. This is a corrupt scheme.
Zh: “我是个腐败的警察，金，这就是个腐败的体制。”

En: I did it for mankind. For *all of mankind*.
Zh: “我这么做是为了人类，为了*全人类*。”

En: Sire, the tent -- 'twas a *securitie risk*! And in here? Sanctuary! 'Twas only noble of you.
Zh: 陛下，帐篷——就是个*危险品*！而这里？则是避难所！您真的太高尚了。

En: Okay. The lieutenant keeps it laconic.
Zh: “好吧。”警督言简意赅。

En: Okay. The lieutenant keeps it laconic.
Zh: “好吧。”警督言简意赅。

En: Okay. The lieutenant keeps it laconic.
Zh: “好吧。”警督言简意赅。

En: Okay. The lieutenant keeps it laconic.
Zh: “好吧。”警督言简意赅。

En: Okay. The lieutenant keeps it laconic.
Zh: “好吧。”警督言简意赅。

En: What he means is: You can't possibly have handed them this real estate for such a flimsy reason -- then again, whatever keeps your nose out of the bottle and on the job.
Zh: 他的意思是：你不能因为这样一个站不住脚的理由就把这套建筑送给他们——其次，只要能让你远离酒精，埋头工作就好。

En: What he means is: I don't care if you have a corrupt scheme, almost everyone does. Whatever keeps you sober and concentrated on work.
Zh: 他的意思是：我不关心你的体制是否腐败，几乎每个人都是如此。任何能让你保持清醒并专注于工作的东西就行。

En: What he means is: I don't believe you actually think you did it for all mankind. But whatever keeps you in high spirits is okay by him.
Zh: 他的意思是：我不相信你真的以为自己这么做是为了全人类。但无论什么能让你保持高昂的情绪，对他来说都行。

En: What he means is: You're right. I do not understand. Do whatever you have to do to keep your nose out of the bottle.
Zh: 他的意思是：你说得对，我不懂。做任何你必须做的事，让自己远离酒精。

En: What he means is: You're right, actually. The tent was a safety hazard and this place was deserted anyway.
Zh: 他的意思是：实际上，你是对的。帐篷有安全隐患 ，至于这里反正也没人住。

En: He nods: "What's on your mind?"
Zh: 他点点头：“你在想什么？”

En: The young man gives you an uncharacteristic smile as you're turning to leave.
Zh: 当你转身离开时，那个年轻人对你露出了非同寻常的微笑。

En: My dedication to the hard core underground is tenuous at best. I have questions, however.
Zh: “我对于地下硬核组织的投入充其量也只能说是微不足道。不过，我的确有几个问题。”

En: What's on your mind? He looks up into the darkness under the roof.
Zh: “你在想什么？”他抬头望着屋顶下的黑暗。

En: It's a miracle of carpentry. Dead bodies carved into *total* shapes. Now it can be something more. He rubs his hands together.
Zh: “真是奇迹般的木工，尸体被雕刻成*各种*模样。现在它可以承载更多了。”他搓着手说。

En: What style is this church built in?
Zh: “这座教堂是什么风格的？”

En: He shakes his head. "I don't say much anything as a carpenter anymore. They tried to make me into a reckoner and a leveller. Made me a bit manic, y'know?"
Zh: 他摇摇头。“作为一名木匠，我不方便多说了。他们试图把我变成一个计算者和平衡者。这让我有点狂躁了，知道吗？”

En: You say that as a carpenter yourself?
Zh: “你是以自己木匠的身份这么说的吗？”

En: I regret the time I dedicated to that profession, and that worker collective. I say things more as a member of the hard core psi-dance community these days.
Zh: “我很后悔自己把时间都奉献给了那份职业、那个工人集体。如今，我更像是以一名硬核舞蹈社团成员的身份说的这番话。”

En: What did you mean by *dead bodies*?
Zh: “你口中的*尸体*指的是什么？”

En: Dead bodies of perennial plants. He taps on the wood. "Sigma functions have left this place. It's a good thing we came along -- the spiritual collapse has been total."
Zh: “多年生植物的尸体。”他敲了敲木头。“西格玛函数已经离开，幸好我们过来了——精神完全崩坏了。”

En: How old do you think this church is -- over 300 years?
Zh: “你觉得这座教堂有多少年头了——超过300年了吗？”

En: How old do you think the church is?
Zh: “你觉得这座教堂有多少年头了？”

En: Three-hundred and twenty years. A little more. The first settlers built it, plus six more like it. On the coast here. Was one of the first things they did, must've been really scared of something. I understand...
Zh: “320年，再多一点。第一批定居者们建造了它，以及另外六个类似的建筑。这片海岸上的，是他们第一件作品。他们一定是发自内心的恐惧着什么，我能理解……”

En: That's right. The first settlers built it, plus six more like it. On the coast here. Was one of the first things they did... must've been really scared of something. But I understand...
Zh: “没错。第一批定居者们建造了它，以及另外六个类似的建筑。这片海岸上的，是他们第一件作品……他们一定是发自内心的恐惧着什么，但我能理解……”

En: Alone on an uninhabited archipelago, forced to face themselves and nature. Pre-industrial quantities of solitude. The sea. Perhaps something more... fundamental.
Zh: “孤独地生活在无人的岛屿上，被迫直面自己与自然。无数未经工业化的孤寂，大海，或是某些更加……基本的东西。”

En: He means something para-natural. He must...
Zh: 他指的是某些超自然的东西，他一定是……

En: I would want to build a safe place for myself and my own as well... His voice echoes in the wooden cavern of the church.
Zh: “我想为自己建造一个属于自己的安身之地……”他的声音在教堂的木质雕刻中回荡着。

En: A cop who's into building critique. He taps a floorboard. "Okay then. This is folk Dolorianism, lawmongerer. It's a subset of early *Dolorian* architecture."
Zh: “一个热衷于建筑批判的警察。”他敲着地板。“好吧。这就是传说中的德洛莉丝主义风格，治安官。早期*德洛莉丝*架构的子集。”

En: *Total*. Everything between an ancient concrete cathedral and a glass cube is Dolorianism. This is just a homespun version of it, folksy stuff, early mass production. They made thousands like this. Does that help you out?
Zh: “*整合*。与古老的混凝土教堂和玻璃立方相关的一切即为德洛莉丝风格。这只是个相对朴素的版本，民间产物，早期大规模生产出来的东西，他们做了成千上万的。这对你有帮助吗？”

En: Liked to show off large and intricate structures, arches, spires. Put you down with them. They were really into painting everything white too. Virginal shit, you know. Marriage shit. Virtue and tyranny.
Zh: “喜欢炫耀宏大而复杂的结构、拱门与尖塔，高高在上。他们还很喜欢把一切都漆成白色，无暇的象征，你懂的。婚姻之类的，美德与暴政。”

En: Stands to reason it used to be white on the outside. He peeks out of a small window in the dark. "Before the sea wind took all the paint off."
Zh: “很明显，它的外表曾经是白色的。”他透过一扇黑暗中的小窗向外看去。“在海风吹掉所有的油彩之前。”

En: This church isn't painted white, as far as I can tell.
Zh: “据我所知，这座教堂并没有被漆成白色。”

En: Okay -- and what is Dolorian architecture like?
Zh: “好吧——德洛莉丝式建筑风格是什么样的？”

En: Yeah, enough architecture.
Zh: “好了，建筑的话题就说到这里。”

En: I'm not just a cop. I'm an Art Cop.
Zh: “我不仅仅是个警察，还是一个艺术警探。”

En: Hard of core. He nods appreciatively.
Zh: “强硬的核心。”他点头赞赏。

En: The church creaks in response. The speedfreak is too busy listening to it with a suspicious expression to say anything.
Zh: 教堂嘎吱作响，仿佛在回应你。这个瘾君子正忙着用一副怀疑的表情聆听它的回答，什么都没说。

En: What would a Dolorian building look like?
Zh: “一座德洛莉丝式建筑会是什么样子的？”

En: Like that woman there, he nods toward the stained glass window. "Vertical, thin, white, a false image of grandeur. The source of the system is up there, you're at the bottom. They really dug that power vertical."
Zh: “就像那边的那个女人，”他朝彩绘玻璃点点头。“笔直、瘦削、苍白、一副虚伪庄严的形象。体制的源头高高在上，而你身处底层。而他们对权力的控制垂直向下直达底端。”

En: Year after year, flake after flake, white washed clean, then covered in green moss...
Zh: 年复一年，片片剥落，白漆被冲刷干净，覆上青色的苔藓……

En: Slowly peeled by the wind... Your skin crawls from the sensation as you look around.
Zh: 被海风慢慢剥落……当你环视四周时，你的皮肤上蔓延着同样的感觉。

En: It was probably white and gold, with light red flower motives. Part of you -- assumed to be lost to nerve damage -- knows this style to be Ubi Dolorianism.
Zh: 它可能是白色金色交相辉映，点缀着淡红色花朵图案。你心中的一部分——可能是神经损伤所致——认为这种风格属于尤比德洛莉丝主义。

En: Is there such a thing as *Ubi Dolorianism* or did I just make that up?
Zh: “真有*尤比德洛莉丝主义*这种东西吗？还是我自己杜撰出来的？”

En: I don't wanna sound like an art guy.
Zh: 我不想听上去像个搞艺术的。

En: Good catch, Art Cop. He crosses his arms. "The herdsmen of the Ubi Sunt? islands came here on the first boats. Their flowery version of Dolorianism could be what we're standing in."
Zh: “好眼光，艺术警探。”他交叉着双臂。“尤比萨特？岛上的牧民们是乘着第一艘船来到这里的。我们所在的地方可能就是他们所建的花式德洛莉丝风格建筑。”

En: True. He crosses his arms and nods. "The herdsmen of the Ubi Sunt? islands came here on the first boats. Their version of Dolorianism could be what we're standing in..."
Zh: “正是。”他抱起双臂，点点头。“尤比萨特？岛上的牧民们是乘船到达的第一批人。他们眼中的德洛莉丝主义可能就是我们身前的这种……”

En: Then why do you have all this cultural analysis up here?
Zh: 那你为什么还要在这里大肆谈论文艺呢？

En: The Ubi are known for their partiality to socialism and sheep. They come from an island called Ubi Sunt?, drifting in the pale off the coast of Vesper. It's the only place in the world that has a question mark in its name. Things are not well there: It's an island of poverty and entroponetic collapse.
Zh: 尤比人以其对于工人主义和羊群的热爱闻名。他们来自一座名为尤比萨特？的岛屿，从威斯珀的海岸起航，漂流在灰域之中。它是世界上唯一名字里有个问号的地方。那里的状态不是很好：它是一座贫穷而且受到灰域崩溃影响的孤岛。

En: How are you settling in?
Zh: “你过得如何？”

En: Let's talk about something else, Noid.
Zh: “我们来谈点别的吧，诺伊德。”

En: It never was. A three-thousand-year-old regime of History, built by hundreds of generations of self-appointed *intellectuals*... He looks around. "It's false to the core."
Zh: “从来都不是。一个有着三千年历史的政权，由数百代自诩的*知识分子*建立而成……”他向四周看了看。“是与其核心相违背的。”

En: Spiritual collapse?
Zh: “精神崩坏？”

En: Okay, then.
Zh: “那，好吧。”

En: Okay, he nods.
Zh: “好的，”他点了点头。

En: End of quote. This is a high-quality carcass. He kicks the floorboard. "The power of anodic beats and hard bass is needed to reanimate it."
Zh: “别再引经据典了，这是具高质量的遗骸。”他踢了踢地板。“需要阳极的节奏和重低音的力量来让它重获生机。”

En: 'I saw some piglets suckling their dead mother' -- have you heard this one, cop-man? He continues without waiting for an answer: "'After a short while they shuddered and went away.'"
Zh: “‘我看到小猪仔们在吮吸亡母的乳汁’——听过这个吗，警官？”他不等你的回答便继续道：“‘不多时，它们打了个寒颤便离开了。’”

En: They had sensed that she could no longer see them and that she wasn't *like* them anymore. What they loved in their mother wasn't her body, but whatever it was that made her body live.
Zh: “它们觉察到她已经无法再照看它们，已经变得跟它们不*一样*了。它们所爱的不是母亲的身体，而是能够让她的身体存活的东西。”

En: First, where is that quote from?
Zh: “首先，这句话从何而来？”

En: What you're saying is: religion has stopped being *hard core*?
Zh: “你的意思是：宗教已经不再*硬核*了？”

En: What you're saying is: you're not a big fan of the innocentic system?
Zh: “你的意思是：你不太喜欢无罪体系？”

En: A Seraise man, who lived a long time ago. An ancient hard core brother.
Zh: “很久以前的一个萨弗里人，古老的硬核兄弟。”

En: Then I did right not to agree with all that talk about the Ecclesiastes being okay with this then.
Zh: “那么，我不同意传道书中关于这一点的说法是正确的了？”

En: How do you like the glasswork? (Point to the stained-glass window.)
Zh: “你觉得这里的玻璃做工如何？”（指向彩绘玻璃窗。）

En: What exactly *are* you saying?
Zh: “你到底*在*说什么？”

En: This religion of History is false-core. It has collapsed.
Zh: “宗教的历史是*伪核*的，已经崩坏了。”

En: A three-thousand-year-old tyrannical regime of History, built and maintained by hundreds of generations of self-appointed *intellectuals*... He looks around. "It's false-core."
Zh: “历史长达三千年之久的专制政权，建立和维护者不过是数百代自诩*知识分子*的家伙……”他环顾四周。“那是伪核。”

En: The way he says it, the *false* in false-core is invested with twenty kilotons of disgust.
Zh: 听他的语气，那句彻头彻尾的错误中的*伪核*被赋予了二十千吨重的厌恶。

En: But you guys said the Ecclesiastes were all about love and hard core -- before, remember?
Zh: “但在此之前——你们都说传道书中的内容都是关于爱与硬核的，还记得吗？”

En: *I* only said: *Unity*. One word. Figures of authority always misquote you. He points to his friends.
Zh: “*我*只说了：*团结*，这一个词。当权者总会错误地引用你的话。”他指着自己的朋友。

En: I even agreed with you. About the Ecclesiastes being okay with this...
Zh: “我甚至还认同了你的说法，关于传道书对此表示赞同什么的……”

En: The Founding Party doesn't give two shits about this place. Look around. He spreads his arms. "They do not have enough love for the *human crew* to oppose anything anymore. We're on our own."
Zh: “立国党根本不在乎这个地方，看看这周围吧。”他张开双臂。“他们对*人类*的爱已经不足以反对任何事物了，我们只能靠自己。”

En: But were you wrong? The Founding Party is okay with everything. Look around. He spreads his arms. "They do not have enough love for the *human crew* to oppose anything anymore. We're on our own."
Zh: “立国党根本不在乎这个地方，看看这周围吧。”他张开双臂。“他们对*人类*的爱已经不足以反对任何事物了，我们只能靠自己。”

En: And you propose dance music will supplant this system?
Zh: “你认为舞曲能取代这种制度吗？”

En: *Anodic* dance music, he nods. "Regular dance music wasn't hard enough. And yes, I do."
Zh: “是*阳极*舞曲，”他点点头，“普通的舞曲不够硬核。没错，我是这么认为的。”

En: You know what this kind of stuff goes well with?
Zh: 你知道这类东西跟什么很配吗？

En: Only a little.
Zh: “一点点。”

En: Don't you have to be on drugs for that though?
Zh: “难道不是得嗑药才行吗？”

En: I don't need to be a narc.
Zh: 我没必要化身为缉毒警。

En: Your pleasure response was more like... just wondering if he has any?
Zh: 你的快乐反应更像是……想知道他会不会有？

En: Cool.
Zh: “很好。”

En: Not cool. Shake your head.
Zh: “不好。”摇摇头。

En: All large human gatherings are narcotic. Ask any such undertaking in history -- this included. He nods toward a human-shaped pillar nearby. "Chemistry is true to its word."
Zh: “一切大型人类集会都是要嗑药的，去看看历史上的那些吧——也包括这个。”他朝附近的一根人形柱点点头。“化学真的是名副其实。”

En: It's a necessity. All large human gatherings are narcotic. It's just the way of the spinal cord.
Zh: “那是个必需品。一切大型人类集会都是要嗑药的。脊髓就是这么个运作方式。”

En: Khm, the lieutenant interjects: "There is a difference between narcotics and group elation. One kills you, the other does not."
Zh: “咳，”警督插话道：“嗑药和集体狂欢还是有区别的，一个会让你丧命，另一个不会。”

En: The supercharged humanism that the innocentic system has been feeding us, on giant city squares? That's not a drug? The sugar and wheat it feeds us is healthy?
Zh: “在巨大的城市广场里，这个无罪体系一直在向我们灌输的人道主义，那不是毒品吗？它喂给我们的糖和小麦是健康的吗？”

En: Forget it. He waves his hand. "It would become an imbecilic discussion. You two continue. It's more *hard core* that way."
Zh: “算了吧。”他摆摆手。“这会变成一场愚蠢的争论。你们俩继续吧，那样更*硬核*些。”

En: You're not going to ask me how I knew?
Zh: “你不准备问问我是怎么知道的吗？”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: Why? You're a cop. I carry carpentry tools.
Zh: “为什么这么问？你是个警察，而我携带着木匠工具。”

En: The material knowledge is mega, though. He looks around the hollow hall of the church. "Guild shit. Teaches independence."
Zh: “关于物质的知识倒是无穷无尽。”他环顾教堂空洞的大厅。“行会之类的。教导独立自主。”

En: Something more fundamental? You mean the sound anomaly?
Zh: “更基本的东西？你是指声音异常吗？”

En: Right.
Zh: “没错。”

En: Maybe. He looks up under the rafters. "Maybe they were unable to face the nature of the world -- perishing."
Zh: “或许吧。”他抬头看着椽子下方。“或许他们无法面对世界的本质——消亡。”

En: People are always afraid of all sorts of shit. They're right to be. The world is a trap...
Zh: “人们总会害怕各种各样的东西。那就对了。整个世界都是陷阱。”

En: Marriage is shit, yeah.
Zh: “婚姻就是垃圾，是的。”

En: Hey, marriage is great. Marriage is sacred.
Zh: “嘿，婚姻是美好的，是神圣的。”

En: You're entitled to your wrong opinion. He inspects a pillar nearby, with a sharp gaze.
Zh: “你有权保留自己错误的观念。”他用锐利的目光凝视着附近的柱子。

En: Right. He takes a bolt from the toolbox and spits on it before shining it.
Zh: “没错。”他从工具箱中取出一颗螺钉，朝上面啐了几口，然后把它擦亮。

En: Andre doesn't care about the Ecclesiastes. He just wants the operation to run smoothly. And Egg is a demi-beast. You shouldn't listen to what people say, you should listen to what they are.
Zh: “安德烈才不关心什么传道书，他只希望一切运营顺利。而爱凡客就是半头野兽。你不应该去听人们说了什么，而应该去听他们是什么。”

En: He does not feel left out. In fact he probably just wanted to say "hard core."
Zh: 他不觉得自己被冷落了。事实上，他可能只是想说*硬核*。

En: It *does* feel good to say it. Maybe the speedfreaks are on to something?
Zh: 说出来的感觉*的确*很好，或许这些瘾君子们在密谋着什么？

En: I don't. He looks over his shoulder. "Fucker giving me the evil eye."
Zh: “我不喜欢。”他回头看了看，“混蛋在用恶毒的眼光看我。”

En: That's still a lie! RE-ASSERT YOURSELF.
Zh: 那仍然是句谎言！重新主张自己。

En: Perfidy! You lied to me. I am *tired* of people lying to me.
Zh: “背信弃义！你骗了我。我*讨厌*别人对我说谎。”

En: That wouldn't be cool. I want to be cool.
Zh: 那样就不酷了，我想耍酷。

En: Mewling wimp! Pathetic...
Zh: 啜泣的懦夫！真可悲……

En: Well... The young man pushes his chest out, the skeleton of suspenders rattling...
Zh: “这个嘛……”年轻人鼓起胸膛，背带裤的吊带啪嚓作响……

En: Noid-man! Mellow out, the dancing hedgehog yelps at his friend. "Stop aggroing the law!"
Zh: “诺伊德兄弟！放松，”舞动着的刺猬头对他的朋友叫着。“别再违法乱纪了！”

En: His chest returns to its normal proportions. "I'm sorry."
Zh: 他的胸膛又缩了回去。“我很抱歉。”

En: That's Her Innocence Dolores Dei, mind your words.
Zh: “那可是无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛，说话注意点。”

En: I'm getting some real negative vibrations from her too.
Zh: “我也从她那里受到了一些负面影响。”

En: You wanted to get inside the church and now you don't like the stained-glass window.
Zh: “你想进入教堂，现在却又不喜欢它的彩绘玻璃窗了。”

En: You defend her, law-minion. He cracks his neck. "But you said she was a war criminal? A mass murderer. What's up with that?"
Zh: “为她辩护吧，法官大人。”他扭扭脖子。“但你又说她是个战犯？一个大杀戮者。这算什么呢？”

En: You defend her, law-minion. He cracks his neck. "She was a *mass murderer* -- what's up with that?"
Zh: “为她辩护吧，法官大人。”他扭扭脖子。“她就是个*大杀戮者*——这算什么呢？”

En: But she's the innocence of humanism. Humanism seems to be a pretty big deal around here.
Zh: “但她是人道主义的无罪女王，人道主义在这里似乎很重要吧。”

En: Humanism leads to eating sugar and pigs. Humanism was invented to mass produce billions of humans. Billions of humans can mass produce *hundreds* of billions of pigs.
Zh: “人道主义让人们食用糖和猪肉，人道主义是为了大量生产数以亿计的人类而发明的，而数以亿计的人类可以批量养殖出数以千亿计的猪。”

En: She liked games. Her legacy, the thing we're living, isn't *real* life. It's a strategy for some kind of victory. Against a long-dead opponent. But -- yo -- I'm only the Noid. What do I know?
Zh: “她喜欢游戏。她的遗产，我们赖以为生的东西，并不是*真实的*生命，而是某种胜利的策略，用于对抗一个早已死去的对手。但——嘿——我只是个偏执狂而已。我能知道什么呢？”

En: Mellow, man! Mellow! yells his friend. "No one's a mass murderer, this is a house of *love!*"
Zh: “冷静，兄弟！冷静！”他的朋友叫着。“没有人是杀戮者，这里是个充满了*爱*的地方！”

En: Mass murder on the floor!
Zh: “地板上的大屠杀！”

En: No wonder. He cracks his neck. "We have to get rid of it. Dismantle it. Can't dance with a giant *mass murderer* lookin' at you. Not a good look for the club."
Zh: “难怪。”他把脖子扭得咔咔作响。“那我们就得除掉它，摧毁它。没人能在那种*大屠杀*凶犯的注视下跳舞。夜总会可不需要这种形象。”

En: I didn't know it was in here. He cracks his neck. "We have to get rid of it. Dismantle it. Can't dance with a giant *mass murderer* lookin' at you. Not a good look for the club."
Zh: “我不知道这里还有这种东西。”他把脖子扭得咔咔作响。“那我们就得除掉它，摧毁它。没人能在那种*大屠杀*凶犯的注视下跳舞。夜总会可不需要这种形象。”

En: I wanted to talk some more about this place.
Zh: “我想再聊聊这个地方。”

En: Dead body, spirit entered. What is there to talk of?
Zh: “尸体，萦绕的灵魂，有什么好说的？”

En: Let's talk about the glasswork again. I've been thinking about her...
Zh: “我们再来谈谈那个破璃制品吧。我一直在想她……”

En: Let's talk about that glasswork again.
Zh: “我们再来谈谈那个破璃制品吧。”

En: The mass murderer? Why?
Zh: “那个大杀戮者？为什么？”

En: But she's pretty.
Zh: “但她很漂亮。”

En: Yeah, but, like, who *isn't* accused of being a mass murderer these days?
Zh: “是啊，可是，就像，现在谁*没*被指控过是大杀戮者呢？”

En: I *do* feel there is something terrifying about her.
Zh: “我*确实*觉得她有点可怕。”

En: Isn't she supposed to be an embodiment of the World Spirit?
Zh: “她不应该是世界神灵的化身吗？”

En: Yeah, I'm done talking about her. I don't want to think about her anymore.
Zh: “是啊，我不想再提她。我不愿再想起她。”

En: The resettlement programs were totally okay. I'm a big fan of resettlement programs, for some reason.
Zh: “重新安置项目完全没有问题，出于某种原因，我非常支持安置项目。”

En: And many, many more tons of sugar!
Zh: 还有好多好多吨糖！

En: She invented the beauty you're feeling. She and her glass-cutters and iconographers. He turns to look up at her face. "You set the standard, alright..."
Zh: “她创造了你所感受到的美，她和她的玻璃雕刻工匠、肖像画家。他转身看着她的脸庞。“你设定了标准，好吧……”

En: Then you meet it. It's effective like that. But it is also very soft-of-core, that so-called 'beauty' of hers.
Zh: “然后你就看到了，就是如此有效。但她也很软弱，那就是她所谓的美。”

En: You're right, I like it harder-core.
Zh: “你说得对，我喜欢更硬核一些的。”

En: It's anything but *soft* core. It's terrifying.
Zh: “这一点也不*软弱*，反而很可怕。”

En: Then it seems I like it soft, because...
Zh: “那我似乎很喜欢这种软弱，因为……”

En: Hmh. The young man nods, he looks worried. "You may be on to something, copper-man. She's got those mass murderin' hips."
Zh: “哼。”年轻人看起来很担忧地点点头。“你也许说到点子上了，警官。她有着杀人不偿命的臀部。”

En: You wouldn't be the first. Millions liked her. The young man frowns, worried. "She's got those mass murderin' lips."
Zh: “你不会是第一个，数百万人都很喜欢她。”年轻人愁眉苦脸。“她有着杀人不偿命的嘴唇。”

En: In the true life of tomorrow, every woman will be an innocence. They will wear neon headbands and leggings, and they will glow in the dark. His eyes smile enigmatically.
Zh: “在明日的真实生活中，每个女人都会是无罪女王。她们会戴上霓虹发带，穿上紧身裤，在黑暗中熠熠生辉。”他的眼睛神秘地微笑着。

En: But you don't like that, you liar. You like this one, the softness of her core is dear to you...
Zh: 但你不喜欢那样，你这个骗子。你就喜欢这个，她软弱的内核对你很亲切……

En: Oh my living god, it's dear. Suddenly you feel like you need to *plummet* into it. Look away, say something!
Zh: 哦我的神啊，我亲爱的。突然间你觉得自己要*骤然跌落*了。移开视线，说点什么！

En: *No one* says Arno van Eyck is a mass murderer. The anodic pioneer Rietveldt is not a mass murderer. He is not accused of mass murder...
Zh: “*没有人*会说阿诺·凡·艾克是个大杀戮者。阳极先驱者里耶维尔特并不是大杀戮者。他没有被指控屠杀……”

En: Likewise, no one says Germaine Egg-Head or Andre are mass murderers. You can live *entirely* outside that suspicion. Billions of people go about not being guilty of mass murder. He nods up. "Just not her."
Zh: “同样的，也没有人会说杰曼尼·爱凡客或者安德烈是大杀戮者，你*完全*可以避开这种怀疑。外面有数十亿人都没有犯下大屠杀的罪行。”他点着头。“唯独她不行。”

En: Are you a commie-cop?
Zh: “你是康米主义警察吗？”

En: I am.
Zh: “我是。”

En: No. (Lie.)
Zh: “不是。”（说谎。）

En: Hmh. Then it's inexplicable. Whatever. He picks up his toolbox, then puts it back down again.
Zh: “哼，那就很令人费解了，随便吧。”他拿起工具箱，然后又把它放下。

En: Then that's why. Communism is just a bloodier humanism, if you ask me. Has *her* love all over it. He nods up.
Zh: “这就是原因了。如果要我说，康米主义就是更加血腥的人道主义。沐浴在*她*的慈爱之中。“他点点头。

En: There is. She is a party-repellent and must be taken down before we can begin partying in here.
Zh: “是的。她讨厌派对，在我们开始狂欢之前，必须把她取下来。”

En: No, that's not it... it's something more. Something closer to your skin...
Zh: 不，不是这样……而是更多，更贴近你的皮肤的东西……

En: Take her down. Crash it. Destroy that window.
Zh: “把她取下来，打碎，毁掉那扇窗户。”

En: Keep it. Keep the beautiful sharp shards. Keep her long face and her hair.
Zh: “留下它吧，留下那美丽锋利的碎片，留下她的脸庞与长发。”

En: Say nothing, stare grimly into the distance.
Zh: 沉默不语，冷冷地望着远方。

En: The speedfreak looks the same way. It is dark there, in the back of the church. For a moment the music echoes strangely.
Zh: 这个瘾君子看着同样的方向。那里很黑，在教堂的后面，音乐奇异地回响了一会了。

En: No, Noid! Stop twisting my melon, man! People are gonna love it. It'll be our *thing*... His friend disagrees. "Plus it keeps the cold out!"
Zh: “不，诺伊德！不要再把我搞得一头雾水了，兄弟！大家都会喜欢的。这会成为我们的*特色*……”他的朋友很不赞同。“而且它很能御寒！”

En: Will do. The speedfreak nods and pets the toolbox as if it were a cat.
Zh: “好的。”瘾君子点点头，像抚摸着一只猫似的抚摸着自己的工具箱。

En: The speedfreak's eyes narrow with suspicion, as if he's looking at a man possessed.
Zh: 这个瘾君子怀疑地眯起眼睛，好像在看一个着了魔的人。

En: It's not coming down. People are gonna love it. It'll be, like, our *thing*. His friend keeps dancing. "Plus it keeps the cold and the rain out!"
Zh: “不能放下来。大家都会喜欢的，这就像，就像我们的*特色*一样。”他的朋友继续舞动着。“而且它还能御寒防雨！”

En: The World Spirit does not have a body. It has *organs*. Hard core is an organ of the world spirit. He raises his left hand.
Zh: “世界神灵没有肉体，只有*器官*。硬核就是世界神灵的器官之一。”他举起左手。

En: This Arno van Eyck track is an organ. The carpentry and glass-cutting that built this house are also organs. She's a thief if you ask me. An organ thief. All innocences are.
Zh: “阿诺·凡·艾克的这首曲子就是一个器官。组成这座建筑的木工与玻璃切割也是器官。依我看，她就是个小偷，一个器官小偷，所有的无罪者都是。”

En: Okay. The young man appears constantly worried. It's just the way his brows are built. The worry never leaves his face.
Zh: “好吧。”这个年轻人看上去总是忧心忡忡，这就是他的眉形，忧虑从未从他的脸上离开。

En: What a strange choice of words...
Zh: 多么奇怪的用词……

En: That can't be healthy. What's happening here? Why do you keep coming back to this window?
Zh: 那样很不健康，究竟发生了什么。你为什么总是回到这个窗口？

En: Caustic, overflowing with negativity.
Zh: 腐烂的气息，充斥着负能量。

En: Nothing, everything's okay.
Zh: 没什么，一切都很好。

En: I don't know.
Zh: 我不知道。

En: It's a nice window.
Zh: 这是一扇很漂亮的窗户。

En: Why do you keep saying that if it isn't making you feel well? Don't come back to this anymore, stop talking about that damn window!
Zh: 如果你觉得不舒服，为什么还要一直说这种话？别再提这件事了，别再提那扇该死的窗户了！

En: Well -- you shouldn't. You shouldn't come back to this anymore. Stop talking about it, please.
Zh: 这个嘛——你不应该的。你不应该再提起这个话题的。请不要再说了。

En: But it isn't. And you shouldn't come back to this anymore. This should be the last time. Stop talking about that damn window, please.
Zh: 但事实并非如此。而且你不应该再提起这个话题了。这应该是最后一次。请不要再提起那扇该死的窗户了。

En: Would you say she was... you know, *human*?
Zh: “你会说她是……你知道的，*人类*吗？”

En: Ha! The young man stretches his ribcage made of suspenders. "I like this question, cop-man. She did not live the life of a human. She lived like someone who is playing a game. The life of an operator..."
Zh: “哈！”年轻人拉扯着自己胸腔图样的背带裤。“我喜欢这个问题，警官。她并非像人类般生活，反倒像个游戏玩家，操纵者一般活着……”

En: Ha! The young man stretches his ribcage made of suspenders. "I like this theory of yours, cop-man. This one I like..." He nods. "She was not human. Not really. She was an operator, playing life like a game..."
Zh: “哈！”年轻人拉扯着自己胸腔图样的背带裤。“我喜欢你的理论，警官，这个我喜欢……”他点点头。“她并非凡人，不算是。反倒像个操纵者，玩着名为人生的游戏……”

En: That's not the life that humans live. She was adored. Humans aren't. I don't know about you, but they *hate* me. And they do not think I'm *innocent*, or some shit like that.
Zh: “那不是人类的生活。她受千万人敬仰，而人类不是。我不知道你怎么样，但他们*讨厌*我。他们也不认为我是*无罪*的，或者诸如此类的玩意。”

En: Yeah, they hate this, too. (Point to yourself.)
Zh: “是啊，他们也很讨厌这家伙。”（指着你自己。）

En: I don't know what you're talking about. I have thousands of fans. I am surrounded by love and support all the time.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么。我有成千上万的粉丝，一直被爱与支持包围着。”

En: Mhm, she was a player! She played us all! It's just a game to her.
Zh: “嗯，她就是个玩家！她玩弄了我们所有人！对她来说，这只是个游戏而已。”

En: Uhm, yes. He lets go of the suspenders and they hit his chest with a slap.
Zh: “嗯，是的。”他松开裤子背带，背带啪的一声打在了他的胸口上。

En: Fame is a false existence. It doesn't happen inside the *head*. He nods mysteriously, then lets go of the suspenders and they hit his chest with a slap.
Zh: “名望是一种虚伪的存在，它并不会在*脑内*发生。”他神秘地点点头，然后松开裤子背带，背带啪的一声打在了他的胸口。

En: Well, they *loved* her. They put all their love in her and forgot all about the rest of us. The young man lets go of the suspenders and they hit his chest with a slap.
Zh: “是啊，他们*爱*她。他们把所有的爱都倾注到了她身上，却忘记了我们。”年轻人松开裤子背带，背带啪的一声打在了他的胸口上。

En: This speedfreak does not quite understand the sudden escalation of emotion in that argument, but he agrees nonetheless.
Zh: 这个瘾君子不太能理解那场讨论中情绪突然的升级，不过他还是同意了。

En: But I thought we'd be okay now -- sine-wise.
Zh: “但是我还以为我们现在已经没事了——正弦波的智慧。”

En: Interesting.
Zh: “有点意思。”

En: That's ridiculous.
Zh: “这太荒谬了。”

En: I suck at socializing, man. Even now our sine synchronization is way off. But I'll see what I can do. He continues to rearrange his tools.
Zh: “我很不擅长社交，老兄。即便到现在我们的正弦同步率还是差的很远，但我会努力看看的。”他继续整理着工具。

En: See? Our sines are way off. He looks down at his tools. "I can already tell this social relationship is gonna take a lot of work."
Zh: “看到没？我们的正弦波失衡了。”他低头看着工具。“我已经看出这层社会关系需要更多努力了。”

En: 'Sine'?
Yeah. Gotta compare, see if we can align.
Zh: “是啊，得对比一下，看看我们能不能结盟。”

En: I'm doing what I can. He continues to rearrange his tools.
Zh: “我正在竭尽所能。”他继续整理着自己的工具。

En: The Ubi are known for their partiality to socialism and sheep. They come from an island called Ubi Sunt?, the only place in the world that has a question mark in its name. There's something else important you should be able to remember about Ubi Sunt?, but all that's coming to mind is the sheep.
Zh: 尤比人以其对于工人主义和羊群的热爱闻名。他们来自一座名为尤比萨特？的岛屿，那里是世界上唯一名字里有问号的地方。关于尤比萨特？岛还有件重要的事情你得记住，但我唯一能想到的就是羊。

En: Sounds like a job for the Remote Viewers Division.
Zh: “听起来像是遥视者的工作。”

En: The young man shrugs. "I doubt it. I don't even think the Ubis properly understood it. What it does. But it's what this Soona person is looking for, and trying to measure." He nods toward the woman.
Zh: 年轻人耸耸肩。“我对此表示怀疑，我甚至觉得尤比人自己都没能完全理解。但这正是那个叫苏娜的女人在寻找并试图衡量的东西。”他朝那个女人点了点头。

En: And if it *can't*, well... He shrugs non-committally.
Zh: “如果它*不行*，那么……”他不置可否地耸耸肩。

En: Hard to say, cop-man. Sines in here are distinctly *wild*. Gonna take a while before everything's properly *synced*...
Zh: “很难说，警官，因为这里的正弦波相当的*野蛮*。想让一切*同步*起来要好一阵子才行……”

En: I did get to talk to the crab-man, though.
Zh: “不过，我确实跟蟹男有过交流。”

En: You mean Tiago?
Zh: “你是指蒂亚戈？”

En: The crab-man?
Zh: “蟹男？”

En: Yeah, you know. The guy who lives in the rafters, walks around upside down on the ceiling. You know, like a crab.
Zh: “是啊，你知道的。那个住在椽子上，倒挂在天花板上走来走去的家伙，像只螃蟹似的。”

En: Are you sure it's a *crab* he reminds you of?
Zh: “你确定他让你联想起的是*螃蟹*吗？”

En: Oh, right. Crab-man, got it.
Zh: “对哦，蟹男，明白了。”

En: Of course I'm sure. What? You think I'm *confused*?
Zh: “我当然确定了，怎么？你以为我*糊涂*了吗？”

En: Eight legs, walks upside down on the ceiling, spins webs sometimes...
Zh: “八条腿，倒挂在天花板上移动，有时候还会织网……”

En: No, you're definitely right. That's entirely crab-like behaviour you're describing.
Zh: “不，完全正确。你所描述的完全就是蟹类行为。”

En: Yeah, a crab.
Zh: “没错，一只螃蟹。”

En: Anyway, he's been giving me kind of a *psychic rundown* of this place...
Zh: “总而言之，他就像我在这片地方的*心灵纲领*……”

En: You begin to wonder if this speedfreak has ever seen a crab before.
Zh: 你开始怀疑这个瘾君子以前是否见过螃蟹了。

En: Dude's seen some crazy shit. But he's actually a lot like us.
Zh: “那家伙看过一些相当疯狂的玩意，但他其实很像我们。”

En: You mean all his *Mother's love* stuff isn't too *spooky* for you?
Zh: “你是说他的那套*母亲之爱*之类的东西对你来说并不是很*吓人*？”

En: You mean all his *sobriety* stuff isn't too *restrictive* for you?
Zh: “你是说他的那套*节制*之类的东西你对来说并不是很有*局限性*？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Talking to him, I realized that sobriety can be pretty *hard core*, too. I gotta respect it...
Zh: “跟他交流过后，我发现节制也可以是很*硬核*的，我必须尊重这一点……”

En: Have you been listening to what Egg's been saying? Love is *hard core* man, and a mother's love is the hardest core of all...
Zh: “你听到爱凡客说的了吗？爱就是*硬核*，兄弟，而母亲的爱是其中最硬核的……”

En: The man picks up on stuff. And he knows *a lot* about the church. I got a lot to learn from him...
Zh: “那人肚子里有点内容，而且他对这座教堂知之*甚多*。我有很多东西要向他学习呢……”

En: Good thing you didn't squash him.
Zh: “幸好你没有赶走他。”

En: No one can find us here.
Zh: “这里没人能找到我们。”

En: This is *our* little utopia.
Zh: “这里是*我们*的小小乌托邦。”

En: The carpentry in here is magnificent.
Zh: “这里的木匠工艺相当华丽。”

En: Are they really gonna let us have this?
Zh: “他们真的会让我们拥有它吗？”

En: Whatever it takes, man... whatever it takes.
Zh: “不惜一切，老兄……不惜一切代价。”

En: New sines, new ideas, new beginning.
Zh: “全新的正弦波，全新的想法，全新的开始。”

En: Hmmm...
... equals equals false, close brackets, equals equals true?
Zh: “……==false，），==true？”

En: No, that's not it.
Zh: “不，不是这个。”

En: “What about... '1 <= getResult() and and getResult() <= getNumSides()'...?”
Zh: “那这个怎么样……'1<=getResult（））andand（getResult（）<=getNumSides（）'……？”

En: What do you mean 'file not found'?
Zh: “你说‘文件未找到’是什么意思？”

En: Where did that 'Õ' come from?
Zh: “那个‘Õ’是哪里来的？”

En: The tent is just tarpaulin fabric covering a pile of stuff. The flap is open. Inside, three young men -- all in forward-looking apparel reminiscent of the sticker on the padlock -- are listening to some new form of music. It's like nothing you've ever heard. One of them looks at you.
Zh: 这顶帐篷不过是通过用一层油布盖着其他东西搭建而成。门帘敞开着。里面有三位青年——都穿着相当前卫的服饰，其造型令人联想起挂锁上的贴纸——他们正在听某种新式音乐。你从来没听过那种东西。其中一人注视着你。

En: The tent is just tarpaulin fabric covering a pile of stuff. The flap is open. Inside, three young men are listening to some new form of music. It's like nothing you've ever heard. One of them looks at you.
Zh: 帐篷只是用油布覆盖着一堆东西搭建而成。拉开挡板，三个年轻人正听着某种新潮的，你闻所未闻的音乐。其中一人朝你看来。

En: C'mon! Get in and close the flap behind you! The warm stuff is getting out!
Zh: “来啊！进来吧，把你身后的挡板拉上！暖气都要跑出去了。”

En: It's safe to assume this is their leader. Or at least he thinks he is.
Zh: 可以肯定这位就是他们的头领，至少他是这么认为的。

En: Sorry. He points his thumb at the lieutenant. "We barely have room for one."
Zh: “抱歉。”他用拇指指着警督。“我们挤不下另外的人了。”

En: You go ahead, I'm too old for this...
Zh: “你进去吧。我年纪大了，这种事不适合我……”

En: [Squeeze in.]
Zh: [挤进去。]

En: Squeeze in.
Zh: 挤进去。

En: No way. [Leave.]
Zh: “不行。”[离开。]

En: C'mon! Get in, the warm stuff is getting out.
Zh: “来啊！进来吧，暖气都要跑出去了。”

En: I'm sure you will feel right at home. I'll keep watch. He gestures for you to squeeze in.
Zh: “我相信你会感觉宾至如归的。我会一直看着的。”他朝你比了个手势，让你挤进去。

En: I'm actually not, he thinks. I just dislike delinquents.
Zh: 他的真实想法是：“其实我不老，我只是讨厌小混混。”

En: You leave the lieutenant outside and squeeze in.
Zh: 你把警督留在外面，自己挤了进去。
